ai_analysis.technical_context: stty.c hardcodes optimal delay flags for ASR-33 (Teletype 33), Teletype Model 37, DEC VT05, GE TermiNet 300, TI Silent 700, and Tektronix terminals. Each had distinct mechanical or electrical characteristics demanding specific CR/NL/FF/TAB delay settings to avoid character loss.
ai_analysis.human_story: When stty.c was written, terminals were physical machines with mechanical inertia. Sending NL too fast made an ASR-33 drop characters because the platen could not roll fast enough. The presets here are tombstones for the machines themselves; by the late 1980s CRT terminals replaced them all, and modern stty.c lost these names. Reading the table is reading a small museum of hardware that vanished.

appraiser_note: stty.c に実在の端末機種名が直接書かれている: ASR-33 / Teletype 37 / DEC VT05 / GE TermiNet 300 / TI Silent 700 / Tektronix。それぞれが必要とした遅延フラグ (CR/NL/FF/TAB/BS) の組み合わせが鎮座する。テレタイプの機械的慣性、CRT の画面消去遅延、サーマルプリンタの印字時間——全てが「機械の物理時間をソフトウェアで補う」設計に表れている。現代の stty.c からはこれらの機種名は消えた。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Baud rates from 0 (hang up) to 9600 are enumerated. Among them, 134.5 baud is unusual—an IBM 2741 terminal speed derived from Selectric typewriter mechanical timing. Both "134" and "134.5" map to B134, supporting historical input strings. EXTA/EXTB are reserved expansion slots; new speeds could not be added without kernel changes.
ai_analysis.human_story: The IBM 2741 Selectric ran at 134.5 baud, a non-integer baud rate that emerged from the typewriter's mechanical clock divisions. No other terminal needed half-baud precision, but Selectrics did. The 1979 stty.c quietly supported it via string-matching "134.5". Today's USB serial supports B921600+ via TIOCSSERIAL, leaving the Selectric speed as a numeric curiosity.

appraiser_note: ボーレート列挙は 0 (hang up) から 9600 まで——その中に「134.5」という小数の速度がある。IBM 2741 Selectric タイプライターの機械的クロックに由来する非整数 baud rate。`134` と `134.5` は同じ B134 定数にマップされ、文字列だけで両方を受け付けた。9600 baud が「最高速」だった時代の通信速度の地図。

ai_analysis.technical_context: '^X' notation in stty becomes a control character via (c & 037). 037 = 0x1F clears the upper 3 ASCII bits, mapping printable @-_ to control characters 0x00-0x1F. This mirrors ASCII's design: the Ctrl key physically clears the same bits. 'H' & 037 = 0x08 = Ctrl-H = Backspace.
ai_analysis.human_story: ASCII was designed so that the Ctrl key physically zeros bits 5 and 6 of the keypress. The 1979 stty.c uses '& 037' to do the same trick in software. Five decades later, 'stty erase ^H' still works the same way, and 'X' & 037 still produces Ctrl-X. Some abstractions outlive the hardware that inspired them.

appraiser_note: `stty erase ^H` のような `^X` 記法を `(*argv)[1] & 037` で制御文字に変換する古典テクニック。037 = 0x1F = 上位 3 ビットマスク。ASCII の設計で「Ctrl キーは物理的に上位ビットをクリアする」というのを、ソフトウェアで再現している。'H' & 037 = 0x08 = Ctrl-H = Backspace。半世紀後の現代 stty でも同じ記法・同じ実装が動いている。

ai_analysis.technical_context: df without arguments defaulted to /dev/rp0a (DEC RP-04/05/06 disk 0, partition a = root) and /dev/rp1g (disk 1, partition g = /usr). The 'rp' device prefix is hardcoded for VAX 11/780. No mount table, no /etc/mtab, no getmntent(). The convention was: ship the source, edit dargv[] to match your hardware.
ai_analysis.human_story: Bell-32V was Unix for the VAX 11/780. Anyone running Unix on a PDP-11 used /dev/rk* (RK-05) or /dev/rl* (RL-01/02) and had to recompile df with their device names baked in. The first five lines of df.c were 'hardware configuration' before /etc/mtab was invented.

appraiser_note: df は mount table を使わず、デフォルトファイル名 /dev/rp0a, /dev/rp1g をハードコード——VAX 11/780 の DEC RP-04/05/06 ディスク (0 番 a パーティション = root, 1 番 g パーティション = /usr) の前提。別ハードウェアなら dargv[] を編集して再コンパイル。/etc/mtab 以前の「ハードウェアごとに df.c の最初の 5 行を書き換える文化」の証拠。

ai_analysis.technical_context: df counts free blocks one at a time by walking the V7 free list. alloc() returns one block number per call. A million free blocks means a million calls. Each NICFREE (~50) blocks requires one extra disk read to fetch the next chain segment. Modern df reads a cached counter in statfs(2); 1979 df actually traverses the list.
ai_analysis.human_story: Reading the source is reading the algorithm: 'while(alloc()) i++;'. No cleverness, no optimization, just count. On a heavily-used RP-04 with hundreds of thousands of free blocks, df could take a minute. 4.2BSD's FFS cached the free-block count in the super block, collapsing O(n) into O(1). The while loop survives in the historical record as a relic of when computers were small and disks slow but the algorithm was honest.

appraiser_note: df の空きブロックカウントは `while(alloc()) i++;` の 3 行——free list を 1 個ずつ巡回する O(空きブロック数) アルゴリズム。100 万ブロックの空きなら 100 万回呼ぶ。50 個 (NICFREE) 取るごとにディスクを 1 回 read してチェーン補充。4.2BSD FFS が super block に累計値をキャッシュするまで、df は実際に free list を巡って数えるプログラムだった。

ai_analysis.technical_context: V7 file system's free list is a chain stored INSIDE the free blocks themselves. The super block holds the first ~50 free block numbers (NICFREE). When that array empties, the last block taken is itself read as a struct fblk containing the next 50 numbers. No bitmap, no separate free-list area—free blocks store their own metadata.
ai_analysis.human_story: Disk space was precious in 1979. Allocating a separate bitmap for free blocks would have wasted megabytes on a 100 MB disk. V7 file system stored the free-block chain inside the free blocks themselves—zero overhead for a free block. The cleverness comes at a cost: random allocation patterns fragment the chain badly, hurting sequential read performance. 4.2BSD FFS adopted cylinder groups + bitmaps because disks grew and the trade-off flipped.

appraiser_note: V7 file system の free list は「フリーブロック自身が次のフリーリストを格納する」再帰的設計。super block の s_free[NICFREE=50] が起点で、尽きたら最後に取ったブロックを `struct fblk` として read、その中の df_free[50] を super block に補充——分離した bitmap や free-list 領域を持たず、フリーブロックがその空き空間を自分のメタデータに使う。ディスク容量が貴重だった時代の節約設計。

ai_analysis.technical_context: du descends via chdir(fname) and returns via chdir("..") rather than carrying full paths in memory. The kernel's cwd holds the recursion state; user-space PATH_MAX (256 in V7) is never exhausted. Trade-off: not thread-safe (irrelevant in 1979), and chdir("..") failures leave the program in an unknown working directory—handled with absolute-path fallback.
ai_analysis.human_story: If you carry the full path string through recursion, deep trees blow the 256-byte buffer. The 1979 author let the kernel hold the recursion stack via cwd. Reading the source feels strange to modern eyes—the program walks the filesystem with its 'self' rather than its 'memory'.

appraiser_note: du の再帰は chdir(fname) で潜り、chdir("..") で戻る——カーネルの cwd を再帰のスタックに使う。ユーザー空間の PATH_MAX (256 in V7) を消費しない。失敗時は絶対パスでもう 1 回 chdir を試みるリカバリ付き。スレッドセーフではないが 1979 年は問題なし。「自分の位置」を memory ではなく kernel に持たせる、独特な再帰スタイル。

ai_analysis.technical_context: When a file has nlink > 1, du records its (dev, ino) in ml[1000] and skips later encounters. This prevents double-counting hardlinked files. The table is statically sized; ml[] silently stops adding past 1000 entries. Directories are excluded because their nlink is always >=2 (. and ..). Linear search means O(N²) worst case but acceptable in typical usage.
ai_analysis.human_story: ln a b creates two names for one inode but one disk allocation. Counting both would lie about disk usage. 1979 du built a 1000-entry table indexed by (dev, ino) and refused to count any duplicate. The author chose static array over dynamic allocation—classic Bell Labs minimalism. Modern du uses hash tables; the semantic guarantee is identical.

appraiser_note: ml[1000] の (dev, ino) テーブルでハードリンク二重カウントを防止。st_nlink > 1 のファイル (ディレクトリ除く) を見るたびに線形検索、未登録なら追加。テーブル満杯時はサイレント諦め——1000 個のハードリンクは 1979 年の VAX には十分。malloc を使わない静的配列の選択は Bell Labs ミニマリズム。

ai_analysis.technical_context: V7 Unix limited each process to NOFILE = 20 open file descriptors. Recursing 18+ directories deep would exhaust them. du's heuristic: if the just-opened fd is > 10 (over half the limit), close it immediately after reading and reopen on the next iteration, lseek-ing back to the saved offset. The recursion still works but at the cost of double the open() calls per directory.
ai_analysis.human_story: V7 had hard limits—NOFILE = 20 meant 20 simultaneously open files per process. A program that opened more crashed. du's defensive 'close at fd>10' is a microoptimization that turns 'crashable on 18-deep trees' into 'works on any depth at half speed'. The pragmatism is striking: fail gracefully by paying a constant-factor cost.

appraiser_note: V7 Unix の NOFILE = 20 制約への自己防衛——`if (dir > 10) close(dir);` で fd が枯渇しそうな時に一度閉じる。次のループで再 open し、保存していた offset へ lseek で復元する reopen-on-demand パターン。深さ 18 以上のディレクトリツリーでも fd が枯れずに巡回できる。コードを 2 倍呼ぶ代わりに、どんな深さでも動く実用性を取った 1979 年のプログラマの判断。

ai_analysis.technical_context: /etc/mtab is a fixed-size binary file holding struct mtab[16]. The kernel's actual mount table is separate; /etc/mtab is a userspace record maintained by the mount/umount commands. Consistency between kernel state and /etc/mtab depends entirely on the commands behaving correctly. read(2) loads the whole 1024 bytes in one call; no fopen, no parsing.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979 Unix split mount state into two: kernel mount table (truth) and /etc/mtab (record). The mount command writes both, hoping they agree. Linux later added /proc/mounts as a kernel-direct view, but /etc/mtab survived for decades as tradition. The 1024-byte binary file design reflects 'whatever read(2) returns is the parsed structure'—the shortest possible code path.

appraiser_note: /etc/mtab はテキストではなく struct mtab[16] のバイナリファイル——1 エントリ 64 バイト × 16 = 1,024 バイトを read(2) 1 回で読む。カーネルの mount table は別管理で、ユーザー空間の mount/umount コマンドが両方を更新する責任を持つ。整合性は「コマンドが正しく動くこと」に依存する二重管理。Linux 後年の /proc/mounts でカーネル直接ビューが入るまで、これが標準だった。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Updating /etc/mtab uses creat(2) (which truncates the file to length 0 before write). Between creat() and write() the file is empty; concurrent readers see partial data. Two parallel mount commands can lost-update each other. The fix—write tmp + rename—is unavailable because rename(2) does not exist in Bell-32V; rename arrives with 4.2BSD (1983).
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979 mount.c knows the race exists and accepts it. System administrators ran mount sequentially from /etc/rc; parallel mount invocations were rare. The pragmatic mindset: ship code that works for the common case, document the limitation in tribal knowledge. Four years later rename(2) makes atomic update possible; mount.c slowly transitions.

appraiser_note: /etc/mtab 更新は creat(2) で全書き直し——atomic ではない。creat の瞬間にファイルは長さ 0 になり、write 完了まで他プロセスから不完全な状態が見える。2 つの mount を並行実行すれば lost update。`tmpfile + rename` で atomic にできるが、rename(2) は 4.2BSD (1983) まで存在しない。1979 年は「管理者がシーケンシャルに mount すれば問題ない」という規律で運用された。

ai_analysis.technical_context: If mtab[16] is full, the for-loop finishes without ever entering the 'found-empty' branch, falling through to the trailing exit(0). The mount(2) syscall succeeded—so the kernel has the new mount—but /etc/mtab does not record it. No warning, no error. The caller assumes success unless mount(2) itself failed.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979 VAX systems mounted 2-3 partitions, not 16. The author chose to skip the 'mtab full' error path entirely; it would never fire in practice. Linux Docker environments today mount 100+, and that limit is wholly unimaginable in 1979. The silence reflects the era's typical scale—if you hit 16 mounts you would already be doing something weird, and discover the silence yourself.

appraiser_note: mtab[16] が満杯なら for ループは何も処理せず exit(0) に到達する——警告なし、errno なし、ユーザーは成功と勘違いする。1979 年の VAX は 2-3 個マウントするだけだったので、16 個の上限は現実に起きない設計。mount(2) は成功するのでカーネルは新マウントを保持、/etc/mtab だけが古いまま——カーネルと /etc/mtab の不整合が静かに発生する稀ケース。

ai_analysis.technical_context: umount.c calls sync() as its very first action. Unix buffer cache delays writes; if a filesystem is detached while dirty buffers remain, the data is lost. sync() requests a flush of all dirty buffers to disk before umount(2) detaches the filesystem from kernel mount table. Note: sync() does not wait for completion (best-effort); the safer fsync(2) per-file existed but no system-wide synchronous flush.
ai_analysis.human_story: mount.c has no sync(); umount.c has sync() in line 11—the first action. The asymmetry tells you which operation is dangerous. Reading a filesystem after mount is harmless; detaching one while writes are pending eats your data. The 1979 author moved sync() to the very top of umount.c so no code path could skip it.

appraiser_note: umount.c はプログラム冒頭で sync() を呼ぶ——buffer cache のダーティバッファを全部 flush してから umount(2) でカーネルからファイルシステムを切り離す。mount.c には sync() がない非対称性が「unmount の方がデータ損失リスクが高い」事実を物語る。ただし sync() は best-effort で、完了を待たない。fsync(2) が個別ファイル用にあったが、システム全体の同期版は無い時代。

ai_analysis.technical_context: To remove an mtab entry, the code zeroes 64 bytes (file[32] + spec[32]) one byte at a time via for-loop. memset() is not yet standardized (arrives with ANSI C 1989); BSD's bzero() arrives with 4.2BSD (1983). 1979 Bell-32V writes the loop inline. The zeroed slot becomes 'empty' and will be reused by the next mount, mirroring mount.c's 'if file[0]==0' check.
ai_analysis.human_story: Modern C says memset(mp, 0, sizeof(*mp)). 1979 says for(p1=mp->file; p1<&mp->file[NAMSIZ*2];) *p1++=0;. Four lines of pointer arithmetic. The library function was waiting in the future; the loop is timeless. Either way, the entry's identity dissolves into 64 zero bytes.

appraiser_note: mtab エントリの削除は file[32] と spec[32] の合計 64 バイトを for ループで 1 バイトずつ 0 に塗る。memset() は ANSI C 1989 まで、bzero() は 4.2BSD (1983) まで存在しない。1979 年は素朴に `for (p1 = mp->file; p1 < &mp->file[NAMSIZ*2];) *p1++ = 0;` の 4 行で済ます——標準ライブラリの不在が、コードに「型に縛られない直接性」を与える。

ai_analysis.technical_context: By the time the 'not in mount table' message fires, umount(2) has already succeeded—kernel has detached the filesystem. The warning signals a state mismatch between kernel mount table and userspace /etc/mtab. Causes: parallel umount lost-update, prior mount-command crash, manual /etc/mtab edit. The kernel detach is irreversible by then; the message is purely diagnostic.
ai_analysis.human_story: Unlike mount.c's silent overflow, umount.c explicitly warns when /etc/mtab is out of sync. The 1979 author judged 'something is missing where it should be' as more dangerous than 'no room left'. Reading the source teaches: the same bug template (mtab inconsistency) can produce silence in one branch and a warning in the other, based on which direction matters more.

appraiser_note: umount(2) が成功した後、/etc/mtab に該当エントリが無ければ「not in mount table」と警告。これは「カーネルからは切り離したが、ユーザー空間 /etc/mtab には記録が残っている／既に消されていた」という二重管理の不整合の検出。前回鑑定した mount.c の「テーブル満杯はサイレント諦め」と対照的——「あるべきものがない」のほうが「入れる場所がない」より深刻という判断。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Instead of using utime(2), Bell-32V touch reads one byte, seeks back to offset 0, and writes the same byte. The file content is unchanged, but the write(2) call updates st_mtime and st_ctime. Why not utime(2)? Because utime's permission model was inconsistent—NULL argument required write access, but specific time required ownership. The read+write trick guarantees a simple rule: 'if you can write to the file, you can touch it.'
ai_analysis.human_story: Modern touch calls utimensat() in one line. The 1979 author chose a four-step dance—open, read, lseek, write—to dodge utime's permission quirks. The 'change nothing while changing everything' trick is what programmers do when the API does not quite fit. Reading the source teaches: the elegance is in what is not used.

appraiser_note: 1979 年の touch は utime(2) を使わない——1 バイト read して lseek(fd, 0L, 0) で先頭に戻し、同じ 1 バイトを write することで mtime を更新する妙技。内容は 1 バイトも変わらないが file system は「書き込みが起きた」と認識する。utime(2) の権限モデルが不一致 (NULL なら書込権限、特定時刻なら所有者) なため、「書き込めるなら touch できる」を保証する read+write のトリックを選んだ。utimensat() 1 行で済む現代から見ると、4 ステップの「変えずに変える」舞踊が美しい。

ai_analysis.technical_context: The 1-byte read trick fails on empty files (nothing to read). touch handles this by routing st_size==0 files through the same 'create' path that handles missing files. creat(0666) truncates if needed (no harm—file was already empty) and updates mtime. Two distinct entry points (missing file, empty file) converge on one creat() call.
ai_analysis.human_story: Edge cases reveal the structure of a solution. The read+write trick covers files with data; the creat path covers files without. The 1979 author saw both branches and chose to merge them via 'goto create'. The result is robust without being complicated—two paths in, one path out.

appraiser_note: 1 バイト read のトリックは空ファイル (st_size == 0) では失敗するため、空ファイルは creat() 経路に自動分岐する。「ファイルが存在しない」と「ファイルが空」の 2 つのケースが creat(name, 0666) という同じ出口に集約される設計。 0666 は umask で 0644 になる。 既存空ファイルを truncate しても結果は同じ (空のまま) で mtime が更新される。 2 つの入口を 1 つの処理で受ける、エッジケース統合の美しさ。

ai_analysis.technical_context: The -c flag suppresses file creation: when the target does not exist, touch -c silently does nothing instead of creating an empty file. The implementation is a single static int and a strcmp in the loop. Behavior matches POSIX touch -c half a century later. The 1979 implementation processes -c left-to-right (touch a -c b applies -c only to b), differing from modern getopt that pre-collects options.
ai_analysis.human_story: Stable APIs are precious. touch -c worked the same in 1979, 1989, 1999, 2009, 2019, 2025. The implementation grew in complexity (timestamp granularity, symlink handling) but the surface stayed identical. The 7 lines of -c logic in the 1979 source are still the spec of touch -c.

appraiser_note: -c フラグでファイル作成を抑制。strcmp() + static int force の 7 行で実装、半世紀後の POSIX touch -c と同じ動作を 1979 年から提供。引数を左から右に順次解釈する素朴さ (touch a -c b で b だけに効く) は GNU coreutils touch で先処理に変更されたが、基本動作は不変。安定 API の結晶。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Usage string lists only +_n[lbc] [file]. No -f, no -F, no --follow. tail -f arrives in 4.3BSD (1986). Before that, tail was strictly 'show last N lines then exit'. Log-monitoring use case had not yet driven the design of follow mode. inotify-like efficient APIs were also absent—follow would have to be implemented via polling sleep+lseek+read.
ai_analysis.human_story: Today, 'tail -f /var/log/messages' is a sysadmin reflex. In 1979 it did not exist. The 163-line tail.c is a snapshot of pre-monitoring computing—you ran tail, saw the result, moved on. Seven years later, BSD added -f and the meaning of 'watching a log' was born. Reading the source teaches: the absence of one flag marks a whole era.

appraiser_note: 1979 年の tail.c には -f フラグが無い。usage は「tail +_n[lbc] [file]」だけ。-f (follow) は 4.3BSD (1986) で追加され、ログ監視という新しい用途を生んだ。inotify 以前は polling (sleep + lseek + read) でしか実現できず、そもそも「ログを継続監視する」発想自体が 1980 年代の発明。163 行の tail.c は「N 行表示して終わる」純粋な姿——1 つのフラグの不在が、1 つの時代を物語る。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tail detects pipes via lseek(0, 0, SEEK_CUR) + ESPIPE. For regular files: fstat to get size, lseek(0, -di, SEEK_END) to jump backwards to the last LBIN-1 bytes. For pipes: read forward LBIN bytes at a time, overwriting the buffer until EOF—the final iteration leaves the last 4096 bytes in memory. Memory cost is fixed (LBIN=4097) regardless of input size.
ai_analysis.human_story: Two paths, same destination: 'keep the last 4096 bytes'. The author thought through both cases—regular file (fast: seek once) and pipe (slow: read all, but bounded memory). The trick of using lseek with SEEK_CUR as a 'probe' to detect piped input, without disturbing the file position, is decades-old Unix wisdom still in use today.

appraiser_note: tail は piped 検出に `lseek(0, 0, SEEK_CUR)` + ESPIPE を使う——副作用のないシークでファイルポインタを動かさず、エラーで「パイプか否か」を判定する。通常ファイルなら `lseek(0, -di, SEEK_END)` で末尾から逆向きシーク (高速)、パイプならバッファ LBIN=4097 を read で塗り直す (固定メモリ)。「末尾 4 KB を維持する」が 2 通りのパスで実現される、賢い設計。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tail -n: show last n lines/bytes/blocks. tail +n: skip first n-1 lines and show the rest. Same numeric argument, opposite meaning. The fromend flag drives the branch; +n uses 'seek from beginning' code (lseek + read forward), -n uses 'seek from end' (lseek SEEK_END + reverse scan). Default is '-10l'.
ai_analysis.human_story: Two complementary operations sharing one CLI surface. tail +n is essentially 'head -inverse'—skip the beginning, show the rest. tail -n is 'show the tail'. The 1979 design unified them under one command via the + / - prefix. POSIX preserved this; modern tail still accepts both. The symmetry hints at a deeper truth: file traversal is one operation, just with direction.

appraiser_note: tail +n と tail -n は同じ数値で逆方向の意味——「先頭から n 行目以降を全部」 vs 「末尾から n 行」。fromend フラグで分岐し、+ は seek-from-beginning、- は seek-from-end のコードパスへ。1979 年から半世紀、POSIX も維持する CLI 表面。デフォルトは `-10l` で「末尾 10 行」。+ と - の対称性が「ファイル走査は方向違いの 1 操作」という設計思想を表す。

ai_analysis.technical_context: size reads struct exec via fread, then prints text + data + BSS sizes — the three-tuple of an executable: machine code, initialized data, uninitialized data. BSS comes from IBM 7090 'Block Started by Symbol'. The same three sections appear in ELF (.text/.data/.bss), Mach-O (__TEXT/__DATA/__BSS), and PE (.text/.data/.bss) half a century later. The concept survives unchanged.
ai_analysis.human_story: Three numbers, one line. That was the executable file's self-introduction in 1979. ELF's .text section, Mach-O's __TEXT segment, PE's .text section — every modern executable format inherits the same trinity. The 46-line size.c is the ancestor pose: 'this is your code, this is your data, this is your zero-filled scratch space.'

appraiser_note: size.c は struct exec を fread() で読み、 text + data + BSS の三つ組を表示する。「機械語コード + 初期化済みデータ + 未初期化データ」 という分類は IBM 7090 アセンブラの BSS (Block Started by Symbol) に遡り、 1979 年の a.out から ELF (.text/.data/.bss)、 Mach-O (__TEXT/__DATA/__BSS)、 PE (.text/.data/.bss) まで半世紀継承される。 BSS はディスク上に値を持たず実行時に 0 で埋められる——「未初期化」 という抽象が物理的に定着した瞬間。 46 行のコマンドが見せる「実行ファイルの自己紹介」。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Four octal magic numbers identify a.out variants: 0407 (OMAGIC: no sharing, text+data contiguous), 0410 (NMAGIC: read-only text, page boundary), 0411 (ZMAGIC: demand-paged, fast startup), 0405 (overlay, PDP-11 era). Each represents a linker mode — ld default produces 0411, ld -N produces 0410, ld -n produces 0407. The numbers are PDP-11 instruction-shaped on purpose: the first 2 bytes could be safely executed as code in certain modes.
ai_analysis.human_story: 0407, 0410, 0411 — three numbers that defined Unix executables for 20 years. They look like accidents but are deliberate: ld picked OMAGIC/NMAGIC/ZMAGIC based on whether you wanted text sharing, read-only protection, or demand paging. ELF arrived in 1995 and replaced them with 0x7F 'E' 'L' 'F' — clearer, but the design pattern (magic-number-identifies-format) was already 16 years old by then.

appraiser_note: size.c は 4 つのマジック数 (A_MAGIC1〜A_MAGIC4 = 0407/0410/0411/0405) を null-terminated 配列で許容する。 0407=OMAGIC (共有なし)、 0410=NMAGIC (read-only text)、 0411=ZMAGIC (demand-paged)、 0405=overlay (PDP-11 名残)——それぞれ ld の異なるリンクモードに対応する。 8 進数で書かれるのは PDP-11 のワード境界 (16 bit) と整合性が良いから——`0407` は 10 進 263、 ビット表記で `100 000 111`、 PDP-11 命令の上位 9 ビットに似た形。 ELF (1995, `0x7F 'E' 'L' 'F'`) に置き換わるまで 16 年、 Unix 実行ファイルの DNA だった。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Run size with no arguments, it targets 'a.out'. The default reflects 1971 PDP-7 Unix where assembler 'as' produced 'a.out' (assembler output) directly. When ld was added, the default output name stayed. Modern cc/gcc still emits 'a.out' without -o, half a century later. The implementation overwrites argv[0] = "a.out", increments argc, decrements argv — as if 'size a.out' had been typed.
ai_analysis.human_story: './a.out' is the first executable every C programmer runs. The name predates the language — it came from 1971 PDP-7 assembler output. When dmr added cc, when ld was rewritten, when ELF replaced a.out internally — the default output name survived. Reading size.c's three-line default-argument trick teaches: traditions stick because they are cheap to keep and expensive to break.

appraiser_note: size を引数なしで実行するとデフォルトで `a.out` を対象にする——3 行のトリック (`*argv = "a.out"; argc++; --argv;`) で 「size a.out と打ったかのように」 ループ条件を整える。 `a.out` の名は 1971 年 PDP-7 Unix のアセンブラ `as` の出力 (assembler output) に由来し、 `ld` が後から導入されてもデフォルト出力名は変わらず、 現代の `cc` / `gcc` も `-o` 指定なしなら `a.out` を出す——50 年以上変わらない慣習。 「`./a.out` を実行する」 ジェスチャーは C プログラマの第一言語、 その起源が 46 行のコマンドに刻まれている。

ai_analysis.technical_context: strip's essence: zero out three fields in struct exec — a_syms (symbol table size), a_trsize (text relocation size), a_drsize (data relocation size). The remaining a_text + a_data is the minimum runnable executable. Idempotent: running strip twice prints 'already stripped' instead of failing. The 'release binary has no symbols' Unix culture starts here.
ai_analysis.human_story: Strip a binary, file size drops by half. The trick? Three numbers go to zero. Symbol tables and relocation info are debugging luxuries — once you ship, you don't need them. The 1979 strip is the cultural origin: 'shipped binaries are slimmed binaries.' Modern strip uses objcopy and debug-link tricks, but the 3-zero principle survives unchanged.

appraiser_note: strip の本質は struct exec の 3 フィールド (`a_syms` シンボルテーブル、 `a_trsize` text relocation、 `a_drsize` data relocation) を 0 にすること——実行に必要な `a_text + a_data` だけが残る。 `a_syms = a_trsize = a_drsize = 0` という 1 行が、 「リリース用バイナリは symbol を削る」 という Unix 文化の起源。 3 フィールド全 0 で 「既に strip 済み」 を検出し idempotent に振る舞うのも気が利く——同じファイルを 2 回 strip しても問題なし。 (long) キャストで `a_text + a_data` を計算するのは 16 ビット越え対策、 size.c でも同じパターン。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Three signals ignored before touching files: SIGHUP (terminal disconnect, important for dial-up), SIGINT (Ctrl-C), SIGQUIT (Ctrl-\). Why? strip rewrites the original binary via creat() — interruption between truncate and write would leave a zero-byte file. SIGKILL (9) can't be caught, kernel panic isn't survivable, but the three user-triggered signals can be ignored. rename(2) for atomic replace arrives in 4.2BSD (1983) — until then, signal masking was the best available 'atomic' approximation.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979 was dial-up era. You'd phone into the timeshare, type strip /usr/bin/mything, and if the line dropped mid-rewrite, the binary would die. SIGHUP ignore says: 'I know you might lose the connection — let me finish first.' Small ceremonies of safety. Modern strip uses rename for atomic replace, but the spirit (block interruption during dangerous regions) is the same.

appraiser_note: strip は元ファイルを creat() で truncate + 書き戻しする——その区間で中断されるとバイナリが空になる。 そのため SIGHUP (端末切断、 dial-up 時代の必須)、 SIGINT (Ctrl-C)、 SIGQUIT (Ctrl-\) の 3 シグナルを SIG_IGN で無視する。 SIGKILL や kernel panic には無防備だが、 「ユーザー操作による中断」 だけは確実にブロック。 rename(2) による atomic 置換は 4.2BSD (1983) で初登場——それまでは signal mask が「**atomic 風**」 を実現する最良の手段だった。 dial-up 時代の防衛コード、 小さな儀式の集合。

ai_analysis.technical_context: mktemp('/tmp/sXXXXX') replaces 5 X's with PID-based unique chars — V7 convention where each tool used its initial as prefix (cc → /tmp/cXXXXX, as → /tmp/aXXXXX, strip → /tmp/sXXXXX). Copy buffer is 512 bytes — exactly V7's BSIZE (filesystem block size = disk sector size = magic number all aligned). Modern glibc deprecates mktemp() due to symlink race attacks, but in 1979 symlinks didn't exist (4.2BSD adds symlink(2) in 1983).
ai_analysis.human_story: Two design fingerprints from 1979: (1) /tmp/sXXXXX — each Unix tool names its temp files with its initial. cc, as, strip, even tar. A naming convention that doubled as debugging info: see /tmp/c12345, you know cc is running. (2) 512-byte buffers everywhere — disk sectors were 512, FS blocks were 512, the number became invisible. Modern code uses BUFSIZ or block_size queries; 1979 code wrote 512 because everyone knew.

appraiser_note: `mktemp("/tmp/sXXXXX")` は V7 の慣習——各 Unix ツールがイニシャルで temp file をプレフィックスする (cc → /tmp/cXXXXX、 as → /tmp/aXXXXX)。 X は PID で置換される。 現代の glibc は symbolic link race attack のため deprecated だが、 1979 年は symlink(2) すら存在しなかった (4.2BSD で 1983 年追加)——時代背景が API 安全性を決定する例。 コピーバッファは **512 バイト固定**——V7 ファイルシステムの `BSIZE = 512`、 ディスクセクタ 512 バイトと完全一致。 「512 は神聖な数」 として `#define` なく裸で書く 1979 流。

ai_analysis.technical_context: ar packs 7 distinct commands (m, r, x, t, d, p, q) into one binary via function pointer dispatch. main() parses first arg char-by-char, calls setcom(fun) which stores fn pointer in global comfun, detects double-set via the same global, then invokes (*comfun)() after arg parsing completes. setcom enforces mutual exclusion: 'only one of [mrxtdpq] allowed'. The man = "mrxtdpq" string doubles as the error message list — adding a command auto-updates the help text.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979 had no OOP, but function pointers gave the same 'pluggable behavior' abstraction. ar's main() became a dispatcher, each command became an isolated function — the structure that powers qsort()'s comparator, signal()'s handler, and eventually C++ virtual methods. Reading ar.c teaches: K&R C wasn't object-oriented, but it was already function-oriented.

appraiser_note: ar は 7 つの動詞 (`m` move、 `r` replace、 `x` extract、 `t` table、 `d` delete、 `p` print、 `q` quick append) を 1 バイナリで提供する。 関数ポインタ `int (*comfun)();` をグローバルに置き、 引数解析時に `setcom(rcmd)` などで設定、 引数処理後に `(*comfun)()` で発火。 `setcom` は二重設定を検出して排他制御する——OOP なき 1979 年でも、 関数ポインタは「動作を変数として渡す」 抽象を提供していた。 `man = "mrxtdpq"` 文字列がエラーメッセージにも使われる DRY の先取り。 後の qsort 比較関数、 signal ハンドラ、 C++ virtual method dispatch、 std::function まで続く系譜の初期例。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Each archive member starts with struct ar_hdr (26 bytes): ar_name[14] + ar_date + ar_uid + ar_gid + ar_mode + ar_size. The 14-char name matches V7's DIRSIZ — filesystem directory entries also held 14-char filenames. The copy loop `if(arbuf.ar_name[i] = *cp) cp++` is K&R C idiom — assignment-in-condition copies until NUL, pads short names with NUL after. 4.2BSD (1983) introduced 255-char filenames; GNU ar later added '#1/' prefix for long names. The basic ar layout (name+date+uid+gid+mode+size) has survived unchanged for half a century.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `gcc -lm` today and libm.a gets linked. That .a file is an archive in the exact format defined by ar.c in 1979. struct ar_hdr's 14-char ar_name reflects a deeper constraint — V7 filesystem only allowed 14-char filenames. So if you had a 14-char file, ar could store it; longer files were impossible to put in archives. When 4.2BSD lifted the filesystem limit, ar had to invent workarounds. The 14-char constant is the fossil of an extinct filesystem.

appraiser_note: ar アーカイブの各メンバーは `struct ar_hdr` (26 バイト) + データ本体。 `ar_name[14]` は V7 ファイルシステムの `DIRSIZ = 14` (ディレクトリエントリの最大ファイル名長) と一致——「ファイル名は 14 文字以内」 という V7 の物理制約をそのまま反映している。 4.2BSD (1983) のロングファイル名 (255 文字) 登場でようやく制約が緩み、 GNU ar は `#1/` 接頭辞で 14 文字超対応。 コピーループの `if(arbuf.ar_name[i] = *cp) cp++` は代入式を if 条件として使う K&R C 流——NUL で停止し、 残り領域は NUL で埋まる。 1 行で「コピー、 NUL 終端、 長さ制限」 を同時実装。

ai_analysis.technical_context: PDP-11 and VAX read memory in 16-bit words and trap on misaligned word access (bus error). ar archive members aligned to 2-byte boundaries via IODD/OODD: if member size is odd, pad with one extra byte so the next member starts on word boundary. IODD adjusts read size, OODD adjusts write size — when copying with header rewrites (HEAD flag), input and output sizes can differ. The padding byte content is undefined; alignment is what matters. The ar_size decrement is always 512 (not i) — at loop end, ar_size goes negative, the > 0 condition stops the loop. Modern ar format (SVR4, BSD) still requires even alignment despite PDP-11 being gone for 30+ years.
ai_analysis.human_story: PDP-11 retired in the 1990s but its alignment habit lives in every modern .a file. Hardware shape leaves fingerprints on data formats. The IODD/OODD mechanism doesn't make sense unless you understand 1979 hardware: 16-bit words, bus errors on misaligned access. Today's GNU ar still pads to even bytes — not because anyone needs it, but because compatibility outlives the hardware that motivated it. Binary formats are archaeology.

appraiser_note: PDP-11/VAX は 16 ビットワード単位でメモリアクセスし、 奇数アドレスから 2 バイト読むと bus error で例外発生。 そのため ar はメンバー間を **2 バイト境界**に揃える——`IODD` (input odd) で読み側、 `OODD` (output odd) で書き側の奇数長を 1 バイト切り上げ。 ヘッダ書換時 (HEAD flag) は入出力サイズが異なるので、 独立に制御できる設計。 パディングバイトの中身は不定、 アラインメントが優先。 `ar_size -= 512` (常に 512) でループ最後に負値で停止する K&R 流。 PDP-11 が引退して 30 年経っても、 現代の GNU ar / SVR4 ar 仕様は偶数アラインメントを継承——ハードウェアの形がバイナリ形式に痕跡を残す典型例。

ai_analysis.technical_context: m4 stores macro definitions in a 199-slot hash table with chaining. 199 is prime — the comment says so explicitly. Why prime? Hash function is character sum mod HSHSIZ, and a composite modulus would cause clustering on input patterns (e.g. all-even ASCII). Prime modulus spreads collisions uniformly. Hash function uses only addition — no XOR, no multiply, no shift. Collisions are handled by linked-list chaining (the next pointer in struct nlist). Inline string comparison (`while (*s1++ == *s2)`) avoids strcmp() call overhead. 199 slots × avg 1-2 entries ≈ 200-400 macros — matches typical sendmail.cf macro sets.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1977 K&R wrote down what every modern hash table textbook teaches. 199 is prime. Chaining handles collisions. The hash function is simple because the modulus is prime. Python's dict, JavaScript's Map, Go's map, Rust's HashMap — all start from this skeleton. The 'prime' comment in 1977 source code is the first textbook page of computer science, written in code.

appraiser_note: m4 のマクロ辞書は **199 スロット素数ハッシュテーブル + chaining**。 コメントに 「prime」 と明記されており、 ハッシュ関数 `hshval += *s1++; hshval %= HSHSIZ` (文字コード単純和の mod 199) が偏りなく分散するための数学的選択。 合成数 modulus だと特定パターン (例: 全偶数 ASCII) で衝突が偏るが、 素数なら均等分散。 ハッシュは加算のみ——XOR/乗算/シフトなし、 K&R 流のシンプル志向。 衝突は linked list (struct nlist の next ポインタ) で吸収。 lookup の `while (*s1++ == *s2)` インライン比較は strcmp() の関数呼び出しコストを嫌う 1979 流。 sendmail.cf マクロセットの 200〜400 個に対応する設計。

ai_analysis.technical_context: 23 builtins (define, undefine, ifdef, ifelse, eval, include, sinclude, syscmd, incr, len, substr, index, translit, divert, undivert, divnum, dnl, dumpdef, errprint, shift, changequote, maketemp, dodef) dispatched via raw pointer comparison: `dp == defloc`. defloc is the def-string pointer returned from install(), so 'is this the define builtin?' becomes 'is dp the same pointer as defloc?' — O(1) ID check, no strcmp. If user redefines define via `define(define, foo)`, the new def-string has a different pointer and falls through to user-defined branch. The else clause handles $1-$9 parameter substitution via pbstr (push-back to input).
ai_analysis.human_story: 23 builtins, dispatched by raw pointer equality. No switch on strings, no hash lookup at expansion time — just (void*) == (void*). The trick: install() returns a pointer that IS the identity. Once you have it, comparison is one machine instruction. Modern OOP method dispatch arrives at the same place via vtables. K&R got there with 23 if-else lines.

appraiser_note: m4 は 23 ビルトイン (define/undefine/ifdef/ifelse/eval/include/sinclude/syscmd/incr/len/substr/index/translit/divert/undivert/divnum/dnl/dumpdef/errprint/shift/changequote/maketemp) を **関数ポインタアドレスの直接比較** (`dp == defloc`) で分岐する。 defloc は `install("define", eoa)` の返り値——def 文字列のポインタが ID として機能する。 `switch(*dp)` の文字列比較ではなく、 ポインタの数値比較なので O(1)。 ユーザーが define を再定義すると新しい def 文字列ポインタになり、 else 節 (`$1-$9` パラメータ置換) に落ちる。 23 個の if-else でも実用上十分速い。 後の OOP の vtable と同じ抽象を、 1977 年のポインタ比較で実現。

ai_analysis.technical_context: m4's most original feature: 9 numbered output streams. divert(N) routes output to stream N (0-9), undivert merges them back into the main flow. Implementation hack: tempname = '/tmp/m4aXXXXX' (mktemp output), then mutate the 7th char to 'a'+N for stream N — one string buffer represents 10 file names. At exit (delexit), iterate i=1..9, read each diversion file, write to stdout, unlink. Enables spatial rearrangement of text — sendmail.mc declares headers/rules/macros/mailers in any order, but the generated sendmail.cf comes out in the right order because each section is diverted to its own stream and merged at the end.
ai_analysis.human_story: m4's diversion is the killer feature that made sendmail configurable. Without it, the order you wrote macros in .mc had to match the order they appear in .cf — unworkable for complex configs. With divert(N), you write 'put this in section 2', 'put this in section 5', and m4 stitches them back together. The implementation is a hack: mutate the 7th byte of a string to spawn 10 file names from one template. Cheap, brilliant, alive 50 years later.

appraiser_note: m4 の最も独創的な機能が **9 ストリーム diversion**——`divert(N)` で出力を 0〜9 の番号付きストリームに振り分け、 `undivert` で本流に結合。 実装は驚くべきハック：`/tmp/m4aXXXXX` という文字列の **7 文字目を `'a'+f` で上書き**して `/tmp/m4bXXXXX`, `/tmp/m4cXXXXX`, ... と 10 個のファイル名を派生させる。 1 つの文字列バッファで 10 個のファイル名を扱う省メモリ手法。 終了時に i=1〜9 順に読み出して stdout に結合、 unlink。 sendmail.mc の宣言順序とは無関係に sendmail.cf を正しい順で生成できる秘密はこの diversion。 「文章を空間的に並び替える」 機能、 GNU autoconf も継承。

ai_analysis.technical_context: putbak/getchr macro pair: getchr() reads from pushback stack first (ip>cur_ip), falls back to file input. putbak() pushes a char to the stack. pbstr() pushes a whole string in reverse order, so subsequent getchr() calls retrieve it forward. SAVS=4096 byte pushback buffer. Why pushback? Macro expansion may produce text containing more macros — instead of writing to output and losing rescan ability, the expansion result is fed back into the input stream where m4 re-scans it. This is the core of macro processing: expansion results re-enter expansion. After-expansion text is indistinguishable from original input.
ai_analysis.human_story: The trick that makes macro processors actually work: pretend the expansion result was always there. `define(foo, bar(x))` — when you expand foo, you get bar(x), but bar is itself a macro. If you wrote bar(x) to output, you've lost. Instead, push bar(x) back to the input. The next getchr() sees b, a, r, (, x, ) just like reading from a file. The macro processor's pretend-rescan-is-real engine. Every modern macro system (Lisp macroexpand, C preprocessor, Rust macro_rules!) descends from this idea.

appraiser_note: m4 のマクロ展開の核は **「展開結果を入力に戻す**」 プッシュバック。 `getchr()` は ip > cur_ip ならスタックから 1 文字、 そうでなければファイルから読む。 `putbak(c)` は `*ip++ = c` で 1 文字積む。 `pbstr(str)` は文字列を **逆順に push** することで、 後の getchr() が正順で受け取る——LIFO スタックの典型活用。 SAVS=4096 byte の pushback バッファ。 マクロ展開結果が **さらにマクロを含む**場合 (`define(foo, bar(x))` の bar)、 出力に直接書くと再スキャンできない——入力に戻すことで「あたかも元から入力にあった」 ように再スキャンできる。 マクロ展開結果が展開対象に再入する、 マクロプロセッサの心臓。 Lisp macroexpand、 C プリプロセッサ、 Rust macro_rules! の祖。

ai_analysis.technical_context: m4 delegates arithmetic evaluation to a 94-line yacc grammar (m4y.y). doeval() sets global `pe` to the expression string, calls yyparse(). Grammar handles logical ops (|, &, !), comparisons (>, >=, <, <=, ==, !=), arithmetic (+, -, *, /, %), exponentiation (**), unary minus, parentheses, integers. Power is implemented inline: `for ($$=1; $3-->0; $$ *= $1)` — multiplication loop, no libm. yylex() advances pe pointer; peek() handles 2-char operators (>=, <=, ==, etc.). This separation (m4 does text, yacc does arithmetic) is the same pattern awk uses.
ai_analysis.human_story: When m4 needs to do math, it doesn't try to parse arithmetic in C — it generates a yacc parser and calls yyparse(). 94 lines of yacc grammar gives a full expression evaluator: precedence, associativity, all operators including exponentiation. ifelse(eval(i<10), 1, 0) becomes possible because eval is a real expression evaluator, not a hack. The same pattern shows up in awk (yacc grammar, C actions). Specialized tool composition is the Unix philosophy made tangible.

appraiser_note: m4 は算術式評価を **94 行の yacc 文法 (m4y.y)** に委譲する。 `doeval()` がグローバル `pe` ポインタに式を渡し、 `yyparse()` を呼ぶ——成功すれば `evalval` に値が入る。 文法は論理演算 (|, &, !)、 比較 (>, >=, <, <=, ==, !=)、 加減乗除剰余、 冪乗 (**)、 単項マイナス、 括弧、 整数を完全サポート。 `POWER` は `for ($$=1; $3-->0; $$ *= $1)` の inline ループで実装、 libm 呼び出しなし。 yylex() は pe ポインタを進めながらトークン化、 peek() で 2 文字演算子を判別。 m4 (テキスト) + yacc (算術) の分離は awk と同じパターン——「特化したツールを組み合わせる」 Unix 哲学の具現化。 ifelse(eval(i<10), ...) のような構造的繰り返しを可能にする。

ai_analysis.technical_context: /etc/utmp is the binary file holding 'who is logged in right now'. struct utmp has 3 fields (V7): ut_line[8] (tty), ut_name[8] (login name), ut_time (time). 20 bytes per record. login(1) writes a record on login, init(1M) NULs ut_name on logout (ut_time persists). who.c checks ut_name[0] == '\0' to skip logged-out slots. /usr/adm/wtmp is the append-only history version that last(1) reads. Linux/BSD still maintain /var/run/utmp and /var/log/wtmp with the same struct utmp (extended to utmpx in POSIX, ut_user[32] instead of ut_name[8]). 50 years, same data model.
ai_analysis.human_story: Today's `who` on a Linux server reads /var/run/utmp. The 1979 who.c read /etc/utmp. Same file, same record structure, same purpose. The only changes: longer name fields, more fields (PID, hostname for remote logins), but the core 'tty + user + time' triple stays. 50 years of Unix tradition starts here. ssh into any modern server, type `who`, you're touching a data model designed in 1979.

appraiser_note: /etc/utmp は「今ログイン中のユーザー」 を保持するバイナリファイル。 V7 の struct utmp は 3 フィールド (`ut_line[8]` tty、 `ut_name[8]` ログイン名、 `ut_time`)、 20 バイト/レコード。 login(1) がログイン時に書き、 init(1M) がログアウト時に `ut_name` を NUL でクリア (ut_time は残る)。 who.c は `ut_name[0] == '\0'` でログアウト済みスロットをスキップ。 /usr/adm/wtmp は同じ構造の追記式ログ、 last(1) が読む。 50 年後の Linux/BSD も /var/run/utmp と /var/log/wtmp を維持、 POSIX utmpx で ut_user[32] へ拡張されたが基本構造 (tty + user + time) は不変。 fread() で 1 レコードずつ読む素朴な実装で 50 年継承。

ai_analysis.technical_context: `who am i` detected purely by argc == 3 — argv[1] ('am') and argv[2] ('i') are never inspected. Any 3-arg invocation triggers self-display mode: `who x y` works identically. Self-identification via ttyname(0): stdin's tty name (e.g. /dev/tty03), then rindex(/) + 1 to extract 'tty03'. Match against utmp.ut_line. Fallback when ttyname(0) returns NULL (stdin redirected, cron): consult /etc/passwd via getpwuid(getuid()), synthesize a fake utmp record with passwd name + 'tty??' + current time. The 'natural language' CLI style (`who am i`) predates getopt and option-flag conventions.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `who am i`. Three words, but the program reads only the count. argv[1] and argv[2] go unexamined — they could be 'who eats lunch' and still work. Unix CLI before getopt: natural-language invocations where word count carried meaning. Modern descendants: `make all`, `git status`, `kubectl get pods`. The pattern starts here. And the fallback — if you have no tty, the passwd file still knows your name — is philosophically pure: 'who are you' has an answer even when 'where are you' doesn't.

appraiser_note: `who am i` は **argc == 3** で検出する——argv[1]='am' と argv[2]='i' の中身は読まない、 個数だけが信号。 `who x y` でも同じ動作。 自己識別は `ttyname(0)` で stdin の tty 名 (`/dev/tty03`) を取り、 `rindex(tp, '/') + 1` で `/` 以降を切り出し `tty03` を得て、 utmp ループで `strcmp(ut_line, tp)` マッチ。 フォールバックが上手——ttyname(0) が NULL のとき (stdin リダイレクト、 cron) は getpwuid(getuid()) で /etc/passwd から名前を取り、 ut_line="tty??" の架空 utmp を合成して表示。 「**utmp に居なくても passwd に居れば名乗れる**」 という存在論的設計。 getopt 以前の自然言語風 CLI、 `make all`/`git status`/`kubectl get pods` の祖先。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Bell-32V is the VAX port of V7 Unix. who.c marks `who am i` output with `(Vax)` prefix on VAX builds, no prefix on PDP-11 builds. The intent: admins running both PDP-11 and VAX machines via terminal could tell which machine they were on without checking hostname. No hostname() syscall existed in 1979 — Unix machines were single, anonymous. `#ifdef vax` is the conditional compilation that lets one source tree build for two architectures. Later: #ifdef sun, #ifdef sparc, #ifdef linux, #ifdef _WIN32. The 'one source, many architectures' design starts in 1979.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979 had two Unix architectures: PDP-11 and VAX. To know which you'd reached over a serial line, who.c slipped (Vax) into the output on VAX builds. Cheap, effective, three lines of #ifdef. By 1985 hostname() existed and the trick became obsolete. But the pattern — one source tree, conditional compilation per architecture — is Unix's core portability technique. macOS, FreeBSD, Linux, AIX, and Windows-Cygwin all build C code from sources peppered with #ifdef. The who.c (Vax) line is the gene of cross-platform Unix.

appraiser_note: Bell-32V は V7 Unix の VAX-11/780 移植版。 who.c は `who am i` 出力に **`#ifdef vax` で `(Vax) ` プレフィックス**を付ける——VAX ビルドだけ「(Vax)」 が出る。 意図：PDP-11 と VAX の両方を持つ管理者がリモートログイン中に「今どっちのマシンに居るか」 を判別したい。 1979 年は hostname(2) 未実装、 Unix マシンは匿名だった。 `#ifdef vax` 3 行が「**1 ソース、 2 アーキテクチャ**」 という Unix の根本設計——後の `#ifdef sun`/`#ifdef sparc`/`#ifdef linux`/`#ifdef _WIN32` の系譜。 macOS/FreeBSD/Linux/AIX/Cygwin が同じ C ソースから build できるのは、 こうした条件コンパイル文化の積み重ね。 62 行の who.c に Unix のクロスプラットフォーム DNA が埋め込まれている。

ai_analysis.technical_context: dd uses key=value arguments (if=, of=, bs=, count=, conv=) — Unix's only mainstream tool with this syntax. Origin: IBM OS/360 JCL's DD (Data Definition) statement: `//SYSUT1 DD DSN=USER.INPUT,DCB=(RECFM=FB,LRECL=80)`. The dd author was likely an IBM mainframe alumni. POSIX 1003.2 specifically exempts dd from its argument syntax guidelines — 'dd shall be dd'. match() does prefix-then-advance: checks if string starts with 's', and if so moves the global `string` pointer past the matched prefix so number() can parse the value. The prefix-consuming pattern reappears in URL parsers and config-file parsers decades later.
ai_analysis.human_story: Every Unix tool uses -flags. dd uses key=value. Why? Because dd was the Unix bridge to IBM mainframes — tape conversion, EBCDIC translation, fixed-width record handling. The dd author kept IBM's JCL-style syntax to make migration easier. 50 years later, that decision stuck. POSIX formally exempted dd from Unix conventions. `dd if=/dev/sda of=/dev/sdb bs=4M` is the same syntax shape as `//STEP01 EXEC PGM=IEBGENER`. The mainframe ghost lives in modern Linux.

appraiser_note: dd は `if=`/`of=`/`bs=`/`count=`/`conv=` の **key=value 引数構文**——Unix で唯一のスタイル。 起源は IBM OS/360 JCL の DD (Data Definition) ステートメント (`//SYSUT1 DD DSN=...,DCB=(RECFM=FB,LRECL=80)`)。 dd の作者は IBM メインフレーム出身だった可能性が高い。 POSIX 1003.2 は dd を引数構文ガイドラインから **明示的に除外**——「dd is dd」 という宣言。 match() は prefix-then-advance パターン：先頭が `s` で始まれば、 グローバルな string ポインタを `=` の直後まで進めて返す。 後続の number() が値部分から読み始められる。 後の URL パーサ、 設定ファイルパーサに継承される手法。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Three 256-byte hand-coded translation tables in dd.c: etoa (EBCDIC→ASCII), atoe (ASCII→EBCDIC), atoibm (ASCII→IBM EBCDIC variant). Tables in octal — period-correct for 1979 PDP-11/VAX programmers. ~100 lines of source (18% of dd.c) is just these data tables. Why octal? PDP-11 dumps were octal, C character constants were often written in octal. Modern iconv(3) supports hundreds of encodings, but in 1979 the world's encoding problem was binary: ASCII (Unix) ↔ EBCDIC (IBM). Three tables covered the migration scenarios. `etoa[ch]` lookup is O(1) — no parsing, no library, just array indexing.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979's encoding problem wasn't UTF-8 vs UTF-16 — it was 'can we read this IBM tape on the PDP-11?'. dd answered yes, with three hardcoded translation tables. Each byte position carefully chosen, all in octal because that's what PDP programmers spoke. 18% of dd.c is data, not code. The world spent decades fighting EBCDIC; dd was the white flag, the diplomat. Modern systems forget EBCDIC, but z/OS Unix still translates between EBCDIC and ASCII daily — dd's descendants run there.

appraiser_note: dd.c には **3 つの 256 バイト変換表**が手書きで埋め込まれている——etoa (EBCDIC→ASCII)、 atoe (ASCII→EBCDIC)、 atoibm (ASCII→IBM EBCDIC 方言)。 表はすべて **8 進**で記述、 1979 年の PDP-11/VAX プログラマには自然な表記。 ソース 541 行のうち約 100 行 (18%) がこのデータ表に占められる。 1979 年の符号化問題は UTF-8 vs UTF-16 ではなく「IBM テープを PDP-11 で読めるか」——dd は yes と答えた、 3 つのハードコード変換表で。 `etoa[ch]` lookup は O(1)——パース不要、 ライブラリ不要、 配列アクセスのみ。 現代の iconv(3) は数百のエンコーディングを扱うが、 1979 年の dd は 3 方向で十分だった。 z/OS Unix では今も EBCDIC↔ASCII 変換が日常的に走る。

ai_analysis.technical_context: dd's number() parses suffix-augmented integers: 'k' multiplies by 1024 (kilo for memory), 'w' multiplies by sizeof(int) (word: 2 on PDP-11, 4 on VAX — platform-dependent value baked in), 'b' multiplies by 512 (block: V7 BSIZE), '*' and 'x' multiply by the next parsed number (recursive call). So `2*1024` works, `100k` works, `4w` works, `2k*4` works. The case '*'/'x' falls through to case '\0' for range check — K&R fall-through used intentionally. Modern GNU coreutils extended with K/M/G/T (SI prefixes, uppercase, capitalized in the 1990s); POSIX dd still requires lowercase k=1024.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `dd if=/dev/zero of=test bs=1M count=1024`. The 'M' here is from 1990s extensions, but the lowercase 'k=1024' you'd write as `bs=1k` is from 1979's dd. The pattern — number followed by unit suffix — spread to du -h, ls -lh, sleep '5m'. The seed: dd's number() with 5 cases. The `*`/`x` recursion enables nested multiplication: `2*3*4` = 24. K&R intentional fall-through routes the result through one range check. Compact, recursive, expressive — all in 35 lines.

appraiser_note: dd の number() 関数は **単位サフィックス付き整数**をパース——`k` で × 1024 (kilo)、 `w` で × sizeof(int) (word: PDP-11 で 2、 VAX で 4 のプラットフォーム依存値)、 `b` で × 512 (block: V7 BSIZE)、 `*`/`x` で次の数値と乗算 (再帰呼び出し)。 `2*1024`、 `100k`、 `4w`、 `2k*4` すべて動く。 case '*'/'x' は case '\0' に **fall-through** して範囲チェックを通る——K&R 流の意図的フォールスルー。 後の du -h、 ls -lh、 sleep '5m' の数値+単位 UX の祖。 GNU coreutils が K/M/G/T (大文字 SI 接頭辞) を 1990 年代に追加したが、 POSIX dd は小文字 k=1024 を維持。

ai_analysis.technical_context: jan1(yr) computes the day of week of January 1 of given year in 6 lines. Base formula: d = 4 + y + (y+3)/4 — offset 4 (year 0 alignment), +y (each year shifts day-of-week by 1 for 365-day non-leap years), +(y+3)/4 (Julian leap rule: every 4 years adds extra day, +3 offset for year 1 alignment). For y > 1800: subtract (y-1701)/100 (Gregorian 100-year non-leap correction starting 1801), add (y-1601)/400 (Gregorian 400-year leap correction starting 1601). For y > 1752: add 3 (compensate for British Calendar Act 1750 changeover). Final d%7: 0=Sun, 1=Mon, ..., 6=Sat. Verified: 2026 → d = 2538 % 7 = 4 (Thursday).
ai_analysis.human_story: Six lines of arithmetic compute any year's January 1 day-of-week, from year 1 to 9999. The trick: Julian calendar contributions are linear (year + leap), Gregorian corrections are conditional (post-1800, post-1752). The +3 at the 1752 boundary embeds British history into the formula. Zeller published a similar formula in 1887; this 1979 K&R version is more compact, hiding centuries of calendar reform inside `if (y > 1752) d += 3`.

appraiser_note: jan1(yr) は **6 行**で任意年の Jan 1 の曜日を計算。 基本式 `d = 4+y+(y+3)/4`——オフセット 4 (西暦元年起点)、 +y (各年で曜日 1 ずれ)、 +(y+3)/4 (ユリウス閏年規則)。 `y > 1800` のとき: `-= (y-1701)/100` (グレゴリオ 100 年非閏年補正、 1801 から)、 `+= (y-1601)/400` (グレゴリオ 400 年閏年補正、 1601 から)。 `y > 1752` のとき: `+= 3` (イギリス Calendar Act 1750 補正)。 最後に `% 7` で曜日 (0=日, 1=月, ..., 6=土)。 検証：2026 年 → d = 2538 % 7 = 4 (木曜) 正解。 Zeller の合同式 (1887) の親戚で、 より簡潔。 `if (y > 1752) d += 3` の 1 行に数世紀の暦改革史が埋め込まれている。

ai_analysis.technical_context: When cal sees year 1752, mon[9] becomes 19 instead of 30. The for-loop iterating from i=1 to mon[m] detects `i==3 && mon[m]==19` and jumps: i += 11 (next iteration is i=14), mon[m] += 11 (restore mon[9] to 30 so loop continues correctly). Output: `1 2 14 15 16 ...` — 11 days missing. This implements the British Calendar (New Style) Act 1750: September 2, 1752 (Wed, Julian) was followed by September 14, 1752 (Thu, Gregorian). 11 days dropped to align with the sun. Run `cal 9 1752` on any modern Linux/BSD/macOS — same gap, 50 years of Unix faithfully recording British calendar reform.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `cal 9 1752` today on Linux, you see September 1752 with 11 days missing — 1, 2, then 14, 15, 16. Why? Britain adopted the Gregorian calendar in 1752. The 11-day drop aligned the British calendar with the sun (and with Continental Europe, which had switched in 1582). Workers reportedly rioted demanding their 11 days' wages back (some historians dispute this). 1979's cal.c carved this historical moment into 5 lines of C. Every Unix descendant carries it forward. The riot may be myth; the calendar is real, and so is the gap.

appraiser_note: 1752 年 9 月のとき mon[9] = 19 にし、 for ループが `i==3 && mon[m]==19` を検出すると `i += 11` (次のイテレーションで i=14)、 `mon[m] += 11` (30 に戻す) で 11 日を飛ばす。 表示は `1 2 14 15 16 ...`——11 日消失。 イギリス Calendar (New Style) Act 1750 の実装：1752 年 9 月 2 日 (水、 ユリウス) の翌日が 9 月 14 日 (木、 グレゴリオ)。 11 日を削って太陽年に合わせた。 大陸ヨーロッパは 1582 年に切替済み (10 日)、 イギリス系は 1752 年 (11 日)、 ロシアは 1918 年 (13 日)。 50 年後の Linux/BSD/macOS で `cal 9 1752` を打てば同じ 11 日が今も消えたまま——イギリスの暦改革が Unix 系の DNA に刻まれた瞬間。 労働者の暴動 (11 日分の賃金を返せ) は俗説と歴史家は言うが、 暦は本物、 空白も本物。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Instead of writing the conventional leap-year predicate `y%4==0 && (y%100!=0 || y%400==0)`, cal.c detects year-length via day-of-week shift: (jan1(y+1) + 7 - d) % 7 where d = jan1(y). Non-leap year (365 days = 52 weeks + 1 day) → shift = 1. Leap year (366 days = 52 weeks + 2 days) → shift = 2. Year 1752 (354 days = 365 - 11) → shift = 4 (default case). The +7 prevents negative subtraction when jan1(y+1) < d (e.g. Mon → Sun wraps). One switch handles all three cases (regular, leap, 1752) using a single jan1() function that already encodes the Gregorian/Julian transition.
ai_analysis.human_story: Most code asks 'is this a leap year?' with explicit divisibility. cal.c asks 'how many days in this year?' via day-of-week diff. The clever answer: 1, 2, or 4. The same switch handles regular, leap, and the once-in-history 1752 short year. By centralizing calendar logic in jan1() (the Julian/Gregorian transitions live there), the leap detection inherits those corrections for free. Three case branches replace what would otherwise be 10+ lines of date-arithmetic special-casing.

appraiser_note: 通常の閏年判定 `y%4==0 && (y%100!=0 || y%400==0)` ではなく、 **曜日差**で 1 年の日数を逆算する独創的アプローチ：`(jan1(y+1) + 7 - d) % 7`。 平年 365 日 = 52 週 + 1 日 → shift = 1、 閏年 366 日 = 52 週 + 2 日 → shift = 2、 1752 年 354 日 = 365 - 11 → shift = 4 (default)。 `+7` は `jan1(y+1) < d` のとき (月→日のラップ) の負値防止。 1 つの switch で 3 ケース (平年/閏年/1752) を統一処理。 jan1() がユリウス→グレゴリオ切替を内包しているので、 閏年判定もその恩恵を受けて補正不要。 3 ブランチで通常の 10+ 行の特殊ケース処理を置き換える、 K&R 流の数学的センス。

ai_analysis.technical_context: crypt.c's opening comment declares its lineage: 'A one-rotor machine designed along the lines of Enigma but considerably trivialized.' Original Enigma I (German WWII) had 3 rotors + plugboard + reflector, ~10^20 key space. Bell-32V's version: 1 rotor (t1) + reflector (t3, involution) + inverse rotor (t2 = inverse of t1), 256 elements per table (bytes, not 26 letters). The comment is unusually honest for cryptographic software: 'don't trust this with real secrets'. Both Morris & Thompson published cryptanalysis papers showing crypt is breakable in minutes with known-plaintext attack.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1918: Scherbius patents Enigma. 1939: Wehrmacht adopts it. 1940-43: Bletchley Park breaks it (Turing et al.). 1979: K&R-era Unix programmers reimplement a toy version in 91 lines of C as `crypt(1)`. 1980: Morris and Thompson publish the cryptanalysis. The opening comment 'considerably trivialized' is honest from the start — this was never meant to be strong. It's Enigma as teaching tool, Enigma as nostalgia, Enigma reduced to its essence: rotor + reflector + state machine. The 91-line homage to Bletchley Park.

appraiser_note: crypt.c の冒頭コメント「**A one-rotor machine designed along the lines of Enigma but considerably trivialized**」 が、 この暗号機の血統を明示する。 オリジナル Enigma I (ナチス・ドイツ WWII) は 3 ローター + プラグボード + 反射板、 鍵空間約 10^20。 Bell-32V 版は 1 ローター (t1) + 反射板 (t3、 対合) + 逆ローター (t2 = t1 の逆)、 256 要素テーブル (バイト単位、 26 文字ではない)。 暗号ソフトウェアとしては異例の正直さ——「これで本物の機密を守るな」 と最初から宣言。 Morris と Thompson が論文で「既知平文攻撃で数分で破れる」 と示した暗号。 91 行の Bletchley Park へのオマージュ、 教育目的の Enigma 縮小版。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Three permutation tables built in one loop: t1 (rotor, random permutation via Fisher-Yates shuffle), t3 (reflector, involution: t3[t3[i]] = i), and afterward t2 (inverse of t1). t1 generation: standard Fisher-Yates — swap t1[k] with t1[ic] where ic ∈ [0..k]. t3 generation: walk through unassigned positions, pair them up — `t3[k] = ic; t3[ic] = k;` creates a swap pair, ensuring t3 is its own inverse. PRNG: linear congruential `seed = 5*seed + buf[i%13]`, modulus 65521 (largest prime < 2^16). t2 derivation: `t2[t1[i]] = i` — exactly the inverse permutation. The mathematical structure (permutation + involution + inverse) recreates Enigma's reciprocal property: encryption == decryption with same key.
ai_analysis.human_story: Three tables, three mathematical objects. t1 is just a shuffle. t3 is special — it's an involution, meaning applying it twice returns the input. That's what makes Enigma's reflector work: light enters, bounces back through the rotors in reverse, and the operator can read the result. t2 = t1⁻¹ closes the loop. The Fisher-Yates shuffle is Knuth's textbook algorithm, but the pairing-up for t3 is bespoke: find an unassigned position, find another unassigned position, marry them. 17 lines of code build the entire mathematical machinery of a rotor cipher.

appraiser_note: 3 つの順列テーブルを 1 ループで構築。 **t1** (ローター、 Fisher-Yates シャッフルで完全ランダム順列)、 **t3** (反射板、 対合 = `t3[t3[i]] = i`)、 そして後で **t2** (t1 の逆順列)。 t1: 標準 Fisher-Yates——`t1[k]` と `t1[ic]` (ic ∈ [0..k]) を swap。 t3: 未割り当て位置を順に走査して **ペア化**——`t3[k] = ic; t3[ic] = k;` で k と ic を相互に対応させ、 自己逆元を構築。 PRNG は線形合同法 `seed = 5*seed + buf[i%13]`、 modulus 65521 (2^16 未満の最大素数)。 t2 = t1 の逆: `t2[t1[i]] = i`。 この数学的構造 (順列 + 対合 + 逆順列) が Enigma の **暗号化 == 復号** という互換性を実現する。 17 行でローター暗号機の全機構を構築。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Textbook fork-pipe-exec pattern: pipe() creates parent-child channel, fork() spawns child, child closes stdin/stdout and dup()s the pipe ends to fd 0/1, then execl()s /usr/lib/makekey (with /lib/makekey as fallback for V7 vs Bell-32V layout). Parent writes 10 bytes (8 password + 2 wrap chars), waits for child, reads 13 bytes back as the actual key. The line `while (*pw) *pw++ = '\0';` zeros the password in argv[1] in-memory — so `ps` no longer shows it, core dumps won't leak it. The same idea is now `explicit_bzero(3)` in modern systems. Two execl() calls in a row: first one succeeds → returns control means fail → try second. exec doesn't return on success.
ai_analysis.human_story: Two textbook patterns in 18 lines. First, fork-pipe-execl: every Unix programming book teaches this. Bell-32V's crypt is one of the early examples. Second, password scrubbing: `while (*pw) *pw++ = '\0';` overwrites the password byte in process memory so it can't leak via ps, core dumps, swap. 1979's programmers were already thinking about memory hygiene — what modern hardening calls 'sensitive data clearing'. The 3-character idiom became `explicit_bzero(3)` in OpenBSD, `SecureZeroMemory` in Windows, `OPENSSL_cleanse` in OpenSSL. The instinct is older than the helper functions.

appraiser_note: 教科書的な fork-pipe-exec パターン：`pipe()` で親子間チャネル、 `fork()` で子分岐、 子側で stdin/stdout を close + dup でパイプ端に置換、 `execl("/usr/lib/makekey", "-", 0)` で makekey 起動 (`/lib/makekey` フォールバック付き、 V7 と Bell-32V のレイアウト差吸収)。 親は 10 バイト (8 パスワード + 2 ラップ文字) 書き、 wait、 13 バイトの鍵を読み戻す。 `while (*pw) *pw++ = '\0';` の 1 行が argv[1] のパスワードを **メモリゼロクリア**——`ps -ef` でパスワードが見えなくなり、 core dump にも残らない。 現代の `explicit_bzero(3)` (OpenBSD)、 `SecureZeroMemory` (Windows)、 `OPENSSL_cleanse` の祖。 2 つの `execl` が並ぶのは「最初が成功なら戻ってこない、 失敗時のみ次へ」 という Unix の作法。 18 行に Unix 教科書の主要パターン 2 つが凝縮。

ai_analysis.technical_context: The cipher's heart: one line that recreates Enigma's signal path. Read inside-out: (i+n1) shifts by rotor1 position, t1[] passes through rotor1 forward, +n2 shifts by rotor2 position, t3[] hits the reflector (involution), -n2 unshifts rotor2, t2[] passes through rotor1 inverse, -n1 unshifts rotor1. All & MASK to wrap mod 256. Then n1++ (right rotor advances per char), and when n1 wraps n2++ (middle rotor advances). The involution property of t3 makes encryption and decryption identical operations with the same key — `crypt < a > b; crypt < b > c` yields c == a. Unlike real Enigma's stepping mechanism (which had irregular notch positions), this stepping is fully predictable from key, contributing to its cryptanalytic weakness.
ai_analysis.human_story: Six function calls, one C expression. Light enters Enigma at the keyboard, traverses the input wheel, passes through three rotors, hits the reflector, returns through the three rotors backward, exits through the output wheel. Bell-32V compresses this to: t2[t3[t1[i+n1]+n2]-n2]-n1. The reflector (t3) is the magic — applying the whole expression twice with the same key returns the original input. Encryption is self-inverse. This is why Enigma operators could decrypt with the same machine setting they used to encrypt. The 1979 C line preserves that fundamental property in 60 characters.

appraiser_note: 暗号機の心臓：1 行で Enigma の信号経路を再構築。 内側から外側へ: `(i+n1)` ローター 1 位置でシフト、 `t1[]` で順方向ローター 1 通過、 `+n2` ローター 2 位置シフト、 `t3[]` で反射板 (対合)、 `-n2` ローター 2 戻し、 `t2[]` でローター 1 逆方向、 `-n1` でローター 1 戻し。 すべて `& MASK` で mod 256 ラップ。 n1++ (右ローター、 1 文字ごと進行)、 n1 ラップ時 n2++ (中央ローター)。 t3 が対合なので暗号化と復号が **同じ操作**——`crypt < a > b; crypt < b > c` で c == a。 本物 Enigma の stepping mechanism (不規則ノッチ位置) と異なり、 この進行は鍵から完全予測可能で、 これが暗号解析的弱さの一因。 6 つの関数呼び出しを 60 文字の C 式に圧縮、 Bletchley Park 縮図。

ai_analysis.technical_context: dc represents arbitrary-precision numbers as byte arrays where each byte holds a base-100 digit (0-99). struct blk has 4 pointers: beg (buffer start), last (buffer end / capacity), rd (read cursor), wt (write cursor). Operations modeled as stdio-style: sgetc/sputc/sfeof/sfbeg/rewind — number manipulation feels like file I/O. Base-100 chosen for the sweet spot: maximum decimal digits per byte (2) while keeping addition's `a + b + carry` within unsigned char range (< 256). 1234567890 stored as [12, 34, 56, 78, 90] in 5 bytes. Addition is exactly grade-school carry arithmetic, just in base 100.
ai_analysis.human_story: Pick your base wisely. Base 10 wastes a byte per digit. Base 256 (binary) is fast but hard to print decimally. Base 100 hits the middle: 2 decimal digits per byte, addition still fits in unsigned char, output to decimal is two %02d printfs. 1979's dc chose this carefully — read each byte, print 2 digits. The struct blk type with rd/wt/beg/last mimics FILE*, making C programmers feel at home with arbitrary-precision arithmetic. Python's int, JavaScript BigInt, Java BigInteger — all eventually do similar tricks. dc was here first.

appraiser_note: dc は任意精度数を **百進法バイト配列**で表現——各バイトが 0〜99 の「百進法 1 桁」、 `struct blk` が beg/last (バッファ境界) + rd/wt (現在のカーソル) の 4 ポインタ。 操作は stdio 風: `sgetc`/`sputc`/`sfeof`/`sfbeg`/`rewind`——数値操作がファイル I/O のように扱える。 百進法選定の理由：1 バイトあたり最大の十進情報量 (2 桁) + 加算 `a + b + carry` が unsigned char 範囲内 (< 256) で済む。 1234567890 → [12, 34, 56, 78, 90] の 5 バイト。 加算は人間の筆算と完全同じ手順、 ただし基底 100。 Python int、 JavaScript BigInt、 Java BigInteger の祖、 dc が最初。

ai_analysis.technical_context: commnds() is the heart of dc: a typical REPL with `while(1) { c = readc(); switch(c) { ... } }`. 60+ case branches, one character = one operation. RPN (Reverse Polish Notation): digits push to stack, operators pop and compute. Case-letter conventions: lowercase/uppercase pairs indicate variations (s vs S = without-pop vs with-pop on register save, p vs P = with/without newline, q vs Q = single quit vs multi-level quit). Symbols carry semantic baggage: ? = read line (HP calculator), ! = shell escape (vi/ed/mail tradition), ^ = exponent (math notation), v = sqrt (single-letter mnemonic). The single-character commands echo HP-35 (1972) calculator's button layout.
ai_analysis.human_story: RPN was popularized by Hewlett-Packard's HP-35 calculator in 1972 — engineers loved it. By 1979, dc brought RPN to Unix command line. One character per operation: type `2 3 +` for addition. The switch statement has 60+ cases because dc covers more than arithmetic — print, duplicate, register save/load, macro execute, conditional execute. The uppercase/lowercase pairs are clever ergonomics: `s` saves without popping (peek-style), `S` saves and pops (consuming). Same letter, two ergonomic variations. RPN survived as Forth, PostScript, calculator keyboards — but dc was its early Unix home.

appraiser_note: commnds() は dc の心臓——典型的 REPL: `while(1) { c = readc(); switch(c) { ... } }`、 60+ ケース、 1 文字 = 1 動作。 RPN (逆ポーランド記法): 数字はスタックに push、 演算子は pop + 計算 + push。 大文字/小文字対の規約: `s` (save without pop) vs `S` (save with pop)、 `p` (with newline) vs `P` (no newline)、 `q` (quit) vs `Q` (multi-quit)。 記号の意味論: `?` = readline (HP 電卓由来)、 `!` = shell escape (vi/ed/mail 慣習)、 `^` = exponent (数学記法)、 `v` = sqrt (頭文字)。 HP-35 電卓 (1972) のボタン配置の系譜——RPN 文化を Unix command line へ持ち込んだ。 Forth、 PostScript、 電卓キーボードの祖だが、 dc が早期 Unix の本拠地。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Macro feature: `[` reads characters until matching `]`, builds a string, pushes to stack. Nesting handled by counter n. `x` pops the string and pushes it onto readstk (input source stack) — subsequent reads come from the macro body. Returns when readstk pops. Combined with 256 registers (stable[TBLSZ]) accessed via S/L (save/load), and `<`/`>`/`=` conditional execute (compare top two, execute named register if true), dc becomes Turing complete. Example factorial: `[d 1 - d 1 <e * lFx] sF` defines F(n) = if n>1 then n*F(n-1). Stack-based control flow predates Forth (1968) by no clear margin; dc's macros (V1, 1971) may be earlier. Register stable[] is per-register linked list (S/L is push/pop, s/l is set/peek) — registers are themselves stacks.
ai_analysis.human_story: dc isn't just a calculator. With `[...]` string literals, register save/load via S/L, conditional execute via <a, dc becomes a Turing-complete language. Write a factorial macro, push 5, execute — get 120. Write a prime tester macro, push numbers, get truth values. The combination of: (1) stack for arithmetic, (2) registers for variables, (3) macros for functions, (4) conditional execute for control flow gives you a programming language in 1979. It's Forth before Forth was famous. PostScript later took this same stack-based model and made it the language of laser printers.

appraiser_note: マクロ機能: `[` で文字列読み始め、 対応する `]` までを 1 つの文字列としてスタック push (nesting カウンタ n で `[[nested]]` も正しく処理)。 `x` で pop した文字列を readstk (入力ソーススタック) に push——以降の readc() はマクロ本体から読み出す、 終了時に readstk を pop して元に戻る。 256 レジスタ (stable[TBLSZ]) に `S`/`L` (save/load) でアクセス、 `<`/`>`/`=` で「top と次の値を比較、 真なら指定レジスタを execute」 という条件分岐。 これで dc は **Turing 完備**。 階乗マクロ: `[d 1 - d 1 <e * lFx] sF` で F(n) = if n>1 then n*F(n-1)。 (1) 算術用スタック、 (2) 変数用レジスタ、 (3) 関数用マクロ、 (4) 条件実行——プログラム言語の完全な構成要素を 1979 年に提供。 Forth 文化、 PostScript の祖。

text:
#ifdef interdata
#define NEGBYTE 0200
#define MASK (-1 & ~0377)
#define sgetc(p)  ( ((p)->rd==(p)->wt) ? EOF :( ((*(p)->rd & NEGBYTE) != 0) ? ( *(p)->rd++ | MASK): *(p)->rd++ ))
#define slookc(p) ( ((p)->rd==(p)->wt) ? EOF :( ((*(p)->rd & NEGBYTE) != 0) ? (*(p)->rd | MASK) : *(p)->rd ))
#define sbackc(p) ( ((p)->rd==(p)->beg) ? EOF :( ((*(--(p)->rd) & NEGBYTE) != 0) ? (*(p)->rd | MASK): *(p)->rd ))
#endif
#ifndef interdata
#define sgetc(p)  (((p)->rd==(p)->wt)?EOF:*(p)->rd++)
#define slookc(p) (((p)->rd==(p)->wt)?EOF:*(p)->rd)
#define sbackc(p) (((p)->rd==(p)->beg)?EOF:*(--(p)->rd))
#endif

ai_analysis.technical_context: Interdata 8/32 (Perkin-Elmer mini, 1970s) — the platform of the first non-Bell Unix port (Wollongong University, Australia, 1977). Its C compiler zero-extended `char` to `int` instead of sign-extending (PDP-11/VAX behavior). dc.h's #ifdef interdata defines sgetc/slookc/sbackc to manually sign-extend when the high bit is set: check `*(p)->rd & NEGBYTE` (0x80), if true OR with MASK (-1 & ~0377 = upper bits all 1) to fill in the sign bits. The else branch (PDP-11/VAX) just does `*(p)->rd++` because automatic sign-extension takes care of it. 4 lines of macros encapsulate a major architectural difference. The 'one Unix source, many architectures' DNA in action.
ai_analysis.human_story: Interdata 8/32 was Unix's first journey outside Bell Labs and Berkeley — Wollongong University, Australia, 1977. Their C compiler treated `char` as unsigned (zero-extension to int), unlike PDP-11/VAX (signed, sign-extension). One macro choice could break arithmetic on negative numbers. dc.h's solution: hand-write the sign extension via NEGBYTE check + OR with MASK. Four #ifdef macros encode the architectural divergence so the rest of dc.c can use sgetc() ignoring the difference. This style — encapsulate platform quirks in macros, write the algorithm once — is the seed of every cross-platform C codebase: Linux, macOS, AIX, Windows-Cygwin, all running the same source files with #ifdef gates.

appraiser_note: Interdata 8/32 (Perkin-Elmer 製 mini、 1970 年代) は Bell/Berkeley 以外の最初の Unix 移植先 (Wollongong 大学オーストラリア、 1977)。 その C コンパイラは `char` → `int` の昇格を **ゼロ拡張**で行った——PDP-11/VAX は符号拡張。 dc.h は `#ifdef interdata` で **手動符号拡張**を sgetc/slookc/sbackc に組み込む: `*(p)->rd & NEGBYTE` (0x80) で最上位ビット検査、 立っていれば `| MASK` (-1 & ~0377 = 上位ビット全 1) で符号拡張を補う。 else 節 (PDP-11/VAX) は自動符号拡張に任せて素朴な `*(p)->rd++`。 4 行のマクロでアーキテクチャの差を吸収し、 dc.c 本体は `sgetc()` を意識せず使える。 「**プラットフォーム差をマクロに閉じ込めて、 アルゴリズムは 1 度書く**」——Linux/macOS/AIX/Cygwin が同じソースから build できる DNA の起源。

ai_analysis.technical_context: init's main is 10 lines but defines Unix boot sequence. #define EVER ;; lets `for(EVER)` read 'forever' — K&R aesthetic. Five-stage cycle: shutdown() kills all processes, single() forks /bin/sh for single-user maintenance, runcom() executes /etc/rc startup script, merge() reads /etc/ttys and spawns getty per tty, multiple() loops on wait() to respawn dead gettys. Normally multiple() never returns (always children to wait on). The for(EVER) reloop happens only via SIGHUP → reset() → longjmp(sjbuf, 1) → setjmp returns and main re-enters. PID 1 cannot exit: kernel panic if init dies. Modern Linux: 'Kernel panic - not syncing: Attempted to kill init!'. 50 years of OS architecture flow from this 10-line main.
ai_analysis.human_story: Ten lines for the most important process in Unix. PID 1. Parent of all. Cannot die. The for(EVER) macro is K&R cuteness — `for(;;)` written as 'forever'. The 5-stage cycle is the Unix boot story: kill, single-user, run startup script, spawn login terminals, watch them. Every modern init system — System V init, BSD init, systemd, launchd, OpenRC, runit, s6 — implements these same 5 phases, just with vastly more configuration. The 1979 init.c is a kernel-blessed user-space miracle: alone in the address space, eternal, parent to everyone.

appraiser_note: init の main は **わずか 10 行**で Unix の起動シーケンス全体を定義。 `#define EVER ;;` で `for(EVER)` を「forever」 と読ませる K&R 美学。 5 ステージサイクル: `shutdown()` 全プロセス kill、 `single()` で /bin/sh fork (シングルユーザー)、 `runcom()` で /etc/rc 実行 (起動スクリプト)、 `merge()` で /etc/ttys 読み + 各 tty に getty fork、 `multiple()` で wait() ループ (respawn)。 通常 multiple() は戻らない (常に wait 対象あり)——for(EVER) ループは SIGHUP → reset() → longjmp(sjbuf, 1) → setjmp 戻り → main 再進入で回る。 PID 1 は exit できない: kernel が panic する (現代 Linux: 「Kernel panic - not syncing: Attempted to kill init!」)。 50 年の OS アーキテクチャがこの 10 行から流れ出る——SysV init/BSD init/systemd/launchd/OpenRC/runit/s6 すべての祖。

ai_analysis.technical_context: multiple() invents 'respawn' — the automatic restart of dead processes. Loop: wait() blocks for any child to die, gets that PID, scans itab[] for matching entry, calls rmut() to log the logout in utmp/wtmp, then dfork() to spawn a fresh getty on that tty. A getty that times out, crashes, or completes login flow gets immediately replaced by a new one. wait() == -1 (ECHILD, no children) returns from multiple(), letting main proceed (this happens after SIGHUP reset kills everything). The respawn keyword later became declarative in /etc/inittab (System V): `1:23:respawn:/sbin/getty 9600 tty1`. systemd's Restart=always, supervisord's autorestart=true, Kubernetes restartPolicy=Always — all descendants of this 14-line function.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does your terminal always show `login:` after you log out? Because of these 14 lines from 1979. getty dies → init's wait() returns → rmut() records the logout → dfork() spawns a new getty → `login:` reappears. The pattern — wait, identify, restart — is the seed of every supervisor system in Unix: System V's respawn, BSD's getty respawn, monit, supervisord, runit's chpst, daemontools, systemd unit files, Kubernetes pod restart policies. 'Self-healing' as a software pattern was invented here, in 14 lines.

appraiser_note: multiple() は **respawn** (死んだプロセスの自動再起動) を発明した 14 行。 ループ: wait() で任意の子プロセスの死を待つ → 死んだ PID を取得 → itab[] を走査して一致するエントリを発見 → rmut() でログアウトを utmp/wtmp に記録 → dfork() で新しい getty を起動。 getty がタイムアウト、 クラッシュ、 login 完了で消えても、 即座に新しい getty に置き換わる。 wait() == -1 (ECHILD、 子なし) で multiple() return、 main へ戻る (SIGHUP reset で全プロセス消えた後)。 後に SysV init で宣言的に: `1:23:respawn:/sbin/getty 9600 tty1`。 systemd `Restart=always`、 supervisord `autorestart=true`、 Kubernetes `restartPolicy=Always`——全部この 14 行の子孫。 「self-healing」 ソフトウェアパターンの起源。

ai_analysis.technical_context: dfork: fork + tty setup + exec getty. Child resets signal handlers (parent ignored SIGHUP/SIGINT for safety), constructs /dev/ttyXX path via maktty(), then carefully resets terminal ownership: chown(tty, 0, 0) = root:root, chmod(tty, 0622) = rw--w--w- (owner read/write, others write only). The 0622 permission is brilliant: lets `write` and `wall` send messages to this terminal (write needed) but prevents eavesdropping (no read for others). open(tty, 2) opens fd 0, dup(0); dup(0); duplicates to fd 1 and fd 2 — establishes stdin/stdout/stderr convention. Then execl(getty, '-', 'tty00', 0): '-' marks login shell, second arg is the tty name passed to getty.
ai_analysis.human_story: Every tty resets to fresh state when init spawns a new getty: ownership becomes root, permissions become 0622 (rw--w--w-). The 0622 magic enables `write user@host` and `wall hello` to broadcast messages while preventing unauthorized snooping. Then fd 0/1/2 all point at the tty — the stdin/stdout/stderr convention is BORN here. Before exec, signal handlers are reset because the parent (init) had them ignored. The 18-line dfork is the canonical 'how to spawn a setuid-aware terminal-attached child' pattern that every Unix shell, ssh-server, container runtime quietly imitates.

appraiser_note: dfork は fork + tty 初期化 + getty 起動。 子側: parent (init) が ignore していた SIGHUP/SIGINT をデフォルトに戻す、 maktty() で /dev/ttyXX パス構築、 **`chown(tty, 0, 0)`** で root:root、 **`chmod(tty, 0622)` で `rw--w--w-`** (所有者 rw、 他者 w のみ)。 0622 が秀逸——`write` や `wall` で他人へメッセージ送信可能 (write 許可)、 でも盗み読みは不可 (read 不許可)。 `open(tty, 2)` で fd 0、 `dup(0); dup(0);` で fd 1 と fd 2 にも複製——**stdin/stdout/stderr 慣習の起源**。 `execl(getty, "-", "tty00", 0)` で getty 起動、 "-" は login shell マーク。 「setuid 対応 + terminal 接続子プロセス起動」 の正準パターン、 shell/ssh-server/container runtime のすべてが内部で模倣する 18 行。

ai_analysis.technical_context: init uses setjmp/longjmp as exception handling for signal-driven reload. setjmp(sjbuf) at main's start saves the stack/PC state. signal(SIGHUP, reset) installs reset() as SIGHUP handler. When admin runs `kill -HUP 1`, kernel invokes reset() which does longjmp(sjbuf, 1) — non-local jump back to setjmp regardless of call depth. setjmp returns 1 (vs 0 first time), main re-enters the for(EVER) cycle, killing all gettys and re-reading /etc/ttys. This launched the 'SIGHUP = reload config' convention: sendmail, nginx, inetd, cron, syslogd, dnsmasq all use SIGHUP for config reload. setjmp/longjmp itself became the C-level exception primitive — used in libpng error recovery, OCaml runtime, Common Lisp catch/throw analogs.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you reload a daemon's config without restarting? In 1979, init.c invented the pattern: SIGHUP triggers a handler that longjmps back to the start of main. The setjmp/longjmp idiom is C's only built-in non-local exit — no exceptions, no goto across functions, just this register-saving primitive. Every major daemon adopted SIGHUP-reloads: sendmail rereads sendmail.cf, nginx rereads nginx.conf, syslogd reopens log files (logrotate's friend), cron rereads crontabs. Type `kill -HUP <pid>` today and you're invoking a convention started by 5 lines in 1979's init.c. The reset() function is 1 line: longjmp(sjbuf, 1). The entire 'reload config' design pattern in 1 line.

appraiser_note: init は setjmp/longjmp を **シグナル駆動リロードの例外処理**として使う。 main の冒頭で `setjmp(sjbuf)` がスタック/PC 状態を保存、 `signal(SIGHUP, reset)` で SIGHUP ハンドラに reset() を設定。 管理者が `kill -HUP 1` 実行 → kernel が reset() を呼ぶ → reset() が `longjmp(sjbuf, 1)` で main の setjmp 地点へ **非ローカルジャンプ** (呼び出し深さ無関係)。 setjmp が 1 を返し (初回 0)、 main が for(EVER) を再進入、 全 getty を kill して /etc/ttys 再読込。 これが **「**SIGHUP = 設定リロード**」 慣習**の起源——sendmail/nginx/inetd/cron/syslogd/dnsmasq すべてが SIGHUP でリロード。 setjmp/longjmp は C の唯一の組み込み非ローカル脱出——例外なし、 関数跨ぎ goto なし。 reset() は 1 行 (`longjmp(sjbuf, 1)`)——「設定リロード」 設計パターン全体がこの 1 行に。

ai_analysis.technical_context: getty cycles through baud rates via table chain. itab[] entries have `tname` (current) and `nname` (next-if-failed). When dialup-line is opened, getty tries the named entry (e.g. '0' = B300), prints login prompt, calls getname(). If user sends BREAK (NUL byte), getname returns 0, getty moves to nname (e.g. 1 = B1200), retries. Cycle: '0' → 1 → 2 → 3 → '0' covering 300, 1200, 150, 110 baud. Fixed-speed entries have nname == tname (no cycling for hardwired consoles). Login messages differ per speed: B300 has VT escape + bell, B110 is plain. Auto-baud detection without explicit speed handshake — the user's BREAK key communicates 'try slower'.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979 dialup: you call the computer, modem answers, getty starts. But what speed is your terminal? 110? 300? 1200? Modems didn't auto-negotiate yet. getty's clever answer: try one speed, print `login:`, see what comes back. If user hits BREAK (their terminal's escape from the wrong speed), getty rotates to next speed and re-prints `login:`. Eventually one works. The login messages embed VT escape sequences and bell characters to help the user know something's there. Pre-Hayes-modem world: getty was the auto-baud detector itself.

appraiser_note: getty は baud rate をテーブルチェインで巡回試行。 itab[] の各エントリに `tname` (現在のテーブル名) と `nname` (失敗時の次) があり、 BREAK (NUL バイト) を受け取ったら nname へ遷移。 巡回: `'0' → 1 → 2 → 3 → '0'` で 300/1200/150/110 baud を覆う。 固定 speed エントリは `nname == tname` (コンソール等ハードウェア固定用)。 login メッセージは speed ごとに微妙に違う——B300 は VT escape + ベル (\007)、 B110 は素朴。 モデムが Auto-Negotiate しない時代 (Hayes 以前) の auto-baud 検出——ユーザーの BREAK キーが「もっと遅い速度を試せ」 という信号として機能した。 1979 年のダイヤルアップ環境の縮図。

ai_analysis.technical_context: V6/V7 Unix terminal control uses struct sgttyb: sg_ispeed, sg_ospeed, sg_erase, sg_kill, sg_flags. stty(0, &tmode) syscall sets terminal mode on fd 0. Flag bits: RAW, ANYP/EVENP/ODDP (parity), ECHO, CRMOD (CR → CR/LF), LCASE (upper → lower), XTABS (TAB → spaces), NL1/CR1/CR2/FF1/TAB1 (character output delays — relics of mechanical teletypes needing physical time for carriage return). 4.2BSD (1983) introduced struct tcsetattr/tcgetattr and richer struct termios with c_iflag/c_oflag/c_cflag/c_lflag (4 flag words instead of 1). getty illustrates 2-stage configuration: iflags during getname() (RAW, no echo), then fflags after name confirmed (cooked, echo on).
ai_analysis.human_story: Before termios (1983), there was sgttyb. Five fields: input speed, output speed, erase char, kill char, flags. The flags packed RAW, ECHO, parity, case conversion, and even physical-delay-after-character into one int. The delay bits (NL1, CR1, CR2) were for mechanical teletypes — Carriage Return took real time, so after CR you needed to wait 1 or 2 character times before sending the next byte, or it would print mid-stroke. getty's two-stage config (iflags for prompt, fflags for after login) is the same pattern modern tty drivers use, just with finer-grained flags. The mechanical past lives in V7's terminal ioctl bits.
