appraiser_note: V6/V7 Unix の **ターミナル制御 API**: `struct sgttyb` (sg_ispeed/sg_ospeed/sg_erase/sg_kill/sg_flags の 5 フィールド) と `stty(0, &tmode)` syscall。 フラグビット: RAW、 ANYP/EVENP/ODDP (パリティ)、 ECHO、 CRMOD、 LCASE、 XTABS、 そして NL1/CR1/CR2/FF1/TAB1 (各文字後の遅延)。 遅延フラグは **機械式テレタイプ**の名残——キャリッジリターンに物理時間がかかるので、 CR 後 1〜2 文字分待たないと印字が行の途中で始まる。 4.2BSD (1983) で `struct termios` (4 つのフラグワード c_iflag/c_oflag/c_cflag/c_lflag) と tcsetattr/tcgetattr に置き換え。 getty の **2 段階設定** (iflags で getname() 中の RAW モード、 fflags でログイン後の cooked モード) は現代の tty driver でも同じパターン。 機械式時代の遺産が V7 ioctl ビットに刻印されている。

ai_analysis.technical_context: RAW mode means kernel doesn't do line editing — getty does it manually. ERASE = '#' deletes previous char (substitute for BS), KILL = '@' clears entire line (substitute for line-clear). Why these? ASR-33 Teletype had no BS key; '#' and '@' are printable-but-rarely-needed characters chosen as in-band edit codes. Username characters classified: a-z increments lower counter, A-Z increments upper counter (and lowered into name buffer), space becomes underscore (since /etc/passwd uses ':' as field separator and spaces in names break parsing). After loop, if upper>0 && lower==0, terminal is all-caps (Teletype ASR-33 style) and LCASE flag gets set so subsequent kernel output translates lowercase → uppercase. Username detection ALSO detects terminal capability — clever multi-purpose code.
ai_analysis.human_story: Modern terminals have a Backspace key. ASR-33 Teletype (1963) didn't — it had no way to erase. So Unix conventions: `#` deletes one char, `@` kills the whole line. These are visible characters, sent like any other, but interpreted by getty as edit commands. The all-caps username detection is even cleverer: if you type `BOB`, getty deduces 'this is a Teletype, output should be uppercase too' and tells the kernel via LCASE flag. One function reads the name AND fingerprints the terminal. Modern bash still inherits the # convention as the comment character partly because of this history.

appraiser_note: RAW モードでは kernel が行編集をしないので、 getty が手動実装。 **ERASE = '#'** が直前 1 文字消去 (BS の代替)、 **KILL = '@'** が行全体クリア。 なぜこれら？ ASR-33 テレタイプ (1963) には BS キーが無く、 印字可能だが滅多に使わない文字を「**行内編集コード**」 として選んだ。 ユーザー名文字の分類: a-z で lower カウンタ、 A-Z で upper カウンタを増やしつつ name バッファには小文字化して格納、 スペースは _ に置換 (/etc/passwd の : 区切り対策)。 ループ後 upper>0 && lower==0 ならテレタイプ ASR-33 風端末と判定、 LCASE フラグを設定して以降の kernel 出力で小文字→大文字変換。 **ユーザー名読みと端末能力検出を同時に行う**——1 関数で多目的、 1979 流の経済設計。 現代 bash の # コメント文字慣習にもこの歴史が残る。

ai_analysis.technical_context: partab[] precomputes even parity bit (bit 7) for each 7-bit ASCII character. To send char c: c |= partab[c & 0177] & 0200 — strip to 7 bits, lookup parity bit, OR it in. Why? Serial dialup lines flip bits due to phone-line noise; even parity (bit 7 set so total 1s count is even) catches all single-bit errors at receiver. Table lookup vs runtime calculation: 128 bytes of memory for O(1) parity instead of ~5-10 instructions of XOR-based bit counting. On PDP-11, the table approach was 2 instructions vs 10. The data table embedded in source (128 bytes octal) is V7-era style; modern compilers use intrinsics or hardware __builtin_parity().
ai_analysis.human_story: Dialup phone lines flip bits. Noise on the analog signal can turn a 1 into 0 or vice versa. 1979's solution: even parity. Set bit 7 so the total count of 1s in the byte is always even. Receiver checks — if it sees odd count, error. 100% detection of single-bit flips. getty's partab[] is the lookup table: 128 bytes, one per ASCII code, with bit 7 = parity. Sending char becomes: lookup, OR, write — 3 instructions. Modern x86 has popcnt and __builtin_parity intrinsics; PDP-11 didn't, hence the table. Fast error detection over noisy phone lines, in 128 bytes.

appraiser_note: partab[] は **ASCII 各文字 (0〜127) の偶数パリティビット (bit 7) を事前計算**した 128 バイトテーブル。 送信時 `c |= partab[c & 0177] & 0200` で下位 7 ビットを取り、 パリティビットを OR。 なぜ？ ダイヤルアップ電話線は **ビット反転ノイズ**が起きる——偶数パリティ (bit 7 で全 1 数を偶数に) で受信側が **1 ビットエラーを 100% 検出**できる。 テーブル方式 vs ランタイム計算: 128 バイトメモリで O(1) パリティ、 ランタイムなら XOR ベースで 5〜10 命令。 PDP-11 では table lookup が 2 命令、 XOR ベースが 10 命令——速度差が決定的。 8 進記述 (0201 = 0x81、 bit 7 + bit 0) は V7 流。 現代 x86 の popcnt や __builtin_parity intrinsic の祖、 ノイジーな電話線を 128 バイトで守った。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Unix authentication core in 5 lines. getpass('Password:') reads input with echo off (tty raw mode toggle inside the function). crypt(input, salt) uses modified-DES 25-round one-way hash with 12-bit salt (2 chars). Trick: pass pwd->pw_passwd as 2nd arg — crypt extracts the first 2 chars as salt, so we don't need to track salt separately. Compare via strcmp. /etc/passwd never stores plaintext; even file disclosure doesn't directly reveal passwords. The salt thwarts rainbow tables (4096x cost increase). 8-char password limit baked into crypt's design. *pw_passwd == '\0' empty-password check allows passwordless guest accounts.
ai_analysis.human_story: Five lines that define Unix authentication for 50 years. getpass turns off terminal echo, reads what you type without showing it, restores echo, returns the string. crypt slow-hashes the password with a 12-bit salt. /etc/passwd holds only hashes — file leak doesn't directly betray passwords. Salt frustrates rainbow tables by 4096x. 25 DES rounds was 'slow enough' in 1979; today it's trivially brute-forced. Modern systems use bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 in /etc/shadow. But the pattern — getpass + slow-hash + compare — survived. Every login prompt in SSH, su, sudo descends from these 5 lines.

appraiser_note: Unix 認証の核心、 わずか 5 行。 `getpass("Password:")` がエコー無しで入力を読み (関数内で tty raw モード切替)、 `crypt(input, salt)` が改造 DES 25 ラウンドの一方向ハッシュ + 12 bit salt (2 文字)。 工夫: 第 2 引数に `pwd->pw_passwd` を渡す——crypt は先頭 2 文字を salt として抽出するので、 別途 salt を管理する必要がない。 strcmp で比較。 /etc/passwd には **ハッシュのみ**保存、 ファイル漏洩でも直接パスワードは漏れない。 salt は rainbow table 攻撃を 4096 倍コストに。 8 文字パスワード制限は crypt 設計上のもの。 `*pw_passwd == '\0'` 空パスワードチェックでゲストアカウントを許す。 SSH、 su、 sudo の認証プロンプトすべての祖。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Defense against username enumeration: if getpwnam() returns NULL (user doesn't exist), substitute a fake struct passwd with pw_passwd='nope' so the password prompt still appears, crypt() still runs, and the comparison still fails. Same timing, same UI, same error message ('Login incorrect') for both 'wrong password' and 'user doesn't exist'. Attacker can't probe usernames by observing whether the password prompt appears or how long it takes. The 'nope' string isn't a valid crypt output (not 13 chars), so strcmp always fails. This is one of the earliest documented timing-attack defenses in Unix — 1979 programmers already understood that login responses leak information.
ai_analysis.human_story: What if you mistype your username? login could say 'No such user' immediately. Or it could pretend the user exists, ask for a password, and reject. The 1979 login chose the second — defense against username enumeration. Type any name, login asks for password, login rejects. Attacker can't tell which usernames exist. The trick: a struct passwd called `nouser` with pw_passwd='nope' substituted when getpwnam returns NULL. crypt runs on user input, strcmp fails. Same code path, same time taken, same error message. Modern PAM and shadow do this exactly the same way. The username enumeration concept is 1990s+; the defense is 1979.

appraiser_note: ユーザー名列挙攻撃への防衛: getpwnam() が NULL を返す (ユーザー不存在) 時、 偽の struct passwd `nouser = {"", "nope"}` を代入。 これでパスワードプロンプトが出る、 crypt() が実行される、 比較が失敗する——本物のユーザーで間違ったパスワードを入れた時と **同じタイミング、 同じ UI、 同じエラーメッセージ「Login incorrect」**。 攻撃者はパスワードプロンプトの有無や応答時間からユーザー名を割り出せない。 `"nope"` は有効な crypt 出力ではない (13 文字ではない) ので strcmp は必ず失敗。 Unix で**最も早期に文書化されたタイミング攻撃対策**の 1 つ——1979 年のプログラマがログイン応答が情報を漏らすと既に理解していた。 「ユーザー名列挙」 概念は 1990 年代だが、 防御は 1979 年。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Privilege descent in 3 syscalls. login was forked from init with uid=0 (root). After authentication: chown(ttyn, uid, gid) transfers tty ownership to the new user (must run as root). setgid(pwd->pw_gid) changes group first — must precede setuid because once you drop root, you lose the privilege to change gids. setuid(pwd->pw_uid) drops root irreversibly. Order is absolute: chown → setgid → setuid. Environment also sanitized: `environ = envinit` replaces inherited env with safe {HOME, PATH} only — prevents env-var-based attacks (LD_PRELOAD, malicious PATH). The empty pw_shell defaults to /bin/sh. This 6-line sequence is the principle-of-least-privilege made executable.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does Unix make sure you can't be root after logging in as yourself? Three syscalls in order: chown the terminal so the new user owns it, setgid to change group (while still root), setuid to become the user (and lose root forever — no syscall to climb back up). Environment also wiped to {HOME, PATH} only — no inherited LD_PRELOAD tricks, no malicious PATH. Six lines is all it takes to transition from 'most powerful process on the system' to 'an ordinary user'. The 1979 sequence is identical to what login does today on Linux. setuid → no going back, by design.

appraiser_note: 3 つのシステムコールで権限降下。 init から fork されて uid=0 (root) で起動した login が、 認証成功後: `chown(ttyn, uid, gid)` で tty を新ユーザー所有に (root 権限必要)、 `setgid(gid)` で group 先 (uid を捨てると gid 変更権限も失うので)、 `setuid(uid)` で **root を不可逆に失う**。 順序は絶対: chown → setgid → setuid。 環境も浄化: `environ = envinit` で継承環境を捨て、 {HOME, PATH} のみの安全な配列に置換——LD_PRELOAD 攻撃や悪意ある PATH 攻撃を防止。 空 pw_shell は `/bin/sh` デフォルト。 6 行で「最強プロセス」 から「一般ユーザー」 への遷移、 **最小権限の原則**を実行可能な形で表現。 50 年後の Linux login も同じシーケンス、 setuid に「戻る道」 は設計上存在しない。

ai_analysis.technical_context: login signals 'this is a login shell' to /bin/sh by setting argv[0] = '-sh' instead of 'sh'. Construction: minusnam[16] = '-' literal; rindex(/bin/sh, '/') finds last slash, namep++ skips past it to 'sh'; strcat(minusnam, 'sh') gives '-sh'. Then execlp(pwd->pw_shell, minusnam, 0) — pwd->pw_shell is the real executable path ('/bin/sh'), but argv[0] is '-sh'. The shell at startup checks argv[0][0] == '-' to identify itself as a login shell, then reads /etc/profile and ~/.profile. All major shells (sh/bash/csh/zsh/ksh/tcsh) implement this convention. `bash -l` is the modern explicit alternative. exec failure falls through to 'No shell' + exit.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does `echo $0` in your terminal sometimes show `-bash` instead of `bash`? Because login.c started bash with argv[0] = '-bash' as a signal: 'this is a LOGIN shell, please source /etc/profile and ~/.bash_profile'. The 1979 trick: stick '-' in front of the basename of the shell, pass it as argv[0]. Every modern shell honors this. bash -l is explicit; -bash is implicit. The minusnam[16] = '-' literal is a tiny piece of code with 50 years of consequences. Inheritance protocols don't need to be elegant to last; they just need to work.

appraiser_note: login は **`argv[0] = "-sh"` で /bin/sh に「これはログインシェル」 と伝える**。 構築: `minusnam[16] = "-"` リテラル、 `rindex(/bin/sh, '/')` で最後の `/` を見つけ、 `namep++` で `sh` の先頭へ、 `strcat(minusnam, "sh")` で `"-sh"` 完成。 `execlp(pwd->pw_shell, minusnam, 0)` で実体は /bin/sh だが argv[0] は -sh。 shell 起動時に `argv[0][0] == '-'` で自身を **login shell** と判定、 /etc/profile と ~/.profile を読む。 全シェル (sh/bash/csh/zsh/ksh/tcsh) がこの慣習を実装。 `bash -l` は現代の明示版。 exec 失敗時は "No shell" + exit。 `echo $0` で `-bash` と出るのは login.c がこの規約を 1979 年に確立したから——50 年の継承を持つ tiny piece of code。

ai_analysis.technical_context: compile() translates regex string to bytecode array (expbuf). 8 opcodes: CCHR (char literal), CDOT (.), CCL (char class), NCCL (negated cc), CDOL ($), CEOF (end), CBRA (\(), CKET (\)), CBACK (\1-\9). STAR (bit 01) is OR'd onto previous opcode: `a*` becomes 2 bytes: (CCHR | STAR), 'a'. The bit-overlay design saves memory — no separate STAR opcode needed. `^` sets circf flag for line-anchored match. The compile→execute split is now standard for regex engines (Python re.compile, JavaScript RegExp constructor, Rust regex::Regex::new), but in 1979 it was novel. compile is one-pass linear; produces expbuf which becomes input to advance().
ai_analysis.human_story: Ken Thompson's regex notation needed an internal form. compile() solves it: walk the pattern, emit opcodes. The STAR-as-OR-bit trick is elegance born of memory scarcity: `a*` becomes 2 bytes, not 3. Modern regex engines compile to richer NFAs or DFAs, but the compile→execute split is universal. Type `re.compile(r'a*')` in Python — you're doing in 2026 what Thompson formalized for ed's regex in the early 1970s and grep inherited in 1973.

appraiser_note: compile() は正規表現文字列をバイトコード配列 (expbuf) に変換。 8 オペコード: CCHR (文字定数)、 CDOT (.)、 CCL (文字クラス)、 NCCL (否定 CC)、 CDOL ($)、 CEOF (終端)、 CBRA (\()、 CKET (\))、 CBACK (\1-\9)。 **STAR (bit 01) は直前 opcode に OR**: `a*` は 2 バイト `(CCHR | STAR), 'a'`。 ビット重ね設計で省メモリ——STAR 専用 opcode が不要。 `^` は circf フラグで行頭アンカー。 compile→execute 分離は現代の regex エンジン (Python re.compile、 JavaScript RegExp、 Rust regex::Regex::new) の標準だが、 1979 年では novel。 ed の Thompson regex を grep が 1973 年に独立化、 そのコンパイル戦略がここに。

ai_analysis.technical_context: advance() is a bytecode VM. CCHR/CDOT/CDOL/CEOF do immediate one-char matches and continue or return. CCHR|STAR implements greedy-then-backtrack: while loop consumes all matching chars (lp advances forward), then star: label uses do-while loop calling advance() recursively, decrementing lp on each failure — this is greedy matching with backtracking. For `a*b` on input `aaab`: first consume all `a`s (lp = end of aaa), then try matching `b` at each position from rightmost backward until success or curlp reached. The inner fast path (if(*ep == CCHR)) skips positions where the next required char doesn't match, avoiding wasteful recursive advance() calls. This bytecode VM + greedy + backtrack pattern is the ancestor of every regex engine: sed, awk, Perl, Python re, Ruby Regexp, Java Pattern, JavaScript RegExp.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why is regex matching so slow on pathological inputs like /a*a*a*a*b/? Because of this: greedy + backtrack. advance() consumes as much as possible (the greedy part), then recursively tries the rest. If it fails, lp-- (give back one char) and retry. Worst case, exponential time. The 1979 design is mathematically correct and easy to understand; modern engines (RE2, Rust regex) avoid backtracking entirely by compiling to DFAs, achieving linear time at the cost of less expressive regex (no backreferences). But every Perl-compatible regex engine — PCRE, JavaScript, Python — still uses Thompson's greedy+backtrack from 1973's grep.

appraiser_note: advance() はバイトコード VM。 CCHR/CDOT/CDOL/CEOF は即時 1 文字マッチ。 **CCHR|STAR が greedy + backtrack**: while ループで一致する限り消費 (lp 前進)、 star: ラベルで残りを advance() 再帰呼び出し、 失敗なら lp-- で 1 文字戻して再試行。 `a*b` で入力 `aaab` の場合: まず全 `a` 消費 (lp = aaa の次)、 そこから後ろ向きに `b` マッチを試し、 成功または curlp 到達まで。 内側の fast path (`if(*ep == CCHR)`) は次の必須文字が一致しない位置をスキップ、 無駄な advance() 再帰を回避。 bytecode VM + greedy + backtrack パターンが全 regex エンジンの祖: sed、 awk、 Perl、 Python re、 Ruby、 Java Pattern、 JavaScript RegExp。 RE2 や Rust regex は backtrack を完全に避けて DFA で線形時間を達成するが、 PCRE / JS / Python は今も Thompson の 1973 年方式。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Char class `[abc]` or `[a-z]` stored as 16-byte bitmap (128 bits, sufficient for 7-bit ASCII). bittab[8] = {1,2,4,8,16,32,64,128} maps bit-index to bit-mask. To set bit for char c: `ep[c >> 3] |= bittab[c & 07]` — c >> 3 = byte index, c & 07 = bit position. Range `a-z` iterates from sp[-2] to *sp setting all bits in between. Negation `[^...]` flips all 16 bytes via XOR with -1, then clears bit 0 (NUL avoidance). Match in advance: `ep[c >> 3] & bittab[c & 07]` — O(1) lookup, no string scan. Modern regex engines extend this to 128KB bitmap for Unicode BMP, but the bit-twiddling logic is identical.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you test 'is c in [a-z]?' in O(1)? Build a bitmap: 128 bits where bit c is 1 if c is in the class. Test = single bit lookup. 1979's grep does this in 16 bytes. Range syntax 'a-z' becomes a for-loop setting 26 bits. Negation 'XOR with -1' flips them all in 16 instructions. The math is high-school combinatorics; the elegance is making it 16 bytes per char class instead of hundreds. Modern PCRE uses 8KB bitmaps for Unicode BMP; the principle is the same as 1979 grep — bit-tested set membership for character classes.

appraiser_note: 文字クラス `[abc]` や `[a-z]` を **16 バイト = 128 ビットのビットマップ**で格納 (7 bit ASCII で十分)。 `bittab[8] = {1,2,4,8,16,32,64,128}` はビット位置からマスクへの変換テーブル。 文字 c のビットを立てる: `ep[c >> 3] |= bittab[c & 07]`——c >> 3 = バイトインデックス、 c & 07 = ビット位置。 範囲 `a-z` は sp[-2] から *sp までループで全ビットセット。 否定 `[^...]` は 16 バイト全部を XOR -1 で反転、 bit 0 (NUL) だけクリア。 advance でのマッチは `ep[c >> 3] & bittab[c & 07]`——**O(1) 一発検査**、 文字列スキャン不要。 現代の PCRE は Unicode BMP 用に 8KB ビットマップに拡張するが、 ビット操作ロジックは 1979 grep と同一。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Fast path optimization: when pattern starts with literal char (opcode CCHR), only test positions where that char appears. The do-while loop with `if (*p1 != c) continue` skips non-matching positions and only calls advance() at matching ones. For pattern 'error' on 1000-line file with 10 occurrences of 'e', advance() is called 10 times instead of (avg-line-length × 1000) times. This is the proto-Boyer-Moore: scan for the first byte, do full match only at candidates. Modern grep variants (GNU grep, ag, ripgrep) implement Boyer-Moore-Horspool or Aho-Corasick with SIMD-accelerated byte scanning. The 1979 fast path is the ancestor.
ai_analysis.human_story: Naive regex matching tries advance() at every input position. Slow. grep's 1979 optimization: if pattern starts with literal 'e', only try advance() at positions where 'e' appears. This is the proto-Boyer-Moore — a poor man's version, decades before BM/BMH/Aho-Corasick became standard. ripgrep (2016) does the same thing with SIMD instructions scanning 32 bytes at a time. The fundamental insight — 'don't call the expensive matcher at positions that can't possibly match' — is from grep's 6-line fast path in 1979.

appraiser_note: fast path 最適化: パターンが**リテラル文字で始まる** (opcode CCHR) 場合、 その文字が出現する位置でのみ advance() を呼ぶ。 do-while ループの `if (*p1 != c) continue` で非マッチ位置をスキップ、 マッチ位置でのみ advance()。 パターン 'error' で 1000 行ファイル中 'e' が 10 箇所なら、 advance() 呼び出しは 10 回 (vs 平均行長 × 1000 回)。 これが **proto-Boyer-Moore**——先頭バイトでスキャン、 候補位置でのみフルマッチ。 現代の GNU grep、 ag、 ripgrep は Boyer-Moore-Horspool や Aho-Corasick + SIMD バイトスキャンで実装、 ripgrep は 32 バイト並列スキャン。 「**マッチしえない位置で高コストマッチャを呼ばない**」 という根本的洞察が、 6 行の fast path に。

ai_analysis.technical_context: grep -y (case-insensitive) is implemented by expanding the pattern itself: each lowercase letter becomes [Uu] char class before compile() runs. Pattern `abc` → `[Aa][Bb][Cc]`. Regex engine itself stays unchanged — char class machinery handles both cases. Existing `[...]` sequences pass through unmodified (the while loop), backslash escapes pass through too. Limitations: uppercase input letters don't expand (only islower triggers), and existing [abc] don't get expanded to [aAbBcC]. Later -i flag (4.0BSD) fixed this by adding case-insensitive logic inside the matcher. But the 1979 design — feature = pattern transformation, not engine extension — is the Unix philosophy in 17 lines.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you add case-insensitive matching without changing the regex engine? Expand the pattern. abc becomes [Aa][Bb][Cc]. The regex engine, blissfully unchanged, sees a normal pattern with char classes. 17 lines added a feature without touching the 300+ lines of compile() and advance(). Limitations exist (uppercase doesn't expand, existing [abc] doesn't get extended), but the design is the Unix philosophy made concrete: features = composition of existing primitives, not extensions to the engine. The -i flag in 4.0BSD took the engine-modification approach instead — more flexible, more code.

appraiser_note: grep -y (case-insensitive) は **パターン自体を展開**して実装: 各小文字を [Uu] 文字クラスに変換、 compile() 前段で。 パターン `abc` → `[Aa][Bb][Cc]`。 **regex エンジン本体は変更ゼロ**——既存の文字クラス機能が両ケースを処理。 既存 `[...]` シーケンスはそのまま通過 (while ループ)、 バックスラッシュエスケープもそのまま。 制限: 大文字入力は展開しない (islower のみトリガー)、 既存 [abc] は [aAbBcC] に拡張されない。 後の -i フラグ (4.0BSD) で matcher 内部に case-insensitive ロジックを追加して改善。 だが 1979 年の設計——「**機能 = パターン変換、 エンジン拡張ではなく**」——は **Unix 哲学を 17 行で具現化**。

ai_analysis.technical_context: ed defines the Unix text editor command language in ~25 single-character commands: a/c/d/i (modify), p/P/l (display), g/v (global), s (substitute), q/Q (quit), r/w (file I/O), k (mark), m/t (move/copy), u (undo), ! (shell), = (address). Each command optionally takes [addr1[,addr2]] prefix. address() parses 6 forms: numeric, dot, dollar, +N/-N, /regex/, 'mark. This grammar (addresses + 1-char command + arguments) became the universal Unix editor language. vi's :%s/foo/bar/g, sed's 1,$d, grep's /pattern/ — all inherit. Written by Ken Thompson in 1971 (V1 Unix), 50 years before POSIX still mandates ed.
ai_analysis.human_story: Twenty-five characters of vocabulary, and you can edit any text file ever written. Ken Thompson invented this language in 1971 for the V1 Unix editor. The grammar was so good that vi (1976) adopted it as the colon-command mode. sed (1973) became 'ed without interaction'. grep (1973) became 'just the g/re/p part'. ex (vi's underlying engine) is ed with extensions. The 25 commands of ed are the vocabulary of Unix text processing — a one-letter language that defined how programmers talk to text for half a century.

appraiser_note: ed は Unix テキストエディタコマンド言語を **約 25 個の 1 文字コマンド**で定義: a/c/d/i (編集)、 p/P/l (表示)、 g/v (global)、 s (substitute)、 q/Q (quit)、 r/w (file I/O)、 k (mark)、 m/t (move/copy)、 u (undo)、 ! (shell)、 = (address)。 各コマンドは optional `[addr1[,addr2]]` 接頭辞を取る。 address() は 6 形式をパース: 数値、 .、 $、 +N/-N、 /regex/、 'mark。 この文法 (アドレス + 1 文字コマンド + 引数) が **Unix エディタ言語**として普遍化——vi の :%s/foo/bar/g、 sed の 1,$d、 grep の /pattern/、 すべて継承。 Ken Thompson が 1971 年 (V1 Unix) に書き、 50 年後の POSIX で今も標準。

ai_analysis.technical_context: ed.c and grep.c share the same regex opcode constants (CCHR=2, CDOT=4, CCL=6, NCCL=8, CDOL=10, CEOF=11, CKET=12, STAR=01, CBRA=1) — because grep was forked from ed's g/re/p command in 1973. The regex engine architecture is identical: compile() translates pattern to bytecode (expbuf), execute() runs a bytecode VM matching against text. Only CBACK differs: 14 in ed, 18 in grep (drifted during fork). The Thompson regex grammar — literal, ., *, [...], [^...], ^, $, \(...\), \1-\9 — became POSIX BRE. Every Unix regex tool (ed/sed/grep/awk/vi/vim) traces back to this engine.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why do ed, grep, sed, and vi all use the same regex syntax? Because they descended from the same code. Ken Thompson wrote the regex engine for ed (V1, 1971); grep (1973) was carved out as 'just g/re/p'; sed (1973) became 'non-interactive ed'; vi (1976) embedded ex which embedded ed. The opcode constants CCHR=2, CDOT=4, CCL=6 are identical across ed.c and grep.c because someone literally copy-pasted them. Half a century later, when you write `grep '^[a-z]*$'` you're typing exactly the syntax Thompson designed for ed in 1971.

appraiser_note: ed.c と grep.c は **同じ regex オペコード定数** (CCHR=2, CDOT=4, CCL=6, NCCL=8, CDOL=10, CEOF=11, CKET=12, STAR=01, CBRA=1) を共有——grep が 1973 年に ed の g/re/p コマンドから fork されたから。 regex エンジン構造は同一: compile() がパターンをバイトコード (expbuf) に翻訳、 execute() がテキストとマッチするバイトコード VM。 CBACK だけ違う (ed: 14、 grep: 18、 fork 後にドリフト)。 Thompson regex 文法 (リテラル、 ., *, [...], [^...], ^, $, \(...\), \1-\9) が **POSIX BRE** として標準化。 ed/sed/grep/awk/vi/vim すべて 1971 年の Thompson エンジンに遡る。

ai_analysis.technical_context: ed stores the entire edited file in /tmp/eXXXXX as 256 blocks of 512 bytes (128KB max). Only two 512-byte buffers (ibuff, obuff) are in memory — a user-space implementation of OS swapping. tline encodes position as (block_no << 8) | (word_offset). zero[] array indexes line numbers → tline pointers (one int per line = 2 bytes on PDP-11). 64K lines × 2 bytes = 128KB line index. When accessing different block: write old buffer to tempfile, read new one. Reading and writing have separate buffers (ibuff/obuff) to avoid thrashing when interleaving reads/writes. The 256-block limit was sufficient for V1 Unix source files. PDP-11 had ~256KB physical RAM total — ed's design used <2KB of buffer space.
ai_analysis.human_story: PDP-11 had 256KB total memory in 1971. Loading a 100KB file would consume 40% of RAM. Thompson's answer: don't load the file. Keep it in /tmp/eXXXXX as 256 blocks of 512 bytes (128KB max). Memory holds just two 512-byte buffers and a line index. Block access patterns work like OS paging: cache hit (same block) is free, cache miss writes old buffer and reads new. ed implemented swapping in user-space, 8 years before the 4.0BSD kernel got demand paging. The 'editor that doesn't fit the file in RAM' design pattern lived on in sed, awk, and grep — all stream-process files block by block.

appraiser_note: ed は **編集中ファイル全体を /tmp/eXXXXX** に **256 ブロック × 512 バイト = 128KB** で保持。 メモリには **わずか 2 つの 512 バイトバッファ (ibuff/obuff)**——ユーザー空間での OS スワッピング実装。 tline は (ブロック番号 << 8) | ワードオフセットでエンコード。 zero[] 配列が行番号 → tline ポインタの索引 (1 行 = int = PDP-11 で 2 バイト)、 64K 行 × 2 バイト = 128KB の行索引。 違うブロックにアクセスしたら古いバッファを tempfile に書き戻し、 新しいブロックをロード。 入力/出力で別バッファ (ibuff/obuff) を持つのは read/write 交互のスラッシング防止。 PDP-11 256KB RAM 時代、 ed のバッファ消費は 2KB 未満。 4.0BSD のデマンドページングより 8 年早い、 ユーザー空間 swapping。 「ファイルを RAM に乗せないエディタ」 設計が sed/awk/grep のストリーム処理へ継承。

ai_analysis.technical_context: global() implements g/regex/command — apply command to every matching line. Algorithm: (1) compile regex, (2) gather command string into globuf, (3) mark matching lines using bit 0 of line address (in-place flag), (4) for each marked line, set dot, redirect input to globuf, recursively call commands(). The 'globp' static enables nested input source switching. `v` is negated global (execute on non-matching lines). The literal command sequence `g/.../d` is so common it gets a dedicated gdelete() fast path (O(n) vs O(n²) for the general case where line numbers shift). The command name `grep` derives from `g/re/p` — this very function. sed's address language and global commands inherit directly.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `g/error/d` in ed and every error-line vanishes. It's the killer feature: apply a command to all matching lines. global() does it via mark-and-execute: scan once to flag matches in the line address's bit 0, then loop executing the command on each flagged line. The recursion (commands() calls itself with input redirected to globuf) lets any ed command be the body. The special case for `g/.../d` is the speed hack — naive implementation is O(n²), the dedicated gdelete is O(n). The name `grep` came directly from this function: G-Regular Expression-Print, extracted into its own command in 1973.

appraiser_note: global() は **g/regex/command** を実装——マッチする全行にコマンド適用。 アルゴリズム: (1) regex コンパイル、 (2) コマンド文字列を globuf に蓄積、 (3) マッチする行に **行アドレスの bit 0** をマーク (in-place flag)、 (4) マークされた各行で dot を設定、 commands() の入力を globuf にリダイレクトして **再帰呼び出し**。 globp 静的変数で入力ソース切替を実現。 `v` は negated global (非マッチ行で実行)。 `g/.../d` は非常に頻繁なので **専用の gdelete() 高速パス** (汎用 O(n²) vs 専用 O(n))。 **コマンド名 `grep` の語源**——g/re/p、 この関数がそのまま 1973 年に独立コマンド化。 sed のアドレス言語と global コマンドが直接継承。

ai_analysis.technical_context: substitute() implements s/old/new/[flags]. Per line: execute regex, if match call dosub(); with g flag, loop until no more matches. dosub() builds genbuf: pre-match text + expanded replacement + post-match text. Replacement supports special chars: '&' = entire match, '\1'-'\9' = backreferences to \(...\) groups. This is the heart of sed — `sed s/old/new/` is exactly ed's substitute() running on each line of stdin. POSIX BRE inherits the & and \1-\9 syntax. vim's :s/.../.../, perl's s///, every regex-replace tool descends from this. The compile/execute regex pair plus substitute() forms the core text transformation engine of Unix.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `s/foo/bar/` in ed — line becomes bar where foo was. Type `s/\(foo\)\(bar\)/\2\1/` — foobar becomes barfoo. Type `s/abc/&!/g` — every 'abc' becomes 'abc!'. These three operations — substitute, backref, '&' for match — are sed's entire raison d'être, vim's :s command, perl's s/// operator. All extracted from ed's 70-line substitute() + dosub() pair. The '&' meaning 'whole match' and \1-\9 meaning 'capture groups' are 1971 conventions still binding in 2026. Five decades of regex-replace UI compressed into one Thompson algorithm.

appraiser_note: substitute() は **s/old/new/[flags]** を実装。 行ごと: regex execute、 マッチしたら dosub()、 g フラグなら更にマッチしなくなるまでループ。 dosub() は genbuf を構築: マッチ前 + 展開された置換文字列 + マッチ後。 置換文字列は特殊文字を解釈: **`&` = マッチ全体**、 **`\1`〜`\9` = `\(...\)` グループのバックリファレンス**。 これが **sed の本質**——`sed s/old/new/` は ed の substitute() を stdin の各行で実行するだけ。 POSIX BRE が `&` と `\1`〜`\9` を継承。 vim の :s/...//、 perl の s///、 全 regex 置換ツールが祖先。 compile/execute regex ペア + substitute() が **Unix テキスト変換エンジンの核心**——5 ペアの操作 (substitute、 backref、 &) が 50 年後の vim/sed/perl の置換 UI を支配。

ai_analysis.technical_context: sed compiles its script into a fixed array ptrspace[100] of union reptr instructions. Each reptr holds: ad1/ad2 (addresses), re1 (regex), rhs (replacement), fcode (write file), command (1-byte opcode), and 4 flag bits (gfl/pfl/inar/negfl). reptr1 variant for normal commands, reptr2 variant for b/t branches (uses lb1 jump target instead of re1). The union shares the first 5 fields so the compiler can iterate the array without checking variant — common-prefix idiom. 25 opcodes: SCOM (substitute), DCOM (delete), GCOM (get hold), HCOM (hold), XCOM (exchange), BCOM (branch), TCOM (test-branch), YCOM (transliterate), ACOM (append), etc. PTRSIZE=100 means max 100 instructions per sed script — realistic in 1975.
ai_analysis.human_story: sed differs from ed in one critical way: it compiles. ed reads a command, executes, reads another. sed reads the whole script up-front, builds an instruction array, then runs each instruction on every input line. The union reptr is the compiled-instruction representation: address(es), regex, replacement, command code, flags. The reptr1/reptr2 union split is the C idiom for 'instructions look slightly different but share metadata' — same pattern modern VMs use for op encoding. PTRSIZE=100 limits scripts; today's GNU sed lifts this, but Lee McMahon's 1975 design assumed concise sed programs.

appraiser_note: sed はスクリプトを **固定配列 ptrspace[100]** の **union reptr 命令**にコンパイルする。 各 reptr が ad1/ad2 (アドレス)、 re1 (regex)、 rhs (置換)、 fcode (w ファイル)、 command (1 バイト opcode)、 4 フラグビット (gfl/pfl/inar/negfl) を持つ。 reptr1 が通常命令、 reptr2 が b/t 分岐 (re1 の代わりに lb1 jump target)。 union が先頭 5 フィールドを共有してコンパイラが variant を意識せず配列を走査できる——「共通プレフィックス」 イディオム。 25 opcode: SCOM (substitute)、 DCOM (delete)、 GCOM (get hold)、 HCOM (hold)、 XCOM (exchange)、 BCOM (branch)、 TCOM (test-branch)、 YCOM (transliterate)、 ACOM (append) など。 PTRSIZE=100 で最大 100 命令——1975 年の現実的上限。 ed (インタラクティブ) との根本差は「コンパイル」 する点、 後の awk/perl/Python pandas DSL の祖。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Two-phase architecture: fcomp() in sed0.c reads sed script (from -f file, -e inline, or first arg) and compiles to ptrspace[]. execute() in sed1.c opens each input file and loops: read line via gline() into linebuf (pattern space), iterate all instructions checking addresses and running commands, auto-print pattern space unless -n. Memory stays constant: linebuf=4KB, holdsp=4KB, ptrspace=100×~50bytes=5KB, regex space=5KB. Total ~18KB regardless of input file size. The key insight: compile once, stream forever. Process 10GB file with fixed memory. This is the Unix pipeline DNA: each filter (grep/sed/awk/sort/uniq) does one job in constant memory, composed via |.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why can you `cat huge.log | sed 's/foo/bar/' | grep error | sort | uniq` and process terabytes of data on a laptop? Because each pipeline stage maintains constant memory. sed pioneered this design: compile the script once, then stream input lines through fixed-size buffers. 1975's Lee McMahon designed 'compile + stream' as the model for text filters. The whole Unix shell-fu culture — chained one-liners processing arbitrary data — depends on every filter behaving like sed: stateless across lines, memory-bounded, composable. Stream editing is fundamental to how Unix scales.

appraiser_note: **2 フェーズアーキテクチャ**: sed0.c の fcomp() が sed スクリプト (-f ファイル、 -e インライン、 または最初の引数) を読んで ptrspace[] にコンパイル、 sed1.c の execute() が各入力ファイルを開いて: gline() で 1 行を linebuf (pattern space) に読む、 全命令を順次評価 (アドレス → コマンド実行)、 -n フラグ無ければ pattern space を自動 print。 メモリ一定: linebuf=4KB、 holdsp=4KB、 ptrspace=100×~50バイト=5KB、 regex space=5KB、 合計 ~18KB——入力ファイルサイズに無関係。 **「コンパイル一度、 ストリーム永久」** の発想——10GB ファイルを固定メモリで処理。 Unix pipeline DNA: 各フィルタ (grep/sed/awk/sort/uniq) が一定メモリで一仕事、 | で結合。 `cat huge.log | sed | grep | sort | uniq` がノート PC で動くのは、 sed が 1975 年に確立した設計のおかげ。

text:
/* sed.h: */
#define CBRA   1   /* same as ed/grep */
#define CCHR   2   /* same */
#define CDOT   4   /* same */
#define CCL    6   /* same */
#define CNL    8   /* sed extension (ed has NCCL=8) */
#define CDOL  10   /* same */
#define CEOF  11   /* same */
#define CKET  12   /* same */
#define CNULL 13   /* sed extension */
#define CLNUM 14   /* sed: line number address */
#define CEND  16   /* sed: $ end-of-input */
#define CDONT 17   /* sed extension */
#define CBACK 18

#define STAR  01

long tlno[NLINES];     /* 256 line-number table */
int nlno;

ai_analysis.technical_context: sed shares regex opcodes with ed (#167) and grep (#166): CCHR=2, CDOT=4, CCL=6, CDOL=10, CEOF=11, CKET=12, CBRA=1, STAR=01. New additions: CLNUM (line-number address like `5d` for delete line 5), CEND ($ as end-of-input rather than end-of-pattern), CNL (newline anchor for multi-line patterns), CNULL/CDONT (internal optimization markers). tlno[256] stores actual line numbers referenced by CLNUM — execute() looks up `lnum != tlno[c]` to check if current line matches. Note: CNL=8 conflicts with ed's NCCL=8 (negated char class) — sed prefers stream-processing primitives over ed's edit-buffer ones. The shared base + sed-specific extensions show evolutionary regex design.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why do ed, grep, and sed all use regex constants CCHR=2, CDOT=4, CCL=6? Because they share lineage. ed (1971) wrote the regex engine; grep (1973) forked it; sed (1975) inherited the same numbers. But sed added stream-specific opcodes: CLNUM for 'line 5', CEND for 'last line of input'. The line-number table tlno[256] is sed-only — ed already knows line numbers via the editor buffer, grep doesn't care, but sed needs them because the user writes `1,$p` or `100d` in the script. The shared+extended design lets sed reuse 90% of Thompson's regex while adding the 10% that stream editing demands.

appraiser_note: sed は ed (#167) と grep (#166) と regex opcode を共有: CCHR=2, CDOT=4, CCL=6, CDOL=10, CEOF=11, CKET=12, CBRA=1, STAR=01。 sed 独自の拡張: **CLNUM** (行番号アドレス、 `5d` で 5 行目削除)、 **CEND** ($ をパターン末尾ではなく **入力末尾**として)、 **CNL** (改行アンカー、 複数行パターン用)、 CNULL/CDONT (内部最適化)。 **tlno[256]** が CLNUM 参照の実際の行番号を保存——execute() が `lnum != tlno[c]` でカレント行が該当するかチェック。 注意: CNL=8 は ed の NCCL=8 (否定 char class) と衝突——sed はストリーム処理プリミティブを ed の編集バッファ用 opcode より優先。 共有ベース + sed 固有拡張で、 Thompson regex の 90% を再利用しながら stream editing 固有の 10% を追加。

ai_analysis.technical_context: sed has TWO buffers, not one: pattern space (linebuf, holds current line being edited) and hold space (holdsp, scratch buffer). Commands move data between them: h (hold=pattern), H (hold+=pattern), g (pattern=hold), G (pattern+=hold), x (swap). This enables multi-line processing despite sed's per-line streaming model. Hold space lets sed remember context: previous line, accumulated data, intermediate results. Example: `1!G;h;$!d` reverses a file by accumulating reversed-order lines in hold space. Example: collapsing blank lines uses hold space to track 'was previous line blank?'. Both buffers are 4000 bytes — sufficient for any line up to 4KB, accumulating maybe dozens of lines. GNU sed extends to dynamic sizing; classic sed's 4000-byte cap is fixed.
ai_analysis.human_story: sed processes one line at a time — how does it handle multi-line operations? Answer: hold space. A second 4KB buffer alongside the pattern space. 5 commands move data between them (h H g G x). Want to remember the previous line? `h` saves it. Want to swap current and saved? `x`. Want to print every other line? Use hold space to track parity. Want to reverse the file? Accumulate lines in hold space with `H`, then print at the end. Hold space turns stateless line-processing into stateful sequential-processing, in 5 commands. The 1975 design is still teaching us 'state in a fixed-size scratch buffer' — the same pattern as register-based VMs, hardware accumulators, calculator displays.

appraiser_note: sed は **2 つのバッファ**を持つ: pattern space (linebuf、 現在編集中の行) + hold space (holdsp、 スクラッチバッファ)。 5 つのコマンドで両者間のデータ移動: **h** (hold=pattern)、 **H** (hold+=pattern)、 **g** (pattern=hold)、 **G** (pattern+=hold)、 **x** (swap)。 sed の per-line streaming モデルにもかかわらず **複数行処理**が可能。 hold space で文脈を記憶: 前の行、 累積データ、 中間結果。 例: `1!G;h;$!d` で逆順表示 (hold space に逆順蓄積)、 `/./,/^$/{/^$/h;/./{x;/^$/d}}` で連続空行を 1 行に。 両バッファとも 4000 バイト——4KB 以内の任意の行、 数十行分の累積に十分。 GNU sed は動的サイズだが古典 sed は 4000 バイト固定。 1975 年の設計が「**固定サイズスクラッチバッファに状態**」 という、 レジスタ VM/ハードウェアアキュムレータ/電卓表示と同じパターンを教える。

ai_analysis.technical_context: sed has goto: `b label` (unconditional branch) and `t label` (test branch — jumps only after successful substitution). `:label` defines a target. ltab[50] is the label table (8-char names). reptr2's lb1 field holds the resolved jump target (filled by dechain() at end of fcomp). At execute time, command() sets jflag=1 on branch, and execute() checks jflag to set `ipc = ipc->lb1`. The b without label jumps to script end (skip remaining commands for this line). With labels + branches + regex matching + 5 buffer commands (h/H/g/G/x), sed is Turing-complete: it can implement addition (in unary), prime checking, even Conway's Life. Most users only ever write 1-line sed scripts, but the language capability is full.
ai_analysis.human_story: Did you know sed is Turing-complete? With `b label` (jump), `t label` (jump-if-substitution-succeeded), labels (`:foo`), regex matching, and the 5 buffer commands, you can compute anything. Someone implemented Conway's Life in sed. Someone wrote a sed prime sieve. Lee McMahon in 1975 didn't intend sed to be a general programming language — but adding labels and branches made it one. Most people use sed for `sed 's/old/new/'` one-liners, never touching `b`/`t`. But the capability is there. The lesson: small composable primitives (matching + state buffers + jumps) can produce universal computation. sed is the smallest accidentally-Turing-complete Unix tool.

appraiser_note: sed には **goto** がある: `b label` (無条件分岐) と `t label` (テスト分岐——`s///` 成功時のみジャンプ)。 `:label` でターゲット定義。 ltab[50] がラベルテーブル (8 文字名)。 reptr2 の lb1 フィールドが解決済みジャンプターゲットを保持 (fcomp 末尾の dechain() で解決)。 実行時 command() が分岐で jflag=1 をセット、 execute() が jflag を見て `ipc = ipc->lb1`。 ラベル無しの b はスクリプト末尾へジャンプ (この行の残りコマンドをスキップ)。 ラベル + 分岐 + regex マッチ + 5 つのバッファコマンド (h/H/g/G/x) で sed は **Turing 完備**——加算 (1 進法)、 素数チェック、 コンウェイのライフゲームまで実装可能。 多くのユーザーは 1 行 sed しか書かないが、 言語としては完全。 Lee McMahon は 1975 年に sed を汎用言語にする気は無かったが、 ラベル + 分岐の追加で意図せず Turing 完備になった。 「**小さな合成可能プリミティブ (マッチ + 状態バッファ + ジャンプ)** で普遍計算が可能」 という教訓。

ai_analysis.technical_context: awk.g.y defines a complete programming language in 269 lines of yacc grammar. Tokens cover comparison operators (LT/LE/GT/GE/EQ/NE), match operators (MATCH/NOTMATCH = ~ !~), arithmetic (ADD/MINUS/MULT/DIVIDE/MOD), assignment (ASSIGN/ADDEQ/SUBEQ/MULTEQ/DIVEQ/MODEQ), control flow (IF/ELSE/WHILE/FOR/IN/NEXT/EXIT/BREAK/CONTINUE), I/O (PRINT/PRINTF/SPRINTF), built-in functions (SPLIT/SUBSTR/INDEX/MATCH), pre/post inc-dec (POSTINCR/PREINCR/POSTDECR/PREDECR). Operator precedence cascade from low (ASGNOP) to high (FIELD INDIRECT). Uses lex tokenizer (awk.lx.l) — yacc + lex applied seriously, unlike ed/grep/sed's hand-written parsers. Aho was co-author of the Dragon Book (Compilers: Principles, Techniques, Tools) — awk is his reference implementation of compiler theory.
ai_analysis.human_story: ed, grep, sed all hand-rolled their parsers. awk did it with yacc + lex — using compiler theory seriously. Why? Because awk needed to be a real programming language with proper operator precedence, not a one-letter command interpreter. Alfred Aho, one of awk's three authors, co-wrote the Dragon Book — the standard compiler textbook. awk is, in a sense, Aho's compiler-theory in action. 269 lines of yacc express what would take 1000+ lines of recursive-descent parser. Lex tokenizes, yacc parses, run.c interprets — a clean compiler pipeline that became the model for Perl, PHP, Python, Ruby all building proper grammar.

appraiser_note: awk.g.y は **269 行の yacc 文法**で完全プログラミング言語を定義。 トークン: 比較演算子 (LT/LE/GT/GE/EQ/NE)、 マッチ演算子 (MATCH/NOTMATCH = ~ !~)、 算術 (ADD/MINUS/MULT/DIVIDE/MOD)、 代入 (ASSIGN/ADDEQ/SUBEQ/MULTEQ/DIVEQ/MODEQ)、 制御フロー (IF/ELSE/WHILE/FOR/IN/NEXT/EXIT/BREAK/CONTINUE)、 I/O (PRINT/PRINTF/SPRINTF)、 組み込み関数 (SPLIT/SUBSTR/INDEX/MATCH)、 前/後置 inc-dec。 演算子優先順位カスケード: 最低 ASGNOP → 最高 FIELD INDIRECT。 lex トークナイザ (awk.lx.l) と組み合わせ——ed/grep/sed の手書きパーサと違い、 **yacc + lex を本気で適用**。 Aho は Dragon Book (Compilers: Principles, Techniques, Tools) の共著者——awk は **彼のコンパイラ理論の参考実装**。 269 行の yacc が再帰下降パーサ 1000+ 行に相当。 後の Perl/PHP/Python/Ruby が proper grammar で書く流派の祖。

ai_analysis.technical_context: awk's pattern-action paradigm: program = BEGIN block + pattern-action statements + END block. Each pa_stat = pattern + action block. Runtime: BEGIN runs once before input, then for each input line iterate all pa_stats (if pattern matches, run action), END runs once after input. Patterns can be /regex/, expressions ($3 > 100), ranges (/start/,/end/), or compound (pat1 && pat2). Actions are statement blocks with full language features (if/while/for/print/arrays). PASTAT AST node holds (pattern, action) pair. This extends sed's /regex/ command model into structured-language scope. Data-driven programming: declare what to do per line type, not how to iterate. Modern descendants: SQL SELECT WHERE, pandas .apply, MapReduce mappers, Spark DataFrame operations.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does `awk '{print $1}'` feel different from `sed s/foo/bar/`? Because awk is declarative: 'for each line, do this'. sed is closer to a command stream. awk's pattern-action model says 'declare what to do when X matches'. BEGIN initializes, patterns trigger actions, END summarizes. This is the data-driven programming pattern: structure code around 'what input shapes deserve what response'. SQL's SELECT...WHERE..., pandas's df.apply(), MapReduce's mappers, Spark's filter+map — all express the same idea: declarative per-record processing. awk pioneered it in 1977 for text streams.

appraiser_note: awk の **pattern-action パラダイム**: プログラム = BEGIN ブロック + pattern-action 文の列 + END ブロック。 各 pa_stat = pattern + action ブロック。 ランタイム: BEGIN を 1 回実行 → 各入力行で全 pa_stats を順次評価 (pattern 一致なら action) → END を 1 回実行。 パターンは /regex/、 式 ($3 > 100)、 範囲 (/start/,/end/)、 複合 (pat1 && pat2)。 アクションは複文 (if/while/for/print/array)。 PASTAT AST ノードが (pattern, action) ペアを保持。 sed の /regex/ command を **構造化言語のスコープ**に拡張。 **データ駆動型プログラミング**: 「どう繰り返すか」 ではなく 「どの入力に何をするか」 を宣言。 後の SQL SELECT WHERE、 pandas .apply、 MapReduce mapper、 Spark DataFrame operations すべて同じ思想——awk が 1977 年にテキストストリームで先駆。

ai_analysis.technical_context: awk's Cell struct holds both string (sval) and numeric (fval) values with a type flag (tval) indicating which is current. getfval auto-converts string to double via atof; getsval auto-converts double to string via sprintf %g. `"42" + 1` works because + forces numeric context, triggering getfval. `42 " lines"` (juxtaposition = concat in awk) forces string context. Field splitting: readrec reads a line, sets $0, then walks FS-separated fields setting $1, $2, ..., updating NF and NR. Field separator FS defaults to whitespace, customizable (e.g. FS=":"). $NF, $(NF-1) work because field references are evaluated. This dynamic typing + automatic field splitting is awk's killer ergonomics: type as little as possible to process structured text.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `awk '{print $1+$2}'` on a file of two numbers per line — it works. The fields $1 and $2 are strings (from text input) but + makes awk convert them to numbers. Type `awk '{print $1".txt"}'` — concatenation, $1 stays a string. The 1977 awk made dynamic typing transparent: declare nothing, awk figures out from context. Combined with automatic field splitting (`$0`, `$1`, `$NF`), awk reads /etc/passwd more naturally than C: `awk -F: '{print $1, $5}'`. Perl, PHP, Python, JavaScript all inherit awk's principle: 'string vs number, just do the right thing'. The Cell struct holding both representations is 1977's gift to modern scripting.

appraiser_note: awk の Cell 構造体は **文字列 (sval) と数値 (fval) の両方**を保持し、 型フラグ (tval) で現在の形式を示す。 `getfval()` は atof で文字列→double 自動変換、 `getsval()` は sprintf %g で double→文字列。 `"42" + 1` が動くのは + が数値コンテキストを強制し getfval をトリガーするから、 `42 " lines"` (awk の juxtaposition = 連結) は文字列コンテキストを強制。 **フィールド分割**: readrec が 1 行読んで $0 設定、 FS 区切りで $1, $2, ... を設定、 NF と NR 更新。 FS デフォルトは空白、 カスタマイズ可 (`FS=":"`)。 $NF, $(NF-1) も評価可能。 動的型 + 自動フィールド分割 = awk のキラーエルゴノミクス: **「構造化テキスト処理に最小限のタイピング**」。 Perl/PHP/Python/JavaScript すべて awk の「文字列 vs 数値、 文脈で判断」 原則を継承。 Cell 構造体は 1977 年の現代スクリプトへの贈り物。

ai_analysis.technical_context: awk associative arrays: a["key"] = value where key is any string. Internally implemented as hash table (symtab[]) with dynamic sizing and chaining for collisions (tran.c). Index expression is evaluated, stringified, looked up or created in the array's symtab. `for (key in array)` iterates all keys. Multi-dimensional via SUBSEP separator: `a[i, j]` becomes `a[i SUBSEP j]` (default SUBSEP = \034 = ASCII FS). APL/SNOBOL/Lisp had associative data structures, but they were for 'programmer' languages. awk brought hash tables to 'Unix user' tool — natural syntax, no setup, just `a[key] = value`. Influence: Perl %hash, PHP $array, Python dict, JavaScript object, Ruby Hash — all descend from awk's syntax and semantics.
ai_analysis.human_story: Before awk, hash tables were a 'programmer' data structure — you used them in Lisp or wrote your own. After awk (1977), `a["key"] = value` was a one-liner for shell users. Want to count word occurrences? `awk '{for(i=1;i<=NF;i++)c[$i]++} END{for(w in c)print w,c[w]}'`. Want to group log lines by date? Same pattern. The hash table — that staple of every modern language — was popularized for everyday text processing by awk. Multi-dim `a[i,j]` via SUBSEP separator anticipated Python tuple keys `d[(i,j)]`. The 1977 design pattern of 'arrays-with-string-keys' is the foundation of dict in every scripting language since.

appraiser_note: awk の連想配列: `a["key"] = value`、 キーは任意の文字列。 内部実装は **hash table (symtab[])**、 動的サイズ + chaining (tran.c)。 インデックス式を評価 → 文字列化 → 配列の symtab で lookup or create。 `for (key in array)` で全キー走査。 多次元は **SUBSEP** で連結: `a[i, j]` は `a[i SUBSEP j]` (デフォルト SUBSEP = \034 = ASCII FS)。 APL/SNOBOL/Lisp も連想構造を持つが「プログラマ言語」 だった。 awk が **Unix ユーザーツール**として hash table を持ち込み——自然な構文、 セットアップ不要、 `a[key] = value` のみ。 影響: Perl %hash、 PHP $array、 Python dict、 JavaScript object、 Ruby Hash——すべて awk の構文と意味論を継承。 1977 年の「文字列キー配列」 デザインパターンが、 全スクリプト言語の dict の基盤。

ai_analysis.technical_context: MATCHOP (~ !~) is a binary operator: lhs is string expression, rhs is regex. `$1 ~ /^abc/` evaluates $1 then matches against /^abc/. Crucially, the regex side can be a variable: `$0 ~ pattern` where pattern is a string. This makes regex first-class values, not just literal patterns. makedfa() compiles regex to DFA (decision-free automaton) — implemented in b.c, separate from ed/grep/sed's NFA backtracking engine. DFA gives linear-time O(n) matching with no backtracking, no exponential blow-up — at the cost of not supporting backreferences. awk chose DFA because data processing requires worst-case guarantees. Later GNU grep, RE2 (Google), Rust regex all chose DFA for the same reason. Programmable regex via variables enables data-driven matching: pattern tables, runtime-constructed patterns, regex-as-config.
ai_analysis.human_story: awk made regex a first-class value. `$1 ~ /pattern/` is a boolean expression you can use anywhere — in if conditions, in compound patterns, in variable assignments. The regex can come from a variable: `pattern = read_config(); if ($0 ~ pattern) ...`. ed/grep/sed treat regex as static syntax; awk treats it as data. The b.c regex engine uses DFA (not NFA backtracking) for linear-time guaranteed performance — essential when processing GBs of log data. Modern DFA engines (RE2, Rust regex, GNU grep -F) descend from this 1977 choice. The MATCHOP operator's syntax (`~` and `!~`) survives in Perl, Ruby, and many other languages.

appraiser_note: **MATCHOP (~ !~)** は二項演算子: lhs は文字列式、 rhs は regex。 `$1 ~ /^abc/` は $1 を評価して /^abc/ とマッチ。 **regex 側を変数にできる**: `$0 ~ pattern` (pattern は文字列)。 これで **regex が第一級の値**になる、 単なるリテラルパターンではない。 makedfa() が regex を **DFA (決定性有限オートマトン)** にコンパイル——b.c で実装、 ed/grep/sed の NFA backtracking とは別。 DFA は **線形時間 O(n) 保証**、 backtrack/exponential blow-up なし——バックリファレンス不可の代わりに。 awk が DFA を選んだのは **データ処理に worst-case 保証が必要**だから。 後の GNU grep、 RE2 (Google)、 Rust regex も同じ理由で DFA 採用。 変数経由のプログラマブル regex で **データ駆動マッチング**が可能: パターン表、 実行時構築、 regex-as-config。 MATCHOP 構文 (`~`/`!~`) は Perl/Ruby/他言語にも継承。

ai_analysis.technical_context: echo.c is 23 lines total including #include. K&R-style C: argc/argv K&R declaration form, register hint for tight loop, nflg int as boolean counter, fputs+putchar rather than printf (avoids format string parsing overhead and vulnerability), exit(0) explicit even though main returning 0 would be equivalent (some pre-C89 compilers didn't honor main's return value). Function does: skip -n if present, fputs each remaining arg with space separator (no trailing space), final newline unless -n. Compiled binary ~1KB. Uses only fputs/putchar/exit from libc — minimal runtime dependency. The 23 lines exemplify Unix's 'do one thing well': no malloc, no error handling beyond exit(0), no format-string interpretation. The simplest useful command.
ai_analysis.human_story: 23 lines for the most-used Unix command after ls. echo is the 'hello world' of Unix tools: it must exist, it must be tiny, it must just work. K&R-era programmers showed off by writing the minimum — register hints, int-as-bool, fputs instead of printf (faster, smaller, safer). The exit(0) is intentionally explicit because pre-C89 compilers couldn't reliably handle main's return value. Every byte of the 23 lines is deliberate. It compiles to ~1KB. Modern shells like bash provide echo as a builtin to avoid the fork/exec cost — but the spec they implement traces to these 23 lines.

appraiser_note: echo.c は `#include` 含め **23 行**。 K&R style C: argc/argv K&R 宣言、 register ヒント、 nflg int を bool counter として、 fputs+putchar (printf 解析オーバーヘッドと format string 脆弱性回避)、 exit(0) 明示 (C89 前のコンパイラは main の return を exit code として扱わなかった)。 機能: -n あれば skip、 各引数を fputs + 空白区切り (末尾なし)、 -n なしなら最後に改行。 バイナリ ~1KB。 libc 依存は fputs/putchar/exit のみ——最小ランタイム依存。 23 行が Unix の **「Do one thing well」** を体現: malloc なし、 エラー処理は exit(0) のみ、 format string 解釈なし。 **最も単純な実用コマンド**——ls の次によく使われる Unix 道具で、 K&R 時代のプログラマが「最小で書く」 ことを誇示した姿。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Bell-32V echo (BSD lineage) interprets -n flag, doesn't process backslash escapes. System V echo does opposite: ignores -n (treats as literal arg), processes \n \t \b \c etc. POSIX 1003.1 couldn't unify and lets both behaviors stand. Result: portable shell scripts must avoid echo or test which version is present. GNU echo defaults BSD-style but adds -e to enable System V-style escapes, -E to force literal. bash builtin echo follows configurable xpg_echo shell option. The fork happened because System V wanted to add features (escape sequences), BSD wanted to add features (-n flag), and neither side compromised. The 1979 Bell-32V design is the BSD origin: 23 lines, -n yes, escapes no.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why is `echo -n hello` portable on macOS but prints "-n hello" on Solaris? Because echo split in two during the 1980s. Bell-32V (1979) had -n, no backslash escapes — BSD inherited this. System V (1983) added backslash escapes \n \t \c, dropped -n. POSIX gave up on standardizing. Scripts dependent on either behavior aren't portable. The lesson: when two designs disagree about a tiny command, the right answer might be 'use printf' — which is what POSIX recommends. echo's 23 lines spawned 40 years of shell-script portability headaches.

appraiser_note: Bell-32V echo (BSD 系) は -n フラグを解釈し、 バックスラッシュエスケープを処理しない。 System V echo は逆: -n を無視 (リテラル引数として扱う)、 \n \t \b \c などを解釈。 POSIX 1003.1 は統一できず**両方を許容**。 結果: 可搬シェルスクリプトは echo を避けるか、 どちらの版か判定する必要がある。 GNU echo はデフォルト BSD 系、 -e で System V escape 有効化、 -E でリテラル強制。 bash builtin echo は xpg_echo shell オプションで挙動切替。 fork の原因: System V は escape 機能追加、 BSD は -n フラグ追加を選び、 互いに妥協せず。 1979 年 Bell-32V の設計が BSD 起源——23 行、 -n あり、 escape なし。 `echo -n hello` が macOS で動くが Solaris で「-n hello」 と出るのはこの分岐の結果。 40 年の shell-script 移植性問題を生んだ 23 行。

ai_analysis.technical_context: echo deliberately uses fputs/putchar instead of printf. Reasons: (1) Performance: printf scans format string at runtime; fputs/putchar skip this. (2) Code size: avoids linking the format-parsing portion of libc, keeping binary tiny (~1KB). (3) Safety: printf with user-controlled format string is the format-string-vulnerability classic (CVE staple); fputs has no format concept, so user input can't escape its data role. (4) Clarity: 'output this string' as `fputs(s, fd)` is more direct than `printf("%s", s)`. Modern GNU echo shares code with printf in coreutils as a side benefit, but the 1979 design separated them. The same fputs-over-printf principle applies in modern critical paths: logging fast paths, embedded systems, kernel printk variants.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why doesn't echo use printf? Three reasons: speed (no format parsing), size (no format module in libc), safety (no format-string injection). In 1979, the third reason wasn't even articulated as a security concept — format-string vulnerabilities became famous in the late 1990s. But Bell-32V's echo accidentally got it right by using fputs, which has no concept of format directives. Modern critical-path logging often follows the same pattern: log handlers prefer raw writes over printf for performance and safety. The 23-line echo applied 'use the right tool' wisdom decades before security researchers formalized it.

appraiser_note: echo は意図的に printf ではなく **fputs/putchar** を使う。 理由: (1) **性能**: printf はランタイムで format 文字列をスキャン、 fputs/putchar はそのコストなし。 (2) **コードサイズ**: libc の format 解析部分をリンクせず、 バイナリを小さく (~1KB)。 (3) **安全性**: ユーザー制御の format 文字列を持つ printf は **format-string vulnerability** の古典 (CVE の定番)——fputs には format 概念がないので、 ユーザー入力がデータ役割から逃げられない。 (4) **明確性**: 「この文字列を出力」 は `fputs(s, fd)` の方が `printf("%s", s)` より直接的。 現代の GNU echo は coreutils で printf と code を共有する副次効果はあるが、 1979 年の設計は分離していた。 同じ「fputs-over-printf」 原則は現代の critical-path logging、 embedded、 kernel printk variant でも適用。 1979 年の echo が、 セキュリティ研究者が format-string 脆弱性を形式化する数十年前に偶然 「正しい道具を使う」 智慧を実践していた。

ai_analysis.technical_context: find uses 4-function recursive-descent parser (not yacc) for the predicate expression language. Functions handle precedence levels: exp() = -o, e1() = -a (explicit or implicit), e2() = !, e3() = parens + predicates. Implicit -a inserted when consecutive predicates lack operator. nxtarg() advances Ai index through argv, the input source — no separate lexer needed since argv pre-tokenized by shell. Randlast tracks 'last token was operand' to catch 'operand follows operand' errors. The hand-written parser is small (~80 lines) for a language with 3 operators + parens + ~11 predicates. Predates yacc availability or just deemed unnecessary for this size of grammar. Pattern: argv-as-input + recursive-descent parser appears in many small Unix tools (test, expr).
ai_analysis.human_story: yacc existed but find didn't use it — for a small predicate language, hand-written recursive descent is shorter and clearer. Four functions exp/e1/e2/e3 stack like nested boxes, each handling one precedence level. Implicit AND: write `find -name foo -mtime 7` and find auto-inserts AND between them — the parser detects 'two predicates in a row' and treats it as `-a`. This 'consecutive predicates AND together' is what makes find readable: you list conditions, not boolean operators. Half the find queries you'll ever write rely on implicit AND. 80 lines of hand parser give a domain-specific language with shell-script ergonomics.

appraiser_note: find は **yacc を使わず**、 4 関数の **再帰下降パーサ**で述語式言語を解析。 関数が優先順位レベルを担当: exp() = -o、 e1() = -a (明示/暗黙)、 e2() = !、 e3() = 括弧 + 述語。 連続述語に演算子無ければ **暗黙の -a** を挿入。 nxtarg() は Ai インデックスを argv に進める——shell が事前トークン化済みなので別 lexer 不要。 Randlast で「直前が operand か」 を追跡し、 「operand follows operand」 エラーをキャッチ。 手書きパーサは約 80 行——3 演算子 + 括弧 + 11 述語の文法には十分。 yacc 利用可能だったが小規模文法には不要と判断。 argv-as-input + 再帰下降パーサのパターンは多くの小さな Unix ツール (test、 expr) で再利用。 連続述語の暗黙 AND が find を読みやすくする——条件を列挙するだけで演算子を書かない。

ai_analysis.technical_context: descend() does depth-first recursive directory traversal. For each entry: stat to get metadata, evaluate predicate AST against current file (via *exlist->F(exlist)), if directory then chdir into it and recurse. Skip . and .. to avoid infinite recursion. V7 struct direct is 16 bytes (2-byte ino_t + 14-byte name); 512-byte block holds 32 entries (dsize >> 4). chdir-based traversal makes stat calls faster (shorter paths to inode lookup) at the cost of needing to remember Home to restore for -exec. Modern find uses fchdir(2) for thread/concurrency safety. Recursion depth = directory depth; deep trees can stack-overflow (no iterative alternative in 1979). The algorithm is preorder DFS: evaluate predicate before descending.
ai_analysis.human_story: Walking a directory tree: stat the entry, run predicates, if directory then descend. Skip . and .. (would loop forever). Read directory entries in 512-byte chunks (V7 directory format = array of 16-byte entries). 1979's chdir-based design: change to current directory, do work, recurse. Faster for stat (shorter paths) but loses absolute-position context — find remembers Home from popen('pwd') at startup. Modern find uses fchdir(2) for safety. The preorder DFS pattern — visit yourself, then visit children — is the canonical 'walk a tree' algorithm, applied here to filesystems.

appraiser_note: descend() は **深さ優先再帰**でディレクトリツリーを走査。 各エントリで: stat でメタデータ取得、 述語 AST を評価 (`(*exlist->F)(exlist)`)、 ディレクトリなら chdir で入って再帰。 **. と .. をスキップ**して無限再帰回避。 V7 struct direct は 16 バイト (2 バイト ino_t + 14 バイト名)、 512 バイトブロックで 32 エントリ (dsize >> 4)。 chdir ベース走査で stat が高速 (パスが短く inode lookup が短い) が、 絶対位置情報を失うので -exec のため Home を popen('pwd') で起動時記憶。 現代 find は fchdir(2) でスレッド/並行安全。 再帰深さ = ディレクトリ深さ、 深いツリーでスタックオーバーフローの可能性 (1979 年に iterative 代替なし)。 **preorder DFS** (自分を訪問してから子へ) の標準アルゴリズムをファイルシステムに適用。

ai_analysis.technical_context: -exec command {} \; is the most powerful find predicate. doex() builds nargv[] by walking from the saved arg position: copy each arg literally except substitute {} → Pathname. Stop at ; sentinel. Fork: parent waits for child exit code; child chdir to original Home (so command runs with sensible cwd) and execvp. The \; is shell-escaped — bare ; would be a shell statement separator. -ok is interactive variant: print prompt, getchar() y/n, run only if 'y'. Both use static char *nargv[50] (max 50 args) reused across calls. Modern GNU find adds + variant: `-exec rm {} +` batches multiple files into one command (xargs-like). 1979 design is one-fork-per-file, simple and unambiguous.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you delete every .tmp file in /tmp? `find /tmp -name '*.tmp' -exec rm {} \;`. The {} is substitution: each matching file gets pasted into the command. The \; is the end marker, escaped from shell. Internally: fork, child changes back to original directory, execvp runs the command, parent waits. Repeat per matching file. -ok is the safe sibling: ask 'y/n?' before each execution. The 1979 design lets any Unix command be plugged into the find pipeline — perfect composability. Modern GNU find -exec ... + batches calls for efficiency. The \; vs + distinction is part of every find tutorial today.

appraiser_note: **-exec command {} \;** が find の最強述語。 doex() が nargv[] を構築: 保存された arg 位置から各引数をコピー、 {} は Pathname で置換、 ; センチネルで停止。 fork: 親は子の exit code を wait、 子は元の Home に chdir してから execvp (コマンドが妥当な cwd で実行されるように)。 \; は shell から守るためエスケープ——裸の ; は shell の文区切り。 **-ok は対話版**: プロンプト出力、 getchar() で y/n、 'y' なら実行。 両方とも static char *nargv[50] (最大 50 引数) を呼び出し間で再利用。 現代 GNU find は **+** バリアント (`-exec rm {} +`) で複数ファイルを 1 コマンドにバッチ化 (xargs ライク)。 1979 設計は 1 ファイル 1 fork——単純で曖昧さなし。 `\;` vs `+` の区別は現代 find チュートリアルの定番。

ai_analysis.technical_context: find's AST nodes are struct anode with 3 fields: F (function pointer to predicate), L/R (left/right children). Node[100] static array, mk() allocates, Nn counter. Evaluation: call (*node->F)(node) — predicate functions are passed the node itself, can access L/R for binary ops, can cast for predicate-specific data layout. The cast is implicit in K&R C: mtime() declares its arg as `struct { int f, t, s; } *p` — reinterprets anode storage as predicate-specific layout. Modern C compilers warn about this; K&R C accepted it. The pattern is interpreter pattern (Gang of Four design pattern, published 1994) implemented in 1979. Same architecture: predicates = leaf nodes, and/or/not = internal nodes, evaluation = tree walk.
ai_analysis.human_story: find's predicates are first-class values: each is a function pointer. The AST is built from anode structs (function + L + R). Evaluating = calling the function with the node as argument; and/or/not call their children's functions, others do the actual work. Predicates reinterpret the anode storage to access their own arg layout — a trick that works because K&R C didn't type-check function pointers. The pattern (Interpreter Pattern in Gang of Four 1994 terminology) implemented in 1979's C. Same architecture as every modern tree-walking interpreter: leaf = primitive, internal = combinator. find is its early Unix example.

appraiser_note: find の AST ノードは struct anode、 3 フィールド: F (述語関数ポインタ)、 L/R (左右の子)。 Node[100] 静的配列、 mk() で割当、 Nn カウンタ。 評価: `(*node->F)(node)` ——述語関数にノード自身を渡し、 二項演算では L/R にアクセス、 述語固有データレイアウトで cast 可能。 K&R C ではキャストが暗黙: mtime() は引数を `struct { int f, t, s; } *p` と宣言——anode の storage を述語固有レイアウトとして再解釈。 現代 C コンパイラは warning するが K&R C は通す。 これは **Interpreter Pattern** (Gang of Four 1994 のデザインパターン) を 1979 年の C で実装。 同じアーキテクチャ: 述語 = leaf、 and/or/not = internal、 評価 = tree walk。 すべての現代 tree-walking interpreter (Lisp eval、 Python AST 評価、 SQL executor) の早期 Unix 例。

ai_analysis.technical_context: find exposes struct stat fields as predicates: mtime/atime/ctime (timestamps in days via /A_DAY=86400), user/group (uid/gid lookup via getunum from /etc/passwd or /etc/group), size (in 512-byte blocks via (size+511)>>9 = ceiling division), perm (mode & 07777, with '-' meaning at-least-these-bits), links (st_nlink), type (st_mode & S_IFMT). Shared scomp() handles +N (greater), -N (less), N (equal) for all numeric predicates. Modern GNU find adds size suffixes (c=bytes, k=KB, M=MB, G=GB), -size with /N for exact, more time predicates (mtime/atime/ctime + -min variants), -regex (vs -name's glob), -printf for formatted output. The 1979 set is the foundation: 11 predicates covering all key stat fields.
ai_analysis.human_story: Want files modified in the last week? `-mtime -7`. Owned by alice? `-user alice`. Larger than 100KB? `-size +200` (200 blocks = 100KB). Permissions 0755? `-perm 755`. find's 11 predicates expose struct stat as a query language. scomp() unifies '+N greater', '-N less', 'N equal' across all numeric predicates — write the comparison once. -perm with '-' prefix means 'at least these bits' (set bits required, others may be set too) — a common gotcha. 1979's set covers all the basics; modern GNU find layers convenience features on top, but the foundation is here.

appraiser_note: find は **struct stat フィールドを述語として公開**: mtime/atime/ctime (タイムスタンプ、 /A_DAY=86400 で日単位)、 user/group (uid/gid lookup via getunum で /etc/passwd や /etc/group 検索)、 size (512 バイトブロック、 (size+511)>>9 = ceiling 除算)、 perm (mode & 07777、 '-' は at-least-these-bits)、 links (st_nlink)、 type (st_mode & S_IFMT)。 **共通 scomp()** が +N (より大きい)、 -N (より小さい)、 N (等しい) を全数値述語で扱う——比較ロジックを 1 箇所で記述。 現代 GNU find はサイズサフィックス (c=バイト、 k=KB、 M=MB、 G=GB)、 -size に /N で完全一致、 より多くの時刻述語 (-min バリアント)、 -regex (vs -name の glob)、 -printf 出力フォーマットを追加。 1979 年のセットは基盤——11 述語で主要 stat フィールドを全部カバー。 SQL の WHERE owner='alice' AND modified_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days' と概念的に同じ DSL を 1979 年に。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cc isn't the C compiler — it's the driver. The actual compiler is split across 5 programs: cpp (/lib/cpp, preprocessor handling #include/#define/#if), ccom (/lib/ccom, C compiler proper doing lex/parse/codegen), c2 (/lib/c2, peephole optimizer, optional with -O), as (/bin/as, assembler), ld (/bin/ld, linker). cc invokes each via callsys() (fork+execv+wait), passing intermediate files between passes. Why split? Memory constraints (each pass fits in PDP-11's 64KB address space), language independence (Fortran uses same as/ld), debuggability (errors localized to one pass), modular evolution (replace ccom without touching others). This 'compiler as pipeline' is Unix's defining architectural decision for compilers — opposite of monolithic compilers in DEC VMS or IBM mainframes. gcc and clang maintain the same structure: cc1 + as + ld.
ai_analysis.human_story: Other OSes had compilers as one giant program. Unix said: split it. cpp does macros. ccom does C-to-assembly. c2 optimizes. as makes machine code. ld links. cc orchestrates. Each pass is small enough to fit PDP-11 memory. Each can be replaced independently — swap ccom for a new C compiler, the rest still works. Same as/ld serves Fortran, Pascal, Ada. This 'compiler as pipeline' is the architectural difference between Unix and the rest of computing in 1979. Modern gcc has cc1+as+ld, clang has clang+as+lld — same pipeline, 50 years later.

appraiser_note: cc は **C コンパイラ本体ではなく、 ドライバ**。 実際のコンパイラは 5 プログラムに分割: cpp (/lib/cpp、 #include/#define/#if のプリプロセッサ)、 ccom (/lib/ccom、 C コンパイラ本体、 lex/parse/codegen)、 c2 (/lib/c2、 peephole optimizer、 -O ありで起動)、 as (/bin/as、 アセンブラ)、 ld (/bin/ld、 リンカ)。 cc は callsys() (fork+execv+wait) で各パスを起動、 中間ファイルでつなぐ。 分割理由: メモリ制約 (各パス PDP-11 64KB に収まる)、 言語独立性 (Fortran も同じ as/ld 使用)、 デバッグ性 (エラーが 1 パスに局所化)、 モジュラー進化 (ccom 入れ替えても他に影響なし)。 「コンパイラ = パイプライン」 は Unix の決定的アーキテクチャ判断——DEC VMS や IBM メインフレームのモノリシックコンパイラと対照的。 gcc も clang も同じ構造を維持: cc1 + as + ld。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cc manages 6 temporary files /tmp/ctm0a..5a (intermediate outputs between passes). tmp4=cpp output (.i), tmp3=ccom output (.s), tmp5=c2 buffer, tmp0=lock-like. Collision avoidance: try fopen, if file exists increment 10th char ('a'→'b'→...) and retry. Signal handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM) invoke dexit() which cunlink()s all tmp files — Ctrl-C cleanup. Modern mkstemp(3) avoids the TOCTOU race (file checked then created — another process could grab the name between); 1979's check-then-create was acceptable when /tmp had few processes. The tmp filename's 10th-char incrementing trick is K&R-style hack: one base name, modify one char to generate the family. Static names like /tmp/ctm0a are deliberate — make's parallel builds were uncommon in 1979.
ai_analysis.human_story: cc creates 6 temp files in /tmp during a build. They hold pass outputs (cpp→.i, ccom→.s, etc.). Two concurrent cc invocations could collide — solved by incrementing the 10th character: ctm0a, ctm0b, ctm0c... Then SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers ensure cleanup on Ctrl-C. The TOCTOU race (check-then-create) was tolerated because /tmp had little concurrency in 1979. Modern mkstemp(3) closes this gap. The trick of mutating one char in the basename to derive 6 related names is K&R economy: one allocation, 6 derived strings via byte writes. Wasteful by today's standards, clever by 1979's.

appraiser_note: cc は **6 つのテンポラリ**を /tmp/ctm0a..5a で管理 (各パスの中間出力)。 tmp4=cpp 出力 (.i)、 tmp3=ccom 出力 (.s)、 tmp5=c2 バッファ、 tmp0=ロック的役割。 衝突回避: fopen で確認、 存在すれば 10 文字目をインクリメント ('a'→'b'→...) してリトライ。 シグナルハンドラ (SIGINT/SIGTERM) で dexit() を呼んで全 tmp を cunlink()——Ctrl-C クリーンアップ。 現代の mkstemp(3) は TOCTOU race (確認後の create 間に他プロセスが取る可能性) を防ぐが、 1979 年は /tmp の並行が少なく check-then-create で十分。 10 文字目の文字変更トリックは K&R 流ハック: 1 つの基底名、 1 文字書き換えで関連 6 名を派生。 現代の標準では無駄、 1979 年では巧妙。

ai_analysis.technical_context: callsys() is fork + execv + wait in 14 lines. Child: execv() replaces process image with the named program; if execv returns it failed (file not found, etc.) — print error and exit(100). Parent: wait(&status) loops until our child returns (wait can return for any child; check t to confirm). Return code extraction: status's lower 7 bits are signal number (non-zero means killed by signal → failure), upper 8 bits are exit code. `(status>>8)&0377` extracts exit code from typical Unix wait status format. This is the canonical fork-exec-wait pattern that every Unix programming book teaches; subprocess libraries (Python's subprocess, Go's exec.Command, Rust's std::process) all wrap this. The 'Try again' on fork failure is from times when NPROC limit was easily hit on small Unix systems.
ai_analysis.human_story: Want to run a program from your program? fork, exec, wait. 14 lines. Every Unix programming tutorial teaches this. cc's callsys is the canonical example: fork creates a child, child execs the target program (failure means program not found), parent waits for completion and returns the exit code. The exit code extraction `(status>>8)&0377` is folklore — wait()'s status word packs exit code in upper byte, signal info in lower byte. Modern subprocess libraries (Python's subprocess.run, Go's exec.Command, Rust's Command) hide this away, but underneath they're all calling fork+exec+wait — the same 14-line pattern from 1979's cc.c.

appraiser_note: callsys() は **fork + execv + wait の 14 行**。 子: execv() がプロセスイメージを指定プログラムで置換、 execv が return したら失敗 (ファイル無し等)——エラー印字して exit(100)。 親: wait(&status) で自分の子の終了を待つ (wait は任意の子で return するので t で確認)。 戻り値抽出: status の下位 7 bit はシグナル番号 (非ゼロならシグナル kill = 失敗)、 上位 8 bit は exit code。 `(status>>8)&0377` で典型的な Unix wait 形式から exit code 抽出。 これが **canonical fork-exec-wait パターン**——Unix プログラミング教科書の定番、 subprocess ライブラリ (Python subprocess、 Go exec.Command、 Rust std::process) すべてのラッパー。 「Try again」 は NPROC 上限が小さい Unix システムで fork 失敗が頻発した時代の名残。

ai_analysis.technical_context: getsuf() extracts file extension (last char after .): walks string counting chars since last /, then checks if 3rd-to-last char is '.', returns the suffix char. setsuf() replaces extension by mutating the last char in-place after copying. Constraints: c<=14 (V7 max filename length), c>2 (need room for X.Y). The 1-char-extension assumption is unique to V7/Bell-32V — .c, .s, .o, .a, .i are the universe. Multi-char extensions like .cpp, .cxx, .so, .dylib came later. Bell-32V cc established the canonical Unix extension vocabulary: .c (C source), .s (assembly), .i (preprocessed), .o (object), .a (archive). Modern compilers add many more (.cpp, .cc, .cxx, .C, .hh, .hpp, .so, .dylib, .dll) but the core 1-char convention from 1979 still dominates Unix source trees.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does cc know to compile hello.c into hello.o? Two helpers: getsuf extracts the single-letter extension (.c → 'c'), setsuf replaces it (.c → .o by mutating last byte). The 1-character extension assumption is V7-specific: .c, .s, .o, .a, .i — the universe of Unix source file types. Multi-char extensions (.cpp for C++, .so for shared object) appeared in the 1980s-90s. Bell-32V's cc established the canonical vocabulary: .c, .s, .o, .a. Half a century later, your gcc still recognizes these as the primary file types. The 14-char limit comes from V7 filesystem; modern systems lifted it, but Unix source trees still use short names by convention.

appraiser_note: getsuf() はファイル拡張子 (. の後の最後の文字) を抽出: 文字列を走査して / 以降の文字数をカウント、 末尾から 3 文字目が '.' ならその後の文字を返す。 setsuf() は拡張子を置換: コピー後、 最後の文字を in-place 書き換え。 制約: c<=14 (V7 最大ファイル名長)、 c>2 (X.Y の最小)。 **1 文字拡張子の前提**は V7/Bell-32V 固有——.c、 .s、 .o、 .a、 .i が宇宙の全て。 .cpp、 .cxx、 .so、 .dylib のような複数文字拡張子は後の時代。 Bell-32V cc が **Unix 拡張子規約**を確立: .c (C ソース)、 .s (アセンブリ)、 .i (前処理済み)、 .o (オブジェクト)、 .a (アーカイブ)。 現代コンパイラは多数追加 (.cpp、 .cc、 .cxx、 .C、 .hh、 .hpp、 .so、 .dylib、 .dll) するが、 1979 年の 1 文字規約が今も Unix ソースツリーを支配。

ai_analysis.technical_context: /lib/crt0.o is C runtime startup — an assembly object that defines _start, the actual entry point of every C executable. Kernel jumps to _start after execve; _start unpacks argv/envp from the stack, calls main(argc, argv), then calls exit(retval) with main's return value. Without crt0.o, there's no glue between the OS process-launch ABI and the C function main(). cc selects crt0 variant based on flags: standard /lib/crt0.o, /lib/mcrt0.o for -p profiling (calls monitor() to set up profiling), /lib/fcrt0.o for Fortran, /lib/crt2.o for alternate memory model. crt0 is the OS-language boundary — it's where 'a process' becomes 'a C program'. Every Unix C compiler still does this: glibc's crt1.o + crti.o + crtbegin.o + crtend.o + crtn.o (broken into more pieces) but the role is identical to 1979's crt0.o.
ai_analysis.human_story: Kernel calls execve() — what runs? Not main(). The kernel jumps to _start, an assembly routine in /lib/crt0.o. _start unpacks argv from the stack, calls main(argc, argv), then calls exit() with main's return value. Without crt0.o, your C program has no way to run. It's the bridge between 'OS launches process' and 'main() function executes'. cc selects different crt0 variants for profiling (mcrt0.o calls monitor()), Fortran (fcrt0.o), alternate memory models (crt2.o). Modern Linux splits crt0 into multiple files (crt1.o, crti.o, crtbegin.o) but the role is unchanged: glue between OS ABI and your main(). 1979's design lives on.

appraiser_note: /lib/crt0.o は **C ランタイム startup**——アセンブリオブジェクトで `_start` シンボルを定義、 全 C 実行可能ファイルの実際のエントリポイント。 カーネルが execve 後に `_start` へ jump、 `_start` がスタックから argv/envp を取り出し、 main(argc, argv) を call、 main の return 値で exit(retval) を call。 crt0.o なしでは OS のプロセス起動 ABI と C 関数 main() を繋ぐ glue が存在しない。 cc はフラグに応じて crt0 バリアントを選択: 標準 /lib/crt0.o、 /lib/mcrt0.o (-p profiling、 monitor() を呼ぶ)、 /lib/fcrt0.o (Fortran)、 /lib/crt2.o (代替メモリモデル)。 crt0 は **OS と言語の境界線**——「プロセス」 が「C プログラム」 になる場所。 現代の glibc は crt1.o + crti.o + crtbegin.o + crtend.o + crtn.o に分割されるが、 役割は 1979 年と同一: OS ABI と main() の glue。 「クルマでいうアイドリング→走行」 の遷移コード。

ai_analysis.technical_context: ld uses 2-pass algorithm. Pass 1 (load1, load1arg): walk all inputs (.o files and -l libraries), build symbol table — what's defined where, what's undefined. Pass 2 (load2, endload): with symbols all known, walk files again copying text/data segments to output and applying relocation (rewriting references with final addresses). The 2-pass design is forced by the chicken-and-egg problem: address of symbol X depends on all earlier code's sizes, but X's references in later files need X's final address. Pass 1 collects sizes/defs, Pass 2 emits with full knowledge. middle() between passes computes final segment bases. brk(nextsym) finalizes the symbol table by extending the data segment. This 2-pass pattern is canonical: GNU ld, LLVM lld, Microsoft link.exe all use it (with internal optimizations like incremental linking on top).
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does linking take two passes? Because addresses are circular: file A references printf, file B (libc) provides printf, but printf's address depends on file A's text size which determines where text+data ends. Solution: walk files once to count sizes and collect symbols (Pass 1), compute final addresses (middle), then walk again to copy and fix up references (Pass 2). 1979's ld established this 2-pass design. GNU ld, LLVM lld, MS link.exe still use it. Modern incremental linking optimizes the common case (re-link with small changes) but the fundamental algorithm is 2-pass.

appraiser_note: ld は **2 パスアルゴリズム**。 **Pass 1** (load1、 load1arg): 全入力 (.o ファイルと -l ライブラリ) を走査、 シンボルテーブル構築——何がどこで定義され、 何が未定義か。 **Pass 2** (load2、 endload): シンボルが既知になった後、 ファイルを再走査して text/data セグメントを出力にコピー、 リロケーション適用 (参照を最終アドレスで書き換え)。 2 パス設計は鶏卵問題で強制: シンボル X のアドレスは先行コードのサイズに依存、 だが後続ファイルでの X 参照は X の最終アドレスを必要とする。 Pass 1 でサイズ/定義を集め、 Pass 2 で完全知識のもとに出力。 middle() でセグメント基底アドレス計算、 brk(nextsym) でデータセグメント拡張によりシンボルテーブル finalize。 **2-pass パターンは canonical**——GNU ld、 LLVM lld、 Microsoft link.exe 全部使用 (incremental linking 等の最適化を内部で追加)。

text:
#define OMAGIC  0407     /* old magic: data immediately after text */
#define NMAGIC  0410     /* new magic: data at page boundary above text */
#define PAGRND  0777     /* page boundary (512 bytes - 1) */

/* a.out file layout:
 *
 *   header (8 words = 32 bytes):
 *     magic, text size, data size, bss size,
 *     symbol table size, entry point,
 *     text reloc size, data reloc size
 *
 *   segment        origin
 *   header:        0
 *   text:          32                                  (4-byte aligned)
 *   data:          32 + textsize                       (4-byte aligned, or page-aligned for NMAGIC)
 *   text reloc:    32 + textsize + datasize
 *   data reloc:    32 + textsize + datasize + textrelsize
 *   symbol table:  ... + drsize
 */

ai_analysis.technical_context: a.out (assembler output) is V7/Bell-32V executable format. 32-byte header followed by text, data, relocation info, symbol table. Two main magic numbers: OMAGIC=0407 (text+data contiguous, simple PDP-11 style), NMAGIC=0410 (data at next 512-byte page boundary, enables shared-text execution where multiple instances share the same physical text pages). PAGRND=0777 is the page rounding mask (512-byte VAX page minus 1). Later ZMAGIC=0411 added for demand paging. The format is brutally simple compared to modern ELF: no sections (just 3 segments), no dynamic linking metadata, no debug sections separate from symbols. Entry point in header — kernel jumps here after execve, typically _start in crt0. Layout makes mmap easy: text starts at offset 32, data starts at offset 32+textsize (or page-aligned), bss is allocated by kernel at runtime (no disk space).
ai_analysis.human_story: a.out is the executable format that made Unix recognizable: read first 4 bytes, see 0407 or 0410 or 0411, you know it's an executable. 32-byte header tells you sizes; text starts at byte 32, data after, then relocation info, then symbol table. Simple. ELF (1995) added sections, dynamic linking, debug info — but the 'header + segments' shape is from a.out. Modern Linux has both: legacy a.out support lingered until 2017, modern executables are all ELF. The 0407/0410/0411 magic numbers are folklore — every Unix programmer of a certain age knows them.

appraiser_note: a.out (assembler output) は V7/Bell-32V 実行ファイル形式。 32 バイトヘッダ + text + data + リロケーション情報 + シンボルテーブル。 2 つのメインマジック: **OMAGIC=0407** (text+data 連続、 シンプルな PDP-11 流)、 **NMAGIC=0410** (data が次の 512 バイト page 境界、 shared-text 実行を可能に——複数インスタンスが同じ物理 text page を共有)。 PAGRND=0777 は page 切り上げマスク (512 バイト VAX page - 1)。 後に ZMAGIC=0411 が demand paging 用に追加。 形式は ELF と比べて極めて単純: セクション無し (3 セグメントのみ)、 動的リンクメタデータ無し、 デバッグセクションがシンボルと別れていない。 ヘッダにエントリポイント——カーネルが execve 後ここに jump、 通常 crt0 の _start。 layout で mmap が簡単: text は offset 32、 data は 32+textsize (or page-aligned)、 bss はランタイムにカーネルが確保 (ディスク不使用)。

text:
/* Symbol types: */
#define UNDEF   0x0       /* undefined (extern reference) */
#define ABS     0x2       /* absolute (compile-time constant) */
#define TEXT    0x4       /* in text segment */
#define DATA    0x6       /* in data segment */
#define BSS     0x8       /* in bss segment */
#define DATAO   0xA       /* data offset */
#define BSSO    0xC       /* bss offset */
#define TEXTO   0xE       /* text offset */
#define ABSO    0x10      /* absolute offset */
