#define COMM    0x12      /* common (Fortran-style) */

#define EXTERN  0x1       /* external visibility bit */
#define TYPE    0x1E      /* type mask (lower 5 bits) */
#define STABTYPS 0xE0     /* stab debug types */

/* Hash table for O(1) lookup: */
SYMBOL *hshtab[HSHSIZ];

ai_analysis.technical_context: ld's symbol types: UNDEF (extern reference, will resolve later), ABS (compile-time constant), TEXT/DATA/BSS (in respective segment), COMM (Fortran-style common), TEXTO/DATAO/BSSO/ABSO (offset variants for relocation). EXTERN bit (0x1) indicates external visibility — set for non-static symbols. TYPE mask (0x1E) extracts the type. STABTYPS (0xE0) for stab debug info — used by adb debugger. Hash table hshtab[HSHSIZ] enables O(1) symbol lookup by name (8-char limit due to V7). The 9 type values express where the symbol lives (segment) and how it's resolved (direct, offset, common). Modern ELF has more nuanced types (FUNC, OBJECT, SECTION, FILE, etc.) but the basic concept of 'type + segment + bind' is from a.out.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does ld know where to put printf? Symbol table. Each symbol has a name (8 chars), a type (UNDEF/TEXT/DATA/BSS/...), and a value (address or offset). UNDEF means 'someone needs this but no one defined it yet' — Pass 1 hopes Pass 2 will resolve it. EXTERN means 'visible to other files' (non-static). Hash table for fast lookup. The 9 types are the universe of 1979 linker semantics. Modern ELF adds more (visibility, binding, function vs object) but the same core idea: type + value + name. The 8-char name limit came from V7 — modern linkers support arbitrary length.

appraiser_note: ld のシンボル型: **UNDEF** (extern 参照、 後で解決)、 **ABS** (コンパイル時定数)、 **TEXT/DATA/BSS** (各セグメント内)、 **COMM** (Fortran-style 共通)、 TEXTO/DATAO/BSSO/ABSO (リロケーション用 offset variants)。 **EXTERN ビット** (0x1) で外部可視性 (non-static で立つ)。 **TYPE マスク** (0x1E) で型抽出。 STABTYPS (0xE0) は adb デバッガが使う stab デバッグ情報。 **ハッシュテーブル hshtab[HSHSIZ]** で名前 O(1) lookup (V7 制約で 8 文字)。 9 型値が「シンボルがどこに居る (セグメント) + どう解決される (直接/オフセット/共通)」 を表現。 現代 ELF は更にニュアンス追加 (FUNC、 OBJECT、 SECTION、 FILE 等) するが、 「型 + セグメント + バインディング」 の基本概念は a.out 由来。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Smart library scan: -l libc.a doesn't link the entire archive — only members that define currently-undefined symbols. load1arg walks archive (.a) members one by one, calls load1(libflg=1, ...) for each. load1 returns ndef = number of newly-resolved undefined symbols. If ndef==0, this member is skipped (libp records loc=-1 conceptually). If ndef>0, this member is 'pulled' — its loc is saved in libp for Pass 2. Order-dependent: -l processes left-to-right per command-line position. cc foo.o bar.o -lm -lc works if foo/bar reference libm functions which reference libc — but cc foo.o bar.o -lc -lm fails if libm symbols are still undefined when libc is scanned. GNU ld --start-group/--end-group repeats scanning until stable. This 1979 design is in every static linker since.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does cc command order matter? Because of how ld scans -l libraries. cc foo.o -lc means: link foo.o, then scan libc, pulling only objects that define foo's undefined symbols. If you wrote cc -lc foo.o, libc has nothing to resolve yet (foo.o not seen), so nothing pulled — link fails with 'undefined reference'. The smart-library-scan is 1979's clever design: don't bloat the executable with the entire libc, just pull what's needed. The order-dependency is the price. GNU ld's --start-group repeats scanning to handle circular deps, but the basic algorithm is unchanged.

appraiser_note: **Smart library scan**: -l libc.a は archive 全体をリンクせず、 **現在未解決のシンボルを定義するメンバー**だけリンク。 load1arg が archive (.a) メンバーを 1 つずつ walk、 各メンバーで load1(libflg=1, ...) を呼ぶ。 load1 は ndef = 新たに解決した未定義シンボル数を返す。 ndef==0 ならスキップ (libp に loc=-1 概念的記録)、 ndef>0 ならこのメンバーは「pulled」——loc を libp に保存し Pass 2 で処理。 **順序依存**: -l はコマンドライン左から右へ処理。 `cc foo.o bar.o -lm -lc` は foo/bar が libm 関数を参照し libm が libc を参照する場合のみ動く。 `cc -lm -lc foo.o bar.o` だと libc 走査時に未定義無し → 何も pull されない → 'undefined reference' エラー。 GNU ld の --start-group/--end-group は安定するまで繰り返し走査。 1979 年の設計が以降すべての静的リンカに継承——「ライブラリ全体ではなく必要分だけ」 の発明。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Relocation entries from .o files describe 'fix up this address': location, target symbol or segment, reference type (PCREL, LEN1/2/4, RTEXT/RDATA/RBSS, REXT, ROFF). PCREL means PC-relative addressing — common for branches in VAX. LEN encodes width (1/2/4 bytes). REXT means resolve via symbol table; without it, resolve via segment base + offset. ld walks relocation entries during Pass 2, computes final value (symbol address or segment-base + offset), adjusts for PCREL (subtract location + size), writes the value at the relocation site. This is the most algorithmically subtle part of linking: each instruction encoding has different relocation requirements. VAX instructions use mostly PCREL for branches. Modern x86-64 has dozens of relocation types (R_X86_64_PC32, R_X86_64_GOT32, etc.). The principle stays: each relocation says 'patch this address with that computed value'.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does the compiler write `call printf` when printf's address is unknown? It writes `call 0` and adds a relocation entry: 'at offset N in text, patch a 4-byte address with printf's final location'. ld processes these entries after all symbol addresses are known. PCREL: PC-relative branches need value = target - (instruction + size) for the offset. LEN: how many bytes to overwrite. REXT: lookup symbol vs segment-base + offset. Every modern linker does this; ELF has R_X86_64_PC32 etc. (dozens of types). a.out's PCREL/LEN1/2/4/REXT are the minimal set that started it all in 1979.

appraiser_note: リロケーション情報は .o ファイルから「**このアドレスを修正せよ**」 と指示: 位置、 対象シンボル or セグメント、 参照型 (PCREL、 LEN1/2/4、 RTEXT/RDATA/RBSS、 REXT、 ROFF)。 **PCREL** は PC-相対アドレッシング——VAX の branch で頻出。 **LEN** で幅エンコード (1/2/4 バイト)。 **REXT** ならシンボルテーブル経由解決、 無ければセグメント基底 + offset。 ld は Pass 2 でリロケーションエントリを walk、 最終値計算 (シンボルアドレスまたはセグメント基底 + offset)、 PCREL なら補正 (location + size を引く)、 リロケーション位置に値を書く。 リンクの最もアルゴリズム的に微妙な部分——各命令エンコーディングが異なるリロケーション要件を持つ。 VAX 命令は branch で主に PCREL 使用。 現代 x86-64 は数十のリロケーション型 (R_X86_64_PC32、 R_X86_64_GOT32 等)。 原理は不変: 各リロケーションが「この位置にあの計算値を patch」 と指示。

ai_analysis.technical_context: doname() is make's heart: recursive depth-first traversal of the dependency graph. Each target has a done flag with 4 states (0=untouched, 1=in-progress, 2=success, 3=failed). State 1 detects cycles (revisiting in-progress = circular dep). For each target: check if already done (memoization), recursively make all dependencies (DFS), compare mtimes, run commands if stale. The recursion + memoization pattern is the algorithm for incremental builds. Modern build systems (GNU make, bazel, cargo) all use the same: DFS traversal + memoization + staleness check. Differences are in staleness criteria (mtime vs hash) and execution model (sequential vs parallel) but the core algorithm is unchanged. Recursion depth = build graph depth, typically 5-50 levels.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does make know what to rebuild after you edit one file? Recursive DFS through the dependency graph. Visit each target, check its done state (avoid redoing work). Recurse to all deps. Compare mtimes. If anything's stale, run the command. Mark done. 1979's algorithm is the foundation of all incremental build systems. Bazel adds content hashing, cargo adds dependency resolution, npm adds package management — but the core DFS + memoization is from this code. Stuart Feldman wrote this in a week as a 22-year-old; it's been the bedrock of software builds for 50 years.

appraiser_note: doname() は make の心臓: 依存グラフの **再帰下降 DFS**。 各 target に **done フラグ** (4 状態: 0=未処理、 1=処理中、 2=成功、 3=失敗)。 状態 1 で循環依存検出 (処理中の再訪 = 循環)。 各 target で: 処理済みなら return (memoization)、 全依存を再帰 make (DFS)、 mtime 比較、 古ければコマンド実行。 **再帰 + memoization パターンが増分ビルドのアルゴリズム**。 現代ビルドシステム (GNU make、 bazel、 cargo) すべてが同じ: DFS 走査 + memoization + staleness チェック。 違いは判定基準 (mtime vs ハッシュ) と実行モデル (順次 vs 並列) だけ。 再帰深さ = ビルドグラフ深さ、 通常 5-50 階層。 22 歳の Stuart Feldman が 1 週間で書いたこのアルゴリズムが、 50 年のソフトウェアビルドの基礎。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Incremental build via mtime comparison: target's mtime (ptime) vs dependency's mtime (td1). If dep newer than target → rebuild. exists() returns st_mtime or 0 (file absent). Special case: ptime==0 && tdep==0 → rebuild (no target, no deps either, must be a phony task). qchain accumulates 'changed dependencies' which becomes $? automatic variable for the command. Modern build systems found this unreliable (filesystem touch can fool it, clock skew breaks it) and moved to content hashing (bazel, buck, sccache). But mtime-based remains the default for make, GNU make, BSD make — 50 years and counting. 1 line condition `if (ptime < td)` is the single insight that powers incremental compilation.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does `make` rebuild only changed files? One line: `if (ptime < td)`. If the target is older than any dependency, rebuild. If equal or newer, skip. That's incremental builds in 1 line. The condition assumes filesystem mtimes are reliable — they usually are, but `touch -d` or clock skew can break it. Modern systems (bazel, buck, sccache) use content hashing for reliability. But the mtime approach from 1979 still works for 99% of cases, which is why GNU make, BSD make, and POSIX make all keep it. Stuart Feldman's 1976 insight: 'compare modification times, rebuild what's stale' — still fundamental.

appraiser_note: **mtime 比較で増分ビルド**: target の mtime (ptime) vs 依存の mtime (td1)。 dep が target より新しければ rebuild。 exists() は st_mtime または 0 (ファイル不在) を返す。 特殊ケース: ptime==0 && tdep==0 → rebuild (target も deps も存在しない = phony task)。 qchain が 「変更された依存」 を蓄積し、 コマンドの $? 自動変数になる。 現代ビルドシステムはこれを信頼できないと判断 (touch でだまされる、 時刻ズレで壊れる) して **内容ハッシュ**へ移行 (bazel、 buck、 sccache)。 だが mtime ベースは make、 GNU make、 BSD make のデフォルトとして 50 年継続。 **1 行の条件 `if (ptime < td)` が増分コンパイルを動かす単一の洞察**。 Stuart Feldman の 1976 年の発想: 「修正時刻を比較、 古いものを rebuild」——今も基本。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Suffix rules: .c.o: defines how to convert .c → .o. When making hello.o with no explicit rule, make searches .SUFFIXES list for pairs: try .c.o (.c source for .o target), .s.o (.s source for .o target), etc. For each pair, check if the source file exists (e.g., hello.c). If yes, apply that rule. Sets $* (stem, 'hello'), $< (source, 'hello.c') as automatic variables. The double-loop walks all sufflist × sufflist combinations — quadratic in suffix count, but suffix count is small (~10). GNU make extended this to pattern rules: %.o: %.c uses the % wildcard for any name. Same concept, more flexible syntax. The 1979 suffix rule is the foundation of every modern build language: cargo's [build-dependencies], npm's scripts, webpack's loaders — all express 'how to convert X type to Y type'.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why is a typical Makefile only 3 lines? Because of suffix rules. Write hello: hello.o util.o, and make figures out that hello.o needs hello.c via the .c.o: rule. You don't need to spell out 'compile hello.c to hello.o' — make knows by suffix matching. GNU make extended this to pattern rules (%.o: %.c) — same idea, wildcards. cargo, npm, webpack, all modern build systems express 'how to convert one file type to another' — descendants of Bell-32V make's suffix rules from 1976. The 3-line Makefile is the achievement: most of what you'd write is implied by convention.

appraiser_note: **サフィックス規則**: `.c.o:` が `.c → .o` 変換を定義。 hello.o を明示ルール無しで make するとき、 make は .SUFFIXES リストから pair を探索: .c.o (.c ソースから .o target)、 .s.o などを試す。 各 pair でソースファイル (例: hello.c) が存在するかチェック、 あればその規則を適用。 $* (stem、 'hello')、 $< (source、 'hello.c') を自動変数として設定。 二重ループで sufflist × sufflist の全組み合わせを walk——拡張子数の 2 乗だが拡張子は ~10 個程度。 GNU make は **パターン規則**に拡張: `%.o: %.c` で % wildcard で任意名対応。 同じ概念、 より柔軟な構文。 1979 年のサフィックス規則が現代ビルド言語の基礎——cargo の [build-dependencies]、 npm の scripts、 webpack の loader、 すべて「X 型を Y 型に変換」 を表現する子孫。 3 行 Makefile の偉業: ほとんどが規約で暗黙。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Automatic variables provide context-dependent values: $@ (current target name), $? (out-of-date dependency list, space-separated), $< (source file in implicit rules), $* (stem without extension). Implementation: setvar() updates global variable table per target; subst() expands $@, $<, etc. in command strings before shell execution. This enables DRY commands: `cc -o $@ hello.o util.o` instead of repeating target name. $? enables efficient archive updates: `ar r $@ $?` only adds changed files. GNU make adds $^ (all deps), $+ (all deps with duplicates), $|, $(@D), $(@F). But the original 4 ($@, $?, $<, $*) from 1976 cover most use cases and survive in every make implementation.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why write `cc -o $@ ...` instead of `cc -o hello ...`? Because $@ becomes the current target's name. DRY: target name appears once (in the rule definition), not twice. $< is the source file in implicit rules: `cc -c $<` works for any .c.o rule. $? is the killer feature: list of changed dependencies. `ar r libfoo.a $?` only adds files that were modified — incremental archive updates. The 4 variables ($@, $?, $<, $*) cover 90% of Makefile needs. GNU make adds more but the originals from 1979 are the workhorses.

appraiser_note: **自動変数**でコンテキスト依存値を提供: $@ (現在の target 名)、 $? (古い依存リスト、 スペース区切り)、 $< (暗黙ルールのソースファイル)、 $* (stem、 拡張子なし)。 実装: setvar() が target ごとにグローバル変数表を更新、 subst() がコマンド文字列内の $@、 $< 等を展開してから shell で実行。 DRY コマンドを可能に: `cc -o $@ hello.o util.o` で target 名を 2 度書かない。 $? で効率的 archive 更新: `ar r $@ $?` で変更ファイルだけ追加。 GNU make は追加: $^ (全 deps)、 $+ (重複含む全 deps)、 $|、 $(@D)、 $(@F)。 だが 1976 年の基本 4 つ ($@、 $?、 $<、 $*) が最も多用される、 全 make 実装で継承。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Special targets (names starting with '.') are make directives, not file targets: .IGNORE (ignore all command errors, same as -i flag), .SILENT (don't echo commands, same as -s), .SUFFIXES (suffix list for implicit rule search), .DEFAULT (fallback rule for unknown targets). main.c checks for these via srchname() and sets corresponding flags. .SUFFIXES is order-sensitive: first-listed suffix wins. Empty .SUFFIXES clears all (disable implicit rules). GNU make adds .PHONY (target is not a file, always run), .PRECIOUS (don't delete intermediates), .SECONDARY, etc. Command-level prefixes (@, -) work like .SILENT, .IGNORE per-command. 1979's special targets pioneered the 'metadata target' idea — encoding build metadata as fake targets that the build tool recognizes specially.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you tell make 'ignore all errors'? Add `.IGNORE:` to Makefile. 'Be silent'? `.SILENT:`. Define what suffixes are special? `.SUFFIXES: .c .o`. These special targets — names starting with '.' — encode build metadata as if they were file targets. Make recognizes them specially. GNU make extended this: `.PHONY: all clean install` marks targets that aren't files. Same idea, scaled up. The trick of 'encode metadata as a special-named target' is the 1979 design that lets makefile language be self-describing — no separate metadata syntax needed.

appraiser_note: **特殊ターゲット**は (`.` で始まる名前) は make ディレクティブで、 ファイル target ではない: .IGNORE (全コマンドエラー無視、 -i フラグ相当)、 .SILENT (コマンドエコーなし、 -s 相当)、 .SUFFIXES (暗黙ルール探索の拡張子リスト)、 .DEFAULT (未知 target のフォールバック規則)。 main.c が srchname() で存在チェックして対応フラグを設定。 .SUFFIXES は順序依存: 先に書いた拡張子が優先。 空 .SUFFIXES で全クリア (暗黙ルール無効化)。 GNU make は追加: .PHONY (ファイルではない target、 常に rebuild)、 .PRECIOUS (中間ファイル削除しない)、 .SECONDARY 等。 コマンドレベル prefix (@、 -) は .SILENT、 .IGNORE のコマンド単位版。 1979 年の特殊ターゲットが **「メタデータターゲット」 アイデアの先駆け**——別のメタデータ構文不要で makefile 言語を自己記述的に。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Stephen Bourne wrote /bin/sh entirely in C, but used C preprocessor macros to make it LOOK like ALGOL 68 (his previous language). IF/THEN/FI WHILE/DO/OD LOOP/POOL SWITCH/IN/ENDSW BEGIN/END replace C's if/while/for/switch/{}. Reverse-spelled terminators (IF→FI, DO→OD) are ALGOL 68 idiom. ANDF/ORF for &&/||, REG for register. The entire 60KB shell source uses this style. Notorious in the C community as 'Bourne ALGOL C' — readable to Bourne, opaque to C programmers. Successor shells (bash, dash) rewrote in normal C, but Bourne's macros remained in shipped /bin/sh until many vendor Unices retired it (2000s). The user-facing shell syntax (case...esac) still has the FI/OD echo: case ends in esac, the reverse spelling of case.
ai_analysis.human_story: Stephen Bourne loved ALGOL 68. He wrote /bin/sh in C but couldn't help himself — he wrote macros to make C look like ALGOL 68. IF...FI, WHILE...DO...OD, the reverse-spelled terminators. Reading sh's source feels like reading a different language. Other Bell Labs programmers hated it. Doug Comer called them 'his notorious macros'. But Bourne shipped /bin/sh this way for 20+ years. The user-facing case...esac syntax preserves the spirit (esac = case reversed). Most modern shells (bash, dash, mksh) rewrote in normal C — but they kept the user syntax. 1977's source style is gone; 1977's language design persists.

appraiser_note: **Stephen Bourne の ALGOL 68 マクロ**: /bin/sh は完全に C で書かれているが、 プリプロセッサマクロで **ALGOL 68 風**に見せる。 IF/THEN/FI、 WHILE/DO/OD、 LOOP/POOL、 SWITCH/IN/ENDSW、 BEGIN/END が C の if/while/for/switch/{} を置換。 **逆綴り終端子** (IF→FI、 DO→OD) は ALGOL 68 イディオム。 ANDF/ORF が &&/||、 REG が register。 60KB の shell ソース全体がこのスタイル。 C コミュニティで悪名高い 「Bourne ALGOL C」 ——Bourne には読みやすい、 C プログラマには不可解。 後継 shell (bash、 dash) は普通の C で書き直したが、 vendor Unix の /bin/sh は 2000 年代まで Bourne 流のまま。 ユーザー向け shell 構文 (case...esac) には FI/OD の精神が残る——esac は case の逆綴り。

text:
execute(argt, execflg, pf1, pf2) {
    SWITCH type IN
    case TCOM:      /* simple command: cmd arg arg */
    case TFORK:     /* fork off subprocess */
    case TPAR:      /* (subshell) */
    case TFIL:      /* pipe: cmd1 | cmd2 */
    case TLST:      /* sequence: cmd1 ; cmd2 */
    case TAND:      /* short-circuit AND: cmd1 && cmd2 */
    case TORF:      /* short-circuit OR: cmd1 || cmd2 */
    case TFOR:      /* for v in list; do ...; done */
    case TWH:       /* while cmd; do ...; done */
    case TUN:       /* until cmd; do ...; done */
    case TIF:       /* if cmd; then ...; else ...; fi */
    case TSW:       /* case x in pat) ...; ;; esac */
    ENDSW
}

ai_analysis.technical_context: execute() is the shell's AST interpreter. Dispatches on 12 node types: TCOM (simple command), TFORK (subprocess), TPAR (subshell in parens), TFIL (pipe), TLST (sequence ;), TAND (&&), TORF (||), TFOR (for-in), TWH (while), TUN (until), TIF (if-else), TSW (case-esac). Each case recursively walks the tree: TFIL spawns two executes with pipe between them; TAND only runs right if left exit code is 0; TFOR loops assigning each list element to variable. The recursive descent on AST is the standard interpreter pattern. The 12-node taxonomy covers all shell control flow. Modern shells (bash, zsh) add functions, case-modifiers, arithmetic expansion, but the core AST node types unchanged since 1977.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does shell execute `cmd1 && cmd2 || cmd3 | cmd4`? Parser builds an AST: TORF(TAND(cmd1, cmd2), TFIL(cmd3, cmd4)). execute() walks the tree: try cmd1 (TAND left), if success try cmd2; if AND fails, try TFIL: pipe cmd3 to cmd4. 12 node types cover everything — sequencing, branching, looping, pipes, subshells. The recursive interpreter pattern is from Bourne 1977. Bash and zsh use the same approach, just with more node types (functions, arithmetic, parameter expansion). 12 types from 1977 cover the bulk of shell semantics.

appraiser_note: execute() は shell の **AST インタプリタ**。 12 ノードタイプで dispatch: TCOM (単純コマンド)、 TFORK (子プロセス)、 TPAR (サブシェル `(...)`)、 TFIL (パイプ)、 TLST (順次 `;`)、 TAND (`&&`)、 TORF (`||`)、 TFOR (for-in)、 TWH (while)、 TUN (until)、 TIF (if-else)、 TSW (case-esac)。 各 case が木を再帰 walk: TFIL は左右に execute を spawn してパイプで接続、 TAND は左の exit code が 0 のときのみ右、 TFOR はリスト要素を変数に代入してループ。 **AST の再帰下降は標準インタプリタパターン**。 12 ノード分類がすべての shell 制御フローをカバー。 現代 shell (bash、 zsh) は関数、 case-modifier、 算術展開を追加するが、 **コア AST ノードタイプは 1977 年から不変**。

text:
case TFIL:
    BEGIN
        INT pv[2];
        chkpipe(pv);                /* pipe(2) syscall */
        IF execute(t->lstlef, 0, pf1, pv) == 0
        THEN execute(t->lstrit, execflg, pv, pf2);
        ELSE closepipe(pv);
        FI
    END
    break;

case TFORK:
    /* child branch: */
    IF treeflgs&FPIN
    THEN
        rename(pf1[INPIPE], 0);     /* stdin from pipe read end */
        close(pf1[OTPIPE]);
    FI
    IF treeflgs&FPOU
    THEN
        rename(pf2[OTPIPE], 1);     /* stdout to pipe write end */
        close(pf2[INPIPE]);
    FI
    initio(t->treio);                /* other redirections */
    execa(com);                       /* exec the command */

ai_analysis.technical_context: Unix pipe implementation: TFIL node has left and right children. shell calls chkpipe() = pipe(2) syscall to create pipe (returns 2 fds: read end pv[0], write end pv[1]). execute left side with pv as output target — left's child process renames pv[1] to stdout (1), closes pv[0]. execute right side with pv as input source — right's child renames pv[0] to stdin (0), closes pv[1]. Both children run in parallel via fork. Three-pipe chains like cmd1 | cmd2 | cmd3 parse to TFIL(cmd1, TFIL(cmd2, cmd3)) — recursive TFIL. The 5 syscalls (pipe, fork, dup2, close, exec) compose into the most powerful Unix abstraction: chained processes streaming data. Doug McIlroy proposed pipes in 1972; Bourne shell made them syntactic in 1977.
ai_analysis.human_story: ls | grep foo | sort: three programs running in parallel, each consuming the previous's output. How? Bourne shell's TFIL node + 5 syscalls. pipe(2) makes a pipe (2 file descriptors). fork(2) creates a child. dup2(2) redirects stdin/stdout to pipe ends. close(2) drops unused ends. exec(2) replaces child with the command. The shell does this twice (for cmd1|cmd2) or recursively for longer pipes. The result: data flows from ls's stdout, through grep's stdin, processed, out grep's stdout, into sort's stdin. McIlroy's 1972 pipe concept + Bourne's 1977 syntactic surface = Unix's most beloved abstraction.

appraiser_note: **Unix パイプ実装**: TFIL ノードは左右の子。 shell が **chkpipe() = pipe(2)** syscall でパイプ作成 (2 fd: 読端 pv[0]、 書端 pv[1])。 左側を execute、 出力先として pv を渡す——左の子プロセスが pv[1] を stdout (1) に rename、 pv[0] を close。 右側を execute、 入力元として pv を渡す——右の子が pv[0] を stdin (0) に rename、 pv[1] を close。 両方の子が fork で **並行実行**。 3 段パイプ `cmd1 | cmd2 | cmd3` は **TFIL(cmd1, TFIL(cmd2, cmd3))** に parse——再帰 TFIL。 **5 つの syscall** (pipe、 fork、 dup2、 close、 exec) が組み合わさって **Unix の最も強力な抽象**を構築: チェーン化されたプロセスがデータをストリーミング。 Doug McIlroy が 1972 年にパイプを提案、 Bourne shell が 1977 年に構文化。

text:
case TCOM:
    /* built-in lookup */
    IF (internal = syslook(com[0], commands)) ORF argn == 0
    THEN setlist(t->comset, 0);
    FI

    SWITCH internal IN
    case SYSDOT:    /* . file (source) */
    case SYSCD:     /* cd */
    case SYSEXIT:   /* exit */
    case SYSEXEC:   /* exec */
    case SYSSHFT:   /* shift */
    case SYSWAIT:   /* wait */
    case SYSREAD:   /* read */
    case SYSSET:    /* set */
    case SYSXPORT:  /* export */
    case SYSRDONLY: /* readonly */
    case SYSEVAL:   /* eval */
    case SYSUMASK:  /* umask */
    case SYSTRAP:   /* trap */
    case SYSBREAK:  /* break */
    case SYSCONT:   /* continue */
    case SYSNULL:   /* : (no-op) */
    case SYSTIMES:  /* times */
    default:
        internal = builtin(argn, com);
    ENDSW

    /* if not built-in, falls through to TFORK + execa() */

ai_analysis.technical_context: Shell distinguishes built-in commands (run inside shell process) from external (fork+exec). Built-ins must modify shell state: cd changes shell's cwd (chdir in fork would lose effect), export modifies environment, exit terminates shell, shift modifies positional params, read sets shell variables, set changes shell flags, trap installs signal handlers, etc. ~18 built-ins via SYSDOT/SYSCD/SYSEXIT etc. syslook(name, commands) hash table lookup matches command name to internal code. External commands fall through to TFORK case → execa() does fork+execvp. The distinction is fundamental: `cd /tmp` only works as built-in because external /bin/cd's chdir(2) wouldn't affect parent shell. Modern shells extend the built-in set (bash has 70+) but the dispatch pattern is from Bourne 1977.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why is `cd` always a shell built-in, never /bin/cd? Because if cd were external, you'd fork a child, child chdir to /tmp, child exits — parent shell's cwd unchanged. The whole point of cd is to change shell's cwd; only running in shell process achieves this. Same for export, exit, read, set. ~18 built-ins in 1977 Bourne sh. Bash extended to 70+. But the boundary — 'commands that change shell state must be built-in' — is fundamental and unchanged. The `type cd` command tells you 'cd is a shell builtin' — that distinction is from 1977.

appraiser_note: shell は **built-in** (shell プロセス内実行) と **external** (fork+exec) を区別。 built-in は shell 状態を変更する必要: cd は shell の cwd を変更 (fork した子の chdir は親に反映されない)、 export は環境変数変更、 exit は shell 終了、 shift は位置パラメータ変更、 read は shell 変数設定、 set は shell flags 変更、 trap はシグナルハンドラ設定、 等。 **約 18 個の built-in** を SYSDOT/SYSCD/SYSEXIT などの定数で dispatch。 syslook(name, commands) のハッシュテーブル lookup でコマンド名を内部コードに対応。 external コマンドは TFORK case に fall through、 execa() で fork+execvp。 区別は根本的: `cd /tmp` が動くのは built-in だから——external /bin/cd の chdir(2) は親 shell に影響しない。 現代 shell は built-in を拡張 (bash は 70+) するが、 dispatch パターンは 1977 年 Bourne から。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Shell control flow: TFOR loops over a word list assigning each to variable; TWH/TUN handle while/until (cleverly unified via `(execute()==0)==(type==TWH)` — same code for both); TIF dispatches on exit code (0 = true); TSW does glob matching for case-esac (uses gmatch with shell glob syntax: * ? [abc], not regex). These 5 statements transform shell from command interpreter to programming language. Pre-Bourne, Thompson shell had no control flow — `if` was a separate program. Bourne integrated them as syntax. exit-code-as-boolean (0=success=true) became Unix's calling convention. case-esac uses shell glob (filename wildcards), not regex — same patterns used in ls *.c. The control flow's recursive execute() calls share variable scope with parent — `for i in list; do ...; done` modifies $i visible after loop.
ai_analysis.human_story: Pre-Bourne, you couldn't write `if cmd; then ...; fi` in shell — `if` was a separate program. Bourne made control flow part of shell syntax: for, while, until, if, case. 5 constructs transformed shell from command launcher to programming language. Look at the TWH/TUN trick: same code handles while AND until via `(execute==0)==(type==TWH)` — true equals true for while, false equals false for until. Genius brevity. Today's bash if/then/elif/else/fi, for/in/do/done, while/do/done, case/esac all descend directly from these 1977 case branches. The exit-code-as-boolean convention shaped Unix's `test`, `[`, `grep`, etc.

appraiser_note: **shell 制御フロー**: TFOR は word リストを走査して変数に代入、 TWH/TUN は while/until を処理 (巧妙に統一: `(execute()==0)==(type==TWH)` で同じコードが両方を扱う)、 TIF は exit code で dispatch (0=true)、 TSW は case-esac の glob マッチ (shell glob `* ? [abc]` を使う、 regex ではない)。 **5 つの構文が shell をコマンドインタプリタからプログラミング言語に変えた**。 Bourne 以前の Thompson shell には制御フロー無し——`if` は別プログラムだった。 Bourne が構文に統合。 exit-code-as-boolean (0=success=true) が Unix の呼び出し慣習に。 case-esac は shell glob (`ls *.c` で使うパターン) を使い regex ではない。 制御フローの再帰 execute() 呼び出しは親と変数スコープを共有——`for i in list; do ...; done` で $i は loop 後も見える。 現代 bash の if/then/elif/else/fi、 for/in/do/done、 while/do/done、 case/esac はすべて 1977 年の case ブランチの子孫。

ai_analysis.technical_context: bc translates algebraic infix to dc's RPN postfix via yacc + bundle(). Each rule like `e '+' e = bundle(3, $1, $3, '+')` produces 'left right +' — RPN. yacc's bottom-up LALR(1) parsing reduces inner-most expressions first, which naturally generates RPN in output order. Operator precedence from %right/%left handles `a + b * c → a (b c *) +` correctly. Variables map to dc's single-letter registers: `x = 5` becomes ` 5dsx` (push 5, dup, save to x). Function calls: `f(5)` → ` 5lFx` (push arg, load F, execute). This is a classic transpiler: parse one language, emit another. The transpiler pattern was novel in 1975; today it's everywhere (CoffeeScript→JS, TypeScript→JS, Sass→CSS, JSX→JS).
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `1 + 2 * 3` in bc — yacc parses it as `1 + (2*3)`. Then bundle() emits RPN: ` 2 3 * 1 +` (postfix order). Send to dc, which is an RPN calculator. dc runs it, prints 7. bc is purely a frontend — it translates and delegates. The 'transpile + delegate' pattern is 1975's gift. CoffeeScript translates to JavaScript and delegates execution to V8. TypeScript does the same. Sass translates to CSS. Markdown translates to HTML. bc was the prototype: yacc parser + RPN emitter + dc executor. The 3-line bundle rule `e '+' e = bundle(3, $1, $3, '+')` is the entire algorithm.

appraiser_note: bc は代数中置記法を dc の RPN 後置記法に **yacc + bundle()** で翻訳。 各規則 `e '+' e = bundle(3, $1, $3, '+')` が「left right +」 = RPN を生成。 yacc の **bottom-up LALR(1) parsing が内側式から reduce** されるので、 自然に RPN 順で出力。 %right/%left の演算子優先順位で `a + b * c → a (b c *) +` を正しく扱う。 変数は dc の単一文字レジスタにマップ: `x = 5` → ` 5dsx` (5 を push、 dup、 x に save)。 関数呼び出し: `f(5)` → ` 5lFx` (引数 push、 F load、 実行)。 **古典的 transpiler**: 1 言語を parse、 別の言語を emit。 transpiler パターンは 1975 年に novel、 今は遍在 (CoffeeScript→JS、 TypeScript→JS、 Sass→CSS、 JSX→JS) ——全部 bc の祖。 3 行の bundle ルールがアルゴリズム全体。

ai_analysis.technical_context: bc compiles if/while/for to dc macros + conditional execute. dc has [...] (string literal), sX (save to register), lXx (load and execute). bc allocates unique register names via CRS (starting from '0', skipping '[' and 'a-z'). For `if (cond) body`: emit body as macro saved in register A, then emit `cond A>` to conditionally execute. For `while`: emit body + re-evaluate condition + recursive macro call, all as one macro; initial call evaluates condition once. For `for(init; cond; incr) body`: emit body + incr + re-eval as macro, init + first cond as launcher. This is compile-time macro generation — no runtime if/while interpretation in bc. The dc bytecode is fully self-contained loop. Tail-recursive style: macro ends by calling itself.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does `while (i < 10) { i = i + 1 }` work in bc? bc compiles it to a dc macro: `[li1+dsi li10 >A] sA li10 >A`. The macro increments i, then re-tests i<10, then re-calls itself if true (tail recursion). Initial line tests once and starts the loop. No runtime while-loop in bc — it's all dc macros generated at compile time. This is 'compile to a stack machine' decades before LLVM. Modern WebAssembly, JVM bytecode, .NET CIL all do versions of this. bc shows the basic idea in 1975: take a high-level loop, compile to stack-machine instructions, execute on a stack VM (dc).

appraiser_note: bc は if/while/for を **dc マクロ + 条件実行**にコンパイル。 dc は [...] (文字列リテラル)、 sX (レジスタ保存)、 lXx (ロード+実行) を持つ。 bc は CRS で **unique register 名**を割り当て ('0' から始め、 '[' と 'a-z' を skip)。 `if (cond) body`: 本体をレジスタ A に保存、 `cond A>` で条件実行を emit。 `while`: 本体 + 条件再評価 + マクロ自己呼び出しを 1 マクロとして emit、 初回呼び出しは条件を評価。 `for(init; cond; incr) body`: 本体 + incr + 条件再評価をマクロに、 init + 初回条件を launcher として。 **コンパイル時マクロ生成**——bc には runtime if/while なし、 dc バイトコードが自己完結ループ。 末尾再帰スタイル: マクロ末尾で自己呼び出し。 1975 年に「スタック機械にコンパイル」 ——LLVM/WebAssembly/JVM bytecode/CIL の祖。

ai_analysis.technical_context: bc functions translate to dc named registers holding macros. `define f(x) { body }` → compile body as macro, save to register F. Arg passing: pp() emits arg-save code in `pre` (function prologue), tp() emits auto var setup in `pre` too. Body in middle. `post` is epilogue. `0 N Q` at end: push 0 (return value if no explicit return), then N (level count), Q (multi-level quit) to exit N macro-call layers. `return (e)` emits `e post N Q` — push value, run epilogue, exit N levels. Function call `f(5)`: ` 5 l F x` — push arg, load F, execute. Recursion works because Q only exits the inner-most calls. bc allows 26 functions (a-z) + arrays.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does bc do recursion? Functions are dc macros stored in named registers. `define f(x) { return f(x-1) + 1 }` becomes a macro F that, when executed, can call itself via `lFx`. The trick: each function call is a macro invocation, and dc maintains the call stack. `Q` exits N levels of macro calls — `return` knows what N is (the current nesting level `lev`) and emits the right number. This dc execution model (stack of macro calls, Q for multi-level exit) supports proper function semantics. 1975 bc had recursion years before many academic languages.

appraiser_note: bc 関数は **dc の named register に保存されたマクロ**にコンパイル。 `define f(x) { body }` → 本体をマクロにコンパイル、 レジスタ F に保存。 **引数渡し**: pp() が引数保存コードを `pre` (関数 prologue) に emit、 tp() が auto var セットアップを `pre` に。 本体が中間。 `post` が epilogue。 末尾の `0 N Q`: 0 (return 値が無い場合のデフォルト) を push、 N (レベルカウント)、 Q (multi-level quit) で N マクロ呼び出しレベル分を抜ける。 `return (e)`: `e post N Q` を emit——値 push、 epilogue 実行、 N レベル抜ける。 関数呼び出し `f(5)`: ` 5 l F x` ——引数 push、 F load、 実行。 **再帰**動作: Q が内側呼び出しだけを抜けるから。 bc は a-z の 26 関数 + 配列を許す。 1975 年の bc が、 多くの学術言語より早く再帰を実用化。

ai_analysis.technical_context: bc exposes dc's k/i/o commands as named variables: scale (output decimal precision), ibase (input number base), obase (output number base). Compound assignment (`scale += 2`) emits K (push current scale) + value + op + k (set new scale). Increment (`scale++`) emits Kd1+k — push, dup (return old value), increment, set new. dc internally computes in base 100 (10^2 digits per byte) for memory efficiency, but presents bases 2-16 (and higher in modern bc) for user. ibase/obase enable arbitrary base conversion: set ibase=16, type 'ff', get 255 in decimal. GNU bc -l adds math functions (s/c/a/l/e/j/sqrt) at bc level on top of dc primitives. POSIX bc requires bases 2-16 minimum.
ai_analysis.human_story: Want 100 digits of pi? `scale=100; 4*a(1)` in bc. scale controls output precision (decimal digits). obase=16 outputs hex, ibase=2 reads binary. These three variables — scale, ibase, obase — make bc an arbitrary-precision base-converting calculator. Behind the scenes, dc does base-100 math (memory-efficient, 2 decimal digits per byte) and converts via i/o commands at I/O time. The user sees `1/3 = 0.333...` to whatever precision they ask. From 1975 bc's design, GNU bc -l added transcendental functions (sin, cos, atan, log, exp, bessel) — but the precision/base controls are all from Bell-32V.

appraiser_note: bc は dc の k/i/o コマンドを **名前付き変数**として公開: **scale** (出力 10 進精度)、 **ibase** (入力基数)、 **obase** (出力基数)。 複合代入 `scale += 2`: `K` (現在 scale を push) + 値 + op + `k` (新 scale 設定) を emit。 インクリメント `scale++`: `Kd1+k` ——push、 dup (古い値を return)、 inc、 新値設定。 dc は内部で **100 進**計算 (10^2 桁/byte、 メモリ効率) だが、 ユーザーには base 2-16 (現代 bc では更に大) を提示。 ibase/obase で任意基数変換: ibase=16 にして 'ff' を入力すると 10 進で 255。 GNU bc -l は dc プリミティブの上に bc レベルで math 関数 (s/c/a/l/e/j/sqrt) を追加。 POSIX bc は最低 base 2-16 を要求。 100 桁の円周率: `scale=100; 4*a(1)` で計算可能。

ai_analysis.technical_context: bc's lexer is hand-written, not lex-generated. Reads char, skips whitespace, handles backslash-newline continuation. For [a-z][a-z] pattern, checks 2-char prefix against ~15 reserved words: if/while/for/sqrt/return/break/define/scale/base/ibase/obase/dibits/auto/length/quit. Two letters of lookahead is enough because all reserved words have unique 2-char prefixes. Single letter [a-z] is a variable name (LETTER token). peekc is the lookahead buffer. The hand-written approach is simple, fast, and avoids the lex dependency. 1979 Bell-32V had lex, but bc chose hand-written for the 15 keywords. Reserved words prefixed with _ in yacc tokens (_IF, _WHILE) to avoid clashes with C keywords. Function quit calls getout() to exit bc.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does bc tell `if` (keyword) from `i` (variable)? Lookahead. Read char, peek next char. If both are a-z, it's a reserved word — match against 15 known prefixes. Otherwise it's a single-letter variable. 30 lines of hand-written lexer, no lex needed. 2 chars of lookahead is exactly enough — all 15 keywords (if, while, for, sqrt, return, break, define, scale, base, ibase, obase, dibits, auto, length, quit) have unique 2-letter starts. Beautifully economical. Modern lexers use lex/flex or PEG, but bc's 1979 hand-roll proves you can do tokenization with one peekc buffer and a switch on 2 chars.

appraiser_note: bc の lexer は **手書き**、 lex 生成ではない。 文字を読み、 空白 skip、 バックスラッシュ改行継続を処理。 [a-z][a-z] パターンには 2 文字 prefix を約 15 個の予約語と照合: if/while/for/sqrt/return/break/define/scale/base/ibase/obase/dibits/auto/length/quit。 **2 文字 lookahead で十分**——全予約語が unique な 2 文字 prefix を持つ。 単一 [a-z] は変数名 (LETTER トークン)。 peekc が lookahead バッファ。 手書きアプローチはシンプル、 速い、 lex 依存を避ける。 1979 年 Bell-32V には lex があるが、 bc は 15 キーワードのため手書きを選択。 yacc トークンの `_` 接頭辞 (`_IF`、 `_WHILE`) は C キーワードとの衝突回避。 `quit` 関数は getout() で bc 終了。 1 つの peekc バッファ + 2 文字 switch で完全なトークン化、 1979 年の手書き経済性。

ai_analysis.technical_context: expr defines its entire expression language in 50 lines of yacc. Tokens: OR/AND/comparisons/arithmetic/MATCH/SUBSTR/LENGTH/INDEX. Precedence ladder from %left declarations: OR (lowest) < AND < comparisons < +/- < */% < MCH (:) < MATCH < SUBSTR < LENGTH/INDEX (highest). Each grammar rule calls a C helper: conj/rel/arith/match/substr/length/index. The final reduction (expr NOARG) prints result and exits with 0/1 based on truthy/falsy. NOARG is the sentinel for end-of-argv. A_STRING is the catch-all token for non-operator arguments. yacc handles precedence and associativity correctly via %left declarations — write `1 + 2 * 3` and it parses as `1 + (2*3)`. The whole language: 9 token classes, 8 precedence levels, 16 grammar productions.
ai_analysis.human_story: Want a calculator that handles 1+2, string comparison, regex match, and substring extraction — all in one tool? Write 50 lines of yacc. expr.y defines tokens, precedence, and productions; each production reduces to a helper function call. yacc generates the parser; expr's main calls yyparse(). The brevity is breathtaking: a complete expression evaluator with operator precedence, in 50 lines. Modern equivalents (Python AST, JavaScript estree, ANTLR grammars) are larger because they support more, but the core idea — 'declare grammar, generate parser, call C helpers' — is from 1979 expr.

appraiser_note: expr は式言語全体を **yacc 50 行**で定義。 トークン: OR/AND/比較/算術/MATCH/SUBSTR/LENGTH/INDEX。 %left 宣言で優先順位ラダー: OR (最低) < AND < 比較 < +/- < */% < MCH (:) < MATCH < SUBSTR < LENGTH/INDEX (最高)。 各文法規則が C ヘルパー (conj/rel/arith/match/substr/length/index) を呼ぶ。 最終 reduction (expr NOARG) で結果を print + truthy/falsy で exit 0/1。 NOARG は argv 終端 sentinel、 A_STRING は非オペレータ引数の catch-all。 yacc が **優先順位と結合性を %left で正しく扱う**——`1 + 2 * 3` は `1 + (2*3)` と parse。 言語全体: 9 トークンクラス、 8 優先順位レベル、 16 文法生成。 簡潔さが息を呑む——完全な式評価器を演算子優先順位込みで 50 行。 現代の同等物 (Python AST、 JavaScript estree、 ANTLR) はより大きいが、 「文法宣言 + パーサ生成 + C ヘルパー呼び出し」 のコア発想は 1979 年 expr から。

ai_analysis.technical_context: expr's lexer is a 15-line yylex(). Each call returns one token = one argv element. No tokenization needed (shell did that), just operator-table lookup. Argi is the argv index, advances per call. (/) are special-cased as ASCII tokens (yacc convention: 1-char tokens use literal ASCII). String matches in operators[] map to op[] yacc tokens; misses become A_STRING (variable/value). NOARG signals end of args, triggering yyparse to finish. This 'argv-as-tokens' pattern is from find (#171), reused in expr, test/[. Compare to bc's handwritten lexer that processes character-by-character; expr is one level higher — shell pre-tokenized the input, so expr's lex is just argv walking. Beautiful exploitation of shell's pre-processing.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does expr have such a tiny lexer? Because shell did the work. When you type `expr 1 + 2`, shell splits on whitespace and passes ["expr", "1", "+", "2"] to expr. expr's yylex just walks argv, mapping each element to a yacc token (or A_STRING for non-operators). 15 lines. find did this first, expr copied the idea, test/[ followed. The insight: in shell-invoked tools, tokenization is already done. Just need argv-to-token mapping. Modern tools that take quoted args (`cmd 'complex arg'`) often regret needing more sophisticated parsing, but for simple cases, argv-as-tokens is unbeatable.

appraiser_note: expr の lexer は **15 行の yylex()**。 各呼び出しが 1 トークン = 1 argv 要素を返す。 トークン化不要 (shell が済ませた)、 オペレータテーブル lookup のみ。 Argi は argv インデックス、 呼び出しごとに進む。 (/) は ASCII トークンとして特別扱い (yacc 慣習: 1 文字トークンはリテラル ASCII を使う)。 operators[] で文字列マッチして op[] の yacc トークンへ; マッチしなければ A_STRING (変数/値)。 NOARG が引数終端 signal、 yyparse 終了をトリガー。 **「argv-as-tokens」 パターンは find (#171) 由来**、 expr で再利用、 test/[ も追随。 bc の文字単位手書き lexer と比べて、 expr は 1 段階上——shell が事前トークン化しているので、 expr の lex は argv 走査だけ。 shell の前処理を美しく利用。

ai_analysis.technical_context: expr internally stores all values as strings, but dynamically detects numbers for comparison/arithmetic. ematch(string, regex) checks if string matches a regex; if both rel() args match [0-9]*$, do numeric comparison via atol(); otherwise string comparison via strcmp(). arith() requires numeric (errors otherwise). The expr regex grammar is ed/grep BRE: . * [a-z] ^ $ etc. Note BUG in LEQ case: should be `i <= 0` but coded as `i >= 0` — has been there since 1979. Probably typo, masked by tests that didn't cover edge cases. Modern coreutils expr fixed it. The auto-string/number duality predates JavaScript's `==` coercion by decades and was Unix's way of letting shell scripts work with both types transparently.
ai_analysis.human_story: expr handles both `expr 10 > 9` (numeric: true) and `expr 'abc' > 'abd'` (string: false). How? Sniff the args with a regex: if both match [0-9]*$, treat as numbers; else treat as strings. Auto-typing. JavaScript's == coercion is the most famous version of this; expr did it in 1979. The arith case is stricter — must be numeric or error. This duality lets shell scripts work naturally with both types. Side note: there's a real bug in case LEQ (`i = i>=0` should be `i<=0`) — present in Bell-32V source for 50 years. Modern coreutils fixed it. The history of bugs in core utilities is the history of software engineering.

appraiser_note: expr は内部的に全値を文字列で持つが、 比較/算術のため数値を**動的検出**。 ematch(string, regex) が文字列の regex マッチをチェック; rel() で両引数が [0-9]*$ にマッチすれば atol() で数値比較、 そうでなければ strcmp() で文字列比較。 arith() は数値必須 (そうでなければエラー)。 expr の regex 文法は ed/grep BRE。 **注: LEQ case にバグ**——`i <= 0` であるべきが `i >= 0` とコーディングされている、 1979 年から残存。 おそらくタイポ、 テストでカバーされなかった edge case。 現代 coreutils expr では修正済み。 **自動 string/number 二重性**は JavaScript の == 強制変換の数十年前、 shell スクリプトが両型を透過的に扱えるようにする Unix の流儀。 1979 年に始まり、 PHP/Perl/JavaScript の動的型に継承。

ai_analysis.technical_context: expr ':' (MCH) operator does regex match. Left side is string, right side is regex (BRE — same as ed/grep). On match: if regex has \(...\) group, return captured string; else return match length. On no match, return '0'. Uses ed's compile/execute engine via ematch wrapper. nbra (number of brackets, i.e., \(...\) groups) and Mstring[1][128] (first captured group buffer) are globals shared with ed's regex code. Only supports 1 captured group (Mstring[1] = 1 element). Modern expr supports more groups. The ':' syntax was Bell-32V's invention — later POSIX standardized it. This is the proto-`=~` operator that bash/Perl/Ruby copied. The 'return match length' semantics is quirky and rarely used; 'return capture' is the common use case.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `expr 'file.txt' : '.*\.\(.*\)$'` and get 'txt'. expr's : operator does regex match and returns the captured group from \(...\). The 1979 invention of 'regex as language operator' is the proto-`=~`: bash's [[ =~ ]], Perl's $1, Ruby's =~. Without explicit grouping, : returns match length (rarely used). The ed-shared regex engine (compile/execute) does the heavy lifting. Single-group limitation (Mstring[1]) was eventually lifted. The : operator is one of those quirky Unix features — newcomers don't recognize it as regex, veterans use it for quick string extraction in shell scripts.

appraiser_note: expr の `:` (MCH) 演算子が **regex マッチ**を行う。 左辺が文字列、 右辺が regex (BRE——ed/grep と同じ)。 マッチ時: `\(...\)` グループがあれば**キャプチャ文字列**を返す、 なければ**マッチ長さ**を返す。 ノーマッチ時は `"0"` を返す。 ed の compile/execute エンジンを ematch ラッパー経由で使用。 nbra (\(...\) グループ数) と Mstring[1][128] (最初のキャプチャグループバッファ) は ed の regex コードと共有するグローバル。 **1 グループのみサポート**——Mstring[1] = 1 要素、 現代 expr は更に対応。 `:` 構文は Bell-32V の発明、 後の POSIX で標準化。 これが **bash `[[ =~ ]]` / Perl `$1` / Ruby `=~` の祖**——「regex を言語演算子に」 の早期実装。 「マッチ長さ返却」 セマンティクスは奇妙であまり使わない、 「キャプチャ返却」 が一般的用途。

text:
expression: expr NOARG = {
    printf("%s\n", $1);
    exit((!strcmp($1, "0") || !strcmp($1, "\0")) ? 1 : 0);
}

/* Result | exit code | meaning
   "0"    | 1         | falsy
   ""     | 1         | falsy
   other  | 0         | truthy
   error  | 2         | error

   Shell usage:
     if expr "$x" '>' 10; then
        echo "x > 10"
     fi

     if expr "$file" : ".*\.c$"; then
        echo "C file"
     fi
*/

ai_analysis.technical_context: expr communicates results two ways: stdout (the value) and exit code (truthiness). Print result to stdout, then exit 0 if truthy or 1 if falsy. Falsy = string '0' or empty string. Truthy = anything else. Errors return 2. This dual-output allows shell to use expr both as value source (`x=$(expr 1 + 2)`) and condition (`if expr ...; then`). The truthiness convention (0/'' = false, other = true) influenced JavaScript, Perl, Ruby, Python truthiness semantics. POSIX expr formalized this. The exit-code-as-boolean Unix convention (0 = success = true) is rationalized differently than language truthiness — expr blends both. Modern shells with $((...)) and [[ =~ ]] reduce expr's need, but POSIX scripts still use it.
ai_analysis.human_story: expr returns two things: the value (stdout) and truthiness (exit code). `expr 1 + 2` prints '3' and exits 0 (truthy). `expr 0` prints '0' and exits 1 (falsy). This dual output is genius for shell scripts: `if expr ...` checks truthiness, `x=$(expr ...)` captures value. The truthiness rule (0/'' = false, else = true) is the ancestor of JavaScript's truthiness, Perl's, Ruby's, Python's. The 0=success/non-0=failure Unix exit code convention is preserved AND extended: 0=success+truthy, 1=success+falsy, 2=error. Three exit codes encoding two semantics. POSIX standardized this. The pattern is so useful that all modern shell-invocable tools follow it.

appraiser_note: expr は **2 通りで結果を伝える**: stdout (値) と exit code (真偽性)。 結果を stdout に print、 truthy なら exit 0、 falsy なら exit 1。 falsy = 文字列 "0" または空文字列。 truthy = それ以外。 エラーは exit 2。 この **dual output** で shell は expr を value source (`x=$(expr 1+2)`) と condition (`if expr ...; then`) の両方で使える。 真偽性慣習 (0/'' = false、 他 = true) が **JavaScript/Perl/Ruby/Python truthiness の祖**。 Unix の exit-code-as-boolean 慣習 (0 = success = true) と言語の truthiness を expr が融合。 3 つの exit code が 2 つのセマンティクスをエンコード: 0=success+truthy、 1=success+falsy、 2=error。 POSIX で標準化、 すべての shell-invocable ツールがこのパターンに従う。 現代 shell の $((...)) と [[ =~ ]] で expr の必要性は減ったが、 POSIX スクリプトでは今も使用。

ai_analysis.technical_context: mbox format: multiple messages concatenated in one file, each starting with 'From sender datetime'. isfrom() checks line starts with literal 'From '. copymt() walks file, recording offsets of each From line in let[] array (indexed message-by-message). MAXLET=300 messages limit. Sendmail prepends 'From $USER $(ctime())' to outgoing message and escapes any body line starting with 'From ' as '>From ' to prevent boundary confusion. Modern systems still use mbox via this exact format — /var/spool/mail/$USER on Linux, mutt's local folders, alpine's INBOX. Maildir (qmail, 1995) avoids the format's ambiguities by using one file per message, but mbox persists because shell tools (grep, awk) process it easily.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does your Linux /var/mail/alice file have lines like 'From bob Mon Mar 17...'? Because of mbox format from 1979 mail. Each message starts with 'From ' (literal, no colon — that's 'From:' the header). Multiple messages stacked in one file. When you write 'From the bank, ...' in body, mail escapes it to '>From the bank, ...' so the next message boundary isn't mistakenly detected. The escape is one-way — receivers see '>From' permanently. 50-year-old format that survives because it's so simple shell tools can parse it. Maildir (qmail) avoids the issues but mbox lingers.

appraiser_note: mbox 形式: 1 ファイルに複数メッセージを連結、 各メッセージが `From sender datetime` で始まる。 isfrom() で行が "From " リテラルで始まるかチェック。 copymt() でファイルを walk、 各 From 行の offset を let[] 配列に記録 (メッセージ毎にインデックス)。 MAXLET=300 メッセージ制限。 Sendmail は送信メッセージに 'From $USER $(ctime())' を prepend、 本文中で 'From ' で始まる行を '>From ' にエスケープして境界混乱を防ぐ。 現代システムも /var/spool/mail/$USER (Linux)、 mutt のローカルフォルダ、 alpine の INBOX で同形式を使用。 Maildir (qmail 1995) はメッセージ毎 1 ファイルで曖昧さを回避するが、 mbox は shell ツール (grep、 awk) で処理しやすいので残存。 1979 年から 50 年継承される形式。

ai_analysis.technical_context: /usr/spool/mail/$USER is the per-user mailbox file (later /var/spool/mail/ or /var/mail/). Send = append to recipient's file (mbox append-only growth). Receive = lock + copy-to-temp + unlock, then iterate over temp, write back on changes. lock()/unlock() prevent concurrent send racing with read+delete (would lose mail). chown() sets recipient as owner so they can manage their own mailbox. setuid(getuid()) drops any setuid privileges before mailbox access — security best practice. umask(MAILMODE) = umask(~0644) creates 0644 mailboxes (rw-r--r--) — readable only by owner. Pattern: spool dir + per-user file + locking + ownership = the foundation MTA inherited (sendmail, postfix all do this).
ai_analysis.human_story: How does Unix store your mail? One file per user in /var/spool/mail/. Sending = append to that file. Reading = lock, copy to temp, unlock, work on temp. The lock prevents the classic race: you delete message 5 while sender appends a new one — without locking, the new one disappears. chown ensures the recipient owns their mailbox (sender writes, then transfers ownership). 1979 setuid(getuid()) drops privileges right away — already practicing least-privilege. Modern Linux /var/mail/$USER still uses this exact layout. sendmail, postfix, qmail's mbox driver all follow the lock-temp-write pattern from Bell-32V mail.

appraiser_note: /usr/spool/mail/$USER がユーザー毎メールボックスファイル (後の /var/spool/mail/ or /var/mail/)。 送信 = 受信者ファイルに append (mbox 追記専用増加)。 受信 = lock + temp にコピー + unlock、 temp を iterate、 変更あれば書き戻し。 lock()/unlock() が **並行 send と read+delete の race を防ぐ** (lock なしだと新メールが消える)。 chown() で **所有者を受信者に**——自分のメールボックスを管理できる。 setuid(getuid()) で **setuid 権限を即降下**——mailbox アクセス前にセキュリティベストプラクティス。 umask(MAILMODE) = umask(~0644) で 0644 メールボックス作成 (rw-r--r--、 所有者のみ read)。 パターン: spool dir + per-user file + locking + ownership = MTA (sendmail、 postfix、 qmail) が継承する基盤。 現代 Linux /var/mail/$USER も同レイアウト。

ai_analysis.technical_context: UUCP (Unix to Unix Copy Protocol) bang paths: name with '!' separators routes through multiple hosts. mail parses left of first '!' as next hop (rsys), spawns uux command to deliver to that host's rmail. If more '!' in remainder, recursive routing continues on next host. Pre-Internet era networking: store-and-forward via dial-up modems. vax135!ucbvax!alice = forward to vax135, then ucbvax, then deliver to alice. Each hop runs uux to fire off rmail on next system. Local thissys="vax135" hardcoded — compile-time configuration per machine. By 1985-90, replaced by Internet SMTP (user@host.domain with DNS). Bang paths inverted right-to-left at some sites — caused the famous 'UUCP address chaos' of the era.
ai_analysis.human_story: Email addresses used to look like vax135!ucbvax!alice — three machines, exclamation marks. Each machine was a hop in the store-and-forward chain. mail parses the first ! to find next hop (vax135), fires uux to deliver to that machine's rmail. That machine repeats. Phone modems carried bytes between systems. By 1990, SMTP + DNS replaced bang paths with alice@ucbvax.edu — way cleaner. But bang paths defined a generation of Unix email. The 'vax135' hardcoded in source had to be edited and recompiled per machine. Imagine recompiling /usr/bin/mail when you renamed your VAX.

appraiser_note: **UUCP (Unix to Unix Copy Protocol) bang path**: `!` 区切りで複数ホスト経由ルーティング。 mail が最初の `!` までを次のホップ (rsys) としてパース、 uux コマンドを spawn してそのホストの rmail に配信。 残り部分にさらに `!` があれば、 次のホストで再帰ルーティング。 **インターネット以前の networking**: 電話モデムで store-and-forward。 vax135!ucbvax!alice = vax135 経由、 ucbvax 経由、 alice 配信。 各ホップで uux で次のシステムの rmail を起動。 ローカル thissys="vax135" はハードコード——コンパイル時設定、 マシン毎にコンパイル必要。 1985-90 年で SMTP (user@host.domain + DNS) に置換。 一部サイトで bang path を右から左に解釈、 「**UUCP address chaos**」 という時代の混乱。 メールアドレスをコンパイル時に決める時代の遺物。

ai_analysis.technical_context: mail's interactive UI: 13 single-character commands at the '?' prompt. ? (help), n/+/Enter (next), -/^ (previous), p (reprint), d (delete - mark only), s/w/y (save), m (mail forward), q (quit, save changes), x (exit, discard changes), ! (shell escape). 2-stage delete: 'd' marks, 'q' commits to mailbox. Forward flag changes traversal order (-r flag). The '!' shell-escape is universal Unix MUA convention — vi, ed, ftp, dc, adb all support it. The 13-command vocabulary is the proto-MUA: mailx, elm, pine, mutt, alpine all inherited this command set. Modern GUI mail clients have buttons for the same operations (Reply, Forward, Delete, Save) but the underlying semantics are from 1979.
ai_analysis.human_story: Press '?' at mail's prompt — 10 commands listed. d (delete), s (save), q (quit), x (exit-without-save), p (print again), - (previous), n (next), m (mail forward), ! (shell escape). 13 single-letter operations cover all mail management. 2-stage delete: 'd' marks, 'q' commits. mailx, elm, pine, mutt, alpine all inherited these commands. Modern Outlook/Gmail buttons are GUI versions of the same operations. The '!ls' shell escape inside mail let you check what's around without quitting — convenience that 1979 multitasking OS could have provided differently (Ctrl-Z), but the in-program shell escape was the established UX pattern.

appraiser_note: mail の対話 UI: `?` プロンプトで **13 個の 1 文字コマンド**。 ? (help)、 n/+/Enter (next)、 -/^ (previous)、 p (再表示)、 d (削除——マークのみ)、 s/w/y (保存)、 m (転送)、 q (quit + 変更保存)、 x (exit + 変更破棄)、 ! (shell escape)。 **2 段階削除**: 'd' でマーク、 'q' で mailbox にコミット。 forward フラグで走査順切替 (-r フラグ)。 `!` shell escape は普遍的 Unix MUA 慣習——vi、 ed、 ftp、 dc、 adb すべてサポート。 13 コマンド語彙が **MUA の祖**: mailx、 elm、 pine、 mutt、 alpine すべて継承。 現代 GUI メールクライアントの Reply/Forward/Delete/Save ボタンも同じ操作の GUI 版。 1979 年の multitasking OS なら Ctrl-Z でも済むが、 プログラム内 shell escape が確立された UX パターン。

ai_analysis.technical_context: On send failure (recipient doesn't exist, mailbox locked too long, etc.), mail saves the message to ./dead.letter in current directory. ZAP flag strips the 'From ' line so it can be re-sent. User can later `mail -f dead.letter recipient` to retry. setuid(getuid()) before opening — drop privileges to user. Print 'Mail saved in dead.letter' to inform. The cwd-based save (not $HOME) is a quirk — common in cron jobs and scripts that cd before running mail. Modern MTAs (sendmail, postfix) have /var/spool/mqueue for retry queuing, but Bell-32V mail is 1-stage rescue: save and notify. The 'dead letter office' analogy from postal service became Unix folklore.
ai_analysis.human_story: Your mail to alice@nonexistent failed. mail saves it to ./dead.letter — not lost forever, you can fix the address and retry with `mail -f dead.letter alice@correct`. The cwd-based save means cron jobs running from /tmp get /tmp/dead.letter (sometimes lost). Modern MTAs do better: /var/spool/mqueue + automatic retry. But the 'dead.letter' filename became Unix folklore — the postal-service term for undeliverable mail, applied to Unix's electronic equivalent. The message 'Mail saved in dead.letter' has been pacifying Unix users for 50 years.

appraiser_note: 送信失敗時 (受信者不在、 mailbox lock timeout 等)、 mail がメッセージを cwd の **./dead.letter** に保存。 ZAP フラグで 'From ' 行を削除して、 後で `mail -f dead.letter recipient` で再送可能に。 setuid(getuid()) で open 前に **権限降下**。 「Mail saved in dead.letter」 で通知。 **cwd ベース**保存 ($HOME ではなく) は癖——cd してから mail 実行する cron スクリプトで重要。 現代 MTA (sendmail、 postfix) は /var/spool/mqueue で再送 queuing するが、 Bell-32V mail は 1 段階救済: 保存 + 通知。 **「dead letter office」** (米国郵政の配達不能郵便用語) の analogy が Unix folklore に。 「Mail saved in dead.letter」 メッセージが 50 年 Unix ユーザーを慰めている。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cpp's control() function dispatches preprocessor directives. # marks line start, identifier following is the directive name. Symbol table lookup matches directive name to pre-registered pointer (defloc, inclloc, ifloc, etc.). Pointer comparison (np == defloc) is O(1) — same pattern as m4's expand() and find's predicate dispatch. SALT='#' prefix on directive names prevents clash with user identifiers (a variable named 'define' won't conflict with #define). flslvl tracks #if nesting depth; when >0, lines are skipped (not emitted). Pattern: tokenize → identify directive → dispatch to do*() handler — used by every preprocessor since.
ai_analysis.human_story: When cpp sees a line starting with #, it tokenizes the next word, looks it up in symbol table, and matches against pre-registered directive pointers (defloc for #define, inclloc for #include, etc.). Pointer compare → call handler. 9 directives in 1978: define, undef, include, if, ifdef, ifndef, else, endif, line. Modern cpp added #elif, #pragma, #error, #warning. But the dispatch pattern from 1978 is unchanged — every preprocessor since (GCC, clang, MSVC) does the same.

appraiser_note: cpp の control() 関数がプリプロセッサディレクティブを dispatch。 # で行先頭、 続く識別子がディレクティブ名。 シンボルテーブル lookup でディレクティブ名を事前登録済みポインタ (defloc、 inclloc、 ifloc 等) と照合。 ポインタ比較 (np == defloc) は **O(1)** ——m4 の expand() と find の述語 dispatch と同じパターン。 SALT='#' 接頭辞でディレクティブ名がユーザー識別子と衝突しない (`define` という変数と `#define` が分離)。 flslvl で #if 入れ子深さ追跡; >0 のとき行を skip。 パターン: トークン化 → ディレクティブ識別 → do*() ハンドラ dispatch ——以降のすべてのプリプロセッサ (GCC、 clang、 MSVC) で同じ。 9 ディレクティブ (define/undef/include/if/ifdef/ifndef/else/endif/line)、 現代では elif/pragma/error/warning が追加。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cpp supports both object-like (#define MAX 100) and function-like (#define SQR(x) ((x)*(x))) macros. dodef parses macro name, then optionally formal parameter list in parens, then accumulates body. In body, identifier tokens matching formals get replaced with a special encoding: <param_index> <WARN_byte>. WARN is a marker byte (0x80-ish) never seen in normal text. At expansion time (subst), encountering WARN means 'next byte is parameter index, substitute with actual argument'. MAXFRM caps parameter count. Redefinition with same body is silent; different body triggers warning. This compact encoding (1 byte for param ref) makes macros memory-efficient. m4 (#157) uses similar param substitution; cpp's WARN trick is its specific implementation.
ai_analysis.human_story: #define SQR(x) ((x)*(x)) — how is this stored? cpp parses SQR as the name, x as the formal parameter, then scans the body. When it sees 'x' (identifier matching formal), it doesn't store 'x' — it stores two bytes: parameter_index=1 + WARN_marker. The body becomes essentially '((' + <1,WARN> + ')*(' + <1,WARN> + '))'. At expansion: replace each <N,WARN> pair with Nth actual argument. Compact, fast, elegant. m4 had similar tricks earlier; cpp's WARN encoding is the C version. GCC/clang use richer AST-based representations today but the substitution-by-position concept is unchanged.

appraiser_note: cpp は object-like (#define MAX 100) と function-like (#define SQR(x) ((x)*(x))) の両方をサポート。 dodef がマクロ名をパース、 オプションの仮引数リストを括弧内で、 そして本体を蓄積。 本体内で **仮引数と一致する識別子トークン**は特殊エンコーディング: `<param_index> <WARN_byte>` に置換。 WARN は通常テキストに現れないマーカーバイト (~0x80)。 展開時 (subst) に WARN を検出すると「次のバイトが仮引数 index、 実引数で置換」 と解釈。 MAXFRM で仮引数数上限。 同じ本体の再定義はサイレント、 違う本体は warning。 **コンパクトエンコーディング** (1 バイトでパラメータ参照) でメモリ効率良い。 m4 (#157) も類似 param substitution、 cpp の WARN トリックはその C 版。 GCC/clang は現代では AST ベースのリッチ表現を使うが、 位置ベース置換の概念は不変。

ai_analysis.technical_context: #include search algorithm: "foo.h" first tries current directory, then Inclist[] paths; <foo.h> tries Inclist[] only. Inclist defaults to /usr/include, augmented by cc -I/path/. File stack: ifno tracks current depth, incfile[ifno] = fd, lineno[ifno] = line counter. #include pushes onto stack; EOF pops back. The 2-form distinction ("" vs <>) separates 'my project headers' from 'system headers' — convention preserved in C/C++ today. Include guards (#ifndef HEADER_X / #define HEADER_X / #endif) prevent infinite recursion and duplicate definitions; cpp doesn't enforce them, programmer convention. Modern compilers added #pragma once (non-standard but widely supported) as alternative.
ai_analysis.human_story: Type `#include <stdio.h>` — cpp searches /usr/include/stdio.h. `#include "local.h"` — try current dir first, then /usr/include. The angle-bracket vs quote distinction is convention: <> for system, "" for project-local. -I flag adds to search path. cpp pushes the included file onto a stack, switches input. EOF pops back to caller. Header guards (#ifndef X / #define X / #endif) prevent multiple inclusion. All this from 1978's doincl(). Modern C/C++ added #pragma once but most projects still use the classic ifndef pattern. The 'two angle styles' is one of the most enduring C conventions.

appraiser_note: #include 検索アルゴリズム: `"foo.h"` はまずカレントディレクトリ、 次に Inclist[] パス; `<foo.h>` は Inclist[] のみ。 Inclist デフォルトは /usr/include、 cc -I/path/ で追加。 **ファイルスタック**: ifno で現在の深さ追跡、 incfile[ifno] = fd、 lineno[ifno] = 行カウンタ。 #include で push、 EOF で pop。 **2 形式の区別** ("" vs <>) で「プロジェクトヘッダ」 と「システムヘッダ」 を分離——C/C++ で現代まで継承される慣習。 **インクルードガード** (#ifndef HEADER_X / #define HEADER_X / #endif) で無限再帰と重複定義防止——cpp は強制しない、 プログラマ慣習。 現代コンパイラは #pragma once を代替として追加 (非標準だが広くサポート)。 「2 形式の山括弧 vs 引用符」 は最も長く続く C 慣習の 1 つ。

ai_analysis.technical_context: scw1 ('superimposed code, word 1'): macbit[c] is an 8-bit array indexed by character; bit N is set if character c appears at position N of any defined macro name. When checking an identifier: walk chars, AND with current position-bit, if zero → impossible to be a macro → skip symbol table lookup. False positives possible (the char/position combo exists in some macro), but no false negatives. This is a proto-Bloom filter (Burton Bloom, 1970), but predates widespread Bloom filter usage. The technique comes from 1960s information retrieval (Knuth TAOCP Vol. 3). With 80-90% of identifiers in C code not being macros (int, char, printf, local vars), this optimization saves 80-90% of symtab lookups. Modern preprocessors (GCC, clang) use perfect hashing or similar techniques; the principle of 'cheap filter before expensive check' is universal.
ai_analysis.human_story: Most identifiers in C code aren't macros — int, char, printf, local variables. Looking them up in the symbol table each time wastes effort. 1978's John F. Reiser solved it with superimposed code: a tiny bit-array (macbit[256]) where bit N is set if some macro name has the corresponding character at position N. Walk an identifier's characters, AND with position bits — if any character fails the test, can't be a macro, skip lookup. 80-90% of identifiers eliminated this way. This is a proto-Bloom filter (Bloom published 1970, but Reiser likely got it from Knuth TAOCP). Modern preprocessors use perfect hashing but the 'cheap filter before expensive check' principle is the same.

appraiser_note: **scw1 (superimposed code、 word 1)**: macbit[c] は文字でインデックスされた 8 ビット配列; ビット N は文字 c がいずれかの定義済みマクロ名の位置 N に現れる場合に立つ。 識別子チェック時: 文字を走査、 現在の位置ビットと AND、 0 なら **マクロ不可能 → symbol table lookup スキップ**。 false positive は可能 (この文字/位置の組み合わせがマクロにある)、 false negative なし。 これは **proto-Bloom filter** (Burton Bloom 1970)、 Bloom filter 普及前。 1960 年代の情報検索分野の技術 (Knuth TAOCP Vol. 3 由来)。 C コード内識別子の **80-90% がマクロではない** (int、 char、 printf、 ローカル変数)——この最適化で 80-90% の symtab lookup を節約。 現代プリプロセッサ (GCC、 clang) は perfect hashing 等を使用するが、 「**安価なフィルタ + 高価なチェック**」 の原則は普遍。 1978 年 Reiser のアイデアが現代コンパイラ性能を支える。

ai_analysis.technical_context: #if expression evaluator is 31 lines of yacc grammar (cpy.y). Supports full C integer expressions: arithmetic (+-*/%), bitwise (&|^~<<>>), comparison (==!=<><=>=), logical (&&||!), unary (-+!~), parens. Operator precedence cascade mirrors C's exactly. defined(MACRO) handled via preprocessor expansion to 1/0 before reaching grammar. No variables/functions — only compile-time integer constant expressions. The 31-line grammar shows yacc's expressiveness: C integer semantics in trivial code. cpp's main can call yyparse() to evaluate #if conditions. This pattern (yacc for evaluating arithmetic-like sub-language) appears in m4y.y, expr.y, awk.g.y — Unix's love of yacc-driven mini-languages.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does cpp evaluate `#if (VERSION >= 200) && defined(LINUX)`? 31 lines of yacc. Standard arithmetic, comparison, logical operators in yacc productions. defined() is handled by cpp before parsing (replaced with 1 or 0). The yacc grammar evaluates the resulting integer expression. Same precedence and semantics as C itself. Compile-time only — no variables, no function calls. The minimalism is beautiful: full integer expression evaluation in 31 yacc lines. Compare to writing a recursive-descent parser by hand (hundreds of lines). yacc lets you express the grammar declaratively and get the parser for free.

appraiser_note: #if 式評価器は **31 行の yacc 文法** (cpy.y)。 完全な C 整数式をサポート: 算術 (+-*/%)、 ビット (&|^~<<>>)、 比較 (==!=<><=>=)、 論理 (&&||!)、 単項 (-+!~)、 括弧。 演算子優先順位カスケードは C と完全一致。 defined(MACRO) はプリプロセッサ展開で 1/0 に置換してから文法に到達。 変数/関数なし——コンパイル時整数定数式のみ。 31 行の文法が **yacc の表現力**を示す: C 整数セマンティクスを単純なコードで。 cpp の main が yyparse() を呼んで #if 条件を評価。 このパターン (算術-like サブ言語の yacc 評価) は m4y.y、 expr.y、 awk.g.y で再現——**Unix の yacc 駆動ミニ言語愛**。 手書き再帰下降パーサと比べて (数百行)、 yacc は文法を宣言的に表現してパーサを無料で得られる。

ai_analysis.technical_context: od combines display formats via bitflag (conv): -o=001 (oct word), -d=002 (dec word), -x/-h=010 (hex word), -c=020 (char), -b=040 (byte oct). Multiple flags combine: -ox shows both octal AND hex. The line() function iterates bit positions (c<<=1), printing each enabled format on its own line, indented after the first. First format gets the offset prefix; subsequent ones get tab-aligned. max tracks widest format for column alignment via pre() padding. The bitflag-per-format pattern enables N-way display: -oxc shows 3 formats per data block, all aligned. Modern hexdump/xxd lost this flexibility (or required custom format strings) in favor of single-style output.
ai_analysis.human_story: od's killer feature: see your binary in multiple representations side-by-side. -ox prints octal AND hex of the same bytes. -xc shows hex on top, ASCII characters below. The bit-flag conv variable enables N formats simultaneously, iterated via shift. First format gets the offset; subsequent ones tab-indent for visual alignment. This let you cross-reference binary structures: 'this hex value is the ASCII character X, which in decimal is N'. Modern hexdump -C combined hex+ascii into one line, but od's 1979 multi-format-per-block display is still uniquely useful for tricky binary forensics.

appraiser_note: od は **ビットフラグ (conv) で表示フォーマット組み合わせ**: -o=001 (oct word)、 -d=002 (dec word)、 -x/-h=010 (hex word)、 -c=020 (char)、 -b=040 (byte oct)。 複数フラグ結合: **-ox で 8 進 AND 16 進を同時表示**。 line() 関数がビット位置を iterate (c<<=1)、 各有効フォーマットを 1 行ずつ印字、 最初以降はインデント。 最初のフォーマットがオフセット接頭辞を取得、 後続は tab で位置揃え。 max が最広フォーマット追跡、 pre() でカラム揃え。 ビットフラグごとパターンで N-way 表示: -oxc で同じデータブロックの 3 フォーマット表示、 全部揃う。 現代 hexdump/xxd はこの柔軟性を失い、 単一形式 (またはカスタム format string 要)。 1979 年の **multi-format-per-block 表示**はバイナリフォレンジックで今も唯一無二。

ai_analysis.technical_context: od +offset seeks before output. Base auto-detection: 'x' or '0x' prefix = hex, leading '0' = octal (default), trailing '.' = decimal, 'b' suffix = multiply by 512 (block size). Matches C numeric literal conventions plus Unix-specific extensions (. for explicit decimal, b for blocks). fseek(stdin, a, 0) works when stdin is redirected from file. addr=a sets the displayed offset starting point. The 'four ways to specify a number' UX is from 1979 Unix philosophy: let user write naturally, infer base from form. Modern utilities (dd, od) preserve this; new tools usually require explicit base flags. The 512-byte block unit reflects V7 BSIZE — disk sector size of the era.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you say 'skip to byte 256 of this file' in od? Four ways: +0x100 (hex), +400 (octal — leading 0 implied), +256. (decimal with dot), +1/2b (half-block? — well, +1b = 512). Same number, four forms. Plus base auto-detected from form. 1979 Unix preferred letting users write numbers in whatever base felt natural (octal was common, hex was emerging, decimal had to be explicit). The 'b' for blocks reminds us — V7 filesystem block was 512 bytes, dd used it, find -size used it. Block-aware numeric arguments were Unix DNA.

appraiser_note: od は **+offset でシーク**、 出力前にファイル位置移動。 **基数自動判定**: `x` や `0x` 接頭辞 = 16 進、 先頭 `0` = 8 進 (デフォルト)、 末尾 `.` = 10 進、 `b` 接尾辞 = 512 倍 (block size)。 C 数値リテラル規約 + Unix 固有拡張 (明示 10 進の `.`、 ブロック単位の `b`)。 fseek(stdin, a, 0) はリダイレクトされた stdin で動作。 addr=a で表示オフセット開始点設定。 「**1 つの数値を 4 通りで指定**」 UX は 1979 Unix 哲学から——ユーザーが自然に書き、 形から基数を推測。 現代 (dd、 od) でも継承、 新しいツールは通常明示基数フラグを要求。 512 バイトブロック単位は V7 BSIZE 反映——時代のディスクセクタサイズ。 dd の bs=、 find -size でも同じ b suffix。

ai_analysis.technical_context: od collapses consecutive identical lines to a single '*' marker. State machine with 3 states via 'same' variable: -1=initial, 0=just printed line, 1=in dedup mode. Compares 8-word buffer (16 bytes) with lastword[]; if identical, suppress output (after first '*'); if different, restart normal printing. Critical for binary files with large zero-filled regions (BSS sections, sparse files, /dev/zero). Without dedup, dumping a 10MB sparse file would scroll forever. The '*' marker is unambiguous (* is not a valid hex/oct character at line start) and visually obvious. Modern hexdump/xxd inherit this behavior (xxd outputs '*' too).
ai_analysis.human_story: Dump /dev/zero and you'd expect infinite scroll of '000000 ...'. Instead od shows one line, then '*', then nothing more (until pattern changes). 1979 designers anticipated long runs of identical data — BSS sections, sparse files, zero-padded structures. State machine: track 'same as previous' via lastword[] comparison. When same, print '*' once and stop printing duplicates. When different, resume normal output. Saves the user from infinite scrolling. xxd and hexdump still do this exactly. The '*' is so universally understood in binary dumps that it's almost invisible to experienced users.

appraiser_note: od は **連続同一行を `*` 1 つに省略**。 `same` 変数で 3 状態ステートマシン: -1=初期、 0=直前 line 印字、 1=dedup 中。 8 word バッファ (16 バイト) を lastword[] と比較; 同一なら出力抑制 (最初の `*` 後)、 異なれば通常印字再開。 **BSS セクション、 sparse file、 /dev/zero のような巨大ゼロ領域**を持つバイナリで決定的に重要。 dedup なしだと 10MB sparse file のダンプが無限スクロール。 `*` マーカーは曖昧性なし (行頭の `*` は有効な hex/oct 文字でない)、 視覚的に明白。 現代 hexdump/xxd はこの挙動を継承 (xxd も `*` 出力)。 1979 年の設計者が長い同一データの run (BSS、 sparse、 zero-padded 構造体) を予期して、 state machine で「**同じ**」 を追跡。 経験豊富なユーザーには `*` は当たり前すぎて意識しない。

ai_analysis.technical_context: putn prints n in base b with exactly c digits (zero-padded). Recursive: prints higher digits first by recursing on n/b, c-1. Base case: c==0 returns. Digit conversion: 0-9 -> '0'-'9', 10-35 -> 'a'-'z' (covers bases up to 36). Recursion depth = c (max digit count). The 8-line function handles all of od's number output: offsets, octal, decimal, hex. Compare to iterative version that needs a buffer and reversal — recursive elegance wins for fixed digit count. Stack usage minimal (16 levels for 16-digit hex). The same recursive printing pattern appears in many K&R-era programs; modern code often uses sprintf with format strings, losing the educational clarity.
ai_analysis.human_story: Print 255 in 4-digit hex: '00ff'. How? 8 lines of recursion. putn(n/b, b, c-1) recursively prints higher digits first; then we print the current digit. Recursion bottom: c=0 returns without printing. The 0-9 vs a-z conversion is one if-else. Same function prints octal, decimal, hex — base is a parameter. Iterative version needs a buffer (collect digits backward, reverse at end); recursive uses the call stack as implicit buffer. K&R-era programs frequently used this idiom; today's sprintf-with-format-strings is more flexible but less educational. The 8-line putn is a masterclass in recursive elegance.

appraiser_note: putn は n を基数 b で正確に c 桁 (zero-padded) 印字。 **再帰**: putn(n/b, b, c-1) で上位桁から先に再帰、 そして現在桁を印字。 基底ケース: c==0 で return。 桁変換: 0-9 → '0'-'9'、 10-35 → 'a'-'z' (基数 36 までカバー)。 8 行関数が od の全数値出力を処理: オフセット、 8 進、 10 進、 16 進。 反復版はバッファ + 逆順必要——再帰の優雅さが固定桁数では勝つ。 スタック使用は最小 (16 桁 16 進で 16 階層)。 同じ再帰印字パターンは K&R 時代のプログラムで頻繁、 現代は format string 付き sprintf で柔軟だが教育的明快さを失う。 8 行の putn は **再帰の優雅さの教科書例**。

ai_analysis.technical_context: od reads 8 unsigned shorts = 16 bytes per iteration. Standard line layout: 7-digit octal offset + 8 6-digit octal words = 79-column line (fits 80-col terminal). 16-byte width chosen for: (1) VAX cache line of era, (2) 80-col terminal fitting, (3) power-of-2 binary friendliness, (4) human visual chunk size. Final line: printout total file size as last offset. This 16-byte convention propagated to hexdump, xxd, GNU od, and basically every binary dump tool since. Even GUI hex editors (HxD, Bless, Hex Fiend) default to 16 bytes per row. The choice from 1979 became universal because it works at every level — display, machine, human.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why are binary dumps always 16 bytes wide? Because od said so in 1979. 16 bytes = 8 shorts = fits in 80-column terminal + offset = comfortable visual chunk size. Modern hexdump, xxd, every GUI hex editor (HxD, Bless, Hex Fiend) defaults to 16-byte rows. A choice driven by 1979 terminal width + cache-line ergonomics that became universal. The final line shows the file's total size as the closing offset — a free byte-count for the user. 50 years later, every binary dump has the same shape because of this one decision.

appraiser_note: od は反復ごとに **8 unsigned short = 16 バイト**読む。 標準行レイアウト: 7 桁 8 進オフセット + 8 × 6 桁 8 進ワード = 79 桁 (80 桁端末に収まる)。 16 バイト幅の選択理由: (1) 時代の VAX キャッシュライン、 (2) 80 桁端末収容、 (3) 2 の累乗で binary friendly、 (4) 人間の視覚的 chunk サイズ。 最終行: ファイル総サイズを最終オフセットとして印字——ユーザーへの無料の byte カウント。 この **16 バイト規約**が hexdump、 xxd、 GNU od、 以降の全バイナリダンプツールに伝播。 GUI hex editor (HxD、 Bless、 Hex Fiend) でも 1 行 16 バイトがデフォルト。 1979 年の選択が普遍化したのは、 **表示・マシン・人間の全レベルで機能する**から。 50 年後の全バイナリダンプが同じ形状なのは、 この 1 つの決定のおかげ。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tar header is 512 bytes, union of dummy[512] byte array and struct header. Header fields: name[100], mode/uid/gid/size/mtime/chksum (ASCII octal strings), linkflag (1 byte), linkname[100]. Total 257 bytes; remaining 255 bytes are zero-padded. ASCII octal for numbers (not binary) for endian-independence + human readability. File contents follow header, padded to 512-byte boundary. Modern ustar (POSIX 1003.1-1988) adds prefix[155] for longer paths (255 chars total) and magic field for format identification. GNU tar pax format effectively removes length limits. The union pattern (raw bytes / structured access) is K&R C idiom. Same 512-byte block size as V7 BSIZE, dd, ed tempfile. Half-century of tarballs all use this layout.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why is your linux-6.7.tar.xz internally structured the way it is? Because 1979's tar set the format: 512-byte header per file, ASCII octal numeric fields, 100-char name limit. The union hblock is K&R C trick: same 512 bytes accessed as raw bytes (for I/O) or structured header (for parsing). ASCII octal because endian-independent + human-readable: hexdump a tar file and you can read filenames and sizes. POSIX ustar extended name limit to 255 in 1988; pax format made it unlimited in 2001. But the basic header layout is unchanged. Docker images, Linux distros, npm packages — all use 1979's tar format inside their wrapper compression.

appraiser_note: tar ヘッダは **512 バイト**、 union hblock で dummy[512] バイト配列 と struct header の両方アクセス。 ヘッダフィールド: name[100]、 mode/uid/gid/size/mtime/chksum (ASCII 8 進文字列)、 linkflag (1 バイト)、 linkname[100]。 合計 257 バイト、 残り 255 バイトは zero-padded。 **ASCII 8 進数値** (バイナリではない) で endian 非依存 + 人間可読。 ファイル内容はヘッダの後、 512 バイト境界に padded。 現代 ustar (POSIX 1003.1-1988) で prefix[155] を追加して長いパス対応 (合計 255 文字)、 GNU tar pax 形式は事実上無制限。 union パターン (raw bytes / structured access) は K&R C イディオム。 V7 BSIZE、 dd、 ed tempfile と同じ 512 バイトブロック。 半世紀のすべての tarball がこのレイアウトを使用。 Docker image、 Linux distro、 npm package——全部内部で 1979 年の tar 形式。

ai_analysis.technical_context: putfile recursively traverses directory trees using chdir. For directories: open, read entries via struct direct (V7 16-byte dirent), skip . and .., recurse on each. After recursion returns, chdir('..') restores parent. Pattern matches find's descend(). The close(infile) before recursion saves fd count (V7 NOFILE=20); re-open '.' after to continue. lseek to next entry position. V7 struct direct (16 bytes fixed) lets sizeof(dbuf)*i compute position; 4.2BSD's variable struct dirent broke this trick, requiring readdir(3) API. SIGINT handler sets 'term' to allow Ctrl-C interruption mid-traversal. For files: tomodes() converts stat to ASCII octal header fields, checksum(), writetape() emits header then data in 512-byte chunks.
ai_analysis.human_story: tar cf foo.tar /usr — how does it find all files? putfile() recursively walks the tree. For each dir: chdir into it, read entries one by one, skip . and .., recurse into each. After recursion, chdir('..') to climb back. For each regular file: open it, fill the tar header from stat info, checksum, write header, write data in 512-byte chunks. The close-then-reopen-and-seek pattern saves file descriptors (V7 only had 20 per process). V7 struct direct's fixed 16-byte size let lseek compute next position arithmetically — a trick 4.2BSD broke when dirent went variable-length. Recursion depth = directory depth, fine for normal trees.

appraiser_note: putfile が chdir を使って **ディレクトリツリーを再帰下降**。 ディレクトリの場合: open、 struct direct (V7 16 バイト dirent) でエントリを 1 つずつ read、 . と .. を skip、 各エントリで再帰。 再帰 return 後、 chdir('..') で親に戻る。 パターンは find の descend() と同じ。 再帰前の close(infile) が fd 数節約 (V7 NOFILE=20)、 後で '.' を再 open して continue、 lseek で次のエントリ位置へ。 V7 struct direct (16 バイト固定) で sizeof(dbuf)*i で位置計算可能、 4.2BSD の可変 struct dirent でこのトリックが壊れ readdir(3) API 必要に。 SIGINT ハンドラで 'term' をセット、 Ctrl-C で走査中断可能。 通常ファイルの場合: tomodes() で stat を ASCII 8 進ヘッダフィールドに変換、 checksum()、 writetape() でヘッダ + 512 バイトチャンクのデータ。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tar preserves hard links to avoid storing duplicate data and to restore link relationships. Each file with st_nlink > 1 is checked against linked list of (inum, devnum) tuples. First encounter: archive normally + add to list with pathname. Subsequent encounter (same inode): write header with linkflag='1' and linkname=original path; no data follows. Restoration creates ln(2) link to original. Without this, hard-linked files would be stored multiple times and restored as separate files (breaking link relationships). The (inum, devnum) tuple uniquely identifies a Unix inode (devnum needed because inodes are per-filesystem). Linear search through linkbuf list — fine for thousands of files, slow for millions. Modern GNU tar uses hash tables for O(1) lookup.
ai_analysis.human_story: ln /usr/bin/ar /usr/bin/ranlib — two paths, one inode, one set of bytes. If tar archived them as two separate files, you'd waste space and lose the link relationship on restore. tar's clever: track (inode, device) tuples in a linked list. First encounter: archive normally + remember. Second encounter (same inode): write a link reference, no data. On restore, ln(2) creates the hard link from the second path to the first.
