Saves space, preserves semantics. This works because Unix's file = (inode, device) — same data has one canonical location, multiple paths. 50 years of tar preserve this perfectly.

appraiser_note: tar は **ハードリンクを保存**——重複データを避け、 リンク関係を復元可能に。 st_nlink > 1 の各ファイルを (inum, devnum) タプルの連結リストと照合。 初回遭遇: 通常 archive + リスト追加 (pathname も)。 後続遭遇 (同 inode): linkflag='1' + linkname=元パス のヘッダを書く、 データ無し。 復元時に ln(2) で元へのリンク作成。 これなしだとハードリンクファイルは複数回保存され、 別ファイルとして復元される (リンク関係破壊)。 (inum, devnum) タプルが Unix inode を一意識別 (inode はファイルシステム毎なので devnum 必要)。 linkbuf リストの線形検索——数千ファイルは問題ない、 数百万で遅い。 現代 GNU tar はハッシュテーブルで O(1) lookup。 ln /usr/bin/ar /usr/bin/ranlib のような典型例で機能。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Header checksum = sum of all 512 header bytes. Self-reference problem: chksum field is part of the header. Convention: fill chksum with 8 ASCII spaces (0x20 × 8 = 256) during calculation, then sprintf overwrites with octal sum. Reader pads with spaces too before verifying. Simple byte sum (not CRC) — weak error detection (bit-flip pairs cancel) but adequate for occasional tape errors. 6-digit octal = max 262143 = covers 512 × 255 = 130560 (max possible sum). Modern archive formats (zip, gzip) use CRC-32 for stronger error detection; tar's checksum is more about format identification than reliability. Failed checksum signals 'this might not be a tar archive' more than 'data is corrupt'.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does tar know an archive isn't corrupt? Header checksum: simple byte sum of the 512-byte header. Self-reference solved by writing spaces in chksum field during calculation, sprintf overwriting after. Reader does the same: zero-out chksum during recalc, compare to stored value. Weak by modern standards (CRC-32 catches more), but enough for tape errors. Also serves as format identifier — if you cat a non-tar file expecting tar, checksum fails immediately and you stop. The convention-based self-reference (spaces during calc) was elegant 1979 problem-solving; modern formats put checksums in a separate frame to avoid the issue.

appraiser_note: ヘッダチェックサム = 512 バイトヘッダ全部の **バイト和**。 自己参照問題: chksum フィールドがヘッダの一部。 **規約**: 計算時に chksum を 8 個の ASCII スペース (0x20 × 8 = 256) で埋める、 sprintf で 8 進和を書き込む。 読み手も検証前にスペースで埋める。 **単純バイト和** (CRC ではない)、 弱いエラー検出 (ペアビット反転は cancel) だが磁気テープエラーには十分。 6 桁 8 進 = 最大 262143 = 512 × 255 = 130560 (最大可能和) をカバー。 現代アーカイブ形式 (zip、 gzip) は **CRC-32** でより強力なエラー検出、 tar のチェックサムは信頼性より形式識別の意味合いが大きい。 チェックサム失敗は「tar アーカイブでないかも」 を示唆。 1979 年の規約ベース自己参照解決 (計算時スペース) はエレガント、 現代形式はチェックサムを別フレームに置いて問題を回避。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tar's default target is /dev/mt1 (magnetic tape unit 1) — hence the name 'Tape ARchive'. 1970s Unix backed up to 9-track magnetic tape: 2400-foot reels holding ~175 MB at 1600 BPI. Tapes were sequential-only, no random access. magtape[7] mutation trick changes '1' to '0' for /dev/mt0. -f flag overrides to use a regular file. nblock (1-20) sets blocking factor: write N×512 bytes per I/O for better tape utilization (fewer inter-record gaps). By 1990s, tapes were replaced by disk-based backup, but tar format stayed: .tar.Z (1980s compress), .tar.gz (1990s gzip), .tar.bz2/.tar.xz/.tar.zst (later). Docker image layers, npm packages, Linux kernel sources — all use tar inside compression wrapper. The 'Tape' in tar is vestigial; 'ARchive' is universal.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why is the format called 'tar'? Tape ARchive. 1979's default target was /dev/mt1, a magnetic tape unit. 9-track reels held ~175 MB; tar wrote files sequentially. By 1990 mag tape was retreating, but .tar files stayed: pipe through compress (.tar.Z), then gzip (.tar.gz), then bzip2/xz/zstd. Today's Docker image layers are tar+gzip. The 'Tape' is gone (most users never touched mag tape), but 'ARchive' is universal. The magtape[7]='0' or '1' trick is from when /dev/mt0 and /dev/mt1 were the two main tape drives. The blocking factor (-b) optimized tape throughput by reducing inter-record gaps. All vestigial in modern tar, but the syntax persists for backward compatibility.

appraiser_note: tar のデフォルトターゲットは **/dev/mt1** (magnetic tape unit 1) ——名前の由来「Tape ARchive」。 1970 年代 Unix は **9-track 磁気テープ**にバックアップ: 2400 フィートリール、 1600 BPI で ~175 MB。 テープは sequential、 random access 不可。 magtape[7] mutation トリックで '1' を '0' に変えて /dev/mt0 へ。 -f フラグでファイル指定。 nblock (1-20) で blocking factor 設定: テープ効率良くするため N×512 バイト/IO (inter-record gap 削減)。 1990 年代にテープがディスクバックアップに置換、 でも tar 形式は残存: .tar.Z (1980s compress)、 .tar.gz (1990s gzip)、 .tar.bz2/.tar.xz/.tar.zst。 Docker image layer、 npm package、 Linux kernel ソース——全部圧縮ラッパー内で tar。 「Tape」 は痕跡的 (現代ユーザーは触れない)、 「ARchive」 は普遍。 magtape[7] のトリックは /dev/mt0 と /dev/mt1 が主要 2 テープドライブだった時代の名残。 後方互換性のため構文は残存。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cpio binary header: 26-byte fixed struct + variable filename. Magic 070707 octal (= 0x71C7) identifies the format. All fields are 16-bit shorts: dev, ino, mode, uid, gid, nlink, rdev; mtime and filesize as short[2] pairs (= 32-bit long). Filename up to 256 bytes, NUL-terminated. Contrast with tar's 512-byte ASCII-octal header — cpio's binary is compact (26 vs ~257 bytes header overhead) and faster to parse (direct memory access vs atoi conversions). But binary format is endian-dependent and not human-readable. Modern 'newc' cpio format (SVR4, 1990s) switched to 110-byte ASCII hex header with magic '070701' or '070702', solving both endian and readability issues while preserving the cpio model.
ai_analysis.human_story: 070707 octal — the cpio magic number, instantly recognizable in hex dumps as 0x71C7. cpio chose binary headers (26 bytes, machine-fast) over tar's ASCII octal (257 bytes, human-readable). Trade-offs: cpio is compact and parse-cheap, but endian-sensitive and opaque to `od`. The 'newc' format (SVR4) fixed both by going ASCII hex. Linux initramfs uses cpio (`.cpio.gz`) — kernel parsers prefer binary's simplicity. RPM packages contain cpio archives internally. 1979 cpio binary is the format that hides in modern Unix infrastructure.

appraiser_note: cpio バイナリヘッダ: 26 バイト固定 struct + 可変長ファイル名。 **MAGIC 070707 8 進** (= 0x71C7) で形式識別。 全フィールド 16 ビット short: dev、 ino、 mode、 uid、 gid、 nlink、 rdev; mtime と filesize は short[2] ペア (= 32 ビット long)。 ファイル名は 256 バイトまで、 NUL 終端。 tar の 512 バイト ASCII 8 進ヘッダと対照: cpio バイナリはコンパクト (26 vs ~257 バイトオーバーヘッド)、 パース高速 (直接メモリアクセス vs atoi 変換)。 だがバイナリ形式は **エンディアン依存** + 人間非読。 現代の 'newc' cpio 形式 (SVR4、 1990s) は 110 バイト ASCII 16 進ヘッダ + magic '070701' '070702' に切替、 両問題を解決しつつ cpio モデルを保持。 Linux initramfs は cpio (`.cpio.gz`) ——カーネルパーサがバイナリの単純さを好む。 RPM 内部にも cpio。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cpio's three-mode design: -o (out, write archive), -i (in, extract archive), -p (pass-through, copy to directory). Filename source: stdin (one per line). This is Unix pipe philosophy embodied — cpio doesn't walk directories itself, it accepts a filename stream. find + cpio pipeline: find generates filenames, cpio archives them. Contrast tar which walks internally. Composability: filter filenames via grep/awk before cpio: `find /usr | grep '\.c$' | cpio -o`. Default I/O is stdin/stdout, no /dev/mt1 hardcoding (though tape works via redirect). The mode-driven design (direction of data flow) vs tar's action-driven (verbs) reflects two schools of CLI design. AT&T System V adopted cpio; BSD adopted tar.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why are there two Unix archive formats? Different design philosophies. tar: 'walk dirs, write archive' — monolithic. cpio: 'read filenames from stdin, write headers and data' — composable. `find /usr | cpio -o` puts find in charge of selection, cpio in charge of format. Want only .c files? `find /usr -name '*.c' | cpio -o`. Want from a list? `cat manifest.txt | cpio -o`. The pipe philosophy made cpio more flexible at the cost of being two commands instead of one. AT&T System V loved it (cpio is canonical); BSD loved tar. Linux supports both. RPM uses cpio internally. Linux initramfs uses cpio. The 'one program, one job' Unix philosophy made cpio possible — but it required users to learn pipes.

appraiser_note: cpio の **3 モード設計**: -o (out、 アーカイブ書込)、 -i (in、 展開)、 -p (pass-through、 ディレクトリへコピー)。 ファイル名ソース: **stdin (1 行 1 ファイル名)**。 これが **Unix パイプ哲学の体現**——cpio はディレクトリを自分で walk せず、 ファイル名ストリームを受け取る。 find + cpio パイプライン: find がファイル名生成、 cpio が archive。 内部走査の tar と対照。 **合成性**: grep/awk でファイル名フィルタ可能: `find /usr | grep '\.c$' | cpio -o`。 デフォルト I/O は stdin/stdout、 /dev/mt1 ハードコーディングなし (リダイレクトでテープも可)。 **モード駆動** (データフロー方向) vs tar の動詞駆動——2 つの CLI 設計流派。 AT&T System V が cpio 採用、 BSD が tar 採用。 Linux 両対応、 initramfs が cpio、 RPM 内部も cpio。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cpio -p mode (pass-through) does directory tree copy without intermediate archive. Reads filenames from stdin, copies each to destination dir preserving full metadata: uid, gid, mode, mtime (with -m), hard links (with -l), special files, symlinks. Effectively cp -a from 1979 (cp -a came much later). Used heavily for: chroot environment construction, system migration, large dir tree backup, when rsync isn't available. Flags: -d (create dirs), -m (mtime), -u (unconditional overwrite), -l (hard link). The `find ... -depth | cpio -pdmu /dest` idiom appears in countless Unix sysadmin scripts predating rsync. Modern rsync surpasses it but cpio -p still works in busybox/embedded.
ai_analysis.human_story: cp -r didn't exist in 1979 (or was weak). cpio -p filled the gap: `find . -depth | cpio -pdmu /backup` copies an entire tree preserving everything — owner, perms, mtime, hard links, symlinks, special files. cp -a equivalent, 15+ years before cp got -a. Sysadmins memorized 'pdmu' as the magic flags: pass-through, dirs, mtime, unconditional. Until rsync (1996), cpio -p was the way to clone directory trees on Unix. Today rsync is better (network, deltas, compression), but cpio -p still works in initramfs, busybox, and minimal environments. 1979 versatility: find + cpio = arbitrary tree copy.

appraiser_note: cpio -p モード (pass-through) はディレクトリツリーコピーを中間アーカイブなしで実行。 stdin からファイル名を読み、 各ファイルを destination dir にコピー、 **フルメタデータ保持**: uid、 gid、 mode、 mtime (-m)、 ハードリンク (-l)、 特殊ファイル、 シンボリックリンク。 事実上の **1979 年版 cp -a** (cp -a は後年)。 用途: chroot 環境構築、 システム移行、 大規模ディレクトリツリーバックアップ (rsync 不在時)。 フラグ: -d (ディレクトリ作成)、 -m (mtime 保持)、 -u (無条件上書き)、 -l (ハードリンク)。 **`find ... -depth | cpio -pdmu /dest` イディオム**は rsync 以前の数え切れない sysadmin スクリプトに登場。 1996 年 rsync で凌駕されたが、 cpio -p は initramfs/busybox/最小環境で今も生きる。 1979 年の汎用性: find + cpio = 任意ツリーコピー。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cpio's headers use short[2] pairs for 32-bit values (mtime, filesize). The byte ordering of those pairs differs by architecture: VAX (little-endian) stores low-short first; PDP-11 (PDP-endian, mixed) is different; SUN/MC68k (big-endian) stores high-short first. mklong/MKSHORT detect endianness at runtime via union trick: U.l=1, if U.c[0]==1 then little-endian. Then convert short[] pair to long with appropriate swap. This allows cpio archives to be readable across architectures — critical when tapes traveled between heterogeneous Unix systems in the 1980s. tar avoids this by using ASCII octal numbers (always portable). cpio's binary advantage came with this complexity cost. SVR4 'newc' cpio format dropped binary in favor of ASCII hex, eliminating endian issues while retaining cpio's pipe-friendly design.
ai_analysis.human_story: VAX, PDP-11, SUN — three architectures, three endiannesses, one cpio archive. 1980s Unix shops had mixed CPU fleets; a tape made on VAX needed to read on SUN. cpio's solution: runtime endian detection (union trick: U.l=1, check U.c[0]), then swap as needed. mklong reads short[2] pair correctly regardless of writer's architecture. tar avoided this with ASCII octal — slower to parse but always portable. cpio's binary headers paid a complexity tax for compactness. SVR4 'newc' format later switched to ASCII hex, getting best of both worlds. The runtime endian detect via union is now obsolete (compilers do __builtin_bswap, hardware has ntohl/htonl), but in 1979 this was the elegant solution.

appraiser_note: cpio ヘッダは **short[2] ペア**で 32 ビット値 (mtime、 filesize) を表現。 バイトオーダーがアーキ毎に違う: VAX (little-endian) は low-short が先、 PDP-11 (PDP-endian、 mixed) は独特、 SUN/MC68k (big-endian) は high-short が先。 **mklong/MKSHORT が runtime でエンディアン検出**: union トリック (U.l=1、 U.c[0]==1 なら little-endian)。 short[] ペアを long に適切なスワップで変換。 これで cpio アーカイブが **アーキ間で読める**——1980 年代に混在 Unix システム間でテープが移動する時に決定的。 tar は ASCII 8 進数で回避 (パース遅いが常に portable)。 cpio バイナリヘッダの利点は複雑性のコスト。 SVR4 'newc' 形式は後に ASCII 16 進に切替、 両世界の最良を得て endian 問題消失。 union によるランタイム endian 検出は現代では旧式 (コンパイラ __builtin_bswap、 ハードウェア ntohl/htonl) だが、 1979 年にはエレガントな解。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tar vs cpio: two archive formats from same Bell Labs source tree, diverging by philosophy. tar is monolithic (walks dirs, writes archive, all in one command); cpio is modular (accepts filenames from stdin, writes archive — pairs with find for traversal). AT&T System V standardized on cpio; BSD on tar. Linux supports both; tar dominates user-facing use; cpio dominates system internals (initramfs, RPM). The cpio philosophy aligns with Unix pipe ideal ('one program, one job') at cost of two-command verbosity. tar's mono-design wins for end-users. Modern descendants: rsync supersedes cpio -p for sync, pax is POSIX unification, tar.gz/tar.xz/tar.zst dominate distribution, cpio still owns initramfs and RPM.
ai_analysis.human_story: Same source tree, same year, two archive formats. tar said 'I'll handle everything — walking, archiving, restoring'. cpio said 'I'll handle archiving; let find do the walking'. tar won the end-user war (one command vs two), but cpio won specific niches (Linux initramfs, RPM packages, busybox systems). The split is Unix philosophy in action: should a tool do everything (tar) or compose (cpio)? Both worked. Both survive. tar's simplicity won users; cpio's composability won system designers. 50 years later, you tar.gz your source release but boot from cpio.gz.

appraiser_note: tar vs cpio: 同じ Bell Labs ソースツリーから 2 つのアーカイブ形式、 哲学で分岐。 **tar はモノリシック** (ディレクトリ走査 + archive 書込 + 復元、 全て 1 コマンド)、 **cpio はモジュラー** (stdin からファイル名受取 + archive 書込——走査は find とペア)。 **AT&T System V は cpio 標準化**、 **BSD は tar 標準化**。 Linux 両対応、 tar が end-user 向けで支配、 cpio がシステム内部で支配 (initramfs、 RPM)。 cpio 哲学は **Unix パイプ理想** (「one program, one job」) に沿う、 2 コマンドの冗長性が代償。 tar のモノ設計が end-user 勝利。 現代の派生: rsync が cpio -p を sync で超克、 pax が POSIX 統一、 tar.gz/.xz/.zst が配布支配、 cpio が initramfs と RPM を所有。 「**ソースリリースは tar.gz、 ブートは cpio.gz**」 ——50 年後も両方生きる。

ai_analysis.technical_context: diff's stone() computes Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) between two files using Hunt-McIlroy variant of Harold Stone's algorithm. Each step walks file0 line by line, finds equivalence class in file1, runs binary search through current k-candidates (best-match-per-length list), updates or extends. Output: vector J[] where J[i] = matching line in file1 (0 if no match). Complexity: O((R+N) log N) where R = matching pairs, N = file size. Improves on naive DP O(NM) significantly when files are similar (R is small). Doug McIlroy implemented it; algorithm from James Hunt & Tom Szymanski (CACM 1977). Foundational for all later diff tools — git, mercurial, svn use evolved variants (Myers diff 1986, patience diff, histogram diff) but the core 'find LCS, show non-LCS as changes' paradigm is from this 1976 work.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does diff know what changed between two files? It finds the longest sequence of lines they have in common (LCS), then everything not in that LCS is a change. The naive way is O(NM) — too slow for big files in 1976's 256KB RAM. Hunt and McIlroy found a way: group lines into equivalence classes via hash, walk file0, maintain a list of 'k-candidates' (best match for each subsequence length), binary search to update. Result: O((R+N) log N). McIlroy's diff (1976) was the first practical implementation. git diff today uses Myers' improved algorithm (1986), but the concept — LCS = common, rest = diff — is from this 60-line stone() function. Every code review, every pull request, every merge depends on this idea.

appraiser_note: diff の stone() が 2 ファイル間の **LCS (Longest Common Subsequence)** を Hunt-McIlroy 変種 (Harold Stone アルゴリズム) で計算。 file0 を行ごとに走査、 file1 内の等価類を見つけ、 現在の k-candidate (各長さの最良マッチリスト) に binary search で更新/拡張。 出力: ベクトル J[] で J[i] = file1 のマッチ行 (0 なら不一致)。 計算量 O((R+N) log N)、 R = マッチペア数、 N = ファイル長。 素朴な DP O(NM) より大幅に高速 (file が似ていれば R は小さい)。 Doug McIlroy が実装、 アルゴリズムは James Hunt & Tom Szymanski (CACM 1977) から。 **後の全 diff ツール (git/hg/svn) の基盤**——Myers diff (1986)、 patience、 histogram は進化形だが、 「LCS = 共通、 残り = 差分」 の概念は 1976 年から不変。 256KB RAM の PDP-11 で大ファイル diff を可能にした歴史的アルゴリズム。

ai_analysis.technical_context: diff's memory efficiency comes from hashing: each line becomes a 16-bit hash via polynomial h = h*0177432 + c (0177432 = 65082 = ~2^16 multiplier). Lines compared by hash, not by content — much faster + more compact. Hashed lines sorted by value to group equivalence classes (same hash = candidate match). After LCS algorithm produces J[] match vector, check() re-reads files and verifies actual line content matches, breaking spurious matches (hash collisions) — recorded as 'jackpot' but harmless to correctness. Memory budget per comment: 6n words for n-line file. Compare to naive O(N*M) DP table requiring 4 bytes per cell — 1000-line files would need 4MB, impossible on 256KB PDP-11.
ai_analysis.human_story: Comparing two 1000-line files with naive LCS needs a 1000×1000 table = 4MB. PDP-11 had 256KB. Hunt-McIlroy's trick: hash each line to a 16-bit value, compare hashes (4 bytes per line, not the whole line). Sort by hash to group equivalent lines. Hash collisions possible — handled afterward by check() which reads files again to verify, marking false matches. The polynomial hash h = h*0177432 + c is the simple rolling hash of the era. Modern git uses SHA-1 of blobs for object identity; diff uses 16-bit hash for LCS speedup. Different purposes, same idea: 'compare hashes when possible, verify with content when needed'.

appraiser_note: diff のメモリ効率は**ハッシュ**から: 各行を 16 ビットハッシュに、 多項式 `h = h*0177432 + c` (0177432 = 65082 = ~2^16 乗数)。 行はハッシュで比較 (内容ではなく)、 高速 + コンパクト。 ハッシュ済み行をソートして**等価類**作成 (同ハッシュ = マッチ候補)。 LCS アルゴリズムが J[] マッチベクトルを生成した後、 check() が実際の行内容を再確認、 偽マッチ (ハッシュ衝突) を破棄——'jackpot' として記録、 正しさへの影響なし。 メモリ予算 (コメント): n 行ファイルで 6n words。 素朴 DP テーブル O(N*M) なら 1000 行 × 4 バイト = 4MB——256KB PDP-11 で不可能。 git は SHA-1 をオブジェクト ID に使うが、 diff は 16-bit ハッシュを LCS 高速化に使う——目的違うが同じ発想: 「ハッシュで比較、 必要なら内容で検証」。

ai_analysis.technical_context: k-candidate data structure: (x=file0 line, y=file1 line, pred=predecessor index). Each candidate links backward via pred index (not pointer — survives realloc). After stone() produces best k-candidate for each subsequence length, unravel() walks pred chain backward from the longest candidate, recovering the actual LCS as match positions in J[]. Index-based linking (vs pointer) is crucial because newcand uses ralloc which may relocate clist. Modern equivalent: same algorithm written with vectors of indices for stability. The pref/suff offsets account for prune()'s removal of common prefix/suffix before running LCS.
ai_analysis.human_story: After finding the longest common subsequence, how do you recover the actual matching pairs? Walk the predecessor chain. Each k-candidate (x, y, pred) points back to the k-1 candidate it extends. Start from the longest, follow pred to k-1, then k-2, ..., back to 0. Each step gives one (x, y) pair to record in J[]. The pred field is an index, not a pointer — important because realloc moves the array. unravel() reconstructs LCS in O(K) where K = LCS length. This 'build chain forward, walk backward to recover' pattern is the standard DP/algorithmic reconstruction. Modern competitive programming still uses it for LCS, shortest path, etc.

appraiser_note: k-candidate データ構造: (x=file0 行、 y=file1 行、 pred=前任者の index)。 各 candidate は pred index で後方リンク (ポインタではない——realloc 後も生存)。 stone() が各 subsequence 長さの最良 k-candidate を生成した後、 unravel() が最長 candidate から pred chain を後方 walk、 実際の LCS を J[] のマッチ位置として復元。 **index-based linking** (vs ポインタ) が重要——newcand は ralloc を使うので clist が再配置される可能性。 現代の等価実装: 同じアルゴリズムを index ベクトルで安定化。 pref/suff オフセットは LCS 実行前の prune() による共通プレフィックス/サフィックス削除を考慮。 「**チェインを前方構築、 後方 walk で復元**」 パターンは標準 DP/アルゴリズム再構築——現代の競技プログラミングでも LCS、 最短経路等で使用。

ai_analysis.technical_context: diff aggressively reuses buffers: file[0] storage becomes class, then klist, then J across phases. file[1] becomes member, then ixnew. ralloc() is a hand-rolled realloc that frees first then re-allocates — combined with dummy = malloc(1) trick, possibly defragmenting V7 malloc's freelist. The memory budget of '6n words for n-line file' is achievable only via these overlays — naive allocation would need 12-15n words. Trade-off: code complexity (must track buffer's current meaning at each phase) for memory efficiency. PDP-11 with 256KB RAM forced these tricks. Modern systems with GB of RAM make this unnecessary, but the discipline shows in libraries like SQLite which still carefully manage memory pools.
ai_analysis.human_story: 1979's PDP-11 had 256KB RAM. Diffing two 5000-line files naively would exceed it. McIlroy's solution: reuse the same memory for different purposes across algorithm phases. file[0] memory becomes class (equivalence classes), then klist (k-candidate list), then J (final match vector). Each phase the buffer means something different — code must remember which. ralloc() is a custom realloc + free + malloc dance, probably working around V7 malloc's freelist quirks. The 6n words budget per the comment is achievable only this way. Modern programming has gigabytes; these tricks are obsolete. But the discipline lives on in SQLite, embedded systems, and other memory-constrained code.

appraiser_note: diff は **バッファを徹底再利用**: file[0] storage が phase ごとに class、 klist、 J になる。 file[1] が member、 ixnew に。 ralloc() は手作り realloc で先に free してから再確保、 dummy = malloc(1) トリックと組み合わせて V7 malloc の freelist 断片化対策の可能性。 「6n words for n-line file」 のメモリ予算はこのオーバーレイで初めて実現可能——素朴な確保なら 12-15n words 必要。 トレードオフ: コード複雑性 (各 phase でバッファの現在の意味を追跡必要) vs メモリ効率。 PDP-11 の 256KB RAM がこのトリックを強制。 現代の GB RAM ではこの工夫は不要だが、 SQLite や組み込みコードに同じ規律が生きる。 1979 年のメモリ制約下でのアルゴリズム実装の典型例。

ai_analysis.technical_context: diff -e outputs the difference as ed editor commands: `3,4c` (change lines 3-4) followed by new lines and `.` terminator, or `7a` (append at 7), or `10d` (delete line 10). Feeding this to ed transforms old file into new file: `ed old < patch.ed`. This is the prototype of patch(1) — Larry Wall's 1985 patch tool that became central to open-source development. Before patch(1) became standard, projects shared `diff -e` outputs as 'patches' applied via ed. Modern unified-diff format (introduced in GNU diff 1990s) is more readable but conceptually the same: encode changes as a script that can be machine-applied. git diff, code review tools, all descend from this 1979 concept of 'difference as machine-readable transformation'.
ai_analysis.human_story: diff -e outputs the difference as an ed script: `3,4c` followed by replacement lines then `.`. Feed it to ed and the old file becomes the new file. This was the prototype of patch(1) — Larry Wall in 1985 built patch as a more robust applier of diff output, but the core idea ('encode changes as machine-applicable transformation') is from 1979 diff -e. Before patch, programmers shared `diff -e` output via mailing lists: 'apply this to your file 3.2 source'. Modern unified diff (1990s) and git patches inherit the concept. Code review, pull requests, kernel patches — all built on 'difference as data, applicable mechanically'. The 1979 diff -e flag started a revolution in collaborative software development.

appraiser_note: **diff -e** は差分を **ed エディタコマンド**として出力: `3,4c` (3-4 行 change) + 新しい行 + `.` 終端子、 または `7a` (7 行目に append)、 `10d` (10 行目 delete)。 これを ed に喰わせれば古いファイルが新しいファイルに: `ed old < patch.ed`。 これは **patch(1) (Larry Wall, 1985) の祖型**——patch がより強力な diff 出力適用ツールとして登場、 オープンソース開発の中心ツールに。 patch(1) 普及前は、 プロジェクトが **`diff -e` 出力をメーリングリストで共有**: 「これを 3.2 source に当ててください」。 現代の unified diff (GNU diff 1990s) や git patch も概念継承——「**機械適用可能な変換として差分をエンコード**」。 コードレビュー、 プルリクエスト、 カーネルパッチ——全部 1979 年の diff -e の発想の上に。 協力ソフトウェア開発の革命の始点。

ai_analysis.technical_context: diff3 is split into shell wrapper (diff3.sh, user-facing) and C binary (/usr/lib/diff3, internal helper). Shell runs diff(1) twice — diff f1 f3 > d3a, diff f2 f3 > d3b — capturing both differences against base file f3. C binary takes both diff outputs and the three original files, performs the merge. The split keeps each piece simple: shell handles arg parsing/temp files/cleanup, C binary handles merge algorithm. /usr/lib/ vs /usr/bin/ convention: helpers hidden from user. RCS (1982) and CVS later called diff3.sh internally — composition of existing Unix tools rather than reimplementing diff. The 'pipe two diffs together' approach is the same idea behind tar+gzip, find+xargs, ls+grep.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why is diff3 split into shell + C? Reuse. The shell wrapper runs diff twice (alice vs base, bob vs base), pipes outputs to the C binary which does the merge logic. Each piece is simple: shell handles plumbing (temp files, signals, args), C handles algorithm. The C binary hides in /usr/lib/diff3 — user calls /usr/bin/diff3 (shell wrapper). This composition of existing diff(1) + new merge logic is pure Unix philosophy. Modern git embeds 3-way merge as one huge binary, but conceptually it's still 'diff twice, merge results'.

appraiser_note: diff3 は **shell wrapper** (diff3.sh、 ユーザー向け) と **C binary** (/usr/lib/diff3、 内部ヘルパー) に分割。 shell が diff(1) を 2 回実行——`diff f1 f3 > d3a`、 `diff f2 f3 > d3b` ——基準 f3 に対する両方の差分を取得。 C binary が 2 つの diff 出力 + 3 つの元ファイルを受け取り merge 実行。 分割でそれぞれシンプル: shell が arg parse/temp/cleanup、 C binary が merge アルゴリズム。 /usr/lib/ vs /usr/bin/ 慣習: ヘルパーをユーザーから隠す。 後の RCS (1982) と CVS が内部で diff3.sh を呼び出し——既存 Unix ツールの**合成**で diff を再実装せず。 「2 つの diff を pipe で繋ぐ」 アプローチは tar+gzip、 find+xargs、 ls+grep と同じ Unix 哲学。 現代 git は 3-way merge を 1 つの巨大バイナリに統合するが、 概念的には「2 回 diff、 結果を merge」。

ai_analysis.technical_context: diff3 parses diff(1)'s output into struct diff arrays. Each struct diff has old (range in modified file) and new (range in base file f3). struct range uses half-open intervals [from, to) — to-from = length, from==to = insertion point. readin() parses diff output formats: 'a' = append (line after), 'd' = delete, 'c' = change. Adjusts indices to half-open: a++ for append (line after target), c++ for delete (insertion point shifts), b++/d++ to make exclusive. NC=200 limits total changes. last[1-3] tracks line correspondence across 3 files during merge. The half-open interval convention is consistent throughout computer science (Python range, C++ STL, etc.) — making length calculations and emptiness checks uniform.
ai_analysis.human_story: diff outputs strings like '3,5c4,6' meaning change lines 3-5 in old to lines 4-6 in new. diff3 parses these into struct diff arrays: each entry has 'old' range (in alice or bob) and 'new' range (in base). Half-open intervals [from, to) make math uniform: to-from = length, from==to = empty (insertion). readin() handles 'a' (append), 'd' (delete), 'c' (change) by adjusting indices. NC=200 caps total changes per merge — typical files fit easily, huge refactors might not. The clean data structure makes the merge algorithm tractable: walk d13[] and d23[] in parallel, dispatching based on overlap.

appraiser_note: diff3 は diff(1) の出力を struct diff 配列にパース。 各 struct diff は old (変更ファイル内の範囲) と new (基準ファイル f3 内の範囲)。 struct range は **半開区間 [from, to)** ——to-from = 長さ、 from==to = 挿入点。 readin() が diff 出力形式をパース: 'a' = append (target の後)、 'd' = delete、 'c' = change。 半開区間に正規化: append なら a++ (target の次の行)、 delete なら c++ (挿入点シフト)、 b++/d++ で exclusive 'to'。 NC=200 で最大変更数制限。 last[1-3] で 3 ファイル間の行対応を merge 中追跡。 **半開区間慣習**はコンピュータサイエンス全般で一貫 (Python range、 C++ STL 等)、 長さ計算と空判定が統一的になる。 構造のクリーンさが merge アルゴリズムを実行可能に。

ai_analysis.technical_context: merge() walks d13[] and d23[] in parallel, dispatching on 5 cases: (1) only f1 has change here → emit f1's change marked '====1'; (2) only f2 has change → '====2'; (3,4) merge consecutive overlapping changes within same file; (5) both files changed same range → check if identical via duplicate(); if so emit '====3' (resolved), if not emit '====' (CONFLICT). Overlapping ranges extended to coincide then re-evaluated. This 5-case taxonomy is the canonical 3-way merge algorithm. Modern git uses essentially the same logic (with refinements like recursive merge for criss-cross histories). The 'CONFLICT means both modified the same content differently' rule is the heart of merge.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does git know when there's a conflict? Same algorithm as 1979 diff3: parse two diffs (yours vs base, theirs vs base), walk them in parallel. If only you changed a range: take yours. If only they changed: take theirs. If both changed the same range identically: take it (no conflict). If both changed the same range differently: CONFLICT. This 5-case dispatch in 1979 merge() is the algorithm. git, mercurial, svn, perforce — all use this. Refinements like 'recursive merge' (when history has criss-crosses) are extensions, but the basic 5-case logic is from McIlroy's diff3.

appraiser_note: merge() が d13[] と d23[] を並行 walk、 **5 ケース dispatch**: (1) f1 だけここで変更 → f1 の変更を '====1' 付きで出力; (2) f2 だけ変更 → '====2'; (3,4) 同ファイル内の連続変更を統合; (5) 両ファイルが同範囲を変更 → duplicate() で同一かチェック、 同一なら '====3' (解決済み)、 違うなら '====' (CONFLICT)。 範囲オーバーラップは一致するまで拡張、 再評価。 この **5 ケース taxonomy が canonical な 3-way merge アルゴリズム**。 現代 git は本質的に同じロジック (recursive merge for criss-cross histories の改良あり)。 「**両方が同じ内容を違うように変更 = CONFLICT**」 ルールが merge の心臓。 git/mercurial/svn/perforce 全部この方式——McIlroy の 1979 年 diff3 の 5 ケース dispatch が現代分散開発の DNA。

ai_analysis.technical_context: diff3's output uses '====' separator with optional number suffix: ==== (unresolved CONFLICT, requires human resolution), ====1 (only file 1 changed, take f1's version), ====2 (only file 2 changed, take f2's), ====3 (both changed identically, take either). Each section follows with '<file_num>:<range>' headers showing what each file has at the conflict point. The ==== marker is the proto-conflict-marker for all later VCSs. Git's <<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>> markers are diff3's idea reformatted to embed in the file body instead of as a separator between sections. `git merge --conflict-style=diff3` explicitly outputs in diff3-compatible form with HEAD, common-ancestor, branch sections matching f1, f3, f2.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does git put <<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>> in conflicted files? Because diff3 had ==== in 1979. Same idea: mark the conflict, show both versions, let human resolve. diff3 output: ==== (4 equals = unresolved), ====1 (resolved by taking file 1), ====2 (file 2), ====3 (both same, resolved). Git evolved this to embed markers IN the file body for easier editing in place. `git merge --conflict-style=diff3` shows the common ancestor between markers too — making it identical in spirit to 1979 diff3's f1/f3/f2 sections. 50 years of conflict markers descend from those 4 equals.

appraiser_note: diff3 の出力は **`====` セパレータ**: ==== (未解決 CONFLICT、 人間が解決必要)、 ====1 (f1 だけ変更、 f1 採用)、 ====2 (f2 だけ変更、 f2 採用)、 ====3 (両方同じく変更、 採用)。 各セクションに `<file_num>:<range>` ヘッダで各ファイルの conflict 位置を表示。 ==== マーカーが**後の全 VCS の conflict marker の祖**。 git の <<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>> は diff3 のアイデアをファイル本体に埋め込む形に reformat。 `git merge --conflict-style=diff3` は明示的に diff3 互換出力——HEAD/common-ancestor/branch セクションが f1/f3/f2 に対応。 50 年の conflict marker 文化が **4 つの = から**始まった。 「**マーカーで区切る + 両方表示 + 人間が選ぶ**」 が共通哲学、 embedding 方式の違いは表現上のもの。

ai_analysis.technical_context:
diff3 -e outputs the merge as an ed editor script: piping to ed automatically applies the merge. edscript() outputs commands in REVERSE order — critical because ed line numbers shift as edits are applied; processing back-to-front means later edits don't perturb earlier line numbers. The pattern '%da
.
%d,%dc
' emits append-then-change, with '.' terminator for ed's text mode. -e auto-resolves non-conflicting changes; -3 outputs only base file's changes; -x outputs only conflicts. Used heavily by RCS rcsmerge command in 1982: rcsmerge runs diff3 -e and pipes to ed for automatic merge during checkout, falling back to human resolution only on conflicts. This pipeline of small tools (diff + diff3 + ed) is composability incarnate.
ai_analysis.human_story: diff3 -e + ed = the automatic merger. diff3 -e outputs ed commands; pipe to ed and watch the merge happen. The reverse-order output is the clever bit: edit from the end of the file backward, so line numbers stay stable. RCS (1982) used this in rcsmerge — when you check out, RCS runs diff3 -e against your version + the new HEAD + the common ancestor, pipes through ed for non-conflicting merges. Only conflicts need human resolution. Modern git replays the same logic in C code, but the original was 'pipe of small tools' — diff + diff3 + ed. Composition over reimplementation.

appraiser_note:
**diff3 -e** が merge を **ed 編集スクリプト**として出力——ed にパイプすれば自動 merge 適用。 edscript() が**逆順**でコマンドを出力——重要な理由は ed の行番号が編集ごとにずれる、 末尾から処理すれば後の編集が前の行番号に影響しない。 パターン '%da
.
%d,%dc
' は append-then-change を出力、 '.' は ed テキストモード終端。 -e は非競合変更を自動解決、 -3 は base ファイルの変更のみ、 -x は競合のみ。 1982 年 RCS の rcsmerge コマンドが多用——チェックアウト時に diff3 -e を実行して ed にパイプ、 非競合な merge は自動、 競合のみ人間解決。 「**小さなツールのパイプ**」 (diff + diff3 + ed) が合成性の体現。 現代 git は同じロジックを C で再現するが、 オリジナルは pipe ベース——再実装より合成。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cmp's core loop: getc each byte from each file, compare, dispatch by mode. Constant memory (just c1, c2). O(N) time, where N = first diff position (or file size if same). goto loop for tight inner loop. chr counts byte position; line counts newlines for human-readable output. EOF handling: both EOF = same; one EOF = differ (prefix relationship). Modes via lflg: 0 silent (exit immediately), 1 default (one-line message), 2 long (octal dump of differences, continues to scan). Compare with diff which builds LCS in memory; cmp's stream-based loop fits any file size in 2 bytes of RAM. Modern equivalents (cmp + sum + md5sum) all preserve this 'byte-stream comparison' model for definite answers.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does cmp compare two files? Read byte 1 from both, compare. Different? Done. Same? Read byte 2. Repeat. The simplest possible algorithm — getc, compare, getc, compare. Memory: 2 bytes (just the current bytes). Speed: O(N). 1979's cmp does this in 25 lines of inner loop. Modern cmp (GNU) is essentially the same loop with extra options. When you need to know 'is this byte-for-byte identical?', this is unbeatable. Hashing (md5sum) is faster across many files but cmp is the gold standard for one-vs-one comparison — no false positives ever.

appraiser_note: cmp の核心ループ: 各ファイルから getc で 1 バイトずつ読み、 比較、 モードで dispatch。 **定数メモリ** (c1、 c2 のみ)。 O(N) 時間、 N = 最初の差分位置 (or 同じならファイルサイズ)。 goto loop で tight な内部ループ。 chr がバイト位置をカウント、 line が改行を数えて人間向け出力。 EOF 処理: 両方 EOF = 同じ; 片方 EOF = 違う (prefix 関係)。 モードは lflg で: 0 silent (即 exit)、 1 default (1 行メッセージ)、 2 long (8 進ダンプ、 走査続行)。 diff が LCS をメモリに構築するのと対照——cmp の stream ベースループは **2 バイトの RAM で任意サイズのファイル**を処理可能。 現代の cmp + sum + md5sum すべて「**バイトストリーム比較**」 モデルを保持。 ハッシュより遅いが false positive なし、 「one-vs-one 比較の決定論的標準**」。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cmp established the 3-tier exit code convention now standard across Unix comparison tools (diff, comm, grep, expr): 0=success/match, 1=normal failure/no-match, 2=error. Distinct from binary 0/non-zero — three categories let shell scripts distinguish 'differs' (normal outcome to handle) from 'broken' (abort). This convention codified in POSIX 1992. Critical for shell idioms: `if cmp -s a b; then ...` works because 0 is truthy in shell. `case $? in 0|1) ok;; 2) abort;; esac` pattern. POSIX explicitly mandates these codes. The convention spread: grep returns 0 (found), 1 (not found), 2 (error); diff: 0 (same), 1 (differ), 2 (error). 1979 cmp set the template that 50 years of Unix tools follow.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does `cmp -s file1 file2; echo $?` produce 0, 1, or 2? Because cmp established the 3-tier convention: 0 = same, 1 = differ, 2 = error. This lets shell scripts distinguish 'expected outcome' (differ) from 'broken' (couldn't open). The pattern: `case $? in 0) match;; 1) nomatch;; 2) error;; esac`. grep follows it (found/notfound/error), diff follows it, comm follows it. POSIX standardized it in 1992. 1979 cmp's choice to use 3 codes instead of 0/non-zero gave shell scripts much more expressiveness. 50 years of `if cmp -s` and `if grep` shell idioms rely on this.

appraiser_note: cmp が **3 段階 exit code 慣習**を確立、 現在 Unix 比較ツール (diff、 comm、 grep、 expr) で標準: **0=success/match、 1=normal failure/no-match、 2=error**。 binary 0/non-zero と異なり、 3 カテゴリで shell script は「**differ** (期待される結果) と **broken** (中断必要)」 を区別可能。 POSIX 1992 で codify。 シェルイディオムに必須: `if cmp -s a b; then ...` は 0 が truthy だから動く。 `case $? in 0|1) ok;; 2) abort;; esac` パターン。 POSIX が明示的にこのコードを要求。 慣習の伝播: grep (0 found / 1 not / 2 error)、 diff (0 same / 1 differ / 2 error)、 comm 等。 **1979 年 cmp が 50 年の Unix ツールの template** を設定。 `0/non-zero` binary より 3 段階の方が shell スクリプトに表現力を与えた。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cmp uses single int lflg as 3-level verbosity: 0=silent (-s), 1=default, 2=long (-l). Increment/decrement to set: -s decrements to 0, -l increments to 2. Compact representation, easy to test (if lflg==0/==1/else). Three modes serve distinct needs: -s for shell scripts (just exit code), default for human use (one-line summary), -l for binary forensics (octal dump of all diffs). The 'increment-decrement to choose level' trick allows future -ss or -ll extension if needed, though no Unix tool actually uses this. Modern GNU cmp keeps the same three modes but adds -b (with byte chars), -i (initial skip), -n (max bytes) — more dimensions but same verbosity axis.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does cmp know if you want silent, normal, or verbose output? One variable. -s decrements (1→0=silent), -l increments (1→2=long). Default is 1 (one-line summary). Three levels serve three needs: shell scripts want -s (exit code only), users want default (one message), debuggers want -l (all differences as octal dump). The single-variable design is elegant: 3 lines of code handle all 3 modes. GNU cmp added more flags later (-b, -i, -n) but kept the 3-level verbosity. The 'lflg++ vs lflg--' trick is K&R era compactness; modern code would use enums.

appraiser_note: cmp は **単一 int lflg で 3 段階詳細度**: 0=silent (-s)、 1=default、 2=long (-l)。 増減で設定: -s は decrement で 0、 -l は increment で 2。 コンパクト表現、 テストしやすい (if lflg==0/==1/else)。 **3 モードが異なるニーズに対応**: -s は shell script (exit code のみ)、 default は人間用 (1 行サマリ)、 -l はバイナリフォレンジック (全 diff 8 進ダンプ)。 「increment-decrement でレベル選択」 トリックで -ss や -ll の将来拡張も可能 (実際は使われない)。 現代 GNU cmp は同じ 3 モードを保持、 -b (byte chars)、 -i (initial skip)、 -n (max bytes) を追加——次元は増えたが verbosity 軸は同じ。 単一変数設計のエレガンス: 3 行コードで 3 モード全部処理。 lflg++/lflg-- トリックは K&R 時代の簡潔さ、 現代なら enum を使う。

text:
/* In -l mode, print each difference as octal: */
printf("%6ld %3o %3o\n", chr, c1, c2);
goto loop;

/* %6ld: long integer, right-aligned in 6 chars
   %3o:  octal, right-aligned in 3 chars (range 000-377)
   
   Example output:
        1 060 052
        2 061 053
       64 040 010
      100 060 050
     1024 165 145
   
   Columns:
     position   - byte offset in file (1-indexed)
     file1 byte - octal value of byte from file1
     file2 byte - octal value of byte from file2
*/

ai_analysis.technical_context: cmp -l output format: %6ld for offset (long, 6-char field), %3o twice for the two differing bytes in octal (3-char fields, since byte range 000-377 octal). Octal was natural for 1979 PDP-11/VAX programmers — same as od (#180), dd's EBCDIC tables, ar's modes. Modern programmers find hex more natural; GNU cmp added -b flag for printable chars alongside octal. The %6ld accommodates offsets up to 999999 (~1MB) cleanly aligned; larger files lose alignment but stay readable. Right-aligned columns enable easy visual scanning. Format mirrors od's output style — Unix's binary-inspection vocabulary is consistent across tools.
ai_analysis.human_story: cmp -l shows differences in octal: '1024 165 145' = at byte 1024, file1 has 0o165, file2 has 0o145. 1979 prefers octal because PDP-11/VAX dumps were octal, od is octal, dd's tables are octal — octal was the lingua franca of 1979 binary work. Today's hex generation finds it odd; GNU cmp's -b flag adds printable chars (' u  e' for the example) to ease reading. But the 8th-bit pattern of octal — 3 digits = 1 byte = 9 bits + sign — preserves 1979 binary culture. Format mirrors od's; the visual style of Unix binary inspection is consistent.

appraiser_note: cmp -l 出力フォーマット: %6ld でオフセット (long、 6 文字フィールド)、 %3o を 2 回で違う 2 バイトを 8 進 (3 文字フィールド、 バイト範囲 000-377 8 進)。 8 進は 1979 年 PDP-11/VAX プログラマには自然——od (#180)、 dd の EBCDIC テーブル、 ar のモードと同じ。 現代プログラマには 16 進が自然、 GNU cmp は -b フラグで印字可能文字を併記。 %6ld で 999999 (~1MB) までクリーン整列、 大きいファイルでは崩れるが読める。 右寄せカラムで視覚スキャンが楽。 **フォーマットは od の出力スタイルを継承**——Unix のバイナリ検査語彙はツール間で一貫。 1024 165 145 = byte 1024 で file1=0o165、 file2=0o145。 octal が 1979 年バイナリ作業の lingua franca、 GNU cmp -b は ' u  e' のような printable chars 追加で読みやすく。

ai_analysis.technical_context: cmp embodies 'do one thing well': 121 lines, 2 functions, 5 standard library functions, 2 headers. Comparable: diff (#183) 645 lines, diff3 (#184) 438 lines. cmp does what neither does — fast definite answer to 'are these byte-identical?'. No LCS, no merge, no algorithms beyond stream comparison. Binary ~2KB on 1979 VAX. Modern GNU cmp ~1500 lines with -b/-i/-n flags, internationalization, additional output formats — but core comparison loop identical. The minimalism enables: fast startup, no malloc overhead, perfectly composable in shell scripts (`if cmp -s ...`), tiny attack surface. cmp's role in Unix is to be the dependable simple option when you don't need diff's sophistication.
ai_analysis.human_story: cmp is 121 lines. diff is 645 lines. Why so much smaller? Because cmp does less. cmp asks 'same or different?'. diff asks 'where exactly and how?'. By limiting scope, cmp achieves: fast startup, tiny binary (~2KB), no memory allocation, perfectly reliable. When a shell script needs 'did the file change?', cmp -s gives the answer in microseconds with definite truth. Modern GNU cmp adds polish (locale, more flags) but the core 2-function design from 1979 is unchanged. 121 lines doing one thing perfectly is a Unix philosophy success story. Bigger tools have niches; cmp owns the 'are these identical?' niche.

appraiser_note: cmp は **「Do one thing well」 の体現**: 121 行、 2 関数、 標準ライブラリ関数 5 つ、 ヘッダ 2 つ。 比較: diff (#183) 645 行、 diff3 (#184) 438 行。 cmp はそれらができないことを行う——「**バイト一致か?**」 の高速・決定論的回答。 LCS なし、 merge なし、 stream 比較以外のアルゴリズムなし。 1979 年 VAX で ~2KB バイナリ。 現代 GNU cmp は ~1500 行 (-b/-i/-n、 国際化、 追加出力形式) だがコア比較ループは同一。 ミニマリズムが実現するもの: 高速起動、 malloc なし、 シェルスクリプトで完璧合成可能 (`if cmp -s ...`)、 小さな attack surface。 **cmp の役割は信頼できるシンプルオプション**——diff の精緻さが不要な時の選択肢。 1979 年の 2 関数設計が現代まで不変、 シェルスクリプトで「ファイル変わった?」 にマイクロ秒で確定回答。

ai_analysis.technical_context: comm output uses tab-indented columns: column 1 (no indent) = file1 only, column 2 (1 tab) = file2 only, column 3 (2 tabs) = in both files. ldr[] array holds the leader strings. wr(str, n) prints leader + line. Suppression flags (one/two/three) skip a column entirely. When -1 suppresses column 1, ldr[1] becomes empty and ldr[2] shortens to 1 tab — keeps remaining columns visually aligned. The tab-based layout works with cut/awk for downstream processing. Modern GUI shows columns visually; comm produces text-pipeline-friendly output.
ai_analysis.human_story: comm's killer output: 3 columns separated by tabs. Run comm file1 file2 and see at a glance what's only in file1, only in file2, in both. Tab indentation makes it visually obvious. -1/-2/-3 flags suppress columns to get just the difference or intersection. The leader strings ldr[0]/ldr[1]/ldr[2] = ''/\t/\t\t are adjusted when columns are suppressed to keep the remaining columns aligned. 1979 simplicity — three pre-computed strings. Modern tools render columns with terminal codes; comm prefers pipeline-friendly tabs.

appraiser_note: comm 出力は **タブインデント 3 列**: 列 1 (インデントなし) = file1 only、 列 2 (1 タブ) = file2 only、 列 3 (2 タブ) = 両方。 ldr[] 配列がリーダー文字列を保持、 wr(str, n) がリーダー + 行を印字。 抑制フラグ (one/two/three) で列を完全に skip。 -1 で列 1 抑制時、 ldr[1] が空、 ldr[2] が 1 タブに短縮——残り列の視覚的整列を保つ。 タブベースレイアウトは cut/awk で後段処理可能。 現代 GUI は列をビジュアル表示、 comm はパイプラインフレンドリーな出力を選択。 ldr[0]/ldr[1]/ldr[2] = ''/\t/\t\t の事前計算文字列がシンプル。 list comprehension で 3 行を 1 行に出すよりも、 列の中で区別する設計が Unix 流。

ai_analysis.technical_context: comm's algorithm: linear merge of two sorted streams. compare() returns 3-valued result (0=equal, 1=less, 2=greater). Main loop dispatches: equal → emit both column 3 + advance both; less → emit file1 column 1 + advance file1; greater → emit file2 column 2 + advance file2. On EOF in one file, copy() dumps the rest of the other to its appropriate column. O(N+M) time, O(1) space (just 2 line buffers). Requires sorted input (assumes lexicographic order). The 3-way dispatch is the classic merge join algorithm, also used in merge sort's merge step, SQL merge join, Python heapq.merge(), Apache Spark sort-merge join.
ai_analysis.human_story: comm's algorithm in 30 lines: walk two sorted files in parallel, compare current line of each. If equal, both have it (column 3). If file1's line is less, only file1 has it here (column 1) — advance file1. If file2's is less, only file2 has it (column 2) — advance file2. Linear time. Constant memory. This is merge join, the workhorse of database query planners and merge sort. comm in 1979 used the same algorithm that powers modern data systems. Speed: bounded by I/O. Memory: just 2 lines. Beautiful.

appraiser_note: comm のアルゴリズム: **2 つの sorted stream の線形 merge**。 compare() が 3 値結果を返す (0=等しい、 1=小さい、 2=大きい)。 メインループで dispatch: 等しい → 両方を列 3 に出力 + 両方進める; 小さい → file1 行を列 1 + file1 進める; 大きい → file2 行を列 2 + file2 進める。 片方 EOF なら copy() で残りを該当列にダンプ。 **O(N+M) 時間、 O(1) 空間** (2 行バッファのみ)。 ソート済み入力必須 (辞書順前提)。 この 3-way dispatch は古典的 **merge join アルゴリズム**——merge sort の merge ステップ、 SQL merge join、 Python heapq.merge()、 Apache Spark sort-merge join で同じ。 1979 年の comm が現代データシステムを動かすアルゴリズムを使用、 線形時間 + 定数メモリ、 美しい。

ai_analysis.technical_context: comm's -1/-2/-3 flags suppress columns, enabling set operations via column combinations: -12 outputs only column 3 (intersection ∩), -23 outputs only column 1 (file1-file2 difference), -3 outputs columns 1+2 (symmetric difference). 8 combinations total cover all set operations. Equivalent SQL: comm -12 = INTERSECT, comm -23 = EXCEPT, comm -3 = symmetric XOR. Equivalent Python: set1 & set2, set1 - set2, set1 ^ set2. comm provides these without database or scripting language — just shell. Streaming O(N+M) makes it faster than hash-based set operations for huge inputs. Modern shell scripts use `comm -12 <(sort A) <(sort B)` idiom for set intersection on sorted streams.
ai_analysis.human_story: What's INTERSECT, EXCEPT, UNION in shell? comm with flags. comm -12 = INTERSECT (lines in both). comm -23 = EXCEPT (file1 minus file2). comm -3 = symmetric difference. comm provides all SQL set operations from 1979, before SQL standardized them, before Python's set existed. 8 flag combinations cover every set operation you'd want. Run `comm -12 <(sort yesterday.log) <(sort today.log)` to get common entries. Faster than hash-set for huge files (streaming O(N+M) vs hash table memory). 1979's clever realization: 'classify into 3 columns, suppress columns to get specific operations'.

appraiser_note: comm の -1/-2/-3 フラグで列抑制、 **集合演算を列の組み合わせで実現**: -12 で列 3 のみ (積集合 ∩)、 -23 で列 1 のみ (file1-file2 差集合)、 -3 で列 1+2 (対称差)。 8 通りの組み合わせで全集合演算をカバー。 SQL 等価: comm -12 = INTERSECT、 comm -23 = EXCEPT、 comm -3 = symmetric XOR。 Python 等価: set1 & set2、 set1 - set2、 set1 ^ set2。 comm はデータベースや scripting language なしで、 shell だけでこれらを提供。 ストリーミング O(N+M) でハッシュベース集合演算より巨大入力で高速。 現代シェルスクリプトのイディオム: `comm -12 <(sort A) <(sort B)` で sorted stream の集合積。 SQL 標準化前 (1979)、 Python set 登場前——「3 列分類 + 列抑制 = 集合演算」 という 1979 年の独創。

ai_analysis.technical_context: comm processes inputs as streams — never holds more than 2 lines (one per file) in memory. Total RAM: ~600 bytes regardless of input size. LB=256 max line length. rd() reads one line into a 256-byte buffer, returning 0 on success, -1 on EOF. Output is written immediately on each iteration — no buffering. Contrast with Python set: must load entire file into memory before operating. comm scales to GB+ files trivially; Python hits memory limits. Bash idiom `comm -12 <(sort A) <(sort B)` chains sort (which may use external merge for huge files) and comm (constant memory) for unlimited-size set operations.
ai_analysis.human_story: comm's secret superpower: streaming. Never loads more than 2 lines (one per file). Total memory: 600 bytes, doesn't matter if files are 1KB or 100GB. Compare with Python: set(open('file1')) — slurps the whole file. comm streams. For huge files (database dumps, log archives), comm is the only way that fits in memory. `comm -12 <(sort big1) <(sort big2)` works on terabyte files; Python's set() would die. The 1979 streaming design from a 256KB machine still serves modern big-data needs.

appraiser_note: comm は **入力をストリーム処理**——メモリに 2 行 (ファイル毎 1 行) 以上保持しない。 総 RAM: ~600 バイト、 入力サイズに無関係。 LB=256 最大行長。 rd() が 1 行を 256 バイトバッファに読み、 success で 0、 EOF で -1 を返す。 出力は各イテレーションで即書き込み、 バッファなし。 Python set との対照: ファイル全体をメモリに load してから処理。 comm は GB+ ファイルに自明に対応、 Python はメモリ制限。 bash イディオム `comm -12 <(sort A) <(sort B)` で sort (巨大ファイルに external merge を使う) と comm (定数メモリ) をチェーン、 無制限サイズの集合演算。 256KB PDP-11 時代のストリーミング設計が、 現代の big-data ニーズにも対応——TB ファイルでも comm は動く、 Python set() は死ぬ。

text:
/* The Unix comparison family (Bell-32V 1979): */

/* cmp (#185, 121 lines)
   Byte-by-byte binary comparison
   Use: "are these byte-identical?"
   Output: "X Y differ: char N, line M" or exit code
*/

/* comm (#197, 166 lines)
   Sorted line set comparison (3 columns)
   Use: "set operations on lines"
   Output: 3-column tab-aligned classification
*/

/* diff3 (#184, 438 lines)
   3-way merge with conflict detection
   Use: "merge two branches with common ancestor"
   Output: ==== conflict markers + change blocks
*/

/* diff (#183, 645 lines)
   LCS-based text difference
   Use: "what changed line-by-line?"
   Output: 3,5c4,6 ed-style change blocks
*/

/* All four tools share:
   - Exit codes 0 (same/match), 1 (differ), 2 (error)
   - Read files or stdin (- as filename)
   - Composable via pipes
   - POSIX standardized
*/

ai_analysis.technical_context: Bell-32V 1979 ships 4 comparison tools covering distinct problems: cmp (binary byte compare, 121 lines) for 'are these identical?', comm (sorted set compare, 166 lines) for 'set operations', diff3 (3-way merge, 438 lines) for 'merge with conflicts', diff (LCS text compare, 645 lines) for 'what changed'. Each does one thing well; users compose them via shell. Combined LOC: 1370 lines for 4 specialized tools. Compare to single-monolithic-tool approach where 1 binary handles all cases — would be more code, less composable, harder to maintain. The 4-tool family demonstrates Unix philosophy at its peak: small, focused, composable, with shared conventions (exit codes, stdin support, POSIX flags).
ai_analysis.human_story: Bell-32V 1979 shipped 4 comparison tools instead of 1 big one. cmp = bytes. comm = sorted sets. diff3 = 3-way merge. diff = LCS. Each does ONE thing well; users compose via shell pipes. Total: 1370 lines for 4 tools. A monolithic tool with -mode-binary -mode-sets -mode-merge -mode-lcs would be 3000+ lines and harder to maintain. The Unix philosophy of small composable tools beat the 'do everything in one' approach. Modern git embeds these (git diff, git merge), but Unix shell still uses the 4 originals. 50 years of comparison work, all founded on 1979 Bell Labs design decisions.

appraiser_note: Bell-32V 1979 は **4 つの比較ツール**を出荷、 異なる問題をカバー: cmp (バイナリバイト比較、 121 行) で「同じか?」、 comm (ソート集合比較、 166 行) で「集合演算」、 diff3 (3-way merge、 438 行) で「conflict ある merge」、 diff (LCS テキスト比較、 645 行) で「何が変わった?」。 各が one thing well、 ユーザーが shell で合成。 合計 LOC: 4 ツールで 1370 行。 単一モノリシックツールアプローチ (1 バイナリで全部処理) と比較——コードは多く、 合成性は低く、 保守性も悪い。 **4 ツールファミリーが Unix 哲学の頂点を示す**: 小さく、 集中、 合成可能、 共通慣習 (exit code、 stdin サポート、 POSIX フラグ)。 現代 git は内部で同等機能 (git diff、 git merge) を持つが、 Unix shell では 1979 オリジナル 4 つを今も使う。 50 年の比較作業が 1979 ベル研の設計判断に基づく。

ai_analysis.technical_context: join treats text files as relational tables: lines = records, separator-delimited tokens = fields. input() reads one line, splits in-place by overwriting separators with NUL, stores field pointers in ppi[file][field_idx]. -t flag changes separator. -j1/-j2 specifies join column per file. NFLD=20 max fields. The in-place tokenization is K&R efficiency: no malloc per field, just point into the buffer. Separator handling supports both space-default (collapses multiples) and explicit char (-t ',' for CSV). This text-as-relation abstraction is the basis for Unix data processing — no schema required, fields identified by position. Modern CSV libraries handle quoting/escaping which 1979 join doesn't.
ai_analysis.human_story: join treats text files like database tables: each line is a record, each field is a column. The first column (default) is the join key. input() reads a line and splits it in-place — replaces separators with NUL bytes and points to each field. No malloc, no copy, just pointer arithmetic. -t flag picks the separator (default space/tab, comma for CSV, colon for /etc/passwd). The whole abstraction of 'text file as relational table' came from this design — fields by position, no schema needed. CSV with quoting wasn't a thing yet; join's input model is simple.

appraiser_note: join はテキストファイルを **リレーショナルテーブル**として扱う: 行 = レコード、 区切り文字で分けたトークン = フィールド。 input() が 1 行を読み、 区切り文字を NUL で上書きして in-place 分割、 フィールドポインタを ppi[file][field_idx] に格納。 -t フラグで区切り文字変更。 -j1/-j2 で各ファイルの join カラム指定。 NFLD=20 最大フィールド数。 **in-place トークン化**は K&R 効率: フィールド毎の malloc なし、 バッファへのポインタだけ。 区切り文字処理は space-default (連続をスキップ) と明示文字 (-t ',' で CSV) の両方サポート。 **テキスト = リレーション**抽象化が Unix データ処理の基盤——スキーマ不要、 位置でフィールド識別。 quoting/escaping の現代 CSV ライブラリ機能は 1979 join には無い、 シンプルな入力モデル。

ai_analysis.technical_context: join -o syntax: comma-or-space separated list of '<file>.<column>' (e.g., '1.1 2.3 1.2'). Parsed into olist[] (column number) and olistf[] (file F1/F2). Output() iterates olist, printing fields in specified order with separator between, newline at end. Missing/empty fields substituted with 'null' string (-e flag, default ''). Equivalent to SQL 'SELECT f1.col1, f2.col3, f1.col2 FROM ...'. The -o syntax predates SQL standardization (1986) by 7 years. Modern tools: awk '{print $1, $3, ...}' for column reordering, but join -o uniquely combines reorder + join. pandas: .merge(...).loc[:, ['col1', 'col3']].
ai_analysis.human_story: Want columns 1 from file1, 3 from file2, 2 from file1 — in that order? `join -o 1.1 2.3 1.2 file1 file2`. The -o syntax came 7 years before SQL standardized SELECT. Each entry is '<file>.<column>' — clear, position-based, no quoting needed. Output prints fields in your specified order with separators, NULLs replaced by -e string. SQL: SELECT f1.x, f2.y, f1.z FROM ... — same idea, more verbose. awk can also pick columns but doesn't join. join -o is the singular tool for 'JOIN + reorder + project' in shell.

appraiser_note: join -o 構文: '<file>.<column>' 形式のリスト (例: `1.1 2.3 1.2`)。 olist[] (カラム番号) と olistf[] (ファイル F1/F2) にパース。 output() が olist を iterate、 指定順でフィールドを区切り文字で出力、 末尾改行。 欠損/空フィールドは null 文字列 (-e フラグ、 デフォルト '') で代入。 SQL の 'SELECT f1.col1, f2.col3, f1.col2 FROM ...' 等価。 **-o 構文は SQL 標準化 (1986) より 7 年早い**。 現代ツール: awk '{print $1, $3, ...}' でカラム並び替え、 でも join -o はユニークに **reorder + join** を結合。 pandas: .merge(...).loc[:, ['col1', 'col3']]。 シェルでの 'JOIN + reorder + project' の単一ツール、 1979 年から不変の API。

ai_analysis.technical_context: join supports LEFT (-a1), RIGHT (-a2), and FULL (-a) OUTER JOIN via aflg bitfield. INNER JOIN is default (no -a). Loop continues while either file has records (aflg!=0 && n1+n2>0). For unmatched records: -a1 outputs file1's unmatched, -a2 outputs file2's. -e STR replaces NULL fields. SQL standardized LEFT/RIGHT OUTER JOIN in 1986 and FULL OUTER in 1992 — Unix join had all three 7-13 years earlier. The relational algebra textbook (Codd 1970) didn't include OUTER JOIN; SQL added it for practical needs. Unix join's pragmatic design preceded the theoretical canon — 1979 implementers needed it for real workflows.
ai_analysis.human_story: Need all employees, even those without salaries? `join -a1 -e 'N/A' employees salaries`. 1979 join had LEFT OUTER JOIN before SQL standardized it (1986). And FULL OUTER too (-a, no number) — SQL didn't standardize that until 1992. Codd's relational algebra paper (1970) doesn't have OUTER JOIN — it's a practical extension. Unix programmers in 1979 needed 'show me all records, with nulls for missing matches' and added it directly. SQL caught up later. The aflg bitfield (1=LEFT, 2=RIGHT, 1|2=FULL) elegantly handles all three cases.

appraiser_note: join は **LEFT (-a1)、 RIGHT (-a2)、 FULL (-a) OUTER JOIN** を aflg ビットフィールドでサポート。 INNER JOIN がデフォルト (-a なし)。 ループは片方のファイルにレコードがある限り続行 (aflg!=0 && n1+n2>0)。 不一致レコード: -a1 で file1 の不一致を出力、 -a2 で file2 の不一致を出力。 -e STR で NULL フィールド置換。 **SQL 標準化は LEFT/RIGHT OUTER が 1986、 FULL OUTER が 1992** ——Unix join はこれらを **7-13 年早く実装**。 Codd のリレーショナル代数論文 (1970) には OUTER JOIN なし——SQL の実用拡張。 Unix join の実用主義設計が理論的体系より先行——1979 年実装者が実 workflow で必要として直接追加。 aflg のビットフィールド (1=LEFT、 2=RIGHT、 1|2=FULL) で 3 つのケースをエレガントに扱う。

ai_analysis.technical_context: join handles many-to-many (M:N) joins via fseek backtracking. When key matches: emit all combinations within the matching group of file2 against current file1 line. Then for next file1 records with same key, fseek file2 back to bot2 (group start) and replay. When file1 moves to a new key, fseek to top2 (past matched group) to skip already-processed file2 entries. Result: Cartesian product of matching groups. Requires file2 to be seekable (regular file, not pipe). GNU join later added memory buffering to support pipes. The seekable-file requirement is the main practical limitation of Bell-32V join — but it makes the algorithm work in O(N+M+K) time and constant memory.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does join handle duplicate keys on both sides? Backtracking. When file1 line matches file2 line, emit all file2 entries with this key (advance file2). When file2 group ends, rewind file2 to group start (fseek bot2), advance file1. If file1 still has same key, replay through file2 group. When file1 moves on, skip file2's matched group (fseek top2). Result: every (file1, file2) pair with same key gets emitted — Cartesian product within key groups. Needs file2 to be seekable (a real file, not a pipe). Modern GNU join buffers file2 in memory to allow pipe input. 1979 simplicity: just use the filesystem.

appraiser_note: join は **多対多 (M:N) join** を fseek バックトラッキングで処理。 キーマッチ時: 現在の file1 行に対して file2 のマッチグループの全エントリを出力 (file2 を進める)。 次の file1 レコードが同じキーなら、 file2 を bot2 (グループ開始) に **fseek で戻して**再生。 file1 が新しいキーに移ったら、 top2 (マッチグループ後) に fseek して既処理エントリをスキップ。 結果: マッチグループの **Cartesian product**。 file2 が seek 可能必須 (通常ファイル、 pipe 不可)。 GNU join はメモリバッファリングで pipe サポート追加。 **seek 可能ファイル要件**が Bell-32V join の主な実用制約——でも O(N+M+K) 時間 + 定数メモリで動く。 1979 年のシンプルさ: filesystem を使うだけ。

ai_analysis.technical_context: join (1979) implements relational JOIN operations 7-13 years before SQL standardization: INNER JOIN (default), LEFT (-a1) and RIGHT (-a2) OUTER (SQL-86), FULL OUTER (-a) (SQL-92), column projection (-o, SQL-86 SELECT), NULL substitution (-e, SQL COALESCE). Codd's relational algebra paper (1970) provided theory; join provided first practical implementation. Limitations vs SQL: single-column join only (no composite keys), equi-join only (no theta-joins like a.x < b.x), no built-in aggregation (use awk for that). But the basic JOIN concept and OUTER variants are all here. Combined with sort + uniq + awk, Unix shell provided SQL-like data processing throughout 1980s — many companies ran payroll, inventory, billing this way.
ai_analysis.human_story: join is SQL's grandfather. INNER JOIN, LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER JOIN, column projection, NULL handling — all in Unix join (1979) before SQL standardized them (1986/1992). Codd's relational algebra paper (1970) had the theory; join made it practical. 1980s companies ran their data processing in shell: sort + join + awk = SQL. RDBMSs were expensive and limited; Unix tools were free and composable. join's design (text as relation, sort then join, OUTER for missing) shaped how people thought about data. SQL added composite keys, theta-joins, aggregation. But the JOIN core is from 1979 Unix.

appraiser_note: join (1979) は **SQL 標準化 7-13 年前にリレーショナル JOIN 演算を実装**: INNER JOIN (デフォルト)、 LEFT (-a1) と RIGHT (-a2) OUTER (SQL-86)、 FULL OUTER (-a) (SQL-92)、 カラム射影 (-o、 SQL-86 SELECT)、 NULL 置換 (-e、 SQL COALESCE)。 Codd のリレーショナル代数論文 (1970) が理論を提供、 join が**最初の実用実装**。 SQL との制限: 単一カラム join のみ (複合キー不可)、 等価 join のみ (a.x < b.x のような theta-join 不可)、 組み込み集約なし (awk 使用)。 でも JOIN 基本概念と OUTER variants は全て揃う。 sort + uniq + awk と組み合わせ、 Unix shell は 1980 年代を通じて SQL ライクなデータ処理を提供——多くの企業が payroll、 inventory、 billing をこの方式で運用。 SQL が複合キー、 theta-join、 集約を追加したが、 **JOIN コアは 1979 年 Unix から**。 join は SQL の祖父。

ai_analysis.technical_context: sort uses external merge sort: read until 32KB memory full, qsort in-memory, write to temp file, repeat. MEM=32KB tuned for 1979 PDP-11/VAX. lspace holds line pointers, tspace holds line text. Allocation via sbrk: grow data segment until brk succeeds or fails (reduce by 512 each retry). Each pass produces one sorted temp file. For large inputs, many temp files created. Then merge() phase combines them. This 'chunks of in-memory sort + N-way merge' is the classical external sort, taught in every algorithms course. Knuth TAOCP Vol 3 (1973) describes the theory; Bell-32V sort is the canonical Unix implementation. Modern sort (GNU, busybox) uses bigger memory but same algorithm. MapReduce shuffle is conceptually the same applied to distributed nodes.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you sort a file bigger than your memory? Knuth taught us in 1973: sort chunks that fit in memory, write them to disk, then merge. Bell-32V's sort (1979) does this: 32KB at a time, qsort, dump to /tmp/stmXXXXXaa. Read more, qsort, dump. Then merge() walks the temp files together to produce the final sorted output. PDP-11 had 256KB RAM; sort took 32KB of it for working memory and could process arbitrary-size files. The same algorithm runs today in GNU sort (with more memory) and in Hadoop/Spark (distributed across nodes). External merge sort is timeless because the I/O cost dominates — minimize disk seeks via sequential read+write.

appraiser_note: sort は **外部マージソート**を使用: 32KB メモリが満ちるまで読む、 qsort で in-memory ソート、 temp ファイルに書く、 繰り返し。 MEM=32KB は 1979 年 PDP-11/VAX 向けチューニング。 lspace がライン配列、 tspace がテキスト本体。 sbrk でメモリ確保——brk 成功するまで、 失敗時は 512 バイト減らしてリトライ。 各パスが 1 つの sorted temp ファイル生成。 大入力で多数 temp ファイル、 次の merge() フェーズで結合。 「**メモリに収まるチャンクごと in-memory sort + N-way マージ**」 が古典的 external sort、 アルゴリズム教科書必修。 Knuth TAOCP Vol 3 (1973) が理論記述、 Bell-32V sort が **canonical Unix 実装**。 現代 sort (GNU、 busybox) はメモリ多いが同じアルゴリズム。 MapReduce shuffle は分散ノードへの同じ適用。 I/O コストが支配的なので、 sequential read+write を最大化する古典手法が今も最適。

ai_analysis.technical_context: sort's merge phase uses N=7 way merge to combine sorted temp files. struct merg holds current line + FILE* per stream. qsort orders ibuf[] by current line value; output smallest; advance that stream. For 100+ temp files, multi-pass merging: combine 7 → 1, repeat until ≤7 files remain, final merge to stdout. N=7 chosen to balance: more streams = fewer passes, but more memory and slower qsort per output. Modern sort uses heap (priority queue) for O(log N) per output instead of qsort's O(N log N), and dynamic memory allows much larger N. But 1979's N=7 + multi-pass works correctly for any input size — just takes log_7(N_files) passes.
ai_analysis.human_story: After sort dumps temp files, merge combines them. N=7 way: keep current line from 7 sorted streams, output smallest, advance. Why 7? Balance: more streams = fewer merge passes = less I/O; but more streams = more memory and slower per-output sort. 7 was the 1979 sweet spot. If you have 100 temp files: pass 1 merges 7→1 (14 outputs + 2 remainders), pass 2 merges those 7→1 again, etc. log_7(100) ≈ 2.4 passes. Modern sort uses bigger N and heap-based merge, but the multi-pass merge concept is unchanged. Hadoop shuffle does this across machines instead of files.

appraiser_note: sort の merge フェーズが **N=7 way merge** で sorted temp ファイルを結合。 struct merg が現在行 + FILE* per stream。 qsort で ibuf[] を現在行値順に並べ、 最小値を出力、 そのストリームを進める。 100+ temp ファイル向けには **多段 merge**: 7 → 1 を繰り返し、 ≤7 ファイルになったら stdout へ最終 merge。 N=7 はバランス選択: streams 多 = passes 少 = I/O 少、 でも streams 多 = メモリ大 + qsort 遅。 7 が 1979 年の最適点。 現代 sort は heap (priority queue) で O(log N) per output、 動的メモリで N 大幅増。 でも 1979 年の N=7 + 多段は任意サイズ入力に対応——log_7(N) パス必要。 100 temp ファイル: log_7(100) ≈ 2.4 パス。 Hadoop shuffle は files ではなくマシン間で同じことを行う。

ai_analysis.technical_context: sort supports multi-field keys via +pos -pos syntax. +pos = start (inclusive), -pos = end (exclusive). field() parses 'major.minor' position with modifiers: b (skip leading blanks), n (numeric), r (reverse), f (fold case), i (ignore non-printable), d (dictionary). Multiple keys via multiple +pos -pos pairs: primary, secondary, tertiary. struct field array holds per-key config. NF=10 max keys. POSIX (1992) deprecated this in favor of -k pos1,pos2 (with 1-origin); but +pos -pos is still supported by GNU sort for compatibility. The 1979 syntax is awkward but expressive: every modifier per key, half-open interval. Modern equivalent: SQL ORDER BY col1 ASC, col2 DESC.
ai_analysis.human_story: Sort by 3rd column numerically, then by 1st column ascending? `sort +2n -3 +0 -1 file`. The +pos -pos syntax (0-origin, exclusive end) is Bell-32V's original. POSIX changed to -k 3n,3 -k 1,1 (1-origin, inclusive). Both work in GNU sort. Per-key modifiers: n (numeric), r (reverse), f (fold), b (skip blanks), i (ignore non-print), d (dictionary). 1979 had the full set; SQL added similar in ORDER BY 7 years later. NF=10 keys max — typical sort jobs use 1-3 keys, so plenty. struct field captures all options per key — clean data design.

appraiser_note: sort は **複数フィールドキー**を +pos -pos 構文でサポート。 +pos = start (inclusive)、 -pos = end (exclusive)。 field() が 'major.minor' 位置 + modifier をパース: b (空白 skip)、 n (数値)、 r (逆順)、 f (fold case)、 i (非印字無視)、 d (辞書順)。 複数キーは +pos -pos ペアを複数指定: primary、 secondary、 tertiary。 struct field 配列に key 毎 config 保存。 NF=10 最大キー数。 **POSIX (1992) で非推奨化、 -k pos1,pos2** (1-origin) に置換。 GNU sort 互換のため +pos -pos も残存。 1979 構文は awkward だが expressive: key 毎の全 modifier、 半開区間。 現代等価: SQL ORDER BY col1 ASC, col2 DESC。 7 年遅れて SQL に同じ機能。

ai_analysis.technical_context: sort uses 256-byte lookup tables for character transformations: fold[] maps lowercase to uppercase (for -f case-insensitive), nofold[] is identity, nonprint[] marks ignore-if-1 (for -i), dict[] keeps only alphanumeric+space (for -d). Comparison uses code[*p] - code[*q] for O(1) per-char transformation. Table-driven design is faster than if/else chains and easily extensible. Modern equivalents: locale collation tables (LC_COLLATE), Unicode collation algorithm tables. The lookup-table-per-rule pattern is reused across Unix: dd's EBCDIC tables (#159), ar's mode strings (#156), getty's parity table (#164), and now sort's collation tables. 'Encode behavior in data, not code.'
ai_analysis.human_story: How does `sort -f` (case-insensitive) work? Lookup table: fold[i] is the canonical form of byte i. For 'a' (0x61), fold[0x61] = 0x41 ('A'). For 'A' (0x41), fold[0x41] = 0x41 ('A'). Compare canonical forms: 'a' and 'A' are equal. For `-d` (dict order), dict[i] = 1 if char i should be ignored (punctuation), 0 if kept (letters/digits/space). Compare while skipping ignored chars. Table-driven design: change behavior by swapping tables, no if/else changes. Same pattern as dd's EBCDIC tables (1979 was the year of 256-byte tables). Modern locale handling extends this to Unicode.

appraiser_note: sort は **256 バイト変換テーブル**で文字変換: fold[] が a-z を A-Z にマップ (-f case-insensitive)、 nofold[] が identity、 nonprint[] が ignore-if-1 (-i)、 dict[] が alphanumeric + space のみ keep (-d)。 比較は code[*p] - code[*q] で **O(1) 文字単位変換**。 **テーブル駆動設計**は if/else チェーンより高速 + 拡張容易。 現代等価: locale collation テーブル (LC_COLLATE)、 Unicode collation algorithm。 lookup-table-per-rule パターンは Unix で再利用: dd の EBCDIC テーブル (#159)、 ar のモード文字列 (#156)、 getty のパリティ表 (#164)、 そして sort の collation テーブル。 **「動作をデータにエンコード、 コードではなく**」。 1979 年は 256 バイトテーブルの年——多くの Unix ツールがこのパターン。

ai_analysis.technical_context: sort enables the entire Unix data processing pipeline. uniq requires sorted input. comm requires sorted input. join requires sorted input. The Unix philosophy: small composable tools. sort+uniq=group+count. sort+uniq+sort=frequency analysis. sort+comm=set operations. sort+join=relational query. Without sort, downstream tools become awkward. With sort as foundation, shell becomes a data processing language. 1980s before SQL databases were ubiquitous, companies ran ETL via shell pipelines: awk extract | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head. Modern hadoop streaming and Spark are scaled-out versions of the same pipeline pattern.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does sort matter so much? Because comm, join, uniq all need sorted input. sort is the glue that makes Unix data pipelines work. Without it, you can't count uniques (sort | uniq -c), can't set-intersect files (comm -12 sorted1 sorted2), can't relational join (sort | join). The whole 'shell as data language' rests on sort. 1980s companies ran their data analytics via awk-extract | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head — top-N report in one line. Modern equivalent: SQL SELECT ... GROUP BY ... ORDER BY ... LIMIT. The 1979 shell pipeline beats SQL on ad-hoc ease for many tasks; sort makes it possible.

appraiser_note: sort は **Unix データ処理パイプライン全体の土台**。 uniq は sorted 入力必須、 comm は sorted 必須、 join も同様。 Unix 哲学: 小さな合成可能ツール。 sort+uniq = group+count、 sort+uniq+sort = frequency 分析、 sort+comm = 集合演算、 sort+join = リレーショナルクエリ。 sort なしには後段ツールが awkward に。 sort が土台になることで、 **shell がデータ処理言語に**なる。 1980 年代 SQL DB 普及前、 企業は ETL を shell パイプラインで運用: awk 抽出 | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head——top-N レポートを 1 行で。 現代等価: SQL SELECT...GROUP BY...ORDER BY...LIMIT。 1979 shell パイプラインはアドホックタスクで SQL より楽な場合が多い——sort が可能にする。 Hadoop streaming/Spark はこのパイプラインパターンのスケールアウト版。

ai_analysis.technical_context: uniq's core algorithm uses two alternating buffers b1[1000] and b2[1000] to detect adjacent duplicates. b1 = current group's representative line, b2 = newly read line. If equal: b2 is duplicate, advance. If not equal: output b1, then swap roles (now b1 is the new line, b2 is the prior representative). The dual-buffer pattern avoids memory copies — just read into the other buffer. Memory consumption is ~2KB constant regardless of input size — streaming design suitable for GB-scale sorted files. linec tracks group size for -c (count) mode. 1979's 1KB/line limit was adequate for typical text; GNU uniq uses dynamic buffers. The 'two-buffer alternating' pattern is also seen in tape/disk merge algorithms and double-buffered I/O.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you detect adjacent duplicates without storing the whole stream? Bell-32V uniq's answer: two buffers, b1 and b2. Read first line into b1. Read next into b2. If equal, b2 is a duplicate — read next over b2 again. If different, output b1, then the roles flip: b1 holds the prior line, b2 the new — output b2 when the group ends. Total memory: ~2KB, regardless of how big the input is. This streaming design is why `sort huge.log | uniq -c` works on GB files. The dual-buffer pattern shows up everywhere in systems programming — disk merge, audio DSP, network packet handling. 1979 already had it in 142 lines.

appraiser_note: uniq の **核心アルゴリズム**は 2 つのバッファ b1[1000]/b2[1000] を **交互に使い回し**で隣接重複検出。 b1 = 現グループ代表行、 b2 = 新しく読んだ行。 等しければ b2 が重複——進む。 異なれば b1 を出力、 役割反転 (b1 が新行、 b2 が前代表)。 **デュアルバッファパターン**はメモリコピー不要——新しい行を読むだけで前のバッファが残る。 メモリ消費 **~2KB 定数**——入力サイズ無関係。 1GB の sorted file でも 2KB で処理可能、 **streaming 設計**の典型。 linec がグループ内行数を追跡、 -c (count) モードで出力。 1979 年の 1KB/行制限は典型テキストに十分、 GNU uniq は動的拡張。 **「2 バッファ交互」 パターン**はテープ/ディスク merge、 ダブルバッファ I/O、 オーディオ DSP、 ネットワークパケット処理など系統的に再利用。 1979 年に既に 142 行で完成。

ai_analysis.technical_context: uniq's output filter is dispatched by mode variable (4 values: 0/u/d/c) inside pline(). Mode 'u' = unique-only (skip if duplicate flag set). Mode 'd' = duplicates-only (output only if duplicate). Mode 'c' = count (prefix '%4d ' formatted count). Default = output one per group. The 'uniq' int is set by equal() on match and reset by pline() after output — single-variable duplicate tracking. Each filter mode corresponds to a SQL HAVING clause: -d = HAVING COUNT(*) > 1, -u = HAVING COUNT(*) = 1, -c = SELECT line, COUNT(*) GROUP BY line, default = SELECT DISTINCT. uniq implements all four SQL group operations in one C switch statement, 14 years before SQL became mainstream. K&R C switch on ASCII char value is idiomatic for command-line flag dispatch.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why do uniq -u, uniq -d, uniq -c exist? Because filtering after group-by is the natural operation. Want lines that appeared only once? -u (HAVING COUNT = 1). Want only duplicates? -d (HAVING COUNT > 1). Want counts? -c (SELECT line, COUNT(*)). All four SQL GROUP BY operations in 142 lines of C, dispatched by a single char switch. SQL (1974 paper, 1979 first commercial) added the same in HAVING clause — but uniq beat it to the typical Unix shop by being free and pipeable. McIlroy's 'small tools, composed' philosophy in action: don't add modes to sort, add a separate uniq with its own modes. The mode variable being a char means case labels are ASCII letters — readable and matches the CLI flag exactly.

appraiser_note: uniq の **出力フィルタ**は mode 変数 (4 値: 0/u/d/c) で pline() 内で dispatch。 'u' = unique のみ (重複なら skip)、 'd' = duplicate のみ (一意なら skip)、 'c' = count (`%4d ` 右寄せ 4 桁 prefix)、 default = グループ毎 1 行出力。 uniq int フラグを equal() で立てて pline() でチェック + リセット——**1 変数で重複追跡**。 各モードが SQL の HAVING 句に対応: -d = HAVING COUNT(*) > 1、 -u = HAVING COUNT(*) = 1、 -c = SELECT line, COUNT(*) GROUP BY line、 default = SELECT DISTINCT。 **SQL GROUP BY 4 操作すべて**を 142 行 C の switch 文 1 つで実装——SQL 主流化の 14 年前。 K&R C の **switch (char) on ASCII** は CLI フラグ dispatch のイディオム——case ラベル = ASCII 文字で**フラグそのまま読める**。 McIlroy の **「小さなツールを合成」 哲学**: sort にモードを足すのではなく、 別ツール uniq に独自モード。 1979 年に **4 つの SQL 集計操作**が完成。

ai_analysis.technical_context: uniq supports partial-line comparison via -N (skip N fields) and +M (skip M chars) flags. skip() advances past the first 'fields' whitespace-separated tokens, then 'letters' more chars. equal() calls skip() on both buffers and compares the remainder. This enables 'compare only the substring' — e.g. uniq +20 ignores the leading 20 chars (a timestamp) and detects duplicate message bodies. POSIX 1992 deprecated -N/+M syntax in favor of -f N / -s M but both still work in GNU uniq for compatibility. The 1979 syntax overloads -<digit> with -<letter> for modes (mode = argv[1][1] when not isdigit), making the parser tricky. Concept ancestor of awk field selection, cut -f, and SQL substring().
ai_analysis.human_story: Need to dedupe log lines but ignore the timestamp? Bell-32V uniq's answer: -N to skip fields, +M to skip chars. `sort logs | uniq +20` ignores the first 20 chars (timestamp), finds duplicate message bodies. The skip() function walks past N whitespace-separated tokens, then M more chars, returning the rest for comparison. POSIX 1992 changed the syntax to `uniq -f 20 -s 20` (clearer), but the concept is identical. This 'compare a substring, not the whole line' idea is the ancestor of awk's field selection, cut -f, and SQL's substring()/regexp_replace functions. 1979 had it in 6 lines of skip() — minimal, expressive.

appraiser_note: uniq は **-N (フィールド skip) と +M (文字 skip)** で部分比較をサポート。 skip() が空白区切り N トークン進めてから M 文字追加スキップ、 残りを返す。 equal() が両バッファに skip() 適用して残りを比較。 **「部分文字列だけ比較」 ——** uniq +20 で先頭 20 文字 (timestamp) を無視、 メッセージ本体の重複検出。 **POSIX 1992 で -f N/-s M に置換** (より明示的) だが Bell-32V 構文も GNU uniq で互換動作。 1979 年構文は -<数字> と -<文字> でモード切替 (mode = argv[1][1] when not isdigit)——パーサ tricky。 **awk の field selection、 cut -f、 SQL の substring()** の概念的祖先。 1979 年は 6 行の skip() で minimal + expressive に実装——「**比較対象を絞る**」 という抽象を関数 1 つで表現。

text:
# The legendary frequency analysis idiom:
$ cat data | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

# Components:
#   cat data    : produce input lines
#   sort        : group identical lines together
#   uniq -c     : count occurrences (REQUIRES sorted input)
#   sort -rn    : reverse numeric sort (highest count first)
#   head -N     : top N

# SQL equivalent:
#   SELECT line, COUNT(*) as cnt
#   FROM data
#   GROUP BY line
#   ORDER BY cnt DESC
#   LIMIT 10;

# Examples:
$ awk '{print $1}' access.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10
#   Top 10 IP addresses by request count

$ tr -cs A-Za-z '\n' < doc.txt | tr A-Z a-z | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | sed 10q
#   Top 10 word frequencies (McIlroy's famous one-liner, 1986)

# Knuth vs McIlroy (1986, Programming Pearls Column 5):
#   Knuth: 10+ pages of literate programming in Pascal
#   McIlroy: ONE LINE of shell using sort + uniq -c + sort -rn

ai_analysis.technical_context: The 'sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -N' pipeline implements SQL's SELECT col, COUNT(*) FROM t GROUP BY col ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT N. Complexity: O(N log N) for sort, O(N) for uniq streaming, O(K log K) for second sort over K unique values, O(N) head. Memory: O(N) for sort (temp files), O(1) for uniq, O(K) for second sort. Without an in-memory hash table for the whole input, this scales to GB inputs. Famously demonstrated by Doug McIlroy in 1986 (Programming Pearls Column 5: 'Programming Style') as a one-line response to Knuth's 10+ page literate programming word-frequency program in Pascal. Hadoop MapReduce (2006) is the distributed version of this exact pipeline: Map emits (key, 1), Sort shuffles, Reduce sums = sort + uniq -c. Modern data engineering still uses this pattern in shell, awk, and SQL.
ai_analysis.human_story: The most famous Unix one-liner: `sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head`. Doug McIlroy wrote it in 1986 (Programming Pearls Column 5) as a critique of Donald Knuth's 10+ page literate-programming solution to word frequency analysis in Pascal. McIlroy: six commands, one line. Knuth: a book chapter. The lesson wasn't that Knuth was wrong — it was that Unix already had the building blocks (sort, uniq -c, head) and composing them solved the problem trivially. Every DevOps engineer who's done `awk '{print $1}' access.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20` to find top IP addresses is using McIlroy's pattern, which rests on the 142 lines of Bell-32V uniq. Hadoop's Map → Sort → Reduce is the distributed version of the same pipeline. SQL's GROUP BY + COUNT + ORDER BY + LIMIT is the relational version. The pattern is older than both — 1979.
