appraiser_note: **`sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -N`** は SQL の SELECT col, COUNT(*) GROUP BY col ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT N。 計算量 O(N log N + K log K)、 メモリは uniq 部 O(1)——**GB 入力にスケール**。 **Doug McIlroy が 1986 年 Programming Pearls Column 5「Programming Style」で Knuth に対する伝説的応答**として書いた——Knuth が word frequency analysis を Pascal の **10+ ページ literate programming** で実装したのに対し、 McIlroy は **シェル 1 行 6 コマンド** (tr -cs A-Za-z '\n' < doc | tr A-Z a-z | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | sed 10q)。 教訓は「Knuth が間違い」 ではなく「Unix が既に部品を持っていた」——sort + uniq -c + head を**合成するだけ**で問題が解ける。 **Hadoop MapReduce (2006) はこのパイプラインの分散版**——Map (key, 1) → Sort shuffle → Reduce sum = sort + uniq -c。 SQL の GROUP BY/COUNT/ORDER BY/LIMIT は同パターンの **関係版**。 1979 年の 142 行 uniq がこの全 ecosystem を可能にする土台——top-N analytics、 access log ranking、 word frequency、 すべてここから派生。

ai_analysis.technical_context: uniq completes Bell-32V's data processing toolkit: sort (902 lines, ordering), uniq (142, dedup/count), comm (166, set ops), join (214, relational), cmp (121, byte compare), diff (645, LCS), diff3 (438, 3-way merge). Total 2628 lines covering DISTINCT, GROUP BY, HAVING, JOIN, INTERSECT, EXCEPT, ORDER BY, LIMIT, WHERE, SELECT — i.e., the entire SQL data manipulation language, implemented as composable shell tools 14 years before SQL became mainstream. 1980s Unix shops ran payroll, inventory, customer reports, and financial summaries via awk+sort+uniq+comm+join pipelines, often cheaper and more portable than Oracle (released 1979 but expensive). SQL added structure (declarative, optimizer-driven); shell pipelines added flexibility (ad-hoc, streaming, composable). Modern data engineering still chooses between the two — and often combines them (SELECT into CSV, pipe to shell).
ai_analysis.human_story: By 1979, Unix had a complete data processing toolkit: sort, uniq, comm, join, cmp, diff, diff3 — 2628 lines of C covering every SQL operation. DISTINCT? sort -u. GROUP BY + COUNT? sort | uniq -c. INNER JOIN? join. INTERSECT? comm -12. All composable via pipes, all streaming, all working on GB inputs with constant memory (per tool). 1980s companies ran their entire business analytics this way — payroll, inventory, customer reports — because Oracle was expensive and Unix was free (in the workplace). SQL won the database market by being declarative and optimizer-driven, but shell pipelines won the ad-hoc analysis market by being flexible and composable. Modern data engineers use both: pull from SQL into CSV, pipe through awk/sort/uniq, push back. The 1979 family of tools is still the foundation.

appraiser_note: uniq が完成させる **Bell-32V Unix 1979 データ処理ツール群**: sort (902 行、 順序付け)、 uniq (142、 重複除去/カウント)、 comm (166、 集合演算)、 join (214、 リレーショナル)、 cmp (121、 バイト比較)、 diff (645、 LCS)、 diff3 (438、 3-way merge)。 **合計 2628 行**で DISTINCT、 GROUP BY、 HAVING、 JOIN、 INTERSECT、 EXCEPT、 ORDER BY、 LIMIT、 WHERE、 SELECT——**SQL の全データ操作言語を合成可能なシェルツールで実装**、 SQL 主流化の 14 年前。 1980 年代 Unix shop は payroll、 inventory、 customer reports、 financial summaries を awk+sort+uniq+comm+join パイプラインで運用——Oracle (1979 リリースだが高価) より**安価でポータブル**。 SQL は**宣言的 + optimizer 駆動**で DB 市場を制し、 シェルパイプラインは**柔軟 + 合成可能**でアドホック分析市場を制した。 現代データエンジニアは両方使う——SQL から CSV へ、 awk/sort/uniq でパイプ、 戻す。 **1979 年の 7 ツール 2628 行が今も土台**。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tr uses a 256-byte code[] lookup table for O(1) byte translation. code[i]=i means identity (pass through), code[i]=j means translate i to j, code[i]=0 means delete. Build phase: walks string1/string2 in parallel via next(), sets code[c]=d for each pair. Then fills identity (code[i]=i) for unset entries, or 0 if -d flag. Main loop is a tight 4-line getc/putchar with single array lookup — fastest possible byte translation in C. squeez[256] marks chars eligible for -s squeeze; vect[256] is temporary boolean used for -c complement construction. & 0377 (octal 0xFF) masks to handle signed char on PDP-11 where char defaults to signed. Total table memory: 768 bytes (3×256), independent of input size. This 'encode behavior in 256-byte table' pattern is the canonical Unix design idiom, shared by dd (EBCDIC), sort (fold/dict), getty (parity), ar (modes).
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you translate every 'A' to 'a' in a 1GB file? Naive: scan each byte, check if it's A, replace. Slow. tr's answer (1979): build a 256-byte table where code['A']='a', code['B']='b', etc., and code[i]=i for everything else. Main loop: read byte c, write code[c]. One array lookup per byte, no branches. This is the table-driven design that runs through all of 1979 Unix — dd uses it for EBCDIC, sort uses it for fold/dict, getty uses it for parity, ar uses it for mode strings. The lesson: when you have a finite, small input domain (256 bytes), encode the operation as data, not code. The loop becomes trivial; the table becomes the program. Modern CPUs love it: hot loop fits in L1 cache, perfect branch prediction. 50 years later, GNU tr still does the same thing with dynamic-sized tables for Unicode.

appraiser_note: tr は **256 バイト変換テーブル `code[]`** で **O(1) バイト変換**: code[i]=i (恒等)、 code[i]=j (i→j 変換)、 code[i]=0 (削除)。 ビルド段で string1/string2 を next() で並行走査、 code[c]=d をセット。 未設定エントリは恒等で埋める、 -d 時は 0 (削除)。 メインループは 4 行の getc/putchar + 配列 lookup——**C で書ける最速の文字変換**。 squeez[256] が -s 圧縮対象、 vect[256] が -c 補集合構築の一時 boolean。 **`& 0377`** (8 進 0xFF) で PDP-11 の signed char 互換マスク。 全テーブルメモリ 768 バイト (3×256)、 入力サイズ無関係。 **「動作を 256 バイトテーブルにエンコード」 パターン**は 1979 年 Unix の **canonical 設計言語**——dd (EBCDIC)、 sort (fold/dict)、 getty (parity)、 ar (modes) と同系統。 教訓: **有限小入力ドメインなら、 操作をデータにエンコード、 ループは自明に**。 現代 CPU でも hot loop が L1 キャッシュに収まり branch prediction も完璧。 50 年後の GNU tr も同じ (動的サイズで Unicode 対応)。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tr's next() implements 'a-z' range expansion as a lazy generator. struct string holds {p: remaining string ptr, last: previous char returned, max: range end (0 if not in range)}. On each call: if in range mode (max != 0), return last+1 until last == max. Else read next char via nextc(); if followed by '-', enter range mode with max = char after '-'. Edge cases: if max < last (e.g. z-a), return '-' as literal; if max == 0 (e.g. 'a-' at end), also literal. nextc() handles \NNN octal escapes (up to 3 digits, base 8) and \X literal escapes. Lazy expansion saves memory: 'a-z' encoded as 3 ints (p, last, max), not 26-byte buffer. For 'a-zA-Z0-9', eager allocation needs 62 bytes; lazy uses 3 ints regardless. This generator pattern predates Python generators (2001) by 22 years.
ai_analysis.human_story: When you write `tr 'a-z' 'A-Z'`, where does the expansion happen? In tr (1979), it doesn't — at least not eagerly. The next() function returns one character at a time, lazily expanding 'a-z' into 'a','b','c',...,'z' as needed. struct string holds three integers: current position in input string, last char returned, and range end. When next() sees '-' after a char, it enters 'range mode' and yields successive chars until reaching the end. This is exactly Python's generator pattern, 22 years before Python had it. Memory savings: 'a-zA-Z0-9' is 9 input chars, eager expansion would be 62 bytes, lazy stays at 3 ints. Edge cases handled gracefully: 'z-a' (reverse range) treats '-' as literal; trailing '-' treats it as literal. K&R C with goto and pointer arithmetic, 24 lines for a lazy character generator with octal escape parsing. Modern equivalent: itertools.chain + str.expandranges().

appraiser_note: tr の next() が **'a-z' 範囲記法**を **遅延 generator** で実装。 struct string が状態保持: p (残り文字列ポインタ)、 last (前回返した文字)、 max (range 終端、 0 なら range 外)。 動作: range モード (max != 0) なら last+1 を last == max まで返す、 そうでなければ nextc() で 1 文字読む、 次が '-' なら range モード突入、 nextc() で終端を max にセット。 エッジケース: max < last (例 z-a) → '-' リテラル、 max == 0 (例 'a-' で終わる) → '-' リテラル。 nextc() が **\NNN 8 進エスケープ** (最大 3 桁) と **\X リテラルエスケープ**をパース。 **遅延展開のメモリ節約**: 'a-z' は 3 整数 (p/last/max) のみ、 eager だと 26 バイト。 'a-zA-Z0-9' なら 62 バイト vs 3 整数。 **Python の generator パターン (2001) を 22 年先取り**。 K&R C の goto + ポインタ算術で **24 行**の lazy character generator + 8 進エスケープ。 1979 年に既に generator パターン完成。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tr's -c flag (complement) inverts string1: instead of operating on chars IN string1, operate on chars NOT in string1. Implementation: 2-step boolean-to-list conversion. (1) vect[c]=1 for each c in next(string1), marking string1 members. (2) Walk vect[1..255], collect chars where vect[i]=0 into vect[0..j], terminating with vect[j]=0. The same 256-byte buffer is reused for both marking and the complement list (different semantics, same memory). Excludes i=0 (NUL) since C strings can't contain NUL. Main loop dispatches: cflag=>iterate compl++ list; otherwise next(&string1). Set-theoretic operation: complement = U \ string1, where U = {1..255}. Equivalent to regex [^...], SQL NOT IN, awk !condition. The boolean-mark-then-collect pattern is also used in tr's squeez[] for -s, and in many other 'two-pass over bitmap' algorithms.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why -c (complement)? Because some questions are easier stated as 'all chars EXCEPT these'. `tr -cd '0-9'` deletes everything except digits — phone number from messy text. `tr -cs 'A-Za-z' '\n'` splits text into words by replacing non-letters with newlines (McIlroy's word-frequency one-liner). Implementation: mark string1 chars in a 256-byte boolean array, then walk it backward to collect unmarked positions into a list. Same buffer, different meanings: first a bitmap, then a list. Memory-efficient and clean. The complement concept appears everywhere in computing: regex [^abc], SQL NOT IN, set theory U\S, awk !match. 1979's tr -c gave shell users the set-complement primitive over byte values, in 9 lines of C.

appraiser_note: tr の **-c (complement)** フラグが string1 を反転——string1 **以外**を変換対象に。 **2 ステップ実装**: (1) vect[c]=1 で string1 メンバーを boolean マーク、 (2) vect[1..255] を走査して vect[i]=0 のものを vect[0..j] に積む、 vect[j]=0 で終端。 **同じ 256 バイト buffer を boolean → list へ役割切り替え**——メモリ節約。 i=0 (NUL) を除外、 C 文字列が NUL を含めないため。 メインループの分岐: cflag なら compl++、 通常は next(&string1)。 集合論的に **補集合 = U \ string1** (U = {1..255})。 等価: 正規表現 [^...]、 SQL NOT IN、 awk !condition。 **`tr -cd '0-9'`** で「数字以外を全削除」、 電話番号抽出。 **`tr -cs 'A-Za-z' '\n'`** が McIlroy word-frequency one-liner の最初の段——非英字を改行に+連続圧縮で**単語分割**。 1979 年に **9 行 C** でシェルユーザーに set-complement プリミティブを提供。 boolean-mark-then-collect パターンは tr の squeez[]、 bitmap アルゴリズム全般に再利用。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tr's -d (delete) and -s (squeeze) flags compose orthogonally with translation. -d: when -d flag is set, code[c]=0 instead of code[c]=d, marking c for deletion. Main loop's 'if (c = code[c] & 0377)' skips when result is 0 — delete is just a special translation to 'output nothing'. -s: build squeez[c]=1 for each c in string2. Main loop checks (!sflag || c != save || !squeez[c]) — output unless squeezing and same as previous output and marked. save variable holds previous output. Combining -cd gives 'keep only string1' (delete its complement). Combining -cs gives 'complement + squeeze' = word splitter idiom. Combining -ds gives 'delete then squeeze remaining'. All 8 combinations (none, -d, -s, -c, -cd, -cs, -ds, -cds) work coherently — flags are truly orthogonal. 1-line main loop handles all 8 cases.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does tr's 1-line main loop handle 8 flag combinations? Through orthogonal flag design. Delete is implemented as 'translate to byte 0' — code[c]=0, and the loop's `if (c = code[c])` skips zero values. Squeeze keeps state in `save` (last output) and squeez[] (which chars are squeeze candidates) — output the byte unless it equals save AND is squeezable. These two checks compose naturally with translation, with complement, with each other. `tr -cs A-Za-z '\n'` (complement + squeeze) replaces every non-letter with newline and collapses consecutive newlines — splitting text into words, one per line. This is the first stage of McIlroy's 1986 word-frequency one-liner. Eight behaviors in 132 lines because the building blocks compose. 1979's lesson: design primitives that combine, not modes that don't.

appraiser_note: tr の **-d (delete) と -s (squeeze) フラグ**は変換と**直交**して合成。 -d: code[c]=0 にセット (削除マーク)、 メインループの **`if (c = code[c] & 0377)`** で 0 をスキップ——削除は「**バイト 0 への変換**」 として実装。 -s: squeez[c]=1 を string2 の各 c に立てる、 メインループで **(!sflag || c != save || !squeez[c])** チェック——squeeze 中 + 前回出力と同じ + 圧縮対象なら出力しない。 save が前回出力保持。 **8 通りの組み合わせ** (なし、-d、-s、-c、-cd、-cs、-ds、-cds) すべて **1 行のメインループ**で動く——フラグが**真に直交**。 -cd で「string1 以外を全削除」、 -cs で「補集合+圧縮」 = **word splitter idiom**、 -ds で「削除+残りを圧縮」 。 **1979 年の教訓**: モードを増やすのではなく**合成可能なプリミティブ**を設計。 8 動作を 132 行で実現できるのは building blocks が compose するから。 McIlroy word frequency one-liner の最初の段 `tr -cs A-Za-z '\n'` がこの直交設計の極限活用例。

text:
# Doug McIlroy's famous 1986 word-frequency one-liner
# (Programming Pearls Column 5, in response to Knuth's 10+ page Pascal solution):

$ tr -cs A-Za-z '\n' < doc.txt | tr A-Z a-z | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | sed 10q

# Pipeline decomposition:
#   tr -cs A-Za-z '\n'   : split into words (one per line)  [stage 1: tr]
#   tr A-Z a-z           : lowercase                          [stage 2: tr]
#   sort                  : group identical words             [stage 3]
#   uniq -c              : count each                         [stage 4]
#   sort -rn             : sort by count desc                 [stage 5]
#   sed 10q              : top 10                             [stage 6]

# tr is the pipeline ENTRY POINT — transforms raw text into
# the format sort/uniq can process (line-oriented, normalized).

# Modern equivalents (all conceptually the same pattern):
#   Python: collections.Counter(re.findall(r'[a-z]+', text.lower())).most_common(10)
#   Hadoop: Map (emit word, 1) -> Sort shuffle -> Reduce (sum)
#   Spark:  df.selectExpr('explode(split(value, ...))').groupBy().count().orderBy()
#   SQL:    SELECT word, COUNT(*) FROM words GROUP BY word ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10

ai_analysis.technical_context: tr is the entry point of Unix shell data pipelines — it transforms raw text into the line-oriented, normalized form that sort/uniq/awk can process. McIlroy's 1986 word-frequency one-liner (Programming Pearls Column 5, 'Programming Style') uses tr twice: stage 1 splits text into words (tr -cs A-Za-z '\n'), stage 2 normalizes case (tr A-Z a-z). The remaining 4 stages (sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | sed 10q) implement SQL's SELECT word, COUNT(*) FROM ... GROUP BY word ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10. The whole 6-stage pipeline replaces Knuth's 10+ page Pascal literate-programming solution. Pattern preserved in modern systems: Python collections.Counter, Hadoop MapReduce, Spark groupBy. The 'tokenize + normalize + group + count + sort + limit' pattern is the canonical text analytics workflow, and tr provides stages 1-2. Bell-32V's 132-line tr is the foundation of every word frequency analysis, log parsing, NLP preprocessing, and text mining task that uses shell.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does McIlroy's 1986 one-liner start with tr? Because raw text isn't line-oriented yet. 'The quick brown fox' is one line, but contains 4 words. sort/uniq need words on separate lines to group them. tr -cs A-Za-z '\n' is the transformation: replace every non-letter with newline (complement), collapse consecutive newlines (squeeze). Then tr A-Z a-z normalizes case (or 'The' and 'the' count separately). Now sort | uniq -c groups identical words and counts. This 'tokenize → normalize → group → count → sort → limit' pattern is the canonical text analytics workflow, born in 1979 Bell Labs. Knuth wrote a 10+ page Pascal program to do the same thing. McIlroy's 6-command shell pipeline became more famous than the 10 pages — not because it's shorter, but because it composes existing tools that the OS already had. tr is the pipeline's mouth, eating raw text and spitting out structured data for the rest to digest.

appraiser_note: tr は **Unix シェルパイプラインの入口**——生テキストを sort/uniq が処理できる**行指向 + 正規化形式**に変換。 **McIlroy 1986 word frequency one-liner** (Programming Pearls Column 5「Programming Style」) は **tr を 2 回使う**: stage 1 で `tr -cs A-Za-z '\n'` で単語分割、 stage 2 で `tr A-Z a-z` でケース正規化。 残り 4 段 (sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | sed 10q) が SQL の SELECT word, COUNT(*) GROUP BY ORDER BY DESC LIMIT 10 を実装。 **6 段パイプラインで Knuth の 10+ ページ Pascal literate programming を置換**。 同パターンは現代でも: Python collections.Counter、 Hadoop MapReduce、 Spark groupBy、 SQL GROUP BY。 「**tokenize + normalize + group + count + sort + limit**」 が **canonical text analytics workflow**、 tr が stage 1-2 を担う。 Bell-32V の 132 行 tr が word frequency、 ログ解析、 NLP 前処理、 text mining——シェルを使うあらゆるテキスト分析の**土台**。 McIlroy の教訓: **既存ツールを合成するだけで OS レベルの問題解決**——書く必要なし、 OS が既に持っている。

ai_analysis.technical_context: wc collects three metrics (lines, words, chars) in a single pass over the input stream, with O(1) memory regardless of input size. The main loop reads one byte at a time via getc(), increments charct unconditionally, and uses a token flag to detect word boundaries (rising/falling edge of printable-vs-whitespace state). Long counters (4 bytes on PDP-11/VAX) support up to ~2 billion. Pure streaming: no seeks, no buffering beyond stdio, works on pipes. The whole loop is 14 lines. Modern CPUs run this loop I/O-bound at multi-GB/sec; CPU is idle. This is faster than sort (O(N log N)), awk (interpreted), Python (overhead), and even SQL COUNT (DB query plan). Single-pass multi-metric collection is the standard idiom for stream analytics — Hadoop reducer combine, Spark accumulator, time-series aggregation.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you count lines, words, and characters in a 1GB log file? Bell-32V wc's answer: read once, count three things at once. The main loop processes one byte at a time — increment charct always, increment wordct on word-start (token rising edge), increment linect on newline. Memory: a few longs. Works on pipes. Faster than anything else. The 'single pass, multiple metrics' idiom is everywhere now: Hadoop reducers, Spark accumulators, time-series aggregators, observability counters. But 1979 wc got there first in 14 lines of K&R C. The performance lesson: when you're already paying for I/O, harvest as much metric value as possible from each byte you touch.

appraiser_note: wc は **3 メトリクス (line/word/char) を 1 パス**で同時集計、 入力サイズ無関係に **メモリ O(1)**。 メインループは getc() で 1 バイトずつ読み、 charct を無条件インクリメント、 token フラグで単語境界検出 (printable vs whitespace の rising/falling edge)。 long カウンタ (PDP-11/VAX で 4 バイト) で最大 ~21 億まで。 **pure streaming**: seek 不要、 stdio 以上のバッファなし、 パイプ入力でも動く。 ループ全体 **14 行**。 現代 CPU では **I/O 律速で multi-GB/秒**、 CPU は idle——sort (O(N log N)) より、 awk (インタプリタ) より、 Python より、 SQL COUNT (DB クエリプラン) より速い。 **「**1 パス・複数メトリクス**」 イディオム**はストリーム解析の標準: Hadoop reducer combine、 Spark accumulator、 時系列 aggregation、 observability counter——すべて 1979 年 wc の 14 行から派生。 教訓: **I/O コストを払うなら、 1 バイトから取れる metric は全部取れ**。

ai_analysis.technical_context: wc's word boundary detection is a 2-state state machine: OUT (token=0, between words) and IN (token=1, inside a word). Transitions: OUT→IN on printable char (rising edge, wordct++); IN→OUT on whitespace/newline (falling edge). The 'count on rising edge' pattern is straight from electrical engineering — same as clock-edge detection in digital circuits. A word is defined as a maximal run of printable chars separated by whitespace. Consecutive whitespace produces no extra word (state stays OUT). Control chars (other than tab/newline) don't trigger transitions (continue preserves state). This minimal lexer pattern is the foundation of every tokenizer — compilers, SQL parsers, awk field splitting, yacc/lex. Bell-32V wc implements the pattern in 6 lines, predating formal automata-theory tools.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you count words in a stream? You can't store the input, so you need state. wc's answer is the simplest possible state machine: one boolean, 'am I currently inside a word?' When a printable char arrives and you're outside, count a new word and flip to inside. When whitespace arrives, flip back to outside. Done. The 'rising edge increments counter' pattern is borrowed from electronics — clock-edge detection, debounce circuits, frequency counters all work this way. Bell Labs in 1979 was a hardware-and-software shop; the engineers thought in signals. The same state machine pattern reappears everywhere: lexers (compilers, SQL parsers, awk), event-detection algorithms, sensor processing. 1979 wc shows it in 6 lines.

appraiser_note: wc の **単語境界検出**は **2 状態の状態機械**: OUT (token=0、 単語外) と IN (token=1、 単語内)。 遷移: OUT→IN は printable 文字 (**rising edge** で wordct++)、 IN→OUT は whitespace/newline (**falling edge**)。 **「rising edge でカウント」 パターン**は**電気工学由来**——デジタル回路のクロックエッジ検出、 debounce、 周波数カウンタと同じ。 単語 = printable 文字の連続、 whitespace で区切られる。 連続 whitespace は state OUT のまま (余計な wordct++ なし)。 制御文字 (tab/newline 以外) は continue で**状態維持**。 この最小 lexer パターンが**字句解析の基礎**——コンパイラ、 SQL パーサ、 awk フィールド分割、 yacc/lex すべて同パターン。 Bell-32V wc が **6 行**で実装、 formal automata theory ツール (lex/yacc) より前。 1979 年 Bell Labs は **hardware-and-software shop**、 エンジニアは信号で思考——同パターンが lexer、 event-detection、 sensor processing で繰り返し現れる。

ai_analysis.technical_context: wc's printable check `' ' < c && c < 0177` directly tests ASCII range 0x21-0x7E (printable, excluding space and DEL). Octal 0177 = 127 = 0x7F is the K&R C tradition (PDP-11 registers and addresses were displayed in octal). The author avoids isprint() because ctype.h wasn't standardized until ANSI C 1989 — in 1979, each Unix vendor had its own ctype implementation with subtle differences, so portable code used inline expressions. The 'low < c && c < high' pattern reads almost like math (low < c < high) and is canonical K&R style. Modern equivalent: isgraph(c) (ANSI C 1989) or isgraph((unsigned char)c) (modern POSIX, explicit cast). Unicode-aware: iswgraph(c). The 1979 expression is ASCII-only but semantically transparent.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why not just write `isprint(c)`? Because in 1979, ctype.h wasn't standard — each vendor's Unix had a slightly different version. Portable code wrote the test inline: `' ' < c && c < 0177`. It reads almost like a math inequality. The octal 0177 is K&R tradition — PDP-11 registers and memory addresses were displayed in octal, so programmers thought in octal. By 1989 ANSI C standardized isprint/isgraph/isspace, and modern code uses those. But for ASCII-only data, the inline check has zero overhead, no header dependency, and is more readable to anyone who knows ASCII codes. The 1979 wc shows the tradeoff: portability via simplicity vs portability via standards. In 1979 there were no standards, so simplicity won.

appraiser_note: wc の printable チェック **`' ' < c && c < 0177`** は ASCII 範囲 0x21-0x7E を直接テスト (printable、 スペースと DEL を除く)。 **8 進 0177 = 127 = 0x7F** は K&R C 伝統——PDP-11 のレジスタとアドレスが 8 進表示、 プログラマも 8 進思考。 作者が isprint() を使わない理由: **ctype.h が ANSI C 1989 まで非標準**——1979 年は各 Unix ベンダー独自実装、 微妙な差異あり、 portable code はインライン式。 **「low < c && c < high」 パターン**は数学不等式 (low < c < high) に近く読める、 canonical K&R スタイル。 現代等価: isgraph(c) (ANSI C 1989) または isgraph((unsigned char)c) (modern POSIX、 explicit cast)、 Unicode 対応は iswgraph(c)。 1979 年の式は **ASCII 限定だが意味的に透明**。 教訓: 1979 年は標準がなかったので、 **portability = simplicity**。 1989 年以降は portability = standards。

ai_analysis.technical_context: wc's output is controlled by a flag string. Default 'lwc' means print line/word/char in that order. User flags override: `wc -lw` sets wd='lw', printing only line and word counts. wcp() iterates the string char-by-char and switches on each, printing the corresponding counter. Output ORDER follows flag string order: `wc -wcl` prints word, char, line — order swaps with the flags. Duplicates are accepted: `wc -ll` prints linect twice. This is implementation-side-effect simplicity, not designed feature. The while+switch pattern is canonical K&R: `while (*wd) switch (*wd++) { ... }` — read char, dispatch, advance. Compare to modern argparse/click which are heavyweight but feature-rich. 1979 wc's 8-line wcp() does the work in a tiny fraction of the code. Pattern reused across Unix: ar mode strings (#156), stty flags, getty terminal setup.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does `wc -l` work versus `wc -lw`? The implementation is: a string 'lwc' (default) or whatever the user typed after '-', walked char-by-char. Each char is a case label in a switch — 'l' prints linect, 'w' prints wordct, 'c' prints charct. The flag string IS the dispatch rule. `wc -wcl` prints word, char, line — order matches flags. `wc -ll` prints linect twice (legal, no bug, just consequence of straightforward implementation). 8 lines of C handle what modern argparse libraries do in hundreds of lines. The trade: 1979 wc lacks --long-options, --help, type checking, error messages for unknown flags. But it does its job in 8 lines, fits in your head, no dependencies. The 'flag string IS dispatch' pattern appears across 1979 Unix: ar uses it for mode strings (#156), stty for terminal flags, getty for setup. The 1979 lesson: when the flag space is small (3 flags), strings are simpler than parsers.

appraiser_note: wc の出力は **フラグ文字列**で制御。 デフォルト 'lwc' が line/word/char をその順序で出力。 ユーザーフラグ `-lw` で wd='lw' に上書き、 line と word のみ出力。 wcp() が文字列を 1 文字ずつ読み switch で dispatch、 対応カウンタを出力。 **出力順序がフラグ文字列順序に従う**: `wc -wcl` は word/char/line を**フラグ通りに**出力。 重複も許容: `wc -ll` で linect 2 回出力——実装の素直さの帰結、 機能設計ではない。 **while+switch パターン** `while (*wd) switch (*wd++)` は canonical K&R——1 文字読み、 dispatch、 ポインタ進める。 modern の argparse/click は heavyweight だが feature-rich。 1979 年 wc の **8 行 wcp()** は同じことを fraction of code で実現。 同パターンが Unix で再利用: ar mode 文字列 (#156)、 stty フラグ、 getty terminal setup。 trade-off: 1979 wc には --long-options、 --help、 型チェック、 未知フラグエラーがない。 でも **8 行で頭に収まり、 依存なし**。 1979 年の教訓: **フラグ空間が小さい (3 フラグ) なら、 文字列 dispatch がパーサより simple**。

text:
# wc as the standard shell pipeline SINK (= aggregating endpoint):

# COUNT(*) idioms:
$ ls | wc -l                          # number of files
$ ps aux | wc -l                       # number of processes  
$ grep ERROR log | wc -l               # error count
$ find . -name '*.c' | wc -l           # number of C files
$ git log --oneline | wc -l            # number of commits

# COUNT(DISTINCT) idioms:
$ sort file | uniq | wc -l              # unique line count
$ awk '{print $1}' log | sort -u | wc -l  # unique IP count

# Aggregated stats:
$ wc -w doc.txt                         # word count
$ wc -c file                            # byte size
$ wc *.c                                # multi-file with total

# McIlroy pipeline + wc combinations:
$ tr -cs A-Za-z '\n' < doc | wc -w     # total words
$ tr -cs A-Za-z '\n' < doc | sort -u | wc -l   # vocabulary size

# TTR (Type-Token Ratio) in 3 shell lines:
#   TOTAL=$(tr -cs A-Za-z '\n' < doc | wc -w)
#   UNIQUE=$(tr -cs A-Za-z '\n' < doc | sort -u | wc -l)
#   python -c "print($UNIQUE / $TOTAL)"

# SQL equivalents (all derived from wc):
#   ... | wc -l                  = SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ...
#   sort -u ... | wc -l           = SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT col) FROM ...
#   grep P ... | wc -l            = SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ... WHERE P
#   wc -w file                    = SELECT SUM(WORDCOUNT(line)) FROM file

ai_analysis.technical_context: wc is the canonical pipeline sink in Unix shell — it consumes a stream and outputs a single number (or three). The '... | wc -l' idiom is SQL's COUNT(*) without a database. 'sort -u | wc -l' is COUNT(DISTINCT). 'grep PATTERN | wc -l' is COUNT(*) WHERE. wc -w on tokenized text gives SUM(LEN_WORDS). Combined with tr's word splitter, wc enables TTR (type-token ratio) analysis in 3 shell lines — natural language analytics from 1979. wc -l is I/O-bound, multi-GB/sec on modern hardware, faster than awk END{print NR}, Python sum(1 for _), or SQL COUNT(*). The pattern 'pipeline | wc -l' is so common it's a Unix idiom — CI/CD line counting, security audit log counts, devops process counts, data science quick checks — all derive from the 1979 86-line wc. Modern equivalents: SQL COUNT, Spark count(), Hadoop reducer, pandas len() — but wc -l predates and outperforms them all for line counting.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why is `| wc -l` so universal? Because most shell queries end with 'how many?'. How many files? `ls | wc -l`. How many errors? `grep ERROR log | wc -l`. How many unique IPs? `awk '{print $1}' log | sort -u | wc -l`. wc is the Unix COUNT(*), and the pipe is the FROM. Combined with grep (WHERE), sort -u (DISTINCT), and tr (tokenize), shell becomes a query language for plain text. 1979's wc enabled this 14 years before SQL became ubiquitous. Modern data engineering uses the same pattern: Spark.count(), pandas len(), SQL COUNT(*) — all derived. But wc -l on a 1GB log is faster than any of them because it does nothing but count: no schema, no query plan, no parsing — just getc and increment. The 86 lines of K&R C are why every devops dashboard, every CI line-count check, every security audit, every quick 'is this thing big or small?' uses wc.

appraiser_note: wc は **Unix シェルパイプラインの canonical sink**——ストリームを consume して**1 つの数値** (または 3 つ) に集約。 **`... | wc -l` イディオム**が DB なしの SQL COUNT(*)、 `sort -u | wc -l` が COUNT(DISTINCT)、 `grep P | wc -l` が COUNT(*) WHERE。 wc -w が tokenized text に対し SUM(LEN_WORDS)。 tr の word splitter と組み合わせで **TTR (Type-Token Ratio) 分析がシェル 3 行で**——自然言語解析が 1979 年からシェルでできた。 wc -l は **I/O 律速、 modern hardware で multi-GB/秒**、 awk END{print NR}、 Python sum(1 for _)、 SQL COUNT(*) より速い。 **「pipeline | wc -l」 パターン**は Unix イディオム——CI/CD 行数チェック、 セキュリティ監査ログ件数、 devops プロセス数、 データサイエンス quick check、 すべて 1979 年 86 行 wc から派生。 現代等価: SQL COUNT、 Spark count()、 Hadoop reducer、 pandas len()——でも wc -l がそれら全てに先行 + 行カウントで凌駕。 **「単純に数えるだけ」 の極致**: schema なし、 query plan なし、 parsing なし、 ただ getc + increment——86 行 K&R C がなぜ全 devops dashboard、 CI、 監査、 quick analysis で使われるかの答え。

ai_analysis.technical_context: look implements binary search over a sorted file using byte offsets. bot=0 (start), top=ftell(dfile) after fseek-to-end (file size), mid=(top+bot)/2. Each step: fseek to mid, sync to next line boundary, read a word, compare to key, narrow range to upper or lower half. Convergence: top<=mid terminates. For a 200KB dictionary (~25000 words), log2(200000)≈17 iterations find any word — vs ~12500 linear scans (~750x faster). Each iteration is 1 fseek + a few getc; OS buffer cache makes subsequent accesses near-instant. 1979 K&R C uses literal whence=2 (later named SEEK_END in ANSI C). This is the canonical application of Knuth TAOCP Vol 3 (1973) binary search to file I/O — before DBMS B-tree indexes (System R 1974, Ingres 1975 used different structures initially), this was THE way to do fast lookups in Unix.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you find a word in a 25,000-word dictionary fast? Linear scan reads ~12,500 entries on average. Binary search: 17 disk seeks. That's the 1979 look algorithm: jump to file middle (fseek mid), sync to next line, compare. Too small? Search upper half. Too big? Search lower half. Repeat 17 times, find any word. The trick that makes it work in 1979 (no mmap!) is fseek + getc + the OS buffer cache — the OS caches the pages, so repeated seeks into the same dictionary stay fast. This is Knuth TAOCP Vol 3 (1973) binary search applied to file I/O. Before DBMS B-tree indexes became commonplace, look was how Unix did fast lookups. Every modern database B-tree, every git packfile index, every binary-search-over-sorted-array, descends from this 17-line loop.

appraiser_note: look は sorted ファイルの**二分探索**を**バイトオフセット**で実装。 bot=0 (先頭)、 top=ftell(dfile) を fseek-to-end (whence=2) 後で取得 = ファイルサイズ、 mid=(top+bot)/2。 各ステップ: fseek to mid、 行境界に同期、 単語読む、 key と比較、 範囲を上下半分に縮める。 収束: top<=mid で終了。 200KB 辞書 (~25000 語) なら **log2(200000) ≈ 17 イテレーション**で任意単語発見——線形 ~12500 比較に対し **~750 倍高速**。 各イテレーション = 1 fseek + 数回 getc、 **OS buffer cache** で連続アクセスは memory-fast。 1979 K&R C は **whence=2 リテラル**を使用 (ANSI C 後に SEEK_END マクロ化)。 **Knuth TAOCP Vol 3 (1973) Binary Search の file I/O への canonical application**——DBMS B-tree index が普及する前 (System R 1974、 Ingres 1975 当初は別構造)、 Unix で fast lookup と言えばコレ。 modern DBMS B-tree、 git packfile index、 binary-search-over-sorted-array すべて 17 行のループから派生。

ai_analysis.technical_context: Binary search over a text file faces a structural problem: byte offsets don't align with line boundaries. fseek(mid) lands mid-line most of the time. look's solution: after seeking, read bytes until '\n' is found, then mid points to the next line's start. The cost is at most one line length of extra reads per iteration — negligible. This 'read to next delimiter to resync' is the canonical self-synchronizing data format pattern, applied in: B-tree page headers (find next entry boundary), JSON Lines / NDJSON (read to next \n), Kafka log segments (offset+length headers), Apache Parquet (read to next page boundary). The same problem appears in network protocols (TCP byte stream → application messages), where applications insert framing. The 1979 look's 4-line do-while is the minimal expression of this pattern — and it remains correct as long as no line spans more than one search step's worth of bytes.
ai_analysis.human_story: Binary search over text files has a problem: fseek(file_middle) lands mid-line, not at a line start. look's fix is so simple it's almost invisible: after seeking, read bytes until you hit '\n', then mid points to the next line. 4 lines of C. The cost: a few extra getc calls per iteration, negligible. This 'random offset → record boundary via resync' pattern is everywhere now: JSON Lines binary search (seek + readline), B-tree page boundaries, Kafka log segments, NDJSON log searchers, even network TCP framing. The pattern is so fundamental it's hard to imagine doing without it — but 1979's look was one of the first to use it on disk files. The lesson: text formats are self-synchronizing if you have a unique delimiter (\n is unique because it can't appear in normal word characters). Binary formats need length-prefixed framing because no byte sequence is forbidden.

appraiser_note: テキストファイルへの二分探索の**構造的問題**: バイトオフセットは行境界と揃わない。 fseek(mid) はほぼ常に行の途中に着地。 look の解決: seek 後、 **`\n` まで getc で読み捨て**、 mid を次行の先頭に揃える。 コスト = 1 イテレーション当たり最大 1 行分の追加 read——無視できる。 「**random offset → record boundary 再同期**」 パターンは **self-synchronizing データフォーマットの canonical pattern**: B-tree ページヘッダ、 JSON Lines/NDJSON (seek + readline)、 Kafka log segment、 Apache Parquet ページ境界、 ネットワーク TCP framing (length-prefix)。 1979 年 look の **4 行 do-while** はこのパターンの**最小表現**——1 行が 1 探索ステップ分のバイトを超えない限り正しく動く。 教訓: **テキストフォーマットは unique delimiter があれば自己同期** (\n は通常の単語文字に現れないので unique)。 バイナリフォーマットは length-prefix framing が必要 (禁止バイト列がないため)。

ai_analysis.technical_context: look's compare() returns 5 values (-2/-1/0/1/2) instead of strcmp's 3 (negative/zero/positive). The extra states encode prefix relationship: -1 = s is prefix of t (key 'he' is prefix of 'hello'), 1 = reverse. This single function detects exact match, prefix match, and strict lexicographic order in one C function with 4-line nested ternary. The binary search uses cases -2/-1/0 to narrow upward (matches are higher or equal) and cases 1/2 to narrow downward. The forward scan after binary search uses cases -1/0 to print and case -2 to stop. Without this 5-value design, look would need separate strcmp + strncmp calls. K&R C's switch fall-through (case -1: case 0:) elegantly handles multiple cases identically. Modern equivalents: C++ string::starts_with(), Python str.startswith(), SQL LIKE 'prefix%', regex ^prefix — all derived from look's compare semantics.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does look's compare() return 5 values instead of strcmp's 3? Because look needs to detect prefix matches, not just exact matches. `look hello` should match 'hello', 'hellos', 'helloed' — anything starting with 'hello'. compare() returns -1 when key is a prefix of word ('he' is prefix of 'hello'), 0 when exact, -2 when strictly less, 1 when key has word as prefix, 2 when strictly greater. The binary search code then uses switch with case fall-through: case -2: case -1: case 0: all mean 'matches are at or above this position', case 1: case 2: mean 'matches are below'. One compare function does the work of strcmp + strncmp in 4 lines of nested ternaries. Modern languages added str.startswith() as a separate method, SQL added LIKE 'pre%', regex added ^anchor — all expressing the prefix-match concept that look's compare() captured in 1979.

appraiser_note: look の compare() が **5 値返却** (-2/-1/0/1/2) ——strcmp の 3 値 (負/0/正) より多い。 追加状態は **prefix 関係**をエンコード: **-1 = s が t の prefix** (key 'he' が 'hello' の prefix)、 1 = 逆。 1 つの関数で exact match + prefix match + strict lexicographic を **4 行のネスト 3 項演算子**で検出。 二分探索が case -2/-1/0 で上方向に絞り (一致以上)、 case 1/2 で下方向に絞る。 binary search 後の forward scan が case -1/0 で出力、 -2 で停止。 この 5 値設計なしには strcmp + strncmp 2 回呼ぶ必要。 K&R C の switch **fall-through** (case -1: case 0:) で複数 case を同一処理——elegant。 現代等価: C++ string::starts_with()、 Python str.startswith()、 SQL LIKE 'prefix%'、 正規表現 ^prefix——すべて look の compare() の semantics から派生。 prefix match の concept が 1979 年の **1 関数 11 行**に込められている。

ai_analysis.technical_context: canon() normalizes both the search key and each dictionary entry before compare(). Modes: -d (dict mode) strips non-alphanumeric chars via isalnum (treats 'don't' as 'dont' and 'state-of-art' as 'stateofart'); -f (fold) lowercases ASCII uppercase via arithmetic ('a' - 'A' = 32, adding to uppercase gives lowercase); -t<char> sets the terminator (default '\n', can be ':' for /etc/passwd-style files). Both inputs run through canon() then compare() — equivalence-class comparison. The 'normalize then compare' pattern predates Unicode collation algorithm (UCA, 1990s) by 11 years; the ASCII version implements the same idea for English text. Modern equivalents: Unicode NFC/NFD normalization, locale collation (LC_COLLATE), case-folding for case-insensitive matching, accent-stripping for diacritic-insensitive search. Database collation specifications (CI_AI = case-insensitive accent-insensitive) are direct descendants of look's -df mode.
ai_analysis.human_story: When you search for 'hello' in a dictionary, should 'Hello' match? What about 'don't' — is the apostrophe significant? look's answer: it depends on flags. -d (dict mode) strips non-alphanumeric chars, so 'don't' and 'dont' are equal. -f (fold) ignores case. -df is the standard spell-check mode. -t: lets look search /etc/passwd by treating ':' as field separator. Both the search key and each dictionary line run through canon() — same normalization on both sides, then compare. This 'normalize then compare' pattern is the ancestor of Unicode collation algorithm (1990s), which normalizes all of Unicode for international text. Database collation specifications like 'CI_AI' (case-insensitive accent-insensitive) descend directly from look's -df mode. The 1979 lesson: equality is defined by what you consider equivalent, and the equivalence class is selectable via flags.

appraiser_note: canon() が比較前に**検索キーと各辞書エントリの両方を正規化**。 モード: **-d (dict)** が isalnum で非英数字を除去 ('don\'t' → 'dont'、 'state-of-art' → 'stateofart')、 **-f (fold)** が ASCII 大文字を小文字化 (算術 'a' - 'A' = 32、 大文字に加算で小文字)、 **-t<char>** がフィールド区切り (default '\n'、 /etc/passwd には ':')。 両入力に canon() を通してから compare()——**同値クラス比較**。 「**正規化してから比較**」 パターンは **Unicode collation algorithm (UCA、 1990 年代) を 11 年先取り**——ASCII 版が英語テキストに対し同じ idea を実装。 現代等価: Unicode NFC/NFD 正規化、 locale collation (LC_COLLATE)、 case-folding、 accent-stripping。 DB の collation 仕様 (CI_AI = case-insensitive accent-insensitive) は look の -df モードの直系子孫。 1979 年の教訓: **等値は何を equivalent とみなすかで定義され、 同値クラスはフラグで選択可能**。

text:
# /usr/dict/words ecosystem (Bell Labs 1979):

# The dictionary itself:
$ ls -l /usr/dict/words
-rw-r--r-- 1 root  ~200KB  # ~25000 sorted English words

# look as the fast lookup primitive:
$ look hello                    # outputs: hello, hellos, helloed, helloing, ...
$ look -df Hello                # case-insensitive: same matches

# spell (McIlroy 1975) uses look internally:
#   1. tokenize document into words (tr -cs A-Za-z '\n')
#   2. lowercase (tr A-Z a-z)
#   3. sort -u
#   4. for each word: look -df word /usr/dict/words
#   5. if not found: report as misspelling

$ spell document.txt
misspelt
typo
recieve

# Modern descendants (all use sorted-dict + binary-search pattern):
#   aspell, hunspell        - desktop spell checkers
#   GNU look                  - POSIX standardized
#   IDE spell check          - VSCode, IntelliJ
#   MS Word, Google Docs     - same pattern, bigger dict
#   Spotlight, grep -F        - 'is this word known?' queries
#   DBMS B-tree index         - same binary search, tree-organized
#   git packfile lookup       - binary search over sorted SHA list

ai_analysis.technical_context: look is the fast-lookup primitive at the heart of the /usr/dict/words ecosystem at Bell Labs 1979. The dictionary is ~25,000 sorted English words (~200KB). look provides O(log N) word existence checks via binary search. spell (McIlroy 1975) uses look as its core: tokenize document, lowercase, sort-unique, look each word, report misses as misspellings — the world's first practical Unix spell checker. The pattern (sorted dictionary + binary search + normalization) is the direct ancestor of: modern spell checkers (aspell, hunspell), DBMS B-tree primary key lookups, git packfile object lookups (binary search over sorted SHA list), Bloom filters (probabilistic existence checks), full-text search inverted indexes (sorted term lists), Spotlight/desktop search. The /usr/dict/words file itself spawned thousands of derivative dictionaries; SCOWL (Spell Checker Oriented Word Lists) used in aspell descends from /usr/dict/words.
ai_analysis.human_story: What did Bell Labs do with /usr/dict/words? In 1976, Doug McIlroy wrote 'spell', the first practical Unix spell checker. Its core was look — for each word in the document, ask look 'is this in the dictionary?'. If not, print as misspelling. This was the world's first command-line spell checker, predating Microsoft Word's by a decade. The architecture — sorted dictionary file + binary search lookups + normalization — is the same one used in modern aspell, hunspell, MS Word's spell check, Google Docs' spell check, every IDE's spell check, and every full-text search engine's term index. /usr/dict/words itself spawned thousands of derivative dictionaries; SCOWL (used in aspell) descends from it. The pattern also generalizes: DBMS B-tree indexes are binary search organized in tree structure; git's packfile index is binary search over sorted SHA lists; Bloom filters are probabilistic existence checks for when even binary search is too slow. The 162-line look program is the seed.

appraiser_note: look は **/usr/dict/words ecosystem の fast lookup プリミティブ**——Bell Labs 1979 年の中核。 辞書は **約 25000 語の sorted English words (~200KB)**。 look が O(log N) で word existence check 提供。 **spell (McIlroy 1975)** が look を内部で使用: tokenize → lowercase → sort-unique → look each word → 見つからなければ misspelling 報告——**世界初の実用 Unix spell checker**、 MS Word より 10 年早い。 「**sorted dictionary + binary search + normalization**」 パターンは現代の直系祖: aspell/hunspell、 DBMS B-tree primary key lookup、 **git packfile object lookup** (sorted SHA list の binary search)、 Bloom filter (確率的 existence check)、 **全文検索 inverted index** (sorted term list)、 Spotlight/desktop search。 /usr/dict/words 自体が数千の派生辞書を生み、 SCOWL (aspell が使用) は /usr/dict/words の子孫。 **DBMS B-tree index は binary search を tree 化したもの**、 **git packfile index は sorted SHA への binary search**、 **Bloom filter は binary search より速い probabilistic version**——すべての種が 162 行の look。

ai_analysis.technical_context: pr's page layout is hardcoded as '2+head+2+page[56]+5' (file comment) totaling 66 lines per page. This is 11 inches at 6 lines/inch — the physical specification of US continuous-form line printer paper. length=66 (total), plength=61 (printable), margin=10 (header area). Header format: 2 blank lines + 1 line with 'Date Time filename Page N' + 2 blank lines + 56 content lines + 5 blank lines. The -t flag (ntflg) suppresses header/footer (plength=length, no sprintf). Header is built with sprintf into linebuf and walked char-by-char via put(). After content, padding newlines fill to 'length' boundary, ensuring next page starts at form-feed boundary on continuous paper. The physical hardware constraint is encoded in the source code — modern enscript/a2ps abstract this to PostScript logical pages, decoupling text formatting from physical paper.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why 66 lines per page? Because line printers in 1979 used 11-inch continuous-form paper at 6 lines per inch — 11 × 6 = 66. The pr comment '2+head+2+page[56]+5' encodes this physical reality: 2 blank lines top, 1 header line, 2 spacing lines, 56 content lines, 5 bottom margin. After printing, padding newlines bring you to line 66 — the next form-feed perforation. Then the next page header starts cleanly. The hardware specification is literally baked into the constants. This worked for 25 years until laser printers and PostScript broke the assumption — pages became logical, paper sizes varied, line spacing changed. Modern enscript/a2ps abstract this away: pr formats for physical 11×6 paper, while modern tools produce PostScript/PDF independent of physical dimensions. 1979's pr shows how tightly software was coupled to hardware before printer abstraction layers existed.

appraiser_note: pr のページレイアウトは **`2+head+2+page[56]+5`** (ファイル冒頭コメント) でハードコード——合計 **66 行/ページ**。 これは **11 インチ × 6 行/インチ**——US 連続用紙 line printer の**物理仕様**。 length=66 (全体)、 plength=61 (本文最大)、 margin=10 (ヘッダエリア)。 ヘッダ書式: 2 行空白 + 1 行ヘッダ (日付/時刻/ファイル名/Page N) + 2 行空白 + 56 行本文 + 5 行下マージン。 **-t フラグ (ntflg)** でヘッダ/フッタ抑制 (plength=length、 sprintf スキップ)。 ヘッダは sprintf で linebuf に組立、 put() で 1 文字ずつ。 本文後、 length 境界まで改行詰めで**連続用紙の form-feed 境界**に揃える。 **物理ハードウェア制約が source code に直接エンコード**——modern enscript/a2ps が PostScript 論理ページに抽象化、 テキスト整形と物理紙の分離。 1979 年の pr は **printer 抽象化レイヤ登場前のハードウェア-ソフトウェア密結合**を示す。 25 年間ワークしたが、 レーザープリンタ + PostScript 普及で前提崩壊——ページが論理化、 紙サイズ可変、 行間可変に。

ai_analysis.technical_context: pr's multi-column output requires reading data for N columns in parallel from a single file (each column N pages apart). Solution: a 6720-byte circular buffer with two sentinel bytes — 0375 (CONTINUE, 'load more here') and 0376 (END, 'EOF reached'). nexbuf() reads up to 512 bytes via fread, places sentinel at the next position. tpgetc() checks each byte: if 0375, call nexbuf() to refill; if 0376, return 0 (EOF); otherwise advance pointer with wrap-around. The sentinels are chosen to be outside printable ASCII (0xFD/0xFE) so they can't appear in normal text data. Each column has its own colp[i] pointer into the shared buffer — N pointers walking the same circular memory independently. This 'ring buffer with out-of-band sentinel bytes' pattern predates Linux kernel ring buffer (printk, ftrace) by 12 years, Kafka log segments by 32 years. Bell-32V pr embedded a userspace ring buffer in 421 lines of C, before 'ring buffer' was a common term.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you output 3 columns from a single file when each column is 56 lines apart? You can't read column 1 fully then column 2 — they're interleaved. pr's answer: a 6720-byte circular buffer with 'magic bytes' marking state. 0375 means 'this is where the loaded data ends, call nexbuf() to load more'. 0376 means 'EOF, stop'. Each column has its own pointer chasing through the same circular memory. The magic bytes are chosen outside printable ASCII (0xFD/0xFE) so they can't accidentally match real text. This 'ring buffer + out-of-band markers' pattern predates Linux's printk ring buffer (1991) by 12 years and Kafka's log segments (2011) by 32 years. The same idea reappears in: protobuf wire format magic bytes, Kafka magic bytes, DBMS sentinel records, lockfree queue with sentinel nodes. 1979's pr embedded a user-space ring buffer in 421 lines of C, before the term existed.

appraiser_note: pr の**多段組み出力**は単一ファイルから N カラム分のデータを並列に読む必要——各カラムは N ページ離れた位置。 解決: **6720 バイトの循環バッファ + 2 つのセンチネル**——**0375 (CONTINUE、 'ここに ロード追加して')** と **0376 (END、 'EOF')**。 nexbuf() が最大 512 バイトを fread、 次の位置にセンチネル配置。 tpgetc() が各バイトをチェック: 0375 なら nexbuf() で補充、 0376 なら 0 (EOF) 返却、 それ以外はポインタ前進 (wrap-around)。 **センチネルが printable ASCII 範囲外 (0xFD/0xFE)**——通常テキストと衝突しない。 各カラムが独自の colp[i] で共有バッファ内を独立に走る——N ポインタが同じ循環メモリを独立に walk。 **「ring buffer + out-of-band sentinel bytes」 パターン**は **Linux カーネル ring buffer (printk、 ftrace、 1991 年) を 12 年先取り、 Kafka log segment (2011 年) を 32 年先取り**。 同 idea が再出: protobuf wire format magic byte、 Kafka magic byte、 DBMS sentinel record、 lockfree queue with sentinel node。 1979 年 pr は **421 行内に userspace ring buffer 内蔵**——「ring buffer」 という用語が定着する前。

ai_analysis.technical_context: pr's multi-column output uses colp[72] as per-column buffer pointers — N independent positions walking the same circular buffer. colw = width/ncol gives each column's character width. The 'prime' phase advances colp[i] to skip ahead by (length-margin) lines × i, positioning each column at its start. Then the output loop weaves: for each output line, iterate columns i=0..ncol-1, read chars from pgetc(i), put them out, pad with spaces (or tab if -s tabc) to lastcol boundary. This is the in-memory transpose of N column-major page-sized streams into a row-major line-sequential output. The same pattern is the core of multi-way merge in external sort (#188), MapReduce shuffle phase, SQL FULL OUTER JOIN row-level, CSS multi-column layout, newspaper typesetting. 1979 pr implements 'merge N parallel streams row-wise' in ~30 lines of C with circular buffer state.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you print column 1, column 2, column 3 side by side from a single file? You read N pages worth of data, then weave them line by line: line 1 of col 1, padding, line 1 of col 2, padding, line 1 of col 3, newline; line 2 of col 1, ... pr's colp[72] array holds one pointer per column, each pointing into the shared circular buffer at a different position. The output loop iterates columns left-to-right for each line, then advances to next line. This is the same 'merge N streams row-wise' pattern as multi-way merge in external sort, MapReduce shuffle, SQL FULL OUTER JOIN, and modern CSS multi-column layout. 1979 pr did it in ~30 lines of C with circular buffer state. The key insight: you don't need N file descriptors for N columns — you need N pointers into a shared buffer big enough to hold the readahead window.

appraiser_note: pr の多段組み出力は **colp[72]** を**カラム別バッファポインタ**として使用——N 独立ポジションが同じ循環バッファを walk。 colw = width/ncol で各カラム文字幅。 **prime フェーズ**で colp[i] を (length-margin) × i 行分先送り、 各カラムを開始位置に配置。 **output ループ**: 各出力行で i=0..ncol-1 をループ、 pgetc(i) で文字読み、 put() で出力、 lastcol 境界までスペース埋め (or -s tabc でタブ)。 これは **「N カラム単位ページサイズストリームを行単位連続出力に in-memory 転置」 ——row-major 化**。 同パターン: external sort の N-way merge (#188)、 MapReduce shuffle phase、 SQL FULL OUTER JOIN 行レベル、 CSS multi-column layout、 新聞組版。 1979 年 pr は **30 行 C + 循環バッファ状態**で実装。 **重要 insight**: N カラムのために N ファイルディスクリプタ不要——**先読みウィンドウを保持する共有バッファへの N ポインタ**で十分。 1979 年が現代の column-layout に至る基本パターンを確立。

ai_analysis.technical_context: pr's -m flag (merge) handles multiple files as parallel columns. mopen() fopens up to 10 files into ifile[] array. tpgetc() in mflg mode bypasses the circular buffer and directly getc() from ifile[i] — each file = one column. EOF returns '\n' (empty column) for that line until --nofile reaches 0 (all closed). Form-feed (FF, 014) within file converts to newline since column mode prevents page breaks. The pattern is N-way file merging row-wise — same as paste -d (System III, 1981) but simpler API. Used for: side-by-side comparison (old.c vs new.c), parallel reports (Q1 sales/support/eng), human-readable diff (before sdiff/diff -y existed). The 10-file limit reflects 1979 PDP-11 file descriptor scarcity; modern pr handles many more. This is the ancestor of vimdiff/sdiff/git difftool side-by-side displays, BSD column tool, CSS multi-column layout for separate sources.
ai_analysis.human_story: Want to print three documents side-by-side on one piece of paper? 1979 pr -m does it: pr -m sales.txt support.txt engineering.txt. Each file becomes a column, lines weave together row-by-row. fopen each file into ifile[i], in tpgetc just getc(ifile[i]) for column i. When a file ends, that column becomes empty '\n's while others continue. Up to 10 files (PDP-11 fd limit). This was the human-readable diff before sdiff (1981), the parallel-report layout before spreadsheets were common on Unix, the 'compare versions side by side' before vimdiff. paste -d in System III (1981) is the cleaner descendant — same idea, simpler flags. Modern vimdiff/sdiff/git difftool side-by-side display all derive from pr -m. The 1979 lesson: when you want to compare or contrast N text streams, line them up in columns and let human pattern recognition do the rest.

appraiser_note: pr の **-m フラグ (merge)** が複数ファイルを並列カラムとして扱う。 mopen() が最大 **10 ファイル**を ifile[] 配列に fopen。 mflg モードの tpgetc() は循環バッファをバイパス、 直接 getc(ifile[i]) で読む——**1 ファイル = 1 カラム**。 EOF は '\n' (空欄カラム) を返し、 --nofile が 0 で全終了。 ファイル内 form-feed (FF, 014) は newline に変換 (カラムモードはページ分割禁止)。 パターンは **N-way ファイル merge 行単位**——paste -d (System III、 1981) より早いが API シンプル。 用途: side-by-side 比較 (old.c vs new.c)、 並列レポート (Q1 sales/support/eng)、 **人間用 diff** (sdiff/diff -y より前)。 **10 ファイル制限**は 1979 年 PDP-11 file descriptor 数の希少性反映、 modern pr は多数対応。 これが **vimdiff/sdiff/git difftool side-by-side、 BSD column ツール、 CSS multi-column** の祖。 1979 年の教訓: **N テキストストリーム比較/対照は、 カラム整列で人間のパターン認識に任せる**。

ai_analysis.technical_context: fixtty() prevents other users on the multi-user Unix system from interfering with a long printing job. ttyname(1) detects if stdout is a tty (interactive terminal output, e.g. to a serial-connected line printer); if not, returns. Otherwise stat() saves current mode (typically 0622 = own rw, others w), then chmod(tty, 0600) revokes group/other write permission. Long pr | lpr jobs can take hours on 1979 printers; without this, another user's `write` or `wall` command would corrupt the output mid-page. done() restores mode on normal exit; onintr() (SIGINT handler) restores even on Ctrl-C, preventing 'tty stuck inaccessible' bugs. The signal(SIGINT, SIG_IGN) != SIG_IGN guard respects parent shell's job control setup. This is the prototype of resource-acquisition-with-guaranteed-release: RAII (C++), defer (Go), try-finally (Java/Python), atexit() — all descend from this signal-based cleanup pattern. The modern `mesg n` shell command implements the same concept independently.
ai_analysis.human_story: It's 1979. You start a 2-hour print job: pr report.txt | lpr. Halfway through, your colleague runs `write you` and types 'hey, lunch?'. Without protection, that message appears mid-page in your printout, ruining it. fixtty() is the defense: detect stdout's tty (e.g. /dev/tty03), save current permissions (0622 — others can write), chmod 0600 (only you can write). When done, restore. The trickier bit: what if pr crashes or is Ctrl-C'd halfway? onintr() handles SIGINT, restores permissions before exit. Without onintr, your tty would be stuck at 0600 — even YOU couldn't write to it from another shell. This 'acquire resource, guarantee release on all exit paths' pattern is the prototype of every defensive programming idiom: C++ RAII destructors, Go defer, Java try-finally, Python with-statements, atexit handlers — all descend from the K&R C signal-based pattern shown here. The modern `mesg n` shell command implements the same idea independently.

appraiser_note: fixtty() がマルチユーザー Unix で**長い印刷ジョブを他ユーザーの干渉から保護**。 ttyname(1) が stdout の tty 検出 (例: シリアル接続 line printer)、 そうでなければリターン。 そうなら stat() で現在 mode (typically 0622 = own rw、 others w) を保存、 **chmod(tty, 0600)** で他者 write 権限を剥奪。 1979 年プリンタの長 pr | lpr ジョブは数時間——保護なしだと他ユーザーの `write` や `wall` が印刷物の途中に混入し台無し。 **done()** が正常終了時 mode 復元、 **onintr() (SIGINT ハンドラ)** が Ctrl-C でも復元、 「tty 詰まり」 バグ防止。 `signal(SIGINT, SIG_IGN) != SIG_IGN` ガードが**親シェルの job control 設定を尊重**。 これが **「リソース取得 + 全終了パスでの確実な解放」** の原型: **C++ RAII デストラクタ、 Go defer、 Java try-finally、 Python with、 atexit ハンドラ**——すべて K&R C の signal ベース cleanup パターンから派生。 現代の `mesg n` シェルコマンドが**同じ概念を独立実装**。 1979 年の教訓: 異常終了でリソースリークしない設計は OS-level プログラミングの基礎。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tee uses openf[20]={1} as a uniform fd table — array initialization places fd 1 (stdout) at index 0, rest zero-initialized. n tracks count, incremented as files are opened. Each file argument is opened via creat() (default mode: truncate-or-create 0666) or with -a flag via open(O_WRONLY) + lseek(SEEK_END) for append. The stash() write loop iterates 0..n-1 uniformly — stdout and files are indistinguishable to the loop. This embodies Unix's core philosophy: stdout is just another fd, no special-casing. The pre-O_APPEND-flag pattern (open + lseek SEEK_END) was canonical in 1979; POSIX later added O_APPEND for atomic append semantics. 20-fd limit reflects PDP-11 file descriptor scarcity. This 'uniform fd treatment' design appears throughout Unix: select/poll/epoll loop over fd arrays, signalfd/timerfd/eventfd unify event sources, /proc/N/fd lists all fds uniformly. Bell-32V tee's 20-element openf[] is the minimal expression.
ai_analysis.human_story: How does tee write to stdout AND files at the same time? It treats them all as the same thing — file descriptors. openf[20]={1} initializes the array with fd 1 (stdout) at position 0, the rest zero. Each command-line file gets fopened (or appended with -a), its fd stored in openf[n++]. The write loop iterates all n positions, calling write() on each — stdout and files indistinguishable. This is Unix's 'everything is a file' philosophy in 95 lines. The pre-O_APPEND pattern (open + lseek SEEK_END) was 1979 standard; POSIX added O_APPEND later for atomic append. The 20-fd ceiling reflects PDP-11 scarcity — modern tee handles many more. The same 'uniform fd loop' pattern shows up everywhere in Unix: select/poll/epoll, signalfd/timerfd/eventfd, /proc/N/fd. 95-line tee was the minimal implementation of this design idea.

appraiser_note: tee は **openf[20] = { 1 }** を**統一 fd テーブル**として使用——配列初期化で **fd 1 (stdout) を index 0** に、 残りは zero-init。 n がカウント、 ファイル open のたびインクリメント。 各引数ファイルは **creat()** (default: truncate-or-create 0666) または -a なら **open(O_WRONLY) + lseek(SEEK_END)** で追記。 stash() の write ループが 0..n-1 を**均一に**反復——stdout もファイルも区別なし。 これが Unix の **「すべてはファイル」 哲学**の体現: stdout は単なる fd、 特殊扱いなし。 **pre-O_APPEND パターン (open + lseek SEEK_END)** は 1979 年 canonical、 POSIX が後に atomic append のため **O_APPEND** 追加。 20 fd 上限は PDP-11 file descriptor の希少性反映。 「**uniform fd 処理**」 デザインは Unix 全体に: select/poll/epoll が fd 配列をループ、 signalfd/timerfd/eventfd が event source を統一、 /proc/N/fd が全 fd を均一リスト——tee の 20 要素 openf[] が**最小表現**。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tee detects tty and pipe outputs to dynamically adjust write chunk size. Detection: (1) fstat(1) checks S_IFCHR (character device = tty); (2) lseek(1, 0, SEEK_CUR) returning -1 with errno=ESPIPE indicates a pipe (not seekable); (3) each output file is checked similarly. If any output is interactive (t>0), stash() uses 16-byte chunks for low latency; otherwise full 512-byte buffer for throughput. Why 16 bytes? Balances write() syscall overhead (32 calls per buffer) against latency (16-byte granularity feels real-time on 1979 CRT terminals). Main loop adds 't > 0 && p > 0 break' to flush partial buffers on tty/pipe — interactive responsiveness. This is the precursor to stdio's _IOLBF (line-buffered for tty) vs _IOFBF (fully buffered for file) default. Modern equivalents: setvbuf() with line-buffering on stderr, tcsetattr noncanonical mode, Linux tty/pty layer. The lseek-ESPIPE trick to detect pipes is still used today; lseek on a pipe always returns ESPIPE.
ai_analysis.human_story: Why does tee write 16 bytes at a time to ttys but 512 to files? Because user experience differs. On a CRT terminal in 1979, 512-byte bursts cause jerky scrolling — text appears in chunks, not flowing. 16-byte chunks scroll smoothly. But files don't care about scrolling, and 32 write() calls per buffer is wasteful overhead — use the full buffer. tee detects this difference via fstat (S_IFCHR = character device = tty) and lseek (returns ESPIPE if target is a pipe). If any output is interactive, switch to 16-byte mode for the whole operation. This is the ancestor of stdio's line-buffered (_IOLBF for tty) vs fully buffered (_IOFBF for file) defaults — same idea, different abstraction layer. The lseek-on-pipe-returns-ESPIPE trick is still the canonical way to detect a pipe in 2026, used by Python, Go, Rust, and many others.

appraiser_note: tee は tty/pipe 出力を検出して **write チャンクサイズを動的調整**。 検出法: (1) **fstat(1) で S_IFCHR チェック** (character device = tty)、 (2) **lseek(1, 0, SEEK_CUR) が -1 + errno=ESPIPE** ならパイプ (seek 不可)、 (3) 各出力ファイルも同様にチェック。 出力に interactive あり (t>0) なら stash() が **16 バイトチャンク**で低レイテンシ、 なければ 512 バイトバッファ全部で高スループット。 **なぜ 16 バイト?** ——write() syscall オーバーヘッド (バッファ当たり 32 回) とレイテンシ (16 バイト粒度なら 1979 年 CRT 端末でリアルタイム感覚) のバランス。 メインループの 't > 0 && p > 0 break' で部分バッファも flush——interactive 応答性。 これが **stdio の _IOLBF (tty で行バッファ) vs _IOFBF (file で完全バッファ) default** の祖。 現代等価: setvbuf() で stderr を line-buffered に、 tcsetattr noncanonical モード、 Linux tty/pty レイヤ。 **lseek-ESPIPE による pipe 検出**は 2026 年現在も canonical——Python、 Go、 Rust 他が同じ手法を使用。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tee's -i flag installs signal(SIGINT, SIG_IGN) to make tee survive Ctrl-C. Why: in a long pipeline like `cmd | tee -i log | grep`, Ctrl-C sends SIGINT to all foreground processes. Without -i, tee dies mid-write, log.txt is truncated — data loss. With -i, tee keeps reading until upstream EOF, ensuring complete file output. The case 'i': case 0: fall-through handles both '-i' and bare '-' (no char after -) — bare - is a conventional 'read from stdin' marker in Unix utilities. signal() is the 1970s API (process-level handler); modern code prefers sigaction() for race-free semantics, but signal() suffices for SIG_IGN which has no race window. The 'robust intermediate pipeline tool' design is the ancestor of nohup (1980), dtach, screen, tmux — tools that survive signals to keep operations going. Modern CI/CD pipelines (Docker, Jenkins) routinely use `2>&1 | tee -i build.log` to guarantee build logs survive even if the build is interrupted.
ai_analysis.human_story: What happens when you Ctrl-C a long `make 2>&1 | tee build.log`? Without -i, tee dies mid-write, leaving build.log truncated. With -i, tee installs SIGINT-as-ignored and survives. It keeps reading until make's pipe closes (EOF), then writes the rest and exits cleanly. build.log is complete. The case 'i': case 0: fall-through quirk handles bare '-' (no flag char) the same as '-i' — '-' alone is Unix convention for stdin marker. The 1970s signal() API has known race conditions, but for SIG_IGN there's no race because there's no handler to call. This 'robust intermediate tool' design is the ancestor of every tool that needs to survive process group signals: nohup (1980), dtach, screen, tmux. Every CI/CD pipeline that does `make | tee -i build.log` relies on this 1979 design choice for log preservation.

appraiser_note: tee の **-i フラグ**が **signal(SIGINT, SIG_IGN)** を install して tee を Ctrl-C から生存させる。 理由: `cmd | tee -i log | grep` のような長パイプで Ctrl-C すると、 foreground process group 全てに SIGINT 届く。 -i なしだと tee が中途で死に log.txt 切り詰め——**data loss**。 -i ありなら upstream EOF まで読み続け、 完全ファイル出力保証。 **case 'i': case 0: fall-through** が `-i` と `-` 単体 (`-` だけ、 後続文字なし) を同様処理——`-` 単体は Unix 慣習で stdin マーカー。 **signal() は 1970 年代 API** (プロセスレベルハンドラ)、 modern は sigaction() 推奨だが SIG_IGN は race window がないので signal() で十分。 「**頑健な中継パイプライン ツール**」 デザインは **nohup (1980)、 dtach、 screen、 tmux** の祖——シグナルを生存して操作継続するツール群。 現代 CI/CD パイプライン (Docker、 Jenkins) が `2>&1 | tee -i build.log` で**ビルド中断でもログ保証**——1979 年の design choice 依存。

text:
# tee's mathematical essence: identity function with side effect

# Normal filter:    f(input) = output       (transformation)
# tee:              f(input) = input        (identity)
#                              + side_effect (write to file)

# This makes tee transparent in pipelines:

$ command | tee file | next
#         |          ↘ side effect: file <- input
#         ↘ passthrough: input -> stdout -> next

# Chain multiple observations:
$ data | tr A-Z a-z | tee /tmp/step1 \
       | sort | tee /tmp/step2 \
       | uniq -c | tee /tmp/step3 \
       | sort -rn | head

# Each /tmp/stepN captures intermediate state without breaking the pipe.

# Modern descendants:
#   Haskell IO monad:   tap-and-pass pattern via >>= and >>
#   RxJS:               .tap(side_effect) preserves stream
#   OpenTelemetry:      annotate-without-modify
#   AOP (aspect-oriented programming):  cross-cutting concerns

# Sudo + redirect problem (tee's killer app):
$ echo 'config' | sudo tee /etc/protected.conf
# > redirect happens in shell (not under sudo), so 'sudo cmd > file' fails
# tee runs UNDER sudo, can write to root-only files

ai_analysis.technical_context: tee is mathematically an identity function with a side effect: f(input) = input + write(file, input). Unlike grep/sort/tr which transform, tee passes the stream through unchanged while writing to file(s) on the side. This makes tee composable with any pipeline without changing semantics — insert tee anywhere to observe. The 'observe without modify' pattern is fundamental in functional programming (Haskell IO monad's tap), reactive streams (RxJS tap, Project Reactor doOnNext), distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry span annotation), AOP (cross-cutting concerns). tee's killer modern application is the sudo + redirect pattern: `echo config | sudo tee /etc/file` — shell redirect happens in non-sudo context, so `sudo cmd > /etc/file` fails for non-root users; tee runs UNDER sudo, can write root-owned files. This 95-line program is the minimal expression of side-effecting identity, predating functional programming's formal treatment of the concept by decades.
ai_analysis.human_story: What is tee, mathematically? An identity function with a side effect. It takes input and outputs the same input unchanged — that's the identity. But on the way through, it writes the data to one or more files — that's the side effect. This means you can insert tee ANYWHERE in a pipeline without changing what the pipeline does, only adding an observation point. Functional programmers later formalized this as the 'tap' pattern: Haskell IO monad's >>=, RxJS's .tap(), Reactor's .doOnNext(), OpenTelemetry's span.annotate(). Distributed tracing systems are essentially massively distributed tee operations. The killer modern application is sudo + redirect: when you write `sudo cmd > /etc/protected.conf`, the > happens in your shell (not under sudo), so it fails for root-only files. The fix is `echo config | sudo tee /etc/protected.conf` — tee runs UNDER sudo and can write. 95 lines of K&R C that became the universal solution to a problem that wouldn't exist for another 20 years.

appraiser_note: tee の数学的本質: **副作用付き identity 関数** f(input) = input + write(file, input)。 grep/sort/tr が変換するのに対し、 tee は**ストリームを不変に通過**、 ファイル書き込みは副作用として。 これでパイプライン**任意位置に挿入可能、 セマンティクス不変**——観測点だけ追加。 **「観測しつつ変更しない」 パターン**は関数型プログラミングの基礎: Haskell IO monad の tap、 RxJS tap、 Project Reactor doOnNext、 OpenTelemetry span annotation、 AOP の cross-cutting concerns。 **tee の killer modern application は sudo + redirect**: `echo config | sudo tee /etc/file`——シェル redirect は非 sudo context で起きるので `sudo cmd > /etc/file` は非 root user で失敗。 tee なら**sudo 配下で実行、 root-owned file に書ける**。 95 行 K&R C が**副作用付き identity の最小表現**、 関数型プログラミングが概念を formalize する数十年前に。 1979 年に「pass through, observe on the side」 という抽象を OS ツールとして実装。

ai_analysis.technical_context: tee's stash() is a 3-line nested loop implementing broadcast write — same data fanned out to N file descriptors. Outer loop walks the buffer in chunks of d bytes (16 if interactive, full buffer otherwise). Inner loop writes the chunk to every output fd via write(openf[k], out+i, ...). The chunk-synchronized order — fd[0] chunk N, then fd[1] chunk N, then fd[2] chunk N, then fd[0] chunk N+1, ... — ensures all outputs progress together. If the process crashes mid-write, all output files are partially-but-equally written, no one is far ahead of the others. This is the minimal implementation of broadcast / fan-out write, which appears in: syslog (broadcast to console/file/network), Kafka topic to multiple consumers, multicast network (1 packet to N sockets), CDN edge cache (1 file to N nodes), Splunk Forwarder. The 'd < p-i ? d : p-i' ternary handles the final chunk size (avoid over-read at end of buffer). K&R C compactness — entire fan-out in 8 lines.
ai_analysis.human_story: How do you write the same data to multiple files at once? Two nested loops. Outer: walk buffer in d-byte chunks. Inner: for each chunk, write to every output fd. Why this order — chunk to all fds, then next chunk to all fds — rather than full content to fd[0], then fd[1], etc.? Crash safety. If tee dies halfway through, all output files have the same partial content — none lags behind. The 'd < p-i ? d : p-i' final-chunk dance avoids over-write at buffer end. This 8-line stash() function is broadcast write, fan-out, multicast — pick your name. Modern systems use the same pattern: syslog forwarding to multiple destinations, Kafka topic with N consumers, multicast packets to N socket subscribers, CDN replication to N edge nodes, Splunk Forwarder, fluentd. Every log shipper in production is doing what tee's nested loop has been doing since 1979.

appraiser_note: tee の stash() は **3 行のネストループ**で **broadcast write** を実装——同じデータを N fd に fan-out。 outer ループがバッファを d バイト chunk で walk (interactive なら 16、 file なら full)、 inner ループが各 chunk を**全出力 fd** へ write(openf[k], out+i, ...)。 **chunk-synchronized 順序**——fd[0] chunk N → fd[1] chunk N → fd[2] chunk N → fd[0] chunk N+1 → ...——で**全出力が同期して進む**。 プロセス crash 時、 全出力ファイルが**部分的だが等しく**書かれる、 どれも先行/遅延しない。 これが **broadcast / fan-out write の最小実装**——syslog (console/file/network broadcast)、 Kafka topic to N consumers、 multicast network (1 packet to N sockets)、 CDN edge cache、 Splunk Forwarder で再利用。 **`d < p-i ? d : p-i` 3 項演算子**で最終 chunk サイズを処理 (バッファ終端の over-read 防止)。 K&R C のコンパクト性——**全 fan-out が 8 行**で。 modern システムでも本質は同じ: syslog forwarding、 Kafka topic、 multicast packets、 CDN replication、 fluentd——プロダクションの log shipper は 1979 年から **tee のネストループ**を続けている。

text:
#include <a.out.h>

#define MAGIC   exp.a_magic
#define BADMAG  MAGIC!=A_MAGIC1 && MAGIC!=A_MAGIC2  \
                && MAGIC!=A_MAGIC3 && MAGIC!=A_MAGIC4

struct exec exp;

main() {
    ...
    fread((char *)&exp, 1, sizeof(MAGIC), fi);      /* read magic only */
    if (MAGIC == ARMAG)
        arch_flg++;                                  /* archive */
    else if (BADMAG) {
        fprintf(stderr, "nm: %s-- bad format\n", *argv);
        continue;
    }
    fseek(fi, 0L, 0);
    ...
    fread((char *)&exp, 1, sizeof(struct exec), fi); /* full header */
    o = (long)exp.a_text + exp.a_data + exp.a_trsize + exp.a_drsize;
    fseek(fi, o, 1);                                 /* skip to symbol table */
    n = exp.a_syms / sizeof(struct nlist);
}

/* a.out file layout (1979):
   +----------------+
   | struct exec    |  ← 16-byte header
   |  a_magic       |    0407, 0410, 0411, or 0413
   |  a_text        |    text section size
   |  a_data        |    data section size
   |  a_bss         |    bss size
   |  a_syms        |    symbol table size
   |  a_entry       |    entry point
   |  a_trsize      |    text relocation size
   |  a_drsize      |    data relocation size
   +----------------+
   | text           |
   +----------------+
   | data           |
   +----------------+
   | text reloc     |
   +----------------+
   | data reloc     |
   +----------------+
   | symbol table   |  ← nm reads from here
   +----------------+
*/

ai_analysis.technical_context: nm parses Unix a.out object files: a 16-byte struct exec header with magic number (0407 normal, 0410 separate text/data, 0411 separate I&D, 0413 demand-paged), followed by text, data, text reloc, data reloc, and symbol table sections. The symbol table starts at byte offset = a_text + a_data + a_trsize + a_drsize from end of header. Each entry is sizeof(struct nlist) = 12 bytes on PDP-11 (n_name[8] + n_type + n_other + n_desc + n_value). nm computes the offset via fseek(fi, o, 1) (whence=1 SEEK_CUR, relative to current). The 4-magic-number scheme distinguishes loading modes — modern ELF uses similar magic + version + abi fields. The a.out format originated in PDP-7 Unix (1969); Bell-32V is essentially the PDP-11 V7 a.out adapted to VAX 32-bit fields. ELF (1989) is the modern descendant; nm's reading pattern remained the canonical template for object-file inspection tools (objdump, readelf, otool).
ai_analysis.human_story: How does nm know where the symbols are inside hello.o? By following the a.out format spec. 1979's struct exec header is 16 bytes: magic number + sizes for text, data, bss, symbols, entry point, relocation tables. nm reads the magic first (sizeof(MAGIC) bytes) to check validity — 4 possible magics indicate different loading modes (no separation, text/data, I&D, demand-paged). Then it reads the full header, computes symbol table offset as text+data+text_reloc+data_reloc, and fseeks there. Each symbol is a fixed-size struct nlist (12 bytes on PDP-11). The a.out format itself traces back to PDP-7 Unix (1969); Bell-32V's version is the V7 a.out adapted for VAX 32-bit fields. ELF (1989) replaced a.out but inherited the magic-number + section-headers + symbol-table architecture. Every modern object inspector (objdump, readelf, otool, IDA Pro) follows the template that nm established in 239 lines.

appraiser_note: nm は Unix a.out オブジェクトファイルをパース: **16 バイト struct exec ヘッダ** (magic number = 0407 normal、 0410 text/data 分離、 0411 separate I&D、 0413 demand-paged)、 続いて text/data/text reloc/data reloc/symbol table セクション。 シンボルテーブルは **byte offset = a_text + a_data + a_trsize + a_drsize** (ヘッダ末から)。 各エントリは **sizeof(struct nlist) = 12 バイト** (PDP-11)——n_name[8] + n_type + n_other + n_desc + n_value。 nm は **fseek(fi, o, 1)** (whence=1 SEEK_CUR、 相対) で offset 計算。 **4 magic 方式**でロードモード区別——modern ELF も類似の magic + version + abi field。 a.out 形式は **PDP-7 Unix (1969)** 起源、 Bell-32V は本質的に PDP-11 V7 a.out を VAX 32-bit field に適応。 **ELF (1989) が modern 後継**、 でも nm の reading パターンが **object-file inspection ツールの canonical テンプレート** (objdump、 readelf、 otool 全部踏襲)。

ai_analysis.technical_context: nm's defining contribution is the symbol type letter convention. n_type byte (masked by N_TYPE-N_EXT to ignore the external bit) is switched: N_UNDF (0x00) -> 'u', N_ABS (0x02) -> 'a', N_TEXT (0x04) -> 't', N_DATA (0x06) -> 'd', N_BSS (0x08) -> 'b', N_FN (0x1f) -> 'f'. Special case: N_UNDF with non-zero n_value becomes 'c' (common, Fortran-style). Then if N_EXT (external/global) is set, toupper() converts to uppercase: T/D/B/U/A/F/C. Output format: 8-digit hex address (blank for undefined) + single type char + symbol name truncated to 8 chars. This format became the universal standard — GNU nm, llvm-nm, Apple nm, all use the same letter convention 50 years later. Even though a.out was replaced by ELF (1989), the type letters T/D/B/U remain the lingua franca of symbol inspection. Any developer who has seen 'undefined reference to' linker errors knows to nm | grep ' U ' the object file.
ai_analysis.human_story: When you run `nm hello.o`, the output looks like '00000000 T main' — what do those letters mean? T = text (function code), D = data (initialized variable), B = bss (uninitialized variable), U = undefined (external reference), A = absolute, F = filename. Uppercase = global (visible to linker), lowercase = local/static (file-scoped). This convention was invented in Bell-32V nm's 1979 switch statement: 6 type values mapped to 6 letters, then toupper() for global. 50 years later, GNU nm, llvm-nm, Apple's nm — they all output the same letters. ELF replaced a.out in 1989, but the T/D/B/U letters survived because they were already the de facto language developers used to describe symbols. When a junior developer asks 'why does my code have undefined reference to foo', the senior engineer says 'run nm and grep for U'. That T/D/B/U vocabulary is encoded in 30 lines of 1979 C.

appraiser_note: nm の最大貢献は **シンボルタイプ文字規約**。 n_type バイト (N_TYPE-N_EXT でマスクして external bit を除外) を switch: **N_UNDF (0x00) → 'u'、 N_ABS (0x02) → 'a'、 N_TEXT (0x04) → 't'、 N_DATA (0x06) → 'd'、 N_BSS (0x08) → 'b'、 N_FN (0x1f) → 'f'**。 特殊: **N_UNDF + n_value != 0 → 'c'** (common、 Fortran 共通ブロック)。 N_EXT (external/global) が立っていれば **toupper()** で大文字化: T/D/B/U/A/F/C。 出力フォーマット: 8 桁 16 進アドレス (undefined は空白) + 型文字 1 + シンボル名 (最大 8 文字)。 **この出力フォーマットが universal standard 化**——GNU nm、 llvm-nm、 Apple nm、 50 年経っても同じ文字規約。 **a.out が ELF (1989) に置換されても、 T/D/B/U の文字は不変**——既に開発者の symbol 議論の lingua franca になっていた。 「undefined reference to」 リンカエラーを見た瞬間、 開発者が反射的に **nm | grep ' U '** する文化が **1979 年の 30 行 C** にエンコードされた。

ai_analysis.technical_context: nm transparently handles ar archives (.a static libraries) in addition to single object files. Detection: read magic bytes; if MAGIC == ARMAG ('!<arch>\n' header), set arch_flg. Then nextel() reads the next ar_hdr (member name, date, size) and updates 'off' to point to the next element. Main do-while loop processes each element: read exec header, validate magic (skip non-a.out members), dump symbol table, repeat. SELECT macro picks arp.ar_name (archive member) or *argv (single file) for display. arp.ar_size is padded to even bytes (ar requires 2-byte alignment between members). The 'open archive, iterate elements' pattern is the ancestor of: JAR files, ZIP archives, .so/.dylib bundles, tarball traversal, Docker image layers. Any tool that needs to inspect 'archive of objects' (jar tf, unzip -l, tar tf, docker image inspect) follows nm's nextel() pattern. The 13-line nextel() function is the minimal expression of streaming archive iteration.
ai_analysis.human_story: Want to know what's inside /usr/lib/libc.a? Run `nm libc.a` — it lists every .o member's symbols, with member names as separators. How does nm know to iterate? Magic byte detection. First it reads the magic; if it's '!<arch>\n' (ar archive marker), enter archive mode. Then nextel() reads each member's header (name + size + date), and the main loop processes them one by one. After each member, advance offset by size (rounded to even for alignment). 13 lines of nextel() implement archive iteration. This 'magic byte + header + iterate' pattern is everywhere: JAR files (zip magic + entries), Docker images (manifest + layers), Mach-O fat binaries (multi-arch + slices), even ZIP files. Anyone who has written code to walk a JAR's contents has reinvented Bell-32V nm's nextel(), 47 years later.

appraiser_note: nm は **ar archive (.a 静的ライブラリ) を透過的に処理**、 単一オブジェクトファイルと同じインターフェースで。 検出: magic バイト読み、 MAGIC == ARMAG ('!<arch>\n') なら arch_flg。 nextel() が次の ar_hdr (メンバ名、 日付、 サイズ) を読み off を次要素へ更新。 メイン do-while ループが各要素処理: exec ヘッダ読み、 magic 検証 (non-a.out メンバはスキップ)、 シンボルテーブルダンプ、 反復。 **SELECT マクロ**が arp.ar_name (archive メンバ) または *argv (単一ファイル) を表示用に選択。 arp.ar_size は **even byte に pad** (ar の 2-byte アライン要件)。 「**archive 開いて要素 iterate**」 パターンは祖: **JAR ファイル、 ZIP archive、 .so/.dylib bundle、 tarball 走査、 Docker image レイヤー**。 「archive of objects 検査」 ツール (jar tf、 unzip -l、 tar tf、 docker image inspect) すべて nm の nextel() パターン踏襲。 **13 行 nextel() がストリーミング archive iteration の最小表現**——47 年後の JAR walker もこれを再発明。

ai_analysis.technical_context: nm uses malloc/realloc to dynamically size the symbol array as it reads from the file. Each new symbol: if symp==NULL, malloc(1*sizeof(nlist)); else realloc(symp, (i+1)*sizeof(nlist)). This incremental realloc is O(N²) total cost (each realloc potentially copies all prior entries), not the modern amortized O(N) doubling strategy (capacity *= 2 when full). However, PDP-11 / VAX programs typically had ~100 to ~1000 symbols, so O(N²) overhead was negligible in practice.
